We ordered the local sardines in onions which were surprisingly very good.  We also ordered the local calamari and a lasagna.  The calamari were light, crunchy and not oily like in the states.  The lasagna was to die for.  We also had the panacota and lemoncelo which I would highly recommend.  The house wine was inexpensive and of high quality.  This place is totally worth the visit if your near by.  Highly recommended.

name: Joanna Pauline
review: Refreshing buzz in a quiet part of town. The food is generous with high quality ingredients and personable and attentive waiters. We loved their Extra Virgin Olive Oil from Monteroduni so much that we even brought a bottle home.

name: Tara Cluve
review: Amazing food, they even made us a special dish since we didn’t know what to choose, a homemade macaroni with some sort of green sauce, seafood and truffles, which was delicious. The people were so sweet and friendly, giving us suggestions on where to go next.

name: kiki Ng
review:
Disappointing experience despite high prices.
The prawns served were clearly spoiled. I raised this with the waiter twice, but each time I was met with dismissive responses, including an eye roll and “that’s how it is.” Even the replacements were in the same condition. Unfortunately, the poor attitude overshadowed the meal, and I believe this will affect the restaurant’s reputation if not addressed.

name: RE.Studio Wellness
review: We had the best dinner in all of Venice here. The best service. Our waiter made the evening. Don’t think, just book. This is everything you need and more

name: Mary Ann
review: Really love this place! Amazing staff, great food. We ordered a few dishes to share — ALL of them tasted so good. Drinks were ✨✨✨. Highly recommend to anyone who comes to Venice.

name: adela42
review:
Our bartender from Dona Palace recommended this place for dinner. He was so right about it!

Not near the crowds of San Marco and only a 10 minute walk. Small, quaint, comfortable. Server was funny, friendly, and not intrusive. Wish I remembered his name!

Had the Octopus with broccoli, artichoke, tsaziki, and chips for a starter. Octobus was tender and the purees were amazing!

Entree I had the macaroni with venison, blueberry, and cheese. To die for!! So flavorful and perfectly cooked.

My friend had the steak with pumpkin and truffle. He said it was the best steak he's had in Italy (even beat the 2 Florentine steaks we tried).

I wish I had more time in Venice because I would come back to try more dishes. Do yourself a favor and go try it!!!

name: emelia keller
review:
For our last night in Venice we spent a few hours at Da Jonny, and I’m so glad we did! The food was incredible, the atmosphere was incredible, and the drinks were out of this world delicious! You have to try the limoncello. Best limoncello around but also incredibly deadly!!!
Alejandro our waiter was fantastic to us, and Jonny himself was a true character! Definitely recommend the sea bass, Fritto Mista and the tuna tacos.

name: Malia Hatem
review: I have been to Jonny’s twice now and both times have been blown away by the amazing food but more than that was the service! You feel like family while you’re there!

name: Bainian Liu
review: Considering the price (we got the 14,50 euro combo), this is probably the best meal we had during our one week Italy stay. The pasta was fresh and flavorful from the seafood. The  seabass was seared with a perfect crispy skin while maintaining juicy and flavorful meat. Even the bread was warm and perfectly crusty while being soft on the inside.

name: oggi mangiamopizza
review: I had a wonderful dining experience here! The dishes were beautifully presented and full of authentic flavor. The pasta was perfectly cooked, the seafood was fresh, and the lasagna was rich and comforting. The atmosphere was cozy, and the staff were friendly and attentive. I would definitely recommend this place to anyone who loves genuine Italian cuisine.

name: KAYLA TURNEY
review: Stumbled on this place on the way back to our Airbnb and was pleasantly surprised!! The Ravioli was the best I’ve ever had!! We also ordered several other things as a party of 6 and all agreed the pumpkin gnocchi was also a fan favorite along with the ravioli! House red wine was 👌 Highly recommend!

name: Robert Shreve
review: Wonderful Meal…..all the food was delicious. We had a party of 6 so we all shared. Great Service!

name: Seaview Getaways
review: We saw the amazing reviews so we chose to enjoy a meal. A little disappointed that asked for the peppercorn steak to be medium rare - came out well done. The scallop pasta dish was also a little disappointing. Expected full scallops, however, a little slivers. Stale bread rolls. Felt like once they got us into the restaurant we were pretty much ignored.  Cute restaurant - Hope you have a better experience if you choose two dine here.

name: Rob D (ROBYZ84)
review: Food was incredible. Excellent location. Super friendly staff. One of the best restaurants I’ve been to in Venice! Highly recommend!!

name: Silvia Zeng
review: I went out with my friend and ordered some lasagna and spaghetti with ragù. Service was quick and the staff had a nice chat with us, and the sauce was really good and fresh. Definitely will be coming back! :)

name: Mahbubur Rahman Peyas
review: Amazing. The spaghetti I ordered had Ham/Bacon but as I can't eat, I asked just if they could remove the ham/bacons but the guy who was attending me got me a new one. I'm touched.

name: Brian Kindall
review: Great pizza spot with an extensive menu. One of our party got one of the best cheeseburgers she'd ever had. We started with the fried polenta and it was highly craveable. The pizza was excellent. Great drink selections. Fair prices. Not the kind of place you'd be drawn into from the street (if you could even find it), but if you like casual dining and extremely good pizza, put this place on your list. It's on the eastern end of the island away from the touristy hustle. Many Italians seemed to eat here. The place almost seemed closed from the front, but it had a sizeable dining room that opened up in the back to a fantastic patio with plenty of seating, shady or sunny. Obviously high-quality ingredients and everyone in the kitchen knew what they were doing. Would have eaten here more had we found it sooner.

name: Harry Lancaster
review:
Beware of their hidden €5 cover charge per person. It’s not mentioned on their menu, website, nor does the wait-staff make this known.

We searched high and low for good pizza in Venice and finally heard about Dai Fioi where we made a reservation. When we arrived, it felt like we walked into the back of the restaurant. We ended up asking the chef if we came in the wrong door? And he pointed us to the bartender.

We were made to wait 5 min for a table despite the fact that there were numerous 2-person tables available which slightly irked us. Then we had to repeatedly wave down our server for help ordering drinks and food and to pay. He was a good server but just inattentive. The pizza options were thoughtful and well put together, but honestly the crust had no integrity and we ended up having to eat the pizza with our fork lol. The flavors were spot on, though.

As we paid the bill, I didn’t recognize “2 x coperto €5” line item. I asked and was told it was their cover charge? This bothered us. Felt like they were taking advantage of the fact that we may have been tourists / non-Italian speakers.

Whatever.

name: Mark Wojciechowski
review: Hosted us without a reservation despite the busiest of afternoons due to the proximity to the marathon finish line.  Great staff and even better food.  The very best meal we had in Venice over the past several days. Good value too.

name: Mohamed Cherchar
review: One of the best pizzas I’ve had in Venice! The place feels authentic, not touristy, and the quality is amazing. The dough was light and perfectly cooked, and everything tasted fresh and delicious. Definitely recommend if you want a real local pizza experience in Venice.

name: Christina Astle-Alvarez
review:
Some of the best pizza I have had! From the get-go Shakil was very attentive to us and very friendly, even getting my mum to order in Italian to everyone's amusement.
Fast service and nice pizzas. Seems like a popular place that can get a bit busy later in the evening.

name: Dustin Altman
review: Service, food, and atmosphere were all top notch. We got a pizza and some pasta. Both dishes were absolutely fantastic. Along with delicious food, the staff were some of the friendliest I've encountered in Venice so far. It's a decent walk from the tourist areas of Venice, but it is well worth it.

name: Brittany Wooldridge
review:
Our pizzas were amazing!
We had the Trip to Italy , un classico and the Nerano.
The wait staff were amazing and quick to serve us our pizzas! The Location is tucked away from the crowds so it was nice to slow down and enjoy our dinner!

name: Jasper Vercammen
review: One of the best pizza’s we ever had. And finally a decent limoncello spritz, not the chemical fluorescent stuff we were server regularly in other places.

name: M L
review: Trattoria Da Gigio is a non-descript and understated restaurant located on a pretty busy street. It doesn't overtly market itself to tourists as it seems to be already frequented by both Italian-speaking customers and random tourist walk-ins based on my lone visit. Their wait staff speak English and were able to take my reservation over the phone. The mixed seafood appetizer was a great introduction to some Venetian classics/specialties. The baked scallop was actually served separately (still bubbling hot) by the chef himself. The pasta dishes came out steaming hot as well and were huge! Each dish can feed one very very hungry person or comfortably feed two people. This place serves food that is delicious, honest, and does seem to be frequented by local or visiting Italians.

name: G Bernie
review:
Delicious food in a welcoming atmosphere.
Staff are attentive and friendly, and the menu is handwritten, suggesting it changes.
The food is amazing, we opted for mussels to start. This was a large bowl, which was more than enough to share. The sauce was incredible.

For main, we had octopus and tuna. Both were cooked to perfection, along with the accompanying polenta and roast vegetables.

Highly recommended

name: Piotr
review: We had octopus ragout and spaghetti allio olio with calms, plus a gigio appetizer for starters. Absolutely delicious! Generous portions, great prices, and top quality. The menu suggests ordering a starter, a first course, a second course, and dessert – but there’s no way you can finish it all, the servings are huge. We can recommend this place with a clear conscience

name: Gaston B
review: Excellent Trattoria! Cooked by local. Fully recommend it, its hard to find in this tourist neighborhood a real Venecian Trattoria. We ate fresh grilled Tuna (with polenta!) and Ravioli di Zuca (pumkin), both dishes 10/10. We will come back!

name: Tanner Smith
review:
Just went for dessert and both the tiramisu and cheesecake were very disappointing.
The cheesecake seemed to be soft yogurt in a glass with a lot of jelly on top.
The tiramisu was also served in a glass and had very little flavor.
Looking at the food dishes that the other people in the restaurant were eating they did look good, but I definitely can’t recommend for dessert.

name: Alice Stou
review: A cozy restaurant with local vibes and food that felt homemade in the best way! Had the scallops and two pasta dishes - the pasta with clams and tomatoes was incredible! Great portion sizes, and a Venezian menu that felt very different and refreshing.

name: Christopher Frometa
review: This was our last night in Venice so we wanted to make sure it ended on a fantastic meal. This restaurant did not disappoint. We first started off with fried calamari with polenta. We were a bit confused at first when the calamari wasn’t served with a side sauce. Let me tell you, it was not needed. Just a little bit of lemon freshly squeezed over it, and it was heavenly. It was crispy, fresh and tasted great on its own. The polenta was also extremely well made and was a good compliment. Our main courses were both fresh handmade pasta with mine being loaded with cured meats, and my wife’s with mushrooms and scallops. Incredibly well sized portions that didn’t leave you hungry and a taste that had you itching for more. We also indulged in a tasty tiramisu which was out of this world. This is a restaurant I highly recommend as there was nothing bad about this entire dining experience!

name: Bitza B
review:
Trattoria da Gigio – wow, what a great choice!
This cozy family-run restaurant serves authentic Venetian dishes full of tradition and flavor. It’s not a tourist trap.
We tried the tuna with vegetables and the veal liver, both prepared Venetian style, and they were beautifully balanced and delicious. A warm, genuine atmosphere. We stood in the small garden with a great smell of lemon in the air, made the experience even better.
Just be sure to book a reservation. It’s worth it

name: Life of Misstep
review: Great lil spot here in Venezia! Great service and food. My wife had the carbonara, I had the Italian sliced beef with rocket and Parm cheese- cooked to perfection! Lenardo was awesome and very attentive. The outdoor garden was lovely.

name: Robin Phillips
review: Amazing food, great service, went here after seeing great reviews and they weren’t wrong. Was a great first night choice for our time in Venice. Squid ink pasta, meaty carbonara, pizza, and the most amazing bruschetta we’ve ever had!

name: Karen Saez
review: We had a great evening! Very good service in a super cozy and atmospheric setting. The pasta with onions was extremely tasty. I also loved my vegan pizza (veggie pizza with no cheese) - the olive oil and fresh basil were delicious. Recommend!

name: Victoria McInnerney
review:
We went here for our 20th wedding anniversary from Australia. The staff went above & beyond for us to make our celebratory dinner wonderful. The food was absolutely amazing.
Thanks so much. We are very grateful.

name: Sydney Power
review:
This is such a warm, cozy and ambient spot for dinner! The staff were kind and accommodating to us and we had a truly incredible meal! The pasta and sauces tasted amazing and will be something we talk about for a long time! We will definitely be coming back in future trips!

We had the Spaghetti Bolognese and the Purple Gnocchi! Would highly recommend both!

name: Chih-Yi Hung
review:
Our dinner at Trattoria Agli Artisti was as great as experiencing Venice is.
It’s a challenge to decide what to try from the exquisite variety of menu options… we chose to start with the cicchetti and to keep going with two first dishes.
The chicchetti were extraordinarily rich, fresh, mixing the best tradition with elegance of flavors and are arrangement…
we went on with the black squid linguine with blue crab …  extraordinary… and with the “scogliera” spaghetti, that they were as rich as they could hardly be.
We thought we had it all for the night, but the tiramisu was again outstanding and satisfying, super creamy, and delicately sweet.
So happy to have stopped by! Thank you Trattoria Agli Artisti for the unforgettable dining experience!

name: Ella Fucanan
review: Great food to welcome us upon arriving! We ordered seafood - Catch of the Day and Seafood Risotto. Everything was perfect, seafood was fresh, and lovingly prepared and served by the staff. Pictures don’t give justice, the fish was already deboned by this time. They bring it straight to your table and we stood in awe of the mastery of removing the bones live.

name: Johann Sanjaya
review: We tried the risotto scampi and entrecote. The risotto was fantastic. The steak is a bit chewy but it was okay. Good ambience and service.

name: Paula Robinson
review: Delightful! Friendly, fresh, and delicious! We were glad to arrive early before it got too crowded. Grilled calamari, lobster lingini were perfect. Denis may have won our week-long tiramisu contest, too!

name: Claire Harrison
review: We had a wonderful first dinner in Venice here! We were seated immediately, and the service was prompt and welcoming. We ordered the king prawns and the fried calamari, and everything was so fresh and flavorful! Great price for the phenomenal value and flavor and experience. Would absolutely recommend! We’ll have to come back when we have room for dessert!

name: Xing H
review: Best local Italian seafood ever we had! 10 stars! Service is great  and both waitress and waiter are super friendly and nice. We  had seafood plate with lobster, giant shrimps and ton of mussels for two and added pasta after. So so yummy and strongly recommend everyone! Price is really cheap for the amount of food we have. Atmosphere is nice ans not crowded with jazz music playing. Come and check it out!

name: pixlando 99
review: Starting the moment we arrived, they were friendly and gracious- they accommodated our request to sit outside and brought an extra table out to set us up properly. They had fast, attentive, and very friendly service, tasty, high quality food, and almost too many wonderful choices on the menu to make up our minds. But we eventually did, getting the tuna tartare, the cheese and mustard tasting platter, a seafood pasta, the orecchiette, and the cutlet in white wine with some sides. Everything was prepared perfectly, with quality ingredients and great flavours. Nothing too heavy, nothing bland. We did have some mosquitoes bothering us as it’s right near a canal- but it was worth it!

name: Alex
review: This trattoria was recommended by the receptionist in our hotel and it was a very good recommendation indeed. I had the cuttlefish and a glass of wine, and shared a typical Venetian antipasto and a tiramisu at the end. Approx. 40 euros per person.

name: Paul Sorescu
review: We had dinner and had a very good time at Denis. The pasta was fresh and very tasty, the sea food was very fresh and tender. Service is impeccable, the staff very nice and helpful. Not to mention the best tiramisu I had in Venice.

name: Gulcan Candogan
review: This is the best place in Venice! The staff is incredibly friendly, and the food is absolutely delicious—from the starters to the main courses and desserts. The grilled local calamari and the pistachio dessert were especially outstanding. I would recommend this place to anyone looking to truly enjoy their meal 10/10

name: Kyle Versa
review:
Trattoria da Denis is one of those rare places that immediately feels special the moment you walk in. It’s tucked just around the corner from where we usually stay in Venice, and every trip, we make a point of coming back here. The atmosphere is relaxed and unpretentious, with that classic Venetian warmth that makes you feel instantly welcome.

I’ve ordered the seafood risotto every single time, and it never disappoints. It’s honestly the best seafood risotto I’ve ever had. The rice is perfectly cooked, creamy without being heavy, and the seafood is fresh, generous, and prepared exactly right. Every bite has something different, from tender calamari to perfectly cooked shrimp, all blended into one incredibly flavorful dish. It’s the kind of meal that lingers in your memory long after the trip is over.

The service has always been outstanding. The staff are kind, attentive, and clearly proud of what they do. Even when the restaurant is busy, they manage to make you feel like they have all the time in the world for your table. The prices are very fair for the quality, and the overall experience is consistently wonderful.

If you’re looking for a restaurant in the heart of Venice that feels authentic, comfortable, and consistently delicious, Trattoria da Denis is it. This place has become part of our tradition every time we return to Venice, and I can’t recommend it enough.

name: Jessica Lui
review: A wonderful restaurant tucked away from the main roads with great service and food. We had the octopus salad, seafood spaghetti (frutti di mare), squid ink pasta with cuttlefish, and tiramisu. Everything was fantastic and well priced!

name: Sailor Pear
review: Perfect in every way. Seafood was fresh, pasta was cooked and seasoned perfectly, servers were perfectly attentive and lovely. We loved every minute of this special experience. Great quality and very reasonable price.

name: Monica Johnson
review: While on vacation in Venice, we had the pleasure of eating at Trattoria Tre Spiedi! It is a cozy restaurant with warm decor: paneled wood walls, a wine wall, white tablecloths, wood beam ceilings and super friendly staff! The food was delicious. We had the Caprese salad, spaghetti carbonara, and vegetable soup and a delicious bottle of Valpolicella. Everything was reasonably priced and it made for a thoroughly enjoyable dining experience. I would absolutely go back here again the next time I am in Venice!

name: Anne M
review:
Dining at Tre Spiedi was an absolute pleasure — a true taste of Venetian tradition both in flavor and service. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with old-fashioned hospitality that’s increasingly rare to find.

A special thank you to Michele and Salam, who provided flawless front-of-house service with warmth, professionalism, and charm. Their attention to detail and genuine care made our evening unforgettable.

The food was authentic and beautifully prepared — exactly what you’d hope for in a classic Venetian meal. To top it all off, we were treated to a complimentary limoncello, a perfect finish to a lovely evening.

We highly recommend Tre Spiedi to anyone seeking a genuine and memorable Venetian dining experience.

name: Christopher Castillo
review:
My friend and I got there pretty late as walk-ins (since we were traveling all the way from Milan) and they almost turned us away, but they gave us a chance and thank god they did.

Their taglioni al nero di seppia and spaghetti alla busara were absolutely amazing; not to mention their tiramisu. I genuinely can’t wait to go back!

name: William Fathers
review: Was walking by and happened to stumble upon a gem of a restaurant. Was greeted at the door and service was impeccable. Salam was an amazing waiter. Food was top tier, pasta was fresh and shrimp was cooked perfectly. Would recommend if you are in the area.

name: Chuck Chen
review: Absolutely delicious! Every dish was crafted with care — fresh ingredients, authentic flavors, and perfect execution. The handmade pasta and sweet sparkling wines were especially outstanding. Highly recommended!

name: Cristiana
review: The food was very good and the service was excellent! All waiters were really helpful and prompt. Definitely recommend the restaurant!

name: Josiah Sheeran
review: Stumbled upon this restaurant and was greeted with friendly and helpful staff, fantastic food and drink. Had their seafood mix pasta and was very pleased. Rich flavor and plenty of quantity. They were able to accommodate for gluten free noodles for my significant other. We spent roughly 55 Euro for two mains and a two glasses of wine. Would recommend to anyone visiting the San Marco area in Venice. All of their staff was friendly but Aldo was our server and was a pleasure to work with.

name: EJ Ross
review:
I was in Venice for 5 nights and returned to Trattoria da Gigi‘s several times during my stay.
I had such a great experience here and highly recommend it.

The prices were very reasonable, the menu had a great variety of choices and the wine and food were really good.
My favorite menu item was the caprese salad, It was to die for. It was better than I make at home.
The pasta dishes did not disappoint, they were great.
My favorite thing about this restaurant was by far the waitstaff.The waitstaff were all incredible!
Most everyone staffed, spoke multiple languages and they were all so patient and kind.
I personally received top shelf service from Joni several times.
I will definitely be returning here on my next trip to Venice!

name: Niko Sota
review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had a wonderful family dinner here 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦. The Albanian team of waiters were incredibly friendly, professional, and attentive, making us feel very welcome. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and clearly prepared with care. The outdoor garden seating 🌿 created a charming and relaxing atmosphere that made our evening extra special .
They also offer an impressive selection of Italian wines 🍷.
This restaurant is more than just a place to eat — it’s truly an experience. Highly recommend for both locals and tourists!

name: Sam Gonzalez
review: Albert was our waiter and he was extremely helpful in explaining what the plates would be like. He has a bubbly personality and did not give an attitude about splitting the check. This restaurant came highly recommended by a few tourists we have met along the way.

name: Erika Uy
review: My partner and I ended up at this restaurant when we visited Venice recently. The food was delicious - probably one of the best pasta we had in our entire Italian trip. I had the Pasta Nero and it was so good! My partner also liked his meatball pasta. Overall highly recommended restaurant in Venice

name: Jacek Matraszek
review: My girlfriend and I had the immense pleasure of dining at this wonderful restaurant today. We were so blown away by the amazing food, the incredible service, and the lovely ambiance that we decided to return just a few hours later to celebrate our engagement! 😁 This is an absolute "must-visit" in Venice for anyone who appreciates superb customer service and exceptionally high-quality food.

name: Paulina Budek
review: Wonderful place with a warm atmosphere and very friendly people. The risotto and pizza were truly delicious, and the whole experience felt welcoming and relaxed. This won’t be our only visit — we’ll definitely come back.

name: danny hutchins
review: Stumbled across this place by accident and so glad we did!!Firstly it’s authentic without being commercial and we got a good feeling for the restaurant because the locals were eating there on both occasions we visited (we just had to go back)Service is good without being to attentive any questions they are ready to help.But the main thing about any restaurant is the food and this place truly delivers on both visits we had different dishes and both were equally stunning .Not overpriced and when we return to Venice we will definitely be returning here.

name: Oscar
review:
Amazing experience on both times that we came. Nice for lunch then dinner the next day. The gnocchi is homemade and the best we’ve had between Rome Florence and all around. First was with the bolognese sauce and then with the amazing pesto that’s to die for.
The pana cotta was also the best around.
The service was amazing with Marwan and the other waiter who was always attending to us together with him was incredible. During the dinner the chef made a chili oil Tortellini that the waiters gave samples of to us and everyone one around, also amazing.
Can’t say enough good things about this place but after 13 days around Italy, this was hands down our best meals. Amazing friendly staff, amazing food as well.

name: limaspa linutzi
review: Wonderful dinner experience in Venice. Small trattoria off from the tourist spots in a side street. Nice and cozy atmosphere. Food was superb, from antipasto over primi piatti to deserts. Home made style Venetian cuisine, very tasty, portions are large and prices are absolutely fair. Service was super, very friendly, supportive and personal, both from the restaurant owner and especially from our waiter Marwan. We recommend to make a reservation to ensure getting a table on the weekend, worked out perfectly. Thank you for the great evening. We would definitely come back on our next visit to Venice.

name: Daniela Becerra
review:
20/10 😍

Best seafood we had over the 2-weeks trip!

We had the fritto misto as appetizer, simply divine! Then the seafood pasta and the bursara were amazing 🤩 made with love 💚

The ambience and the service are also superb!!!

Fernando and his son Estefano are very kind and welcoming 🤗.

name: Micaela (Mikii)
review: Love, love, love this place so much. Food and service and people here are so great! Went once for lunch and then again for dinner for our last night in Venice, probably the best gnocchi I've had so far! Will miss this place so much for sure. Special thanks to Marwan for making it all extra memorable :)

name: Hien Pham
review: We had such a wonderful experience at Trattoria La Divina Comida! Our server, Marwan, was absolutely fantastic , attentive, personable, and made us feel so welcome. The calamari was a perfect 10/10, and the chicken curry was easily one of the best dishes we’ve had in all of Italy. The lasagna was just okay for our taste, so I’d probably skip it next time, but everything else was incredible. We enjoyed it so much that we’re already planning to come back before we leave Venice!

name: Steph K
review: What an amazing place! We happened upon it on our last night in Venice and it was the perfect end to the perfect vacation. The food was incredible and Marwan was the perfect waiter. We had a blast and I can’t recommend this restaurant enough.

name: Brenna Pinckard
review: Amazing experience at La Divina Comida. Marwan went above and beyond. The gnocchi and select spritz were delicious. Service very friendly. Grazi a milli!

name: L Makita
review: I wish I could give 3.5 stars. It was okay, and the house wine — both the red (Cabernet) and the white (Chardonnay) — was lovely. The standout dish was the gnocchi, which was light and pillowy, like little clouds. Unfortunately, the roast beef really missed the mark. It came out dry and overcooked, with oily waxy potatoes that were far from crispy roast potatoes. There was no sauce, just a slice of lemon and the whole dish felt quite dry. We had expected something tender and flavourful, but it just didn’t deliver. A mixed experience overall.

name: Aron Yuen
review: Coming here for dinner the second time in a row during my short stay in Venice.  The seafood pasta was just phenomenal! The service was excellent!  I can’t recommend this trattoria more!

name: Cw
review: The seafood pasta here is more than art. Some people may feel that it is expensive, but the moment you taste the food, that thought disappears. The lunch menu is available after 11:30, and it's worth the wait. The waiter was very friendly and I felt respected throughout the meal. It was my first meal in Venice, and I was able to start my trip very pleasantly. It was the best experience. If I visit Venice next time, I will definitely come again. I recommended this place to my friend.

name: Calvin
review: Great crowd and food was delish. Tried the seafood spaghetti, caprese and beef. All were well done and flavourful. The queue might be a little long but definitely worth it!

name: M L
review: The number of positive reviews for this restaurant speaks for itself. This place came recommended on multiple online sources and was also suggested to us by our Airbnb host. It was busy and full of energy every single time we passed by. My best advice is to call ahead and book. We had to make reservations for Saturday at 7pm about 2 days in advance. There seemed to be people eating outside of the "normal" Italian meal times so double check if they can accomodate you before lunch (noon) or between lunch and dinner (2-7pm). Although Bar Pontini is frequented by a significant number of non-Italian tourists, don't let that dissuade you as the food was delicious and there did seem to be Italian-speaking guests catching up with the waitstaff during our visit.

name: Alina Mitskevich
review: The wait was over for 2 hours: waiting for the table, waiting for drinks, waiting for food, waiting for the bill. The wine was only brought after we reminded them. I understand that the staff are busy, but maybe they shouldn't take extra reservations then? After such a long wait, the food seemed quite good, fairly averagе. If you still want to visit, book in advance.

name: Do dj
review: It was my first time in Venice, the gpt chat recommended this place and I didn't regret going there, the food was very tasty, the portions were good, the whole family ate, so I recommend everyone to go there.There were queues there a couple of times, But it's worth it, especially the tiramisu and pasta were very tasty, and the lasagna is a completely separate kind of deliciousness, I'll definitely go there if I'm in Venice again. (they forgot to take a photo of the lasagna🫣)

name: Dongchan Lee
review:
Highest food quality at very low price.
Friendly staffs and they know what to do.
You will not regret it

name: Flora Fan
review: This place is warm, clean and well-decorated family style. The service was great and the staff are so nice and friendly. The food was amazing! We ordered seafood pasta and cuttlefish ink pasta. The americano caffe was good too. The tiramisu is the best I’ve ever had in Italy so far.

name: Alishia Potter
review: In the mild fall of November - the Trattoria Da Giorgio was fabulous!! Great food, wonderful atmosphere with outdoor canal seating. They had heat lamps so we wouldn’t be chilly and the waiter was pleasant and knowledgeable. Wonderful Chianti!

name: T. Charity
review: Staff were friendly but everything seemed to have a lot of garlic of which I am sensitive to. The focaccia was 9 euro which wasn't mentioned when asked if we wanted it. We thought it was complimentary with our 3 dinners and drinks. The menus had already been collected. The gnocchi with scallops was light and fluffy but it seemed it was just 1 scallop cut into 4 pieces. The lasagna was loaded with cheese, the starter plate of shrimp and zucchini had a nice amount of shrimp. Nice location on a small canal but if it's during 'commute' time you will get some fuel smells from the boats going by.

name: Savvas Asikis
review:
We tried a lot to find a place to eat good food.

Venice is packed with expensive restaurants but with bad quality food.

Here we were surprised.

The food was exceptional and the service very quick.

We had a great view of the canal.

name: Taylor Visscher
review: Such a great spot! We came here for lunch on the first day of our vacation and it did not disappoint. The Aperol spritzes and pasta were excellent, and the canal-side ambiance made the experience even better. The staff was also incredibly friendly, which made the meal all the more enjoyable!

name: neha sharma
review: "Amazing atmosphere, delicious food, sitting arrangments also very nice with beautiful view 🌁waitress was friendly, attentive, and made sure we had everything we needed. Excellent service!" I highly recommend this restaurant with great vibe

name: Annahans
review:
We picked Trattoria DiGiorgio from many options while looking for a restaurant with a cozy vibe. Tables placed outside, right by the water, with the heat lamps, so it was possible to sit without a jacket.
The staff were friendly, and what’s most important, food was delicious. We definitely recommend this restaurant

name: Matthias Miesbauer
review:
We (two) were here to have a late dinner from 9PM to about 10:30PM.

Everyone was really friendly and the food was excellent.

The ambiente is perfect for a Venice holiday.

Would absolutly recommand.

name: Ирина Богданова
review:
Unbelievably delicious! Big portions! Risotto with looots of seafood! Very fresh products, the waiters are super! In the end they gave us limoncello
Highly recommend

name: Abigail Roces
review: The food and service were really great! Everything was delicious. I even went back twice to try more dishes. The service was fast, and everyone was so warm and welcoming.

name: Mary Gibadlo
review: Gianni was such a great host!!  We had no expectations and we were delighted at the wonderful experience, service, food and atmosphere. Thank you all for a great dinner on our Venice trip!!  Can’t go wrong here my friends!!

name: Kyle Sassenhausen
review:
We had a great meal on our final night in Venice. The food was wonderful and the service even better. Our server made went above and beyond to insure my father in-law had a great evening and meal
Thank you for a great evening!

name: Foxy Panda
review: Great service. Friendly staff! The food was amazing. I had the prosciutto pizza and I would 100% recommend.

name: Ruthie Harris
review: Best meal we’ve had so far in Italy! The food was absolutely incredible, and Nicola was by far the best server we’ve met on our trip — so friendly, attentive, and welcoming. Highly recommend coming here, you won’t regret it!

name: Zilan Selçik
review:
We ordered at around 7:30pm. The restaurant wasn’t full and there wasn’t much happening to excuse the long wait.
We waited up until 8:50pm until our meals came. We did order the chicken breast and milanese style chicken.
However to wait over an hour for just two slices of chicken with nothing but a lemon slice is just ridiculous— if it at least tasted good it would be a little better. And to pay the price it is listed at…
We were definitely guided by the good reviews but cannot stand by them.

name: Luca Késmárki
review: The atmosphere was generally good. The food was delicious, and the service was friendly. The also offer gluten-free pizza! The daily fish dish was quite expensive, so it's a good idea to ask about the price beforehand. Otherwise, I recommend the place!

name: George
review:
Terrible diarrhea after having a pizza here.

Had a Hawaiian pizza and my friend had a shrimp pizza. Worst pizza both of us have ever had and then we were violently ill from it the rest of the day.

My Hawaiian pizza looked like it had canned pineapple and stale ham on it, the shrimp on the shrimp pizza were obviously from a can too.

Plus it wasn’t even cheap, seriously avoid this place.

Service sucked too, basically ignored the whole time despite being the only people sitting outside.

name: Myung Lee
review:
I came here late in the evening, but the food was served quickly and it was very delicious.
All the staff were very friendly. The squid ink pasta suited the Korean taste really well, the steak was tender, and the crab pasta was also amazing.
The tiramisu for dessert at the end was absolutely delicious.

name: angela lin
review: We had the squid ink pasta and some fried seafood. Everything was super fresh and tasty! The owner was really friendly and even treated us to two desserts.

name: Taren Krisher
review:
A quiet place out of the way in a Very busy city.
Venice is overcrowded with tourists and by 11am the main attraction areas as packed.  This is an excellent quiet spot to enjoy a delicious lunch, especially if seafood is on your mind.  Situated on a convergence of side streets you get a nice shady spot to relax and enjoy the service and the meal.

name: Jed Andrew Asunción Ramos
review:
Very pleasant service, the waiter who served us of Asian origin attended to us very kindly, he was at all times informing us of how the order was going and he gave us his recommendations, which was to be thankful for and a great success.

​The food was very good, being so central (near San Marcos square) we expected that they would serve us anything at an exorbitant price but in this case the quality-price was unbeatable.

​I would without a doubt return to this place if there was a second opportunity to return to Venice.

Salamat, Kabayan!

name: Katie Rodriguez
review: I don’t like posting bad reviews but this has to be said. This restaurant is owned by Asians who are very nice, but do not know how to cook Italian food. They have spaghetti bolognese on the menu which is the first sign they are catering to tourist, bc spaghetti bolognese doesn’t exist in Italy- bolognese is served with tagliatelle. That was the first problem, but the second was that the sauce wasn’t made correctly either. It has wayyyy too much nutmeg (and tomatoes- bolognese actually has very little tomatoes) in it. My ravioli were in an extremely gloppy sauce that had very little taste. Unfortunately I usually do better research but these good reviews fooled me. I should have realized they were mostly from tourists who don’t know what they are talking about. The restaurant was also almost completely empty, which also makes sense. The waiter was wonderful and so sweet and attentive. I would suggest that the owners should just open a restaurant focusing on their own cuisine since Italy is in dire shape when it comes to Asian/Chinese food and certainly needs options - but please stay away from Italian food. It was not good.

name: Chanapong Konkham
review:
- The female staff took very good care of us. I have been to many cities in Italy and eaten at many restaurants. I think she is the best in service.
- The shop is decorated in a Venetian style. I like the music they play, it makes the food look more delicious.
- The food tastes good. I like the black squid ink spaghetti the most because the noodles are soft and not hard like other restaurants. The other dishes are also delicious.

name: Sumi Choi
review: Everything is PERFECT...!!! Especially Seafood spaghetti was super amazing. I couldn't stop eating. 😫 The staffs is so adorable and kind. Made me feel so comfortable and happy. Please visit here. You're gonna have best pasta. I swear a god. 진짜 레전드. 한국가면 계속 생각날 듯요

name: Bumsoo Park
review: This place is a hidden gem, with a lot of great options! Definitely recommend the seafood menus!

name: Glenn Ferguson
review: Fantastic spot for some lunch away from the most crowded areas of Venice. Outdoor seating area is serene and shaded and the food is amazing! Friendly staff and generous portions of super fresh seafood make it a ‘must eat’ place in Venice!

name: Zane
review:
Best meal I’ve had here so far. The salad was the fullest, most lettuce-forward salad I’ve had in a week (since leaving the US) and the avocado complimented it well. Plus, look at that presentation!

The squid ink pasta with scallops was also delicious, I highly recommend.

The atmosphere was nice - we sat outside and enjoyed the perfect cool summer evening.

name: Stacy Miller
review: We randomly came across this amazing restaurant when we purposely got lost in Venice (each person picked a direction to turn at each junction). That accommodated our large party and created a table for us on their small patio in the alley. The food was amazing! Special of the day was fantastic, would highly recommend the spicy shrimp pasta dish, and the dessert special was a sliced cookie dish that is something we will dream about in the future! Service was excellent and the setting was relaxed. Lots of locals showed up as the evening went on. We would return in a minute!

name: Rhiannon
review:
Nice food - we had the scallop pasta and cutlet. Both were cooked well and we're tasty.
The staff were really nice and would make little jokes which was pleasant.
The bread basket was great because it came with Alice baguette and bread sticks.
The food is quite expensive, but that can be expected in Venice.

name: Graeme
review:
The only thing we were disappointed with was after our lovely main courses we were too full for dessert. When we first sat the tables were empty as I think we arrived just after opening, however they soon filled with other diners. Our waiter was great and recommended various dishes. Food and drink all tasty and hopefully next time we get that dessert.

Update from our first visit above on 8/9/25
We visited again 9/9/25 and managed to find space for dessert. Tried a few different dishes from our first night (except the Bruschetta) and once again enjoyed the tasty food. We sat inside this time to avoid the mosquitos who seem to love our Scottish skin. Finished with Tiramisu, Lemoncello and an espresso.

name: Natalja Kerry
review: Just finished dinner with a group of six - everyone was happy! The food was delicious and the portions generous. I had the octopus salad-  the octopus was tender and I can definitely recommend it. The liver was also a hit-  two of my friends ordered it and loved it, I tried a bite too and it’s a great choice for liver fans. The cuttlefish was another favorite.

name: Gina DeGiovanni
review: Felt very local. Reasonable prices for delightful food and service--menu had good range for someone seeking traditional venetian dishes, and also a teen seeking no-seafood.  Variety of vegetarian options, too. Pasta fresh, service quick and impeccable. Picky Italian-Americans who have been travelling to Venice annually for 20 years.

name: Zsolt Szelecki
review: Remarkable food, but what made the place stand out for us is the service. The guys who run the restaurant, were not only friendly, relaxed, but offered some great recommendations to local food and I sights on culture. We leant what Cankara is in local slang, among other things and insights. Keep on , this was a memorable experience, with great food! We'll come back when we can.

name: Riku Inoue
review: We had the best pasta of our Venice trip here. This place is a hidden gem tucked away in a small alley, yet conveniently close to the station. The clam pasta was full of umami - highly recommended. Seafood pasta and squid ink pasta were also excellent. All the pastas were cooked perfectly. The service was also great. Our server Mike was really friendly and we had a fun conversation about travel stories. He made us feel welcome and made our visit even better. I highly recommend this place when you visit Venice.

name: Sasha Newar
review: This was a great hidden gem!!! The food was DELICIOUS, great wine, lovely service :) the restaraunt doesn't look super fancy from the outside, but we were very happy with our dinner:) best ravioli we had all trip!

name: Alexandra Polisevschi
review:
This is a nice place with good food, hidden away from the main street, but conveniently near the train station.

We had steak, tagliatelle bolognese and sea bass (which came with delicious vegetables), all freshly cooked. The staff was nice and attentive.

name: Shireen Shaikh
review: Such amazing food. Seafood is fresh and well cooked. Service was so so good they gave us 3 bread baskets and a coke for free!!  Highly recommend the fried calamari and the shrimp with red orange pasta!!

name: Carl S
review: Dinner tonight was the bomb! Our waiter Luigi was great! The owner (Claudio)was very personable, fun times with him! As an American I wish I knew more Italian! The food was awesome!

name: Kevin Pham
review: Trattoria Il Vagone offered absolutely amazing food and service. Our group of nine were wandering the streets of Venice for what seemed like hours after deboarding the train from Milan. When we stumbled upon this hidden gem, they weren’t even open yet. Mike welcomed us in with open arms into the dining room for drinks while we waited for the kitchen to open. He was a friendly, personable, and professional young man. The seafood and carbonara pastas are a must get. We will certainly be returning to this establishment!

name: Ngoc Nguyen
review: This is one of the best dinners I have ever had. You have to try the honey tiramisu, it’s dense, creamy, perfectly sweet. I’m in love. The beef with red wine is also amazing. The service is good. I really want to try more but my stomach is full 😭 I will definitely come back!

name: Amro Mohi Eldin
review:
A True Hidden Gem in Venice!

Tucked away from the crowds but still only a short walk from the main areas, this spot gives you peace and quiet while dining outside. The staff are warm, funny, and speak multiple languages, making you feel like old friends even on your first visit.

We had spaghetti al pomodoro e basilico, light, balanced, and full of fresh tomato-basil flavor and fettuccine Alfredo with creamy sauce and tender mushrooms. Finished with a silky panna cotta drizzled in chocolate.

Delicious food, amazing people, and a tranquil setting. Venice dining at its best.

name: John
review: A unicorn in a city full of hidden fees, this restaurant does not charge any cover charge (coperto) or automatic gratuity (servicio), but they earn your patronage with excellent service and fresh food. They understand that treating customers with respect encourages repeat visits, and the number of local clients shows that.  One of the best pesto sauces I've ever had was here. The chicken Milanese and sauteed spinach were also excellent.

name: Estelle Degiorgio
review: The best Meal and service we’ve had on all our trip! The servers were so kind to our daughter and brought her a desert on the house as a treat. We took a pizza Capriciosa, spaghetti bolognese and Chicken escalopes with mushrooms and cannot recommend them enough. Will definitely come again!

name: Shihhwa Hung
review: Fantastic food with fresh ingredients and perfect seasoning. We had the octopus, tuna burger and clam linguine. All are impeccable and impressive!

name: Sasha B
review:
Service is best in Venice, waiter was extremely helpful, kind and knowledgeable.
Food was amazing: light, fresh and sea food cooked to perfection. One thing to point out is that octopus could be polarising, it is definitely pushing taste buds: bitter and sweet, fresh and smoky, crunchy and silky all at once. As for me it was just the right amount of food adventurousness 😋😋😋

name: Grant Jackson
review: Had the lasagne, pretty average. Wine overpriced, my partner had penne with rocket and shrimps, nicely cooked but far too much garlic. Good atmosphere but far better places around

name: Alex Stouparenko
review: Easily the best restaurant experience in Venice! The food is served extremely fresh, seasonal ingredients and the service is super friendly. Everything is made in restaurant and the quality is excellent for everything we have tasted.

name: S B
review: Absolutely stunning food and service. Everything about this place was perfect. On a road with a lot of restaurants can't believe we found here for lunch. The octopus was incredible. The staff so lovely and helpful

name: Nhi Nguyen
review:
Amazing food, great service. Best cheesecake. Seriously, come here and eat the cheesecake, please.

I was really surprised to see that there was no queue for this place. Better enjoy it before it gets popular.

The seafood is fresh. Make sure to check out their special for the day. I really enjoyed mine. It was the grilled razor clams.

We also had:
- Cuttlefish ink pasta: don’t wear white shirt and order this. I liked it but not enough to order again.
- Ragu pasta: Also delicious with an interesting flavour cos no tomatoes.

name: Ekaterina
review: This place is okay. Food was not bad but nothing special. The wait was longer than we’d expected. The toilet is not pleasant

name: Andy S
review: Great restaurant with an extensive menu. Service was excellent with the waiters being really helpful and friendly. We both had pizzas which were fab and enormous! The place was busy and popular which made the atmosphere really nice.

name: umang jain
review: Absolutely Amazing, we tried veg risotto and pizza, both were finger licking good, the best food we had in Venice.

name: Stanimir Tepavski
review: I recommend this place if you are near Piazza San Marco. It is at 10-minute walk along the seafront which makes it less crowded and more enjoyable. The staff were great and the food was very tasty. Don’t miss out the desserts!

name: Blackrose Lyrics
review: We bought lasagna for 1 person and they wanted extra money for a fork (2 euros) the taste was not bad. There were 4 suitable package campaigns written on the menu but they fooled us by saying that none of them were available. I do not recommend it. Their only aim is to get money...

name: Gisel Diaz
review: Food was amazing! The server was also very kind and helpful. The atmosphere and vibes were also great! If I come back to Venice I would definitely come back again!

name: 김봄봄
review:
very disappointed with foods!
very hard bread to start( feels like it was sitting there for a while…) not really fresh seafood pasta and pizza was alright but cold..(i don’t know how) I wouldn’t go to this restaurant for food but the service was really good! they even caught flies and bees for us haha

name: Ghid Al-saeedi
review: Great experience! Amazing service and environment. The owner is very sweet brought us delicious tiramisu!

name: Courtney Whyte
review: We had the most wonderful dinner at Trattoria Cea on the final night of our trip to Italy. The restaurant is located on the quiet side of town, and the space is cozy like you are visiting someone’s home for a home cooked meal. The owner was very friendly and recommended a delicious bottle of wine. The food was excellent. It was the perfect end to our Italian vacation.

name: Christina Vincent
review: I’m such a great place for our first night in Venice. The menu is authentic but it also has the dishes in English as well which I was thankful for. We had the cuttlefish Ink pasta and the sea bass with vegetables. Both were delicious! They gave us an anchovy appetizer that was amazing as well! Staff was so kind and attentive. Definitely recommend

name: Jan Szulc
review: Fell across this restaurant and so pleased  we found it. It’s in a little quiet street with both indoor and outdoor seating. The food was truly  delicious and the service outstanding. Would definitely recommend

name: stanley pawlus
review: The food is exceptional and satisfying. The staff that attends to you goes above and beyond to ensure the dining experience is what anyone should expect coming to Venice. A place for great times and food!

name: Диана
review:
First night in Venice, first dish. We had eaten before at other little restaurants in Milan/Varenna, and it wasn’t great ☠️! But here — this is probably what they mean when they say “The food in Italy is delicious.” I really enjoyed it. Probably more expensive than the average in most restaurants, but if you want truly good food, then…

The waiter was wonderful — very polite and kind! Atmosphere is also good!!! I already want to come back 😭 This has been the best experience in Italy so far.

name: Feng Wu
review: Very nice spot, beautiful outdoor seating, quiet, barely any tourists traffic. Food is very tasty, we ordered two of two courses combination, then added another sea bass main dish. Everyone enjoyed.

name: Nur
review: Beautiful restaurant, away from the busy streets of Venice. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The taste and serving size of the pasta was great, we enjoyed the salad as well.

name: Phillipa Graham
review: A lovely Trattoria serving local cuisine in a picturesque, quiet location. Service was discreet but attentive. Bruschetta and Tiramisu were highlights, but the local squid ink pasta and spaghetti vongole did not disappoint. Great price point for the exy Venetian locale. Highly recommend.

name: John
review: A unicorn in a city full of hidden fees, this restaurant does not charge any cover charge (coperto) or automatic gratuity (servicio), but they earn your patronage with excellent service and fresh food. They understand that treating customers with respect encourages repeat visits, and the number of local clients shows that.  One of the best pesto sauces I've ever had was here. The chicken Milanese and sauteed spinach were also excellent.

name: Estelle Degiorgio
review: The best Meal and service we’ve had on all our trip! The servers were so kind to our daughter and brought her a desert on the house as a treat. We took a pizza Capriciosa, spaghetti bolognese and Chicken escalopes with mushrooms and cannot recommend them enough. Will definitely come again!

review: Fantastic food with fresh ingredients and perfect seasoning. We had the octopus, tuna burger and clam linguine. All are impeccable and impressive!

review:
Service is best in Venice, waiter was extremely helpful, kind and knowledgeable.
Food was amazing: light, fresh and sea food cooked to perfection. One thing to point out is that octopus could be polarising, it is definitely pushing taste buds: bitter and sweet, fresh and smoky, crunchy and silky all at once. As for me it was just the right amount of food adventurousness 😋😋😋

review: Had the lasagne, pretty average. Wine overpriced, my partner had penne with rocket and shrimps, nicely cooked but far too much garlic. Good atmosphere but far better places around

name: Alex Stouparenko
review: Easily the best restaurant experience in Venice! The food is served extremely fresh, seasonal ingredients and the service is super friendly. Everything is made in restaurant and the quality is excellent for everything we have tasted.

name: S B
review: Absolutely stunning food and service. Everything about this place was perfect. On a road with a lot of restaurants can't believe we found here for lunch. The octopus was incredible. The staff so lovely and helpful

name: Nhi Nguyen
review:
Amazing food, great service. Best cheesecake. Seriously, come here and eat the cheesecake, please.

I was really surprised to see that there was no queue for this place. Better enjoy it before it gets popular.

The seafood is fresh. Make sure to check out their special for the day. I really enjoyed mine. It was the grilled razor clams.

We also had:
- Cuttlefish ink pasta: don’t wear white shirt and order this. I liked it but not enough to order again.
- Ragu pasta: Also delicious with an interesting flavour cos no tomatoes.

name: davide galastri
review:
I started with the best and tasty european row fish I have ever experienced.

Then I had Risotto di go’, unbelievable !!! it’s mantecato without using butter so that it keeps intact the scent of the 🌊.

A friend of mine told me this the best fish restaurant of the region. I totally agree :)))

name: Olivier Staquet
review: Great fish restaurant. Everything was fresh and delicious. Service was a bit slow at times. Most of the waiters don't speak English, but you can make yourself understood. Really nice experience.

name: Jarno Pelkonen
review: The restaurant looks nice, but the service is rude against tourists and food is really average at best despite looking great.

name: C Allen
review: Beautiful seafood restaurant in Campalto, close to the lagoon. Perfect for lunch, but do arrive early or book in advance!

name: Misa Stanojevic
review:
Beautiful restaurant, excellent quality of the food, service!
You have to visit and enjoy the experience at Al Passo!
I reccomend this place.
P.S. make a reservation request.

name: Ignacio Arce
review: Exceptional seafood!  Fresh, tasty and expertly prepared

name: Nenad Motika
review: Fantastic food and great service. I can’t decide on the best meal I tried as everything was perfect

name: CHIU FANG LEE HSU
review: The best seafood you can find around this area. pleasant dining experience. Will be back again.

name: Amr Elshahbour
review:
I had a fantastic dining experience in Venice right by the canal with a beautiful view! I ordered the antipasto misto di pesce to start – everything was fresh and full of flavor. The risotto ai frutti di mare was perfectly cooked, rich with seafood and just the right texture. For the main course, I had branzino al forno, which was tender and delicious, with a light seasoning that let the fish shine. I finished with a classic tiramisù, and it was the perfect end to the meal – creamy, balanced, and not too sweet.
Everything I had was excellent. Highly recommend if you're looking for quality seafood and a lovely Venetian atmosphere!
the stuff is very friendly and welcoming , they have a small balcony on the view and the orders are served through the window which is a very nice experience , if you want to sit in the balcony , reservation is a must !

name: Hector Ortiz
review:
Walked by this place a few times and wanted to try it. Had a reservation for their outside table overlooking one of the canals, but unfortunately due to weather they switched us indoors. A little disappointed by that but they more than made up for it with great food and service.

Classic tastes of mussels, pasta and fish. The wine recommendation was on point and the tiramisu is great for desert. Nice touch at the end with some limoncello.

Next time I will try and get the outdoor tables. There’s only 3 so it is a bit hard to secure the reservation, and it requires credit card that will be charge for no show, only on those 3 tables.

name: Dione Júnior
review: We had a good experience at this restaurant, with tasty food that satisfied our hunger. However, it didn't blow us away - nothing exceptional stood out. The staff were friendly and helpful, which was a definite plus. We were particularly looking forward to the tiramisu, but unfortunately, it didn't quite meet our expectations - it was a bit dry and lacked the creamy texture we were hoping for. Despite that, the location is great and the overall experience was still enjoyable. Maybe just manage your expectations a bit, but still worth a visit!

name: Kaolhi Ly
review: This restaurant is great. My friend and I stopped by here to eat. Service was wonderful and food came out pretty quickly. No reservations needed if you're sitting inside, but yes if you're wanting to sit outside. My pasta was delicious. I liked the vibe. Clean restroom is a big plus.

name: Fiona Banham
review: What an amazing place! Had the best carbonara of my life here - the food was incredible! The staff were very lovely and friendly, too, and made us feel very welcome. We were sat on this very cute little balcony outside overlooking the canal. Perfect all round!!

name: Mark E
review: One of the best meals we’ve had in Italy! Delicious food, great service and beautiful ambiance! We had the baked sea bass and the seafood soup - both were so good! Can definitely recommend. Make sure to book ahead as we saw several parties being turned away as they had no reservations. Thank you to our waiter Francesco for being so lovely!

name: Bianca Adami
review:
I had an amazing experience at this restaurant in Venice! 🤍 The funghi pasta was absolutely delicious – perfectly cooked with rich, earthy flavors that made every bite unforgettable. But the real highlight was the chocolate soufflé: light & so fluffy. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming & the service was excellent. 🫶🏻
A true gem in Venice – highly recommended!

name: Atlee Lauder
review: Fantastic food, great service. We enjoyed all of our dishes, but the carbonara was the favourite. One of the best spots we tried on our trip. Great spot with a lovely view. Reserve in advance for a table outside.

name: Kyiv Senses Tours
review: Amazing restaurant in the old part of Venice a little bit away from the hustle and bustle. Big selection of dishes, fresh seafood and Prosecco and they have amazing oysters. We enjoyed the location, food selection and service. Good prices too. Recommend for those who are searching good service for decent money - you dining experience will give you lasting memories.

name: İlhan MERCAN
review:
We stopped here on our way because the kids were very hungry.
I trusted the 4.4 Google rating and decided to give it a try.

We ordered the simplest dishes: a margherita pizza, a vegetarian pizza, and spaghetti bolognese.
Unfortunately, the pizzas were a complete disappointment. The dough was undercooked, the bottom wasn’t crisp, and it felt heavy on the stomach. The margherita pizza was €7 — which seemed fine — but the vegetarian pizza was €13! They just added a few vegetables and charged €6 more, which is quite strange.

The spaghetti was okay, but definitely not worth the price.

It takes real talent to make bad pizza in Italy!

I strongly recommend skipping this place — especially if you plan to eat pizza!

name: Shane J
review:
During our stay in Venice, we passed by this restaurant many times on our way to other eateries!  On our last night we decided to have our meal here and we were pleasantly surprised by how good the meal was and that put big smile on our faces!

It was good weather and the atmosphere was delightful throughout our meal.

I had a good experience and I would recommend!

name: Heather Cyr
review: The food is absolutely delicious. The wine is very affordable and delightful. The wait staff is very friendly and attentive. I highly recommend visiting this restaurant. We came here twice on our visit here.

name: Kire Roylat
review:
I will say Trattoria was an exceptional dining experience which should not be missed. The service is almost intuitive in their ability to anticipate your needs. The food was delicious and quite flavorful.
I would dare to say late dining in Venice is an extraordinary experience and Trattoria is the bright evening star for a magical evening.

name: Emilie Leidig
review:
Turbo fish was excellent. Lasagna was great. Lobster was great. Great service, great food.

Feroz, Roni, Robin were all great.

name: Leanne Morrell
review:
Fabulous service and kept us warm with heaters and blankets during our evening meal!

Risotto was to die for and the whole meal for all was incredible! A must to visit if you come out here!

name: Hannah M
review: Food is amazing and reasonably priced. Drinks are fantastic, and the service is incredible. Everyone here is so lovely and make you feel so welcome and they make sure you are having a brilliant time. 10/10 I would definitely recommend.

name: bita navabi
review: I had a wonderful experience at this restaurant! It’s located right by the water, offering a beautiful and relaxing view. The food was absolutely delicious — every dish was full of flavor and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is great, calm and not crowded, which makes it perfect for taking photos and enjoying your meal peacefully. Highly recommended! 🌊🍽️✨

name: Shifat Islam
review: Good hospitality warming also good.try must their sea food.😀😀

name: Damian Pit
review:
Great pleace with lovely terrace next to canal.
Food and service 5/5

name: Nath Ferraz
review: VERY GOOD, the best pasta I’ve ever had. 🫶

name: Ayman Kotb khedr
review: Excellent Food and very Nice View

name: Sam Hakim
review:
Authentic home made pasta.
Affordable price😁👍.

name: Huihui Zhang
review: Very good food, cozy atmosphere and professional staff!

name: Barbara Majewska
review: Bardzo pyszne jedzenie, miła pomocna obsługa. Miłe widoki. Za kolację dla dwóch osób około 50€

name: Steve K
review: This was one of the stops on a food tour I took and I was so enamored by the restaurant I decided to come back for my last meal in Venice. On the food tour I enjoyed the spaghetti pomodoro which was so simple yet complex I could have licked the plate clean…or scarpetta as they say in Italian. On my “last meal” visit I enjoyed the porchetta and artichokes as my antipasto and the carbonara for my first. (I’m so full I won’t have a second. I sat outside in the garden and reflected on the beauty that is Venice and the incredible time I’ve had in Italy. This was a fantastic representation of everything I’ve learned about local traditional foods…respectful, classic preparations with incredible skill and nothing fussy or Avante Garde. My food was perfectly cooked, perfectly seasoned and it’s exactly the way I wanted to spend my last day! Grazie mille per la cena perfetta!!!

name: Muz Tsn
review:
Very disappointed on all aspect. I’m not sure why the rating of this place is high really… I tried way better places in Venice for same price range. We were 3 people and ordered 3 dishes: supposedly fresh pasta: sauce has literally no taste , almost nonexistent from flavour point of view.
Pizza are similar to New York pizza…
The service is probably the worse part. The waiter aren’t really friendly. We asked for a table outside they said no as all reserved… the entire time we had lunch  the tables were empty… very disappointed

name: Leslie W
review: Fried Seafood Plate was light and flavorful as was the roasted sea bream. Grilled veggies and garden salad sides were a welcome addition after a heavy carb and meat / seafood menu for the last few days.  The service we had was fantastico thanks to the time our excellent waiter took with us.  His family owned the building and we basically asked him to plan our menu. It was delicious.

name: Krystal
review: Lovely ambiance for sure! The pasta with seafood was so tasty! The squid and the branzino were not as good, I wasn't too much of a fan. Decent service. Was recommended to go to this place by a local! Its set in a garden environment which is rare for Venice.

name: Lucy DePaula
review: I had such a wonderful experience at Nono Risoto in San Marco! The customer service was excellent—so welcoming and attentive. I ordered pasta, and it was absolutely delicious, cooked to perfection with amazing flavors. Definitely a place worth visiting if you’re in Venice! 🍝🇮🇹

name: Isak Wistrand
review: Food was awesome! Good service, the staff was friendly and quick. The food came surprisingly fast although a lot of other guests. The atmosphere was incredibly amazing and cozy! The Italian garden style made it even more charming!

name: Batchie villarosa
review:
Beautiful outdoor garden area. Food was good. We had the mixed fry & mussels for appetizers! House sparkling white wine was ✨
Service was ok. You can visibly see they treat locals better. We saw that in Rome and Venice

name: Caterine Clennell
review: Great food and service, recommend a reservation if you want to sit outside. Personally found the 2016 american pop music playlist was a weird choice though and the toilets need a bit more care.

name: Brian Devlin
review: Fantastic restaurant and I really wish we had gone back.  The cicchetti were okay but the restaurant menu was excellent.  The guinea fowl special was one of the nicest dishes I’ve ever eaten.  Very pleasant and interesting wine options at reasonable prices.  You should go to this restaurant.

name: Bryan Perez
review: This is still one of the best restaurants I have ever eaten at. My wife and I were a little nervous getting in the restaurant because we thought we were at the wrong location at first. Once we went inside we were greeted by a wonderful hostess and brought to this long table for the restaurant. The table is for all guests and are meant for openness and communication with all. The sommelier and the hostess welcomed us and treated us with excellence. We have never felt more welcomed in any restaurant like this before. They went through the menu with us and paired us with a really good wine to go with. The wine was excellent, I really wish I can find that wine in the US because it was so smooth and so light. Then began the first course of the food which was a special from the chef and oh my god, it was perfect. Everything they brought out had an element of surprise and was plated to perfection. And the taste was out of this world. Everything from the antipasti to the primi to the dessert was to die for. If I ever come back to Venice again, and I am making sure I stop here first before I get to the hotel. It was that good. I will not be surprised they have a Michelin star by the time I come back to visit. Thank you again, we loved it.

name: Tanja Lokancevic
review:
Very special restaurant an interesting concept- one long dining table for all guests which invites to chat with the strangers next to you and gives a living room kind of vibe to it. The menu is very selected with regional products and modern variation of dishes. We had to wait a little for our seats and were given 2 glasses of wine which we did noy have to pay. You may order a single dish for dinner or choose  between a 4 course or 7 course menue. The 4 course menue could be orderd withoud seafood/fish if wanted. We ordered the 7 course menue and decided to go all in. The waiters were exceptionally friendly and well trained. We were presented 4 different wines to accompany the course, the choice we made was perfect. The wine selection is very exciting and special. We had an amuse bouche and then 6 hearty dishes followed by a desert. Even though I have to admit I am not very into fishy dishes I liked all of them except for one. I did understand the concept though and it was clear it was a well prepared combination. We paid 200€ overall.
Definitely a very special experience offside of the commib touristy restaurants. Would recommend!
Just one little point of constructive criticism: the bathroom was a little dirty and there was neither toilet paper nor hand towels when I went.
Thanks for the great evening over all!

name: Annick Tobojka
review:
A hidden gem on the way to a Michelin star
Such delightful creative cuisine !
Extremely courteous , knowledgeable and professional staff.
We are well traveled and I will say clearly the exquisitely tastefully prepared dishes will totally delight.

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
review: One of my favorite restaurants in Italy - exceptionally beautiful food that showcases not only creativity and technique, but also true to its Venetian roots and locale. I've been three times in the last month, and each time is just as good as the last. Love the sustainability and local factor, with their great care to working closely with local producers for high quality. Excellent wine selection and warm and kind service, witg high attention to detail.

name: Heinz Huber
review: Perfect and delicious food and wine journey without reservation. The long table is the place to discover the Italian way of life. Many thanks to the entire team. We‘ll be back!👍🤩👌

name: Emre Edev
review: Venetian/italian with a a fusion twist. Delicious. Great wines. Don’t miss.

name: Cheryl McKnight
review: La Bomba was the best meal we had our whole week in Venice.  If you are looking for a different experience with incredible food and blends of flavors, this is the place.  Mateo made us feel like we were family sharing a meal at his home.  A meal we will never forget!  Thank you!

name: louisa m
review: Amazing pizza and the dough was lovely and thin and light so didn’t feel too heavy! First day of our holiday food a success! Had the specie burrata pizza and added sausage - so tasty! Great atmosphere and whilst we were too full it appears the tiramisu is popular in here!

name: Marc Pledge
review: A great find in the back streets of Venice. One of the tastiest pizzas I’ve had. They are great value and really big! It looks like a family run restaurant, the staff were very welcoming, spoke perfect English and were very helpful. Two pizzas, a salad, two glasses of wine, water and two coffees came to €55.

name: Margarita k
review: I liked the food there, pizza with seafood was great, salad also. Service and price is good, the portions are large, you can easily share. I recommend❤️

name: Jonathan Velazquez Colon
review: We had a great experience at this trattoria. We ordered the Pizza Diavola along with two others (whose names we can’t remember but were equally delicious), two local beers recommended by our friendly waitress, and ended the meal with a classic tiramisu. The food was tasty, the service was quick and attentive, and the atmosphere felt welcoming. Prices were average for the region and well worth it for the quality and experience. Highly recommend if you’re in Venice looking for a solid, satisfying meal.

name: Jens Ljungkvist
review:
This place is a hidden gem.

No bouncers outside asking tourists to come in. Instead, just a quiet place, great air conditioning and amazing pizzas.

Both adults and kids loved the pizzas and to be honest they were pretty standard Italian pizzas but they used quality ingredients, good mozzarella and they were baked perfectly. It was how eating a pizza in Italy should be.

The service was friendly and efficient and I would love to go back here for dinner.

name: Ashley Spohn
review:
Delicious spot for pizza! Cute little spot, the patio was closed because it was too hot. Otherwise that would’ve been a cute area to sit as well!
Must try the Tedesca! It will allow you, the flavor is great! The Bufaolina was also really good !

name: Bryan Obright
review: Wow!  This place is great.  The pizza is very good.  Staff are friendly.  Well worth the few steps off the main path.

name: xgbdx
review:
The BEST pizza I’ve ever had. So delicious. The base wasn’t floppy and had a nice crunch. The sauce had a lot of flavour and it didn’t even need any condiments.

Staff were lovely. Gave us recommendations for exploring Venice. Would definitely go again. Miss the pizza already! Hard to find it like that in the UK.

Also SO cheap!!!

name: Jenny Zhang
review:
The food was good overall! The Florentine steak was juicy and flavorful, the grilled sea bass was really good, and the lobster pasta was also worth trying. Prices were fair for the area — not cheap, but reasonable for the location.

Everything was great until the bill arrived — there was a €3 per person “coperto” (seat charge) and a “servizio” fee on the bill. When I asked, the waiter said it was a tax, but I later realized it’s actually a service charge. I live in the U.S., where we usually tip, so I just wish they would explain that more clearly.

The food was delicious and the atmosphere cozy. If you ask me whether I’d come back — I’m honestly not sure, because the extra "tax".

name: Lucy Watson
review:
Oh my gosh!!!
Food was divinely delicious , cooked perfectly and right on cue.
Anti pasto dish of prosciutto and sweet, sweet melon followed by a WHOLE sea bass accompanied with , again, perfectly cooked and presented vegetables.
The service impeccable with Armand and Marco delivering with such charm, handsome smiles and very friendly and enticing natures.

name: marc gregory
review:
We were in Venice for 5 days. Our promise to each other was to try a new place for every meal...

This place made us fail that goal in the very best way!

We ended up eating here 3 times. And each time the food was fantastic!

The service staff are the best we have encountered in Venice!

We will be going back again!

name: ardian kaceli
review:
A fantastic experience! A warm and tasteful atmosphere, friendly and professional service, and simply amazing food. Every dish is carefully prepared, preserving the authentic Italian flavor.
An ideal place for a quiet dinner or special moments.
I’ll definitely be coming back! 🇮🇹✨

name: Bethany Jones
review: Marco was so kind and helpful. He was very attentive and caring. We ordered a T-bone to share and it was DELICIOUS. We walked past this restaurant in the morning and agreed how cute the atmosphere was for outdoor eating. We got so excited when we found it again in the afternoon for lunch to finally sit and eat. Service was very quick and the quality of the food did not disappoint!!! The staff even gave us a couple of free Limencello shots at the end of our meal. Overall loved this dining experience!

name: emily mack
review: Fabio was fabulous! Had the duck ragu with gnocchi and my mom had the beef filet, both were delicious. Checks were delivered with limoncello on the house. It was a bit chilly so they offered blankets and there was a heat lamp out. Service was very swift. Would recommend.

name: Alicja Wojecka
review: I ordered cutlets di Milanese, cannot recommend. Fried too long because of what was too hard and there was more breading than meat, my bf ordered fritto misto and tasted very bad too fried in I think old oil because really tasted very bad… service like for Italian standards not good enough they didn’t pour water to the glass…

name: Beka Gvaramia
review:
It’s a great restaurant in the center of venice. Vibes are amazing and food is great! Pastas are much better than Pizzas. I would recommend if you would go for pasta at this place or lasagna. Special shoutout to waiter Lali for extra attention and nice vibes!
