Would definitely worth to visit this place for a nice dinner in Venice!

name: 김봄봄
review:
I visited here for lunch on my last day in Venice and so happy that I experienced this restaurant! on the very peaceful and beautiful street far away from centre and tourist area. and the food was humble and full of flavours and delicious!
attentioned to details and really nice service as well!

name: Simon Merriman
review: We typically tried this trattoria on our last day in Venice. What a shame as it was brilliant. Seems to mainly have locals as guests. The food was all very affordable and tasted fantastic. We had a couple of traditional starters followed by the most amazing pizzas.  The service threw us a little as the lady serving originated from England and this saved us from murdering the Italian language - she was wonderful and gave great insight. Would I go back? Yes, I wouldn’t want to miss this friendly secret.

name: Megan McKenzie
review: Lovely little spot, family owned and operated, if you are staying in the area I highly recommend this restaurant. We had the pizza 2 nights in a row, the charcuterie &cheese board was also nice. The table next to us had the local white fish and she said it was also very good. The owner, Paulo, made us feel at home and spoke very good English. The house wine was also very good.

name: Katrina Avrutov
review:
What can I say? I would love not to share my opinion, so this place will stay the hidden gem forever, but we do not visit that often, here I go! We’ve stayed in Venice 11 years ago and had dinner in this Trattoria every night. We came this week and we were happy to find the same amazing atmosphere of ‘local’ and ‘homey’ and ‘only for those who know:)’
But of course the most amazing is the great food - best pizza ever! Thank you, Marco! - great friendly service - thank you to all hard working people of this restaurant- and thank you Paulo for keeping this amazing place and making it even better!
We had different pizzas every evening, tried all of their pastas, several of the side dishes and local wine and beer of course - everything was perfect!

name: Sonja Hardenbrook
review: On a recent visit to Venice, we stumbled upon this restaurant while exploring our neighborhood. We ended up drinking or eating there 3 times on our 5 day visit to Venice. The pasta, meat, pizza and seafood were all amazing. Desserts finished off the evening perfectly. The staff were warm, friendly and knowledgeable and no tourists were in sight, save for our group. When they learned it was my mother's 80th, they made her a special candle and celebrated with us. I highly recommend this place!

name: Armon Parang
review: Service was bad. Waiter only came outside once, had to stand in line to pay check. Menu didn’t have much options for pasta, seafood or anything you’d expect from a restaurant in Venice. Poor fish was bland, ragu had no flavor. Pizza had over hour wait. Cover charge was expensive. Not worth going here, go somewhere else with more options and better quality and service

name: Nika Šimić
review: The food is good and reasonably priced, but the menu is very limited. None of the staff speak English, and the menu is only in Italian, which makes it almost impossible to order anything unless you speak the language. I get that we’re in Italy, but most places still offer menus in other languages for tourists.Other than that everything else is good.

name: Mook T
review: One of the best restaurant experiences I've had in Italy (out of 15+ visits). Efficient, friendly service, very affordable, great food. Highly recommend the pasta with mussels and the steak.

name: Krishna Swanson
review: We went there for an evening meal with our tour and it was so good that went back the next day to try other things. The seafood sampling plate was delicious with octopus that was delicate and fresh. Risotto was cooked excellently. Pasta dishes were delicious. Fish was bursting with flavor. The service was friendly and attentive. We were there for almost two hours enjoying each course. Nice seating outside away from the crowds.

name: Amy Childress
review:
Delicious food, amazing staff, and they made our family celebration a perfect night to remember!

I can’t say enough good things about my experience at Trattoria Rialto Novo. I was visiting Venice to celebrate my daughter’s engagement and was searching for a good restaurant for the celebration dinner for the whole family. I stopped in at Rialto Novo for lunch to check out the menu and atmosphere.  The first thing I appreciated was that the restaurant is just a block away from the crowds - making it quieter and easier to connect and hear each other for a family dinner. When I shared  the situation, Tani and his team jumped in with their ideas  and options and I could see they were committed to make the night special. I visited the restaurant several times before the event to ask more questions and finalize plans and every time I arrived they made me feel welcomed and comfortable.

In the end, they worked within my budget to put together an incredible menu - I think maybe 6 courses?! - consisting of some dishes that are not on the regular restaurant menu that were very special and delicious. Our menu centered around the fresh seafood that they get in each day. But they also accommodated people in our group who were vegetarian or did not like seafood. They helped me work with the flower store just across from the restaurant to create gorgeous flower arrangements for our table.

The main course of the Mediterranean Sea bass might be the most delicious dish I’ve ever had. And the dessert was incredible - I wish I had the recipe. I forgot to take pictures of every course but they were all presented beautifully!

Everything they did exceeded my expectations and I feel so lucky and grateful that I found them.

name: Mark Johannes
review:
Food (pizza and salad with house wine) was good. Not exceptional or bad. Service was ok. Not great, and not bad. Atmosphere, could be better(sat in courtyard and had leaves and tree parts all over the table and waiter did not clean or brush off the table when we sat).
I was surprised at the cover charge per person. It is shown on the menu (tiny letters at the bottom). To me, this is a hidden fee to get extra money. I definitely don’t feel like the place deserved an extra 5 euros for the quality and service. If they need the money, put an extra euro on all items.

name: Ilirjan Sulkja
review:
A hidden gem just steps from Rialto
We discovered this restaurant by chance, during a walk near the Rialto Bridge, and it turned out to be a real surprise. Far from the tourist chaos, it offers an authentic and relaxed atmosphere.

The staff was very kind and made us feel welcome right away. The menu offers typical dishes of the Venetian tradition, prepared with fresh ingredients and presented with care. Excellent wine.

Fair prices for the quality offered and for the very central location. Highly recommended for those looking for a real Venetian experience, far from the tourist traps!

name: Katie Pownell
review: A nice patio area that is open, but isn't in the crowded main streets. Amazing food. I had the best fish I've ever had, it was so tender and fresh, truly delicious. My dad got crab gnocchi that was simply perfect. The servers were friendly and funny. Wonderful dining experience, with great seafood.

name: George Johnson
review:
Came here and was sat down and given a limited menu for some reason as others ordered pizza and it was not on the menu I was given. I was then given bread and olive oil but no plate, balsamic, or pepper... Definitely disappointed.

Then I got the 4 ravioli for 20 euros and ate them under my dripping umbrella. You're definitely just paying for how close this is to Rialto with the standard 3 euro seating charge

name: Arsen Barbullushi
review:
Nestled in a quiet corner of the city, is the kind of place that instantly transports you to the heart of Italy. From the warm welcome at the door to the last sip of espresso.

The menu is a love letter to classic Italian cuisine.

The wine list features several regional Italian bottles, and the staff are happy to recommend pairings. Our server, Argent, was knowledgeable and attentive without being overbearing — the kind of service that elevates a good meal into a memorable one.
I’ll be back again 👌

name: Kristel Hall
review: My best girlfriend and I came here on our Euro trip, we were only in Venice for two days and chose this restaurant for one of our two dinners, and it was absolutely amazing. We had burrata to start, and then each had a pasta dish, as well as one of their desserts and cappuccino and limoncello, and literally everything was incredible. The service was also immaculate, Antonio was an amazing server and took really good care of us, always coming back just in time to fill our wine glasses so we never had to ourselves. Could not recommend this place more.

name: Christopher Don
review: Feelings because was amazing. The food was super flavorful and being able to sit there on the side right next to the canal was chef's kiss to the night. I especially like this place when the Sun sets. Because it just has that whole vibe and energy. Like a movie scene that you would expect to get from eating at a restaurant in Venice. The staff there is extra friendly and super nice. And really quick to respond, I would definitely go back to this restaurant again.

name: Radoslav Stefanov
review: We had a great time. The restaurant is situated in a very quiet and calm street right next to the canal. The staff were very polite and welcoming. The food was very good and the prices were reasonable. Definitely recommend giving it a try.

name: Sam Cabral
review: Stopped here by chance while getting lost in the streets of Venice and this was one of the best meals I ate in Italy. I lucked out with Adella, a female server who gave me an excellent recommendation for the seabass spaghetti and the homemade panna cotta. Washed it down with a limoncello on the house. And a big plus - there’s ample outdoor seating here on a quieter street away from the main tourist blocks.

name: Tony Martinez
review:
We came across this restaurant while walking around some of the beautiful streets of Venice. They sit you right in front of the canal with beautiful views and it was exactly what we were anticipating when we thought of Venice.
The service was phenomenal from the Host to our waiters. I specifically remember one waitress by the name Adella who was so very kind and helpful. She ensured our experience was incredible and made sure to check on us frequently, (thank you!)
We ordered multiple food items that covered many parts of the menu. Not shown here is the bruschetta with tomato that we ordered and was very tasteful. (See photo)

I would come back again!!

name: Kelly King
review: I came to Venice for the day last minute, alone, with no agenda. As I made my way to the tourist sites, i noticed this spot on a quieter street, lacking crowds, right next to a canal. I was able to get a seat by the water as a solo traveler, after I asked. And they obliged. Greatful for that :) Pizza was good, wine was great, service was attentive and wonderful, and the quiet view was nice while i planned out the rest of my stay. If you’re looking for a quieter spot with great service, i definitely recommend!

name: Barbara Pikl Malis
review: We had an amazing lunch in Venice by the canal! I savored delicious pasta with shrimp, and my husband enjoyed a perfectly cooked steak. The food, service, Masum particularly was very kind, and stunning waterside view made it truly unforgettable!

name: Sharon K. (sk_cali)
review:
From start to finish, everything about this restaurant was exceptional. The ambiance was warm and inviting, the service was friendly and attentive, and the food—simply outstanding. It’s the perfect combination that makes a dining experience truly memorable.

Ferat, our server, was especially wonderful. He made us feel genuinely welcome and took great care to ensure we were enjoying both our food and drinks. His attentiveness and kindness set the tone for the whole evening.

This isn’t one of those typical “tourist trap” spots where you feel like just another passerby. Instead, it feels personal and authentic.

The spaghetti Bolognese was rich and flavorful, while the bruschetta was grilled perfectly—not too hard or crunchy—with fresh tomatoes that struck the ideal balance of sweet and tart.

I highly recommend this place to anyone looking to relax and enjoy excellent food with top-notch service!

name: Sleight Family
review: If you are looking for a restaurant that is on a small canal, where you can watch the gondolas pass by, as you eat a leisurely Italian dinner, this is it. We liked our Cacio e Pepe in the Parmigiano wheel so much the first night, we came back and had Fettuccini Alfredo a second night. The other reason we came back a second time, was for the desert! The chocolate cream and pistachio cream were delicious!!!!!

name: Yingjie C
review: We were only in Venice for 2 days, chanced upon this restaurant on day1 and decided to dine in because the menu looks bomb. Decided to come again on the next day because the food was so so good! (Had grilled king prawns, grilled beef fillet, risotto and ravioli) The servers were all so sweet and friendly. Special shoutout to Jack, thanks for being so attentive to our needs and making the whole eating atmosphere so homely!

name: dana iancu
review:
Very good food, friendly staff and good vibe. Jack, the waiter, was great and Elmar, the host, was awesome throughout the whole evening. Close to Campo S Polo Piazza. Quiet, seats available outside that is  a great plus.
We ordered tagliatelle Tonno - great choice, calamari alla grigllia, talliatelle with somonne, insalatte adriatica, all of them verry good. Can’t believe we were running around for a 4.6 star tripadvisor restaurant where we book a table outside just to get there and have no table outside, only inside. we passed by trattoria il campanile 2 times , and second time we decided to stop. By the way, it” s a 4.3 star on Trip Advisor that we overlooked🙈 Best decision of the day.
Great job!

name: Brandy Stajkowski
review: Diego and his brilliant smile and warm welcome are what brought us in to enjoy a lovely and delicious meal. We enjoyed the food thoroughly!! Highly recommend the Fiorentina steak (to share). Thank you for such a wonderful evening and perfect first evening in Venezia!!

name: Tina Moore
review: Our meal was very delicious. The  wait staff was attentive and explained the food that was on the menu that we were not familiar with. We were seated outside. The foot traffic that passed was respectful and polite. The ambiance was superb.

name: Anne de LOISY
review: Unbelievable but true! The worst pizzas in the world are at Trattoria Al Campanile in Venice. We ordered gluten-free pizzas to please the kids: a margarita, a veggie, and a capriciosa. What a disappointment: industrial sweet pizza crust (and they have the nerve to charge you an extra €3 per pizza crust!), bland tomato sauce, and rubbery lactoserum mozzarella. And for the veggie pizza, bring your own chopsticks if you want to have a chance of finding a vegetable (uncooked, at that!) Avoid at all costs!

name: Jeane C
review: Our family stayed in a small town near Venice for several days, and our favorite place was definitely this restaurant. Every meal was a delight, with a romantic and cozy atmosphere. The staff were not only handsome but also warm and welcoming, making us feel right at home. I highly recommend trying the dishes I picked, especially the seafood—it was incredibly fresh and delicious. Whether it's a rainy or sunny day, dining here offers a unique experience, and the vibe changes between day and night. I’ll definitely visit again next time I’m in Venice! ✨🍽️

name: Stephanie May
review: What a lovely establishment. The food was outstanding and our server Jack was amazing. His friendly approach and humor were much appreciated as we determined the gluten free options on the menu. We definitely would recommend dinner at the Al Campanile.

name: Joachim Tan
review: Used to be my favourite pasta place in Venice, but now it’s just a sad tourist trap. Apparently the ownership changed & now it tastes nothing like it used to. 10/10 in the past, 1/10 now.

name: Mia Gore
review: Dinner for 2 no booking required at 6.30pm. We ordered baked scallops, seafood spaghetti & three cheese gnocchi. All were spectacular! My husband even loved the scallops. The gnocchi was exactly how I envisaged it should be - pillow soft! So much seafood in the pasta, mains came with free bread & butter.

name: The Travel Agent’s Handbag
review:
Right at the back door to Basilica San Marco this restaurant is one of our favourites in Venice. Right outside our hotel (Hotel Concordia) it has an amazing menu for breakfast, lunch, and dinner!  Any of the seafood dishes are well worth a try.
Atmosphere is amazing because it’s close but not right on top of the square, which means prices are good, and you can chat without yelling. We were blessed to have an Opera rehearsal happening while eating dinner.
In our five visits we’ve had nothing but the best service. Staff are attentive, with Khan being a standout, and the little “extras” make you feel so welcomed. Definitely worth a visit.

name: Barie Diy
review: perfect is the way to describe our lunch, best we had so far in our entire holiday, we were able to relax and enjoy our europe summertl trip.

name: Gevorg Sulkhanian
review: Love the food! Big portions, and everything was very tasty—just a great place for dinner! I’m giving 4 stars because of the waiter who kept interrupting every 5 minutes with comments like, ‘Hey boss, it’s good, good, boss, it’s tasty,’ or when new clients came in, he would push me, saying, ‘Ask them if they like it, you like it, boss?’ To the restaurant management: please address this issue and fire him. It made it hard for me to enjoy a nice dinner with my wife.

name: Jeff Breining
review: We had a travel group of 6 and decided to drop in. We were certain that we had lunch there in 2023 while vacationing in Venice. Our Waiter, Khan, seated us immediately and had our orders in minutes. To say the food was great is an understatement. We wined and dined and Khan brought out some cordials as an after dinner treat. Khan gave us amazing service and we will come back next time we're in Venice. Conveniently located on the left just past the Piazzetta dei Leoncini, (Lion Square) as you round the corner from St Mark's Cathedral

name: Mark Dreyer
review: The pizza was excellent. Great crust and tasty spicy salami.. Our waiter, Khan, was more than hellpful.We watched he prepare a steak for the next table and he made it look easy and very mouth watering.

name: Charles Ersimoen
review: A really nice place where you can eat high quality food. The pizza was DELICIOUS!!! 10/10! The staff are very nice but we were told we can't buy a pizza to share because it's small, it turned out to be a 30 cm diameter pizza which would be perfect for us two not-very-hungry people. Also the toilet seat was not properly attached to the bowl. I definitely recommend this place!

name: Dale Squeo
review:
We had planned to visit a panini shop around the corner. The waiter grabbed our attention and pointed out the menu. 2 courses for €16. No cover charge or service fees.
He showed photos on his phone of what the food looked like. We decided to give it a try.
We are sure happy we did.
The service was amazing and the staff is all very friendly.
I had the pasta with seafood and the second dish was fried calamari.
The seafood pasta had plenty of seafood.
The calamari was the best I have ever eaten.
The food is fresh and full of flavour.
I highly recommend this restaurant.
Thanks for a great meal on our final night in Italy.
It was everything I was looking for and expected.

name: Yizhou Wu
review:
No more prawn ravioli, and no more almond tart

It's an okay place, the food isn't mindblowing. Simple flavours, pasta texture was fine

Seafood was quite fresh, portions are big

name: Nik Makepeace
review: Walked in off the street for lunch with my daughter and had the 'menu di mare' which was great: pickled sardines, a Venetian standard, along with a light, fresh tomato and prawn tagliatelle, and grilled king prawns with polenta. Food was rustic but good, great flavours, no pretence. The waiter was very nice to my daughter, and the place just had a good simple vibe.

name: Gracinha Arceo
review: BEST AUTHENTIC ITALIAN FOOD WE’VE HAD! Definitely going back! Everything tasted so fresh and it felt like everything  was home cooked. Service was very fast and professional

name: G
review:
Absolutely delicious and wonderful atmosphere. Hidden in an alley way, you'll miss this gem if you walk too fast.  Pesto ravioli's were so flavorful. They are generous with their house wine. The almond tart was warmed up and perfectly sweet. Definitely give this spot a try.
Special note: they have a traditional toilet with no seat cover

name: Jonathan Stewart
review: The food here was extraordinary! We highly recommend the Gnocchi Bolognese and the Spaghetti al Nero di seppia. We got there as they opened and grabbed a great seat. The staff was welcoming and super helpful. Give this place a try.

name: Alena
review:
A charming, authentic establishment with excellent pasta!

If you want to experience real Venice, this is the place to go!

Friendly service, unbelievably delicious pasta, and a pleasant atmosphere.

I visited twice during my stay because I loved it so much.

My favorite is the pasta with zucchini and shrimp, but the pasta with mussels is also delicious.
The ravioli with cheese could use a little something extra, but it's still good.

By the way, the scallops were too rubbery.

name: Ashley
review: A last minute stop looking for an authentic meal. It did not disappoint. Welcomed in and enjoyed a delicious meal. Started with a cheese plate appetizer and a litre of wine. Followed by gnocchi and pesto pasta. We were not rushed and thoroughly enjoyed our meal here.

name: Cristina Zlatin
review: This was such a great experience! After having horrible experiences in other restaurants in Venice, this was a fresh breath of air. They are great! The food was amazing and at a very fair price. Try the steamed mussels and the baked seabass, you wont be disappointed! The ingredients were fresh and the service was impeccable. We will definitely come back!

name: Parag zade
review: The food was simple and sumptuous. They know the skill of enhancing subtle flavours that give you happiness! The pastas we had didn't feel much different at the moment, but since then that taste has been hard to find!

name: Diễm Lý
review: I love the food here. It was the best food and restaurant i have ever eat when i was in Venice. The flavor of the spaghetti and seafood so Good, and they gave you a lot a lot of seafood in your dish. You get 3 meals for 14 - 15eu. Very affordable price and very friendly staffs.

name: Helen
review: Our first night in Venice couldn’t have started better. The sardine antipasto was refreshing, the black ink pasta was rich and flavorful, and the fried seafood platter was perfectly crispy. Paired with fresh oysters and a glass of white wine, it was a lovely, heartfelt dinner. Warm service and cozy vibes made it unforgettable.

name: shu
review:
I visited this restaurant twice during my two-day stay in Venice—it was so good, I couldn’t resist coming back! The food was outstanding, and the staff were friendly and welcoming. The atmosphere was also very pleasant.

I highly recommend the squid ink pasta, which I found to be especially delicious, but the pizza and risotto are also excellent choices.

If you’re not sure where to eat, I strongly suggest choosing this place over randomly picking a restaurant.

Please note that there is a service charge of around €5 per person, which is quite standard across most restaurants in the area.

name: Cedric Chang
review: Fresh seafood, friendly service, and local flavor all in one spot. The black ink pasta is a must-try—rich, savory, and authentic. Fried seafood was crispy and light, and oysters were fresh. A great value for quality. Highly recommended if you’re visiting Venice.

name: Aaron Brown
review: First ever meal in Venice and it was great. Food was delicious and they had a nice selection of wines. We were walking past and they were able to accomodate our group of 13 without any fuss. Service was very good and food was prepared very quickly. Highly recommended 👍🏼👍🏼

name: Cara Sloat
review: Our late-night heroes!  We learned, to our chagrin, most Venetian restaurants close their kitchens 1-2 hours before their listed close times, and due to travel we were up late and hungry.  This restaurant also stopped cooking ½ an hour before their 12:30am close time (just before we walked in) but were kind and flexible: our server welcomed us and offered several options they could plate (the lasagna!  It was SO GOOD!  The salad – amazing!  The tiramisu – plated with love!  Our beverages – refreshed several times through our meal!)   If you find yourself needing food after 10pm in Venice, try here, and at any time, stop in for good food and caring service.

name: Doug
review: We were walking by and were offered free Prosecco or Limoncello shots so we decided to stay. It wasn't busy so we were a little worried but it turned out to be fantastic. We had the "tourist special" where you can have a 3 course meal for $14.50 Euro. You get to choose from about 5 pasta dishes and another 5 choices and insalata or potatoes. My wife had the spaghetti Bolognese, chicken and insalata. I had the black squid ink pasta, roasted potatoes and fried Calamari. Everything was great especially the fried Calamari. The servings were huge and we were so stuffed when leaving. We did get our Limoncello shots as well. The service was excellent and the host was very funny. Don't pass this place by if it's not busy because it should be. Excellent value in Venice.

name: Tania Veiga
review: We did not have reservations here but they quickly found a table for us. Although the space is small and tight, the service and the food was well worth the close-quarters. We started with baby squid in a bread bowl which was excellent, in a wonderful tomato-based sauce. Our main dishes, a seafood pasta and liver with polenta were so flavorful, and not to mention very large portions! We were so full, there was no room for dessert! Highly recommend Taverna Scalinetto for a wonderful dinner!

name: Taylor E
review: I had this restaurant saved as a recommendation and we went after the Vivaldi concert since it was near the church. It was later in the night on a Friday and the restaurant was mostly empty. However, everything was great and delicious. Service was perfect and the food was fantastic. The bread was the fluffiest and softest bread we had in all of Italy.. it was so delicious. I ordered the spinach ravioli with sausage and my partner ordered duck gnocchi. We then split the tiramisu for dessert and watermelon was provided on the house for us. The interior of this restaurant was beautiful and charming. They had a seasonal/daily menu as well as the standard menu and it is evident that this changes often which is neat. Highly recommend!

name: Josephine W
review:
This was a cosy and charming restaurant, with a nautical themed decor. The ambience was warm and inviting, anchored by quaint, maritime touches.

The seafood was fresh and succulent, with the highlight being the seabass served under a smoked cloche, which added a subtle aroma and a nice dramatic touch. Service was attentive and professional throughout.

name: Amy Akers
review: We were thrilled to find another place with so many gluten free options! The food was great and the staff were incredibly polite and patient. The plates are small as to be expected so after a long day of roaming the city Id recommend at least one 1st dish per two persons or an apatizer dish for each person.

name: Earl Ermita
review: Seafood pasta is really nice! As a solo diner… i felt that not much attention is given than those in tables with more people. My plate  came late… good food, nice people but should be improved.

name: Will
review:
I've been to Venice many times. But it was my first time here. My wife wanted to eat the cuttlefish ink pasta from one of the recommended places by Japanese tourists. We couldn't go to the first place due to the distance. So we chose this restaurant. And let me tell you, I am seriously grateful that other restaurant was too far. Because this restaurant has become my absolute favorite place to eat in Venice.

The decor made me feel relaxed since I was a sailor. The main staff was the nicest guy I've ever met in Italy. The food was so good it deserves a Michelin star. It was so good we not only came on the first day. We made sure it was our last restaurant before leaving Venice.

I had the black ravioli the first visit and spaghetti alla busara the second. For appetizers we had the fish plater on the first day and the meat plater on the second. We had tiramisu on the second visit for dessert.

To all the staff at Taverna Scalinetto, especially the gentleman in the photo, thank you so much for helping make my honeymoon memorable. We loved your restaurant and hope to come back if we visit Venice again in the future!

Until we meet again!
-Will

name: Keely Shofner
review: We had a really nice dinner at Taverna Scalinetto tonight. Both my husband and I wanted pasta and this restaurant was highly rated. We just happened to come across it meandering down the streets of Venice. I have a ravioli with sausage in the red sauce. My husband had a pistachio pesto pasta. We also enjoyed a bottle of wine and a delicious pistachio tiramisu.  The service was great. It was nice and cool inside with the heatwave. And overall such a wonderful meal. I loved my dinner. It was delicious.

name: Kirit Singh
review:
We had such a great experience here. We tried their charcuterie board that came with an assortment of Venetian meats and I will have to say the salami was our favorite! They also provided a complimentary bread basket that we used to eat the meat. The charcuterie board we got did NOT come with cheese! Just a forewarning. But we have a great time nonetheless.

After our appetizer finished they brought out our pistachio pesto pasta. That was by far one of the tastiest pastas we’ve ever tasted! A subtle hint of the pistachio nutty flavor and such a rich taste. Additionally, the pasta didn’t leave you feeling bloated. Such a great experience.
There is also the option of sitting outside in their small courtyard area or inside in their cabin like feel! We opted for inside because it felt very pirate ship but cabin like feel.

Also, there was a 6 euro cover charge per person
.

Overall had a great experience would recommend!

name: Masaki Kawakami
review:
Had dinner at Trattoria Alla Ferrata in Venice, and it was one of the most memorable meals of my trip.
The homemade pasta was incredible — rich, comforting, and cooked to perfection. The staff were genuinely kind and welcoming, bringing that real Italian warmth you can’t fake. The restaurant was lively, filled with locals and travelers sharing great food and wine.
It’s the kind of place that reminds you why Italian dining is so special — not just for the food, but for the heart behind it. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a real, home-style Venetian experience.

name: Elliot Yoon
review:
Great first meal in Venice. The bruschetta, calamari and tagliatelle with mushrooms and shrimp in particular were excellent. The salad wasn’t anything special, but went great with out food. We thought the pizza was just average for Italy standards, but above average for US standards.

Overall, a very nice meal with friendly staff!

name: Kye Vowles
review:
I had the Tagliatelle with porcini mushrooms & local small shrimp & my partner had the Spaghetti Carbonara. The flavours were absolutely AMAZING. Great service, quick and polite.
The white wine was the best house wine I've had here too.

Would 10/10 recommend to anyone.

name: Brittany Lett
review: Hossain was my server tonight and he was amazing. The food was 10/10. The pasta, the wine, the pizza  the fresh ingredients from the garden to make the cocktails. Everything 10/10. Can’t wait to come return and will recommend to all my friends and coworkers!

name: Asela Malawa
review:
This is our second dinner time at Alla Ferrata. We were there 5 years before.
The food is same quality. Service also friendly.
Nice atmosphere at the garden area.
Quite busy the day we were there in summer July 2nd 2025. I celebrated my birthday dinner there. Almost good.

name: Sara Wasson
review: This was our first night in Venice and I am glad we had our dinner here. The food was very good, reasonably priced and came out fast. I also enjoyed the atmosphere of the outside seating. It is enclosed (this is October) and has heat lamps. I would eat here again!

name: Ling Zi Yan
review:
Carbonara pizza 8/10
Taglialette w shrimp and zucchini 6.5/10 a bit too cloi and creamy for my liking
Lasagna 8/10
Pasta MaMaMiYa 9/10
Vongole 8.5/10 super delicious and good portion but slightly salty! Still great though

name: Helena Coelho
review:
I had an amazing experience at this restaurant!
The food was absolutely delicious — I had the best pasta: tagliatelle with shrimp and mushrooms, and it was full of flavor.
The atmosphere was warm and inviting, and the service was impressively fast.
The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which made the whole experience even better.
I absolutely recommend this place to anyone looking for a great meal and a lovely dining experience!

name: Jaime Killion
review: First day in Venice and wanted a quick lunch along the canal in the shade and this delivered! The food was excellent! The staff was welcoming. The portions were generous!   Only wish we could have tried more of the menu but there were only two of us. There is a cover charge of 2€ per person, as a forewarning but totally worth it! Lunch was about 40€ for 2 but with canal views and ample delicious food, it’s worth it!

name: Marta Mortal
review:
We chose this place for lunch, and it was the best choice during our visit to Venice! The spaghetti with scampi was delicious, the food was excellent, and even the grilled fish was top-notch.
Highly recommended, especially for families with small children. The waitress, with her smile and friendliness, made us feel right at home. ❤️ I highly recommend this place; it's definitely worth a visit; you'll remember the taste of the food for a long time!

name: Sabrina Huang
review: Wonderful local trattoria in Venice! The food was fresh and authentic, with generous portions and a cozy atmosphere. The staff was friendly and made us feel at home. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a true Venetian experience.

name: Pasqual Vita
review:
I had a wonderful experience at this restaurant for lunch today. Everything was delicious. The portions were huge, the seafood flavors were exceptional, the view of the canal was beautiful, and the atmosphere was very relaxing!
The service was quick and the waitress was friendly. We'll definitely be back!

name: Kirsty Hepburn
review: We booked a table for 8.30pm but ended up coming earlier. Such lovely staff, food was amazing. It was raining the weekend we in Venice and this was 15 minutes walk from our hotel but it was so worth it!! I would definitely come back here and would highly recommend

name: Ordenadorix_29
review: Of the many good restaurants in Venice, this isn't one of them. I ordered baked macaroni, and honestly, it was terrible. Anyone could cook much better macaroni at home. Plus, they're quite unprofessional, as the photos of the dishes are very different from reality. I'm only giving it 2 stars because some dishes, like the pizza, were of acceptable quality.

name: Darrell Blevins Jr.
review: I am in pasta heaven. The sauce was so delicious I cannot describe it in words. Service was nice and the atmosphere, well it's Venice in the summertime, what else do I need to say?

name: Franco Fantini
review: We're back for the second time. Fantastic place, beautiful interiors, and, above all, AMAZING FOOD and EXCELLENT SERVICE from everyone. Quantity and quality at prices beyond expectations. Recommended!👏👏👏👏👏

name: MFoodieTravelAroundTheWorld
review: It takes so long to get our food (about 45mins) with very basic quality of food, service is not satisfying at all while waitress asked tips in cash. Expensive, not worth it.

name: Paula Kroener
review: I was there for New Years. The restaurant has a unique planning, where you can reserve a table only for two hours. It is understood that after the two hours are over, you have to leave, as the new guests are arriving. It was a new concept for me but I still enjoyed my time and didn‘t feel rushed. The food was excellent (I ordered cuttlefish pasta and braised bass) and came in a timely order. The tiramisu was excellent. The servers were incredibly attentive and could speak several languages. I can only recommend!

name: Life of Misstep
review: Excellent place to eat. Very fancy. Very knowledgeable server with an awesome sense of humor and made me aware of the proper way to consume grappa lol

name: Sudi Fang
review: This restraint is right by the water. Enemy though the location is a bit too close to the bridge, but their service and food make it up. They squally Venice doesn’t provide good service even the 5 star hotels, because the people are too tired of tourism. But this restaurant brings you very home atmosphere. And the seafood lasagna and the black cuttlefish is a must try

name: Pratibha Nair
review: A cute little restaurant with the cutest & oldest servers. One of the servers even played with our son. We ordered the beef Lasagna and grilled chicken. Absolutely loved the Lasagna. Like even if you feel you don’t like Lasagna, this dish will change your opinion. Its that good. It was delicious and worth all the walk to the restaurant.

name: Asan Hu
review: I really LOVE the service here (and ofc the food is awesome), the waiters here are all so friendly, we don’t speak any Italians but they tried their best to talk to us in English and introduced all their personal favorite dishes and combos. When they learn we travelled from France, they even started speaking their limited French with us, we felt extremely welcomed and blessed to eat here, you can feel they aren’t just treating work as work but an enjoyable part of their life! For the food, we tried carbonara, spaghetti with clams, mixed grilled veggies, cuttlefish and grilled sea bass, everything came so good!

name: Krystal
review: The clam pasta was so good! We were recommended to go to this place by a local. When we walked in it was full of thr Gondoliers having lunch which was a very good sign. The service was spectacular. Its tucked in near a bridge so not many tourists

name: Magued Makram
review: The food was great the waiter was really helpful but the place is too hot and the main course was really delayed

name: Darcy Morgan
review: This humble trattoria is warm, friendly and serves excellent food. Traditional Venetian dishes are accompanied by seasonal specialties with local ingredients. Serving staff is attentive and knowledgeable. This would be a 5/5 if the menu prices were more in line with the rustic nature. However, it is Venice. So what isn’t expensive?

name: David Williams
review: We had a wonderful meal here around 19:00 on a Sunday evening. The house specials tuna tartare and tagliolini with spider crab meat were both phenomenal. Would definitely come back the next time we are in Venice!

name: Colin Riddell
review: We stopped here for lunch, sitting at an outside table - our server could not have been more  attentive or helpful with explaining the menu as well as suggesting wine pairings. Our food was served with minimal waiting time and the freshest of sea food accompanied with perfectly cooked pasta followed by a sublime tiramisu. Throughout our server was extremely attentive discreetly checking in with us. Our food and wine were amazing. We would really like to thank the young woman who looked after us and made our meal here so special and a highlight of our trip to Venice. Grazie Mille. 🙏

name: Zack Baynum
review: Food was good but it seems they don’t use salt. The bathrooms were fine for what I’ve seen in Italy. I wouldn’t order the picanha again (not seasoned, tough and no fat or crust) it was a great atmosphere but I’ll make my steak at home from now on

name: Val Abecia (Val)
review:
For 3 days before dinner, I went here for drinks and cicchetti. The experienced servers are amazing at their job. On my last night, I also went for dinner, and the food was very good. The server immediately suggested good wine pairing options. This is the epitome of good service.

If I were to nitpick, I would say that value for money can be improved. But you know, Venice is really expensive. Give yourself a treat!

name: AllAttiCO casa vacanze Matera
review:
We went for a nice lunch and we got it.
The girl has been super nice and she let us try also the famous cicchetti veneziani that you can only taste at the standing table.
The atmosphere is so familiar and the main dishes were really good.

name: Sheryl Benjamin
review: We were seated in the back room which was quieter. Great server - she was so delightful. Food was a bit more upscale. Very good. A bit more pricey though. Still glad they seated 6 of us without a reservation. It was close to our lodging.

name: Arie Rozichner
review: A local guy recommended this place to us to try out Venetian cicchetti (I memorized it as similar to tapas). And it was a great experience! We had several dishes that were available and loved them. Sardines fried and marinated, squid, tuna, salami after cold prosecco made our lunch quite unique. Laura is a true soul of the place, always positive, quick and efficient. The desert cake with espresso was delicious. Highly recommended!

name: Bill Alber
review: This a great local restaurant serving local specialties. If you're staying at the Hyatt Centric or another hotel nearby, don't miss this. The food is creative and fabulous. The staff was friendly, knowledgeable, and just the right amount of attentive. We had a raw seafood platter to start followed by wonderful mushroom and scallop risotto. Slow roasted beef and stuffed sea bass for entrees. The wine they recommended was world class delicious. This was a reasonably priced, world class dining experience.

name: S K
review:
Awesome place, we enjoyed the whole thing (the atmosphere, the food, the friendly staff). The food was just perfect!!
We ordered grilled octopus as an appetizer, then lobster spaghetti, and seafood spaghetti. They tasted amazing!!
The homemade tiramisu was also very delicious.
Definitely we will be back.

name: Patrick Morgan
review:
Could not recommend enough. The food was unreal, I would strongly suggest the seafood platter, or the risotto.

A table right on the canal surrounded by Murano glass, and drinking from it. Couldn't be better.

Service was perfect. As a hospitality manager in the UK I was very impressed, particularly  how they dealt with a rude countrymen of mine who didn't realise closed means closed. They were very courteous and polite despite the described people being a pain.

Would recommend to anyone coming to Murano.

name: Kristin W
review: €5 spritzes (better than most in Murano) served in gorgeous glasses with on a gorgeous sunny/ shaded terrace right over the canal. Save yourself for this spot if you are visiting murano. The glass decor outdoors is stunning, the drinks are served with some peanuts.

name: Raik Henke
review: Not so hidden, but a pure gem! Arrive with ACTV 4.2 at Navagergo, leave the tourists 2 stops before…join this Trattoria, drink 2-5 Bellini to start the day…you won’t forget!

name: John Pit
review: Nice restaurant right on the canal. Good presentation and taste 👌🏻

name: Chris
review:
I highly recommend this restaurant for amazing food and view onto the canal.

We had the shared platter for starters which was varied and super tasty, one of the best starters we had in the Venice area. We had a main each both were delicious, especially the crab pasta with lime air (bubbles). To digest a bit we also had a variety of sorbet scoops and all the flavors were mixes of different fruits and herbs.

The service was super good and everyone really friendly. I highly recommend this restaurant if you’re visiting Murano, especially because it’s right in front of the glass museum! So convenient!

name: Mr F
review: We dined here for our 25th wedding anniversary. Food was fantastic, staff are wonderful and helpful.

name: Karen Eidsvik
review: I asked our guide for a place that had a degustation menu and she recommended here. We came for a 6 course lunch having no idea what to expect. Every bite was a delight. Nothing overpowering . Perfectly balanced salt sweet savoury . All cooked just so. Creative mix like beetroot spaghetti and hearring caviar. Small with potato spaghetti deep fried. Spectacular in every way. A quiet spot next to a canal.

name: Aimee Keiluhn
review: Lovely dinner at al Cantinon. Service was perfect. Everything was brought to us at a perfect pace. The seared scallops were absolutely magnificent. Swordfish melted in my mouth and the pistacchio crust added a wonderful texture. My husband absolutely loved the grilled octopus. It was incredibly tender. We hope to eat here again!

name: Sergio (Serg19)
review:
Perfect spot for a romantic night, by a small canal away from the hustle and bustle. Request an outside table for a perfect evening.
Food matched the location, service could not be faulted.

name: Erin McDonnell
review: Off the beaten path, offering creative takes on traditional foods. If you just want spaghetti marinara this isn’t for you. But if you want marinated salmon crudo artfully presented with flavorful sauces you’ve come to the right place. The pasta Rossi was recommended to us as a chefs specialty, its magenta from beets used in the pasta and served with shrimp crudo. We also loved the octopus which was cooked tender and presented with seared seasonal veggies. Tiramisu also delicious.

name: Marouane
review: The food was delicious—definitely full of flavor and well-prepared. Prices were a bit on the higher side, but overall, the atmosphere made it worth it. A great place to enjoy a special meal!!

name: loz p
review: Incredible food and amazing service, we really had a lovely evening that was one of the best during my month in Venice so definitely recommend, great location and the menu and food was just delicious and well thought through. Thanks for a great night!

name: Cristina Busuioc
review:
What an amazing experience we had at Osteria Al Cantinon. The fish was great, and the tiramisu is something else.
Thank you chef Serghei. We will come back soon!

name: MICHAEL P.
review: Amazing meal on a quieter street with a table directly on one of the canals. Excellent service. Desserts are freshly made. We ate on a Saturday night and I'd recommend making a reservation. I saw multiple people with no reservation get turned away.

name: Sayena Maj
review: What really made the experience memorable was Khorshid, my fellow Iranian, who was so kind and helpful throughout the evening. The drinks are also amazing — the Porn Star cocktail was a standout, and I’d recommend asking the staff for their own suggestions because they really know what to make. Warm atmosphere, great service, and a place I’d happily return to!

name: Michał Zimmer
review: The food is very fresh and everything tasted amazing. The best thing about this place is Jenny and her tiramisu. She has an extremely friendly and caring way about her. We enjoyed our time here very much! We paid about 100 euros for 3 people with mains, dessert and wine. Very reasonable 👌🏻

name: mizunii
review:
when speaking about their service? 10/10. a place for some quick refreshments? avoid it. seating alone was ~€3pp (something about Italy and their tax?) and it means you'd have to pay nearly €10 already just for a singular coffee

in total, 4 drinks, a coffee ice cream (which tasted delicious) and one margherita pizza added up to ~€80

name: Oh My Goshen
review:
Overall a nice restaurant, with a large area outside and a smaller area inside.
When it's cold, the inside has a warm and pleasant vibe.

Although it's called a Trattoria, it's pretty much a normal Touristy restaurant with many dishes and Menu in various language.

Price-wise, it's relatively ok. It's not cheap nor too expensive, but the portion could aren't huge.

We had:

Grilled vegetable - seemed like a smaller portion compared to what I'm used to I'm Rome.
They were good except the eggplant which wasn't grilled enough (we didn't want to complain about it so we just ate it as is 🫣).

Lasagne al ragù - was only OK because it wasn't very rich in flavours like the gnocchi. Also it wasn't very big - although it was a Primi dish, they are sometimes bigger and can serve as a main dish.

Gnocchi al ragù - was very good and with a nice sauce.

Bigoli al salsa (pasta dish)- wasn't very good.

Service:
It was overall good. No complains here 🙂

To conclude:

We came here mainly because we had TheFork voucher to use, but if you are in the area and looking for a place to eat for  a decent price (again, not cheap not expensive) you may consider this place.

The food isn't excellent but it's good enough 🙂

Enjoy!

name: Alicja Sloma
review: Very good experience overall. We shared the grilled squid with vegetables which was great. I had the veal scalloping which was good but the mushrooms tasted marinated instead of fresh. The sauce could have used more seasoning. My friend had the steak fillet with vegetables which was good. The berry panna cotta was also very good but I would have like the berry sauce to be less sugary. The service was great. Our waiter was very attentive and moved quickly.

name: Dayantha Joshua
review:
Nice place to have an early dinner away from the crowds.. especially enjoyable if the sun is out and you are sitting outside in the warmth.  We were there on 27th May 2025 and had the standard 3-course menu offering and wine..

very good quality and good value for money.

name: Dan Skyline
review: Pizza …. Pretty average at least my barometer being margarita. Crust 1/5 too doughy would pass on this place for pizza at least. On basil ? Coffee was good atmosphere nice on plaza. Tourist trap category but would sit here and lounge on water and coffee

name: Sam Saeidi
review:
The meal was absolutely delightful, and the flavors were incredibly well-balanced. It was evident that a great deal of care was put into preparing each dish. The service provided was exceptional, our server kanon went above and beyond and made great suggestions and was supper attentive and friendly throughout the evening. The entire dining experience was truly enjoyable, and I would gladly return for another meal. Overall, the restaurant deserves high praise for its outstanding food and service.

We sampled their baked seabass and vegetables, mushroom risotto, and garlic fuccacia.

The fish was exceptionally flavorful, and the sauce and vegetables complemented the sea bass beautifully.

The risotto was cooked to perfection, and the fuccacia was simply delightful.

name: Sean Bryan
review: Great place! Environment was welcoming and easy going. Far away from the craziness this is a great place for a comforting dinner. The food was all delicious. We also felt the prices were reasonable as well! The staff were fast and made it a nice experience. We had the lasagna and the squid ink pasta (both great)

name: Veronika Komolova
review: A wonderful place with a calm and welcoming atmosphere, excellent wine, and truly authentic Venetian cuisine. We enjoyed it so much that we came back twice to try more traditional dishes. The staff are attentive, professional, and genuinely hospitable, which made the experience even more special. It feels like the kind of restaurant you always want to return to and keep discovering every dish on the menu. We’re so glad we followed our host’s recommendation — the locals really know best!

name: Kristin W
review: We had a lovely dinner in Giudecca at this location. Ordered the sardines (Sarde in saor), which consists of Luke-warm fried sardines marinated with sweet and sour onions, pine nuts, and raisins. Absolutely gorgeous to start with. Then the bavette ai fruiti de mare, at first I was confused because I expect a steak when I order “Bavette” but was pleasantly surprised with a large pasta dish with all manner of shelled sea food in a delicious tomato sauce (a tad over-salted, but still delicioso). Then the desert, probably my highlight. The pistachio crème brûlée is a MUST - not overly sweet, extremely smooth and incredibly tasty. The tiramisu was also gorgeous. All the while you sit right by the water and enjoy the boats going by with the backlighting of Venice. Served by waiters who are very friendly and have great recommendations.

name: Loo Yeo
review:
Trattoria ai Cacciatori greets you warmly, the kind of place that will do its best to find you a table even when full. That welcome sets the tone, but the kitchen’s efforts proved uneven across the two evenings we were there, the first being better..

The house pasta with vegetables and muddica atturata – the Sicilian style toasted breadcrumbs – showed promise but was let down by timid seasoning and a lack of anchovy depth. Handmade and freshly cut, yes, but more middling than memorable. The chicken breast with potato wedges followed the same line: neatly plated, but slightly dry and under-seasoned, an effort that fell short. Even the scallops with pumpkin purée and marinated mushrooms, strong in concept, stumbled on portioning – balance was lost, and the dish simply didn’t come together. The daily catch, a grilled sea bream with vegetables and polenta, ought to have been the safe triumph of simplicity. Instead, it was handled carelessly, proving disappointing.

There were brighter moments. The squid-ink tagliolini with clams, bottarga, and prawn tartare struck the right note, clean and fresh if still wanting more sharpness from lemon and depth from the bottarga (7.5/10). The off-menu fritto misto was the real standout: crisp, well-seasoned, and confidently fried, it captured exactly why such a dish endures (9/10). The scampi risotto, too, was tasty if a little blurred, a clearer stock would have lifted it further (7/10). Grilled octopus with broccoli purée and crispy leeks had potential but lacked smoke and tenderness, while the pistachio semifreddo, chosen with some expectation, closed the meal on a lighter, less satisfying note.

In the end, Ai Cacciatori offers warmth and flashes of skill, but the food too often feels like missed opportunity. With more care and sharper execution, it could match its welcome. As it stands, it’s a house of highs and lows.

name: x d
review: Very good vibes by the sea. Staff was really nice and food was delicious. Prices might be a little bit high but its worth it. They include water in your bill even if you dont drink it so watch out.

name: O Okan
review: This quaint and inviting trattoria is located on the island of Giudecca, right on the waterfront. It's only reachable by boat from San Marco, and just a short walk from the Church of the Holy Redeemer (Chiesa del Santissimo Redentore). The menu features traditional Italian cuisine with a strong focus on seafood. While the food was enjoyable, it was the cozy and authentic atmosphere that truly stood out. The portions were generous and the prices quite reasonable. One example dish was the anchovies on flatbread, which was nearly the size of a full pizza despite being classified as an appetizer. The staff were genuinely warm and welcoming. I’d happily return.

name: Charlene Vergilio
review: Great place for a relaxing meal with delicious food. Scallops appetizer with pumpkin was amazing. Really nice night out.

name: D L
review: Tasty food and amazing seaside. Helpful staff and boss.🤵🏻‍♂️

name: Laura Herrington
review: Visiting Venice for the first time. My dream was organically stumbling across a cozy restaurant and be served a four course meal selected by staff and chef to experience a true taste of Venetian cuisine, I love food so I said “surprise me.” This restaurant delivers just that! Every bite was as delicious as the next and I never would have picked these items from the menu myself. Have fun, and ask your server and chef what they think you should have! We truly enjoyed every moment and bite! Grazie Trattoria Storica! Ciao!

name: Tam Henry Le Nguyen
review:
TL;DR - That one spot we kept wanting to go back to. If you don’t go for yourself, go for us.

Picture this - you’ve had a bit too many drinks the night before, taken a 1 hour taxi, 2.5 hour train, a 30 minute ferry, and 15 minutes of dragging your luggage over the bridges and through the alleys. You’re tired and ready for bed, but your stomach disagrees. Your Airbnb host mentioned that there was a “typical Venetian spot” a few steps away. You muster the last of your strength to make your way over, telling the owners, Stefano and Toni, that Luigi recommended it. What followed might’ve been a fever dream, but we remember it as one of the best meals of the trip.

The pesto linguine (linguine con il pesto al Genovese) and spaghetti alle Vongole were some of the best I’ve ever had. The clams were fresh, the pasta al dente, the sauce light and authentic. We even got a refill of bread to mop it all up! Everything was simple, but made with care. The prices were very fair, especially for Venice. We knew that we had to make our way back at some point in the future. Eat there for us in the meantime.

name: Monica Talma
review: I don't normally give a bad review. But I have never been so disappointed. I should've known when they handed me a flat glass of Prosecco and I said something nicely and all the guy did was top it up a little bit with the same flat Prosecco. The scallops, nothing the right home to mom about. I don't recommend.  The baked Bronzino was OK. Bones and all.  But the worst was the carpaccio.   This is one of the special things I enjoy eating all over the world, including many cruises I've been on and the fact that I've been in the restaurant business pretty much my whole life.  Never have I had carpaccio like this. Just zoom in on that picture , it's like they wanted me to make a roast beef sandwich. It was not delicate at all. It's like he pinched off pieces of cheese and just threw it on the plate and then drizzle some olive oil over it.  It was not carpaccio grade beef by any means. I do not recommend this place. I'm very saddened by having to write this review. I hope I never have to do this again.

name: Darren Clarke
review: Unbelievable fish dishes. The wine was fantastic and the tiramisu was out of this world. The service matched the food. I would highly recommend. Best restaurant in Venice

name: Joseph Yasmeh
review:
I stopped by here for dinner purely because it smelled so good outside.

Bolognese was fantastic. Surprisingly came with fries, and I was in carb heaven.

Read a review saying tiramisu was best they ever had. I concur.

House red wine complemented nicely and everything was reasonably priced for such a great meal.

The kind of place I came to Italy for. Would return.

name: Yeongbae K
review:
I arrived round 13:30 pm and waited for a table in the back of the restaurant.

I ordered spaghetti alla carbonara and insalata mista. The carbonara was good; the salad was fresh and tasty.

name: WenHua 文華 Chao
review:
Super excellent Italian traditional cuisine!
Excellent baked bread, simple cooked spaghetti, excellent cooked seafood risotto, all dishes are delicacy, Italian red wine tasted super good!
Waiters are very skillful and friendly so everyone enjoyed the meal very pleasantly.

name: MF
review: Simple, homely and fresh pasta (pesto was so good) and so so delicious aperol spiritz ✨

name: Larry Landaeta
review: This is a restaurant where my wife & I ate dinner. I had tomato salad, penne all' arrabiatta for my first course, and grilled sea bass for my main course. The tomatoes were very fresh and lovely with balsamic vinegar and olive oil. The spicy penne was perfectly spiced, and the sea bass meat came off the bones easily. It was served with a balsamic reduction and lemon wedge which was perfect with the fish. My wife only had a dinner salad which she liked very much. I highly recommend a visit.

name: Rusudan Badriashvili
review:
Looking at the reviews and ratings, it seems like most guests don’t actually come from TripAdvisor. They have a very active person outside inviting people in, and of course, it’s in a central location.
All in all, during our travels across Italy, we probably haven’t been this disappointed anywhere else. Once you’re inside, the service becomes noticeably less attentive. Unfortunately, the toilet was in very poor condition, and the menu was a bit unclear. They serve fish by weight, but you don’t know what size you’ll actually get. I repeatedly asked the waiter to tell me the price beforehand, but he acted as if he didn’t understand. In the end, the bill was very expensive. The fish & chips were tasteless, and the sea bass was nothing special either.
To top it off, at the end a waitress asked me to scan a QR code to rate the restaurant. I scanned it, and she immediately started selecting five stars herself. Before she could post it, I quickly took back my phone and told her I’d do it on my own. Later I noticed that it wasn’t even a review for the restaurant but for a hotel.
So if anyone is reading this – there are many better options around, and this place is really not worth spending your money on.

name: Diane Moroney
review:
What a wonderful place to eat! Friendly, great service. They have been here 59 years/2 generations! The pasta and seafood are fresh daily.

I had sensational seafood, but people from my tour were impressed by the fresh, beautiful, delicious pasta.

I Highly recommend!

name: Sue Andrews
review: We stumbled upon this restaurant and were invited in.  A table of 6 of us were accommodated.  The wait staff, Antonio and George were amazing.  The food is reasonably priced for the location and was spectacular!  When George found out we were celebrating an anniversary, he sent complimentary cake and Limoncello to our table.  I can’t say enough about the wonderful service and welcoming feel.  We will be back should we be in Venice again!

name: Albert Gonzalez
review:
We left this restaurant without finishing our meal because of roaches.

We found the staff helpful and the food tasted delicious and was well presented but a mommy roach and babies soon became interested in the pasta.  We were promptly moved to another table, the staff were swift to cleanup the table and area it was in and they offered us new dishes.  There was no going back to eating for one of the guests while they offered and brought her a new different dish.  She couldn't look at it.  Moments after my wife spotted yet another roach scurrying in the table cloth and they both bolted leaving by me to handle the bill...which came out to 150€!  I just wanted to get out of there...

name: Charmaine Selby
review:
We had just gotten off the train and were starving, so we stumbled upon Trattoria Bella Venezia—and what a find! The staff was incredibly welcoming, and our waiter made amazing recommendations. He suggested the fish, and it was absolutely the best we've had in a long time. We also tried the spaghetti and meatballs and the risotto—everything was cooked to perfection.

When we asked for a great wine to go with our meal, he didn’t disappoint. The pairing was spot-on and elevated the whole experience. To top it all off, they surprised us with a complimentary tiramisu for dessert—so thoughtful and delicious!

Highly recommended if you're in the area. Great food, great service, and a warm, authentic atmosphere.

name: Roula Zeidan
review:
The reception is very warm and the food is tasty.  The staff is very devoted and you create a kind of bond with them. They d still remember you if you come again. I tried it last year and insisted on coming again this year (22 Aug 25).
Special thanks to wonderful Emile for his attentively care and assistance and smiles. If he s not around i d rather come later :)

name: Basent Baioumy
review:
Amazing food and service! Definitely recommending this place in Venice.
My family ordered grilled salmon with grilled vegetables and spaghetti with seafood and gnocchi Alfredo. Everything was tasty, bathrooms are clean and the service is spectacular!!
You have to eat at this restaurant when you come to Venice, Italy 👍

name: Valarie Causey
review: We ate here for our last night in Venice and it did not disappoint! The service was above and beyond. Everyone was friendly, attentive to our needs and recommended delicious drinks and dinner options. We didn't sit outside because it was very hot that evening but it does have a beautiful view. The food and drinks were delicious and made from scratch. Everything was fresh. I highly recommend trying this place for lunch or dinner.

name: Rachel Swift
review:
We spoke to one of the hosts outside the restaurant and they explained their menu and how we could sit indoor or out on the terrace.

We chose to sit outside on the terrace, it was beautiful- the breeze in the heat was lovely, could watch the gondolas go by listening to all the music and the chatter, just taking it all in.

We ordered lobster 🦞
Chicken and peppers w/salad
Margarita pizza
Ham pizza
Fries
Drinks

The food was delicious 😋

The staff were friendly too 😊

name: Sanad Atamna
review: Had a nice meal at Trattoria Povoledo — the atmosphere is great, with a lovely view and a welcoming vibe. The staff were friendly and attentive, which made the experience really pleasant. The food was decent — not the very best I’ve had in Venice, but still enjoyable and nicely presented. Overall, a good spot if you’re looking for a relaxed place with great service and a classic canal setting.

name: Serena M
review:
Our experience at Trattoria Povoledo in Venice was deeply disappointing and, frankly, felt discriminatory. From the moment we sat down, the service was unprofessional and dismissive. After ordering our food, our server completely disappeared and ignored our table. We noticed that the table next to us, who arrived after us, was immediately provided with a bread basket, plates, olive oil, and attentive service — while we received nothing.

When our food finally arrived, our server was still nowhere to be found. We tried multiple times to order additional items, looking around desperately to get anyone’s attention. Eventually, another server came over, and we explained that we wanted to order more. Another server appeared only briefly, with a noticeable attitude and a dismissive tone, asking “What do you want?” as if we were an inconvenience.

The way we were treated felt unfair, hostile, and racially biased. It was not just poor service — it felt like we were being singled out and disrespected because of who we are. Dining should be a welcoming experience, but this restaurant made us feel unwelcome, ignored, and demeaned. I cannot recommend this place, and I hope others are aware of the way certain patrons may be treated here.

name: Kasia Prus
review: Ravioli still lingers in my mind. The bruschetta is easily among the best I’ve had, with a balance of freshness and flavor that elevates the simple starter to something special. To top it all off, sitting at a table on the terrace by the water makes for one of the most charming dining experiences in the city. A combination of outstanding food and an unbeatable setting - truly five stars.

name: Tommy Tran
review: Fantastic location next to the river. Relaxing and calm environment, enjoyed just watching the world go by. Food was reasonable. Prices fair - about €10-20 for a pizza and pasta.

name: Lia Padilla
review:
Every time I visit Venice I Dine at Trattoria Povoledo. My first time was in December 2015, I got to know the owner and the chef (since it was the off season), we watched Milan win a Futbol match together. Sadly I never took photos during that visit. Fast-forward 3 years later I visited again in August of 2018 and both the owner and chef remembered who I was! I couldn't wait to go back this year, but sadly it's under new management, same family, just different folks running it now.
I always order the same thing and I have never been disappointed and will continue coming back every time I return to Venezia!

name: Ilze Z.
review:
The best Pizza Margarita in Venice, and price was very democratic! You can order one Pizza for 2 kids, it will be enough.
Other food is more expensive, but it is common in Venice.
Very friendly stuff, it was pleasant to take conversation.
They have wonderful terrace, and in sunny day it is so nice to enjoy the view to Grand Canal.
Ciao, and we hope to come back some day! 🤗

name: Jesse J
review:
Found this place completely by luck, so glad we decided to wander off of the main areas.

Absolutely the best Carbonara I have ever had, made with guanciale not bacon. It had me savouring every bite. My wife had the Cannolonni with Spinach, it was also divine.

The staff was very polite and charming. It's a great little spot!

Highly, highly recommend! If we had more time we would definitely stop in again.

name: Charlie Rau
review: Good food all around! Our server was quick to push a large amount of starters for the table that were far more than we needed, but garlic bread and cheeses were delicious. The cuttlefish ink pasta is a Venice specialty, and it was fantastic here. Fried calamari was a bit tough. Service did take quite a bit.

name: Isaac Montanya
review: This was our last meal in Venice, and it was superb, we will definitely go back upon our return to Venice. The complimentary glass of limoncello was wonderful. Recommend.

name: tuvvi d
review: The staff are friendly. It is an authentic restaurant serving the local cuisine. I had Grilled vegetables, vegetable soup, penne Arrabbiata. Everything was fresh and tasty. Tiramisu was tasty as well. I would recommend them.

name: Ieva Vasiliauskaite
review: Worst pizza in Italy... I mean if I want crappy pizza I would order one at my home country...  Guess it's true when they say if the menu is small then it's good quality (menu here was  quite big, so quality suffers)... And service wasn't the best either... The waiter made us feel bad because we didn't order water... really???  Don't even get me started on Aperol Spritz  .... With lemon??? for real???

name: Larissa Gal
review:
This restaurant has been here for 53 years and owned by a very stylish and lively Nona. The food is VERY authentic Italian with several Venetian specialties. I wanted to eat gnocchi but I didn't like the plain sauce on the menu and they offered to make it with another sauce and recommended the seafood marinara which was fantastic. Great food, great service, shaded indoor amd outdoor sitting in a quieter side street very close to the busy areas. Has fans and air conditioning.

You almost miss it if you don't look walking towards a little bridge. I found it on Google maps looking 4+ star restaurants in Venice near where I was. Highly recommend.

name: Kirk Colledge
review: We decided to stop by for some drinks and an appetizer while waiting for an appointment. There were five of us and we all ordered spritzers or beer. We also had some brescetta appetizers. Since we only had thirty minutes we had no time for a full meal. The staff was very rude and seemed upset we weren't ordering more food even though they had many open tables. When the bill came they charged a cover charge for three people, even though they did not mention that when we were ordering.  The food and drinks were adequate. Wevhad great experiences at other restaurants close by. Would not recommend.

name: Jim Tompkins
review: Stopped for a quick bite midday. Our food was delicious and service attentive.  We saw some pasta meals delivered around us and they looked spectacular.

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Sam T
review: This restaurant was recommended by our hotel and we booked about 6-8 weeks ahead for dinner for our first night in Venice. It was by far the best restaurant we ate at during our 4 days in Venice. They offer outdoor seating but I preferred the warmth and atmosphere of the indoor area. If you do eat outside, be sure to check out the wall of bottle openers! The food was incredible from start to finish. The wine was phenomenal as was the presentation when we were served our chosen bottle of red wine for the initial tasting. Ended with grappa to aid our digestion which hit the spot! It is right off the main area tucked away so it is not as busy with tourists. If you are looking for traditional Italian cuisine in a quieter area with great service, you will not be disappointed.

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Andrea Santos
review:
honestly the food is 5 stars and one of the best meals of the trip. however, it was pretty pricy and got bit by mosquitoes after the sun set hence the one star deduction.

eggplant dip: top tier
tuna cracker: also delicious
gnocchi: must try! so flavorful and perfectly cooked

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Alina Rodriguez
review: Came here with a group of friends on vacation (6 total) and had an amazing, memorable meal. Our waiter Alvise was kind and so fun to talk to, making our experience in the beautiful garden space even better. We loved everything we ate including the tartares, red sauce pasta, lamb chops and gnocchi. The chef is Sicilian and has created wonderful dishes in addition to the Venetian cuisine. Truly the best sorbet (rosemary and strawberry) and cannolis of our lives! Come here and you’ll be taken care of.

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Shara Guarnaccia
review: Excellent food, beautifully plated! Kind and knowledgeable staff. Great wine selection  and the interior garden is beautiful and peaceful

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Annie and Sean Silk
review: This is a place my wife and I went to in 2023 for our honeymoon and have thought about coming back to constantly. I took a picture of our food here, forgot to save the places name, and therefore spent over an hour scouring images of restaurants just to find this place and come back! And I was successful! The service is so friendly, and it truly feels like a place where you only see the locals go, and they know what’s up. They seem to change the menu frequently, but if you get lucky enough to have the octopus as a started, then go buy a lotto ticket. The gnocchi were literally the softest we have ever had in our two trips through Italy. I don’t mean like soft and gummy like- I mean soft like you are cutting into ?pillows? Kind of texture??? The sauces are perfectly seasoned, and the portion for the prices seem really fair for the quality and freshness of the ingredients

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Robert Reinius
review:
Excellent price to quality ratio. Simply great quality local specialities, with creative modifications. Very comprehensive wine list.
When you're looking for solid good food in less casual than Bistrot de Venise environment.
Really friendly service.

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Suzanne Lichter
review: Beautiful presentation of the food. Quiet street off the beaten path. Homemade everything. We had the potato gnocchi with scallops and shrimp and the spaghetti with tomato and basil. Appetizer of grilled octopus with potato and honey onions was fabulous. Get a street table for lunch as the interior garden is too sunny. If I am ever back in Venice I would come in the evening and ask to sit there. Cost is worth every penny. We did not add drinks to our estimate.

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Roger Hawes
review:
Was recommended by hotel but it was a terribly experience, particularly compared with other local Trattoria we’d been to. Host was not welcoming (which we put down to poor English skills which seemed strange based on other places) and the mains were terrible. We had lamb and a pork main off the standard menu. When they arrived they were the wrong way round and we said that, but they could distinguish between the two ! We did based on taste. The mains were no better than canteen food and no way worth the 25 euros for lamb and 18 for the pork.

It was a very disappointing evening. I’m sure if we were Italian it would have been much better.

name: Kacper Świerad
review: The food at Trattoria La Battigia was absolutely phenomenal. This is the kind of place that should be written about in local newspapers, as I truly believe it is by far the most worthwhile restaurant to visit in the entire region. Special attention must be given to the gnocchi in porcini mushroom sauce with scampi. This dish takes you on a culinary journey, culminating in a catharsis for your taste buds. Once is simply not enough – one day, I will return here solely for this restaurant and this extraordinary dish. I wholeheartedly recommend it to everybody.

name: Catalina Dascalu
review: We had a fantastic dinner at Trattoria La Battigia! The gnocchi con porcini e gamberi was full of flavours — soft, homemade gnocchi with mushrooms and perfectly cooked shrimps. The Tartufo nero was a real highlight — rich and aromatic without being too much. Friendly staff and a relaxed, local feel. Would definitely come back!

name: Veronika V
review:
Excellent food we had the house plate spagetti with lobster - AMAZING!
recommend to try looked like locals did too

name: RUBY
review:
REALLY RUDE AND TOURIST UNWELCOME!

I’m not sure if the lady owner or waitress was being racist, but from the moment we walked in, she completely ignored us. We stood in the middle of the restaurant for quite a while — even the other diners noticed and tried to help us. When we finally got a table and were ready to order, she still refused to acknowledge us and told us to wait.

What made it worse was that she was very warm and friendly toward the local customers, smiling and serving them promptly. But every time we tried to order, she looked annoyed and told us to wait again. We came to the restaurant to enjoy a meal, not to be treated poorly. Her behavior was extremely rude and disrespectful!

name: Jonathan Darling
review: Amazing meal. The 'house special' was absolutely incredible. The staff were lovely and was an all round memorable meal.

name: Deedee Henderson
review: Awesome food and desserts! The gnocchi with ragu meat sauce was the best I've ever had! The lemon ice cream with Prosecco dessert drink is a must to try!

name: Mira Kovács
review:
Google maps says they open at 18:30. We showed up exactly at that time. They wouldn’t open for 10 more minutes so we went in and said hi. A woman told us we have to wait until 7pm. So we waited patiently in front of the door. A bit after 7pm, when they still weren’t open, a woman came up to the door and locked it in front of our eyes.
Either update your google maps or don’t give the wrong info to people. Very disrespectful

name: C Shek
