review: Food are amazing! From the frittura di pesce, mussels spaghetti to the tiramisu, we love everything. The service is really quick and staff are nice. Atmosphere is good, relaxing and we really enjoy our time there.

name: Naomi S
review: The seafood pasta (no.24) I had was the best one I've ever had! I took it down instantly as you can see from the pics haha. Don't hesitate to come visit this place. Also Deya, our server was amazingly kind and fun 😁

name: Toni Pascua
review:
Great food (fresh seafood) and 5 stars service (Franco)!
Had dinner at Trattoria Pizzeria da Gioia and it was fantastic! We went with the fresh seafood platter, recommended by Mr. Franco—the heart and soul of the place. He and his team made the whole evening fun and welcoming. Great food, friendly service, and a lively atmosphere. Our first time here, but definitely not our last. Highly recommend!

Worth the visit.

name: Darrian Chee
review:
Beware, of hidden charges, additional costs and no clear communication on pricing.

Fish charged to us was 2.2kg… which of course was our fault for not checking at the beginning but scummy behavior for a tourist restaurant.

Meat platter was served with overcooked beef and decent lamb - 2 lamb chops that served 4 of us (this was 80-90 euros btw)

Paid 440 euros for 4 pax in total for average quality food.

name: Denise Gilliland
review: Max was exceptional!! He was a phenomenal server, very professional, suggested best dishes on the menu and was very entertaining! Food was delicious! Highly recommended the sea bass and pasta with spider crab. Be sure to end your meal with the Limoncello!

name: Bri
review: American here, so amazing! the music and atmosphere of the whole place is beautiful. the food is so great and the service was incredible. our server fahim was so sweet and gave us many tips on exploring venice! honestly is my favorite restaurant i’ve visited while being here. would 100% recommend. if you’re looking for somewhere with meat as well this is the place to go, one of the only places we could find with meat and it gave us all the protein we needed!

name: Paul Ridpath
review:
What can we say.  My wife and myself have spent 5 nights in Venice.  The first night, after a delayed train meant we ate really late and we chose Trattoria Pizzeria Da Gioia.  Thank God we did!  We were sat at our table and then from this point were looked after by Max (photo).  Legend!  Funny, smart, considerate, warm and generally, one of the nicest human beings you could wish to meet, who makes sure you feel welcome, appreciated, guided and satisfied.  Whilst speaking about Max in those terms, you can also include the food and of course the other members of staff.
On two nights we had simple fare of pizza, pasta and salads, and despite how busy it was, there was little to no wait and yet we felt unhurried.  On the third night we ate there, which was also our last night in Venice, we enjoyed the Fillet steak with Barolo sauce (paired with a 2017 half bottle of Barolo, perfect balance, and also the sole (Griglia, with a recommended bottle of Soave).  Immense! Perfect! Just a fabulous meal, full stop.  Compliments to the chef and staff for the atmosphere, but to Max our huge thanks, for remembering us, for making us laugh and for making us feel at home in another country, enjoying the best food and company. Thank you!  Next year we return with my mother in-law!

name: Letty Soriano
review: First class service! I love how Franco & Deya very attentive, gave us their best recommendation and it was superb, all the foods we ordered were delicious. We’ll come back & not hesitate to recommend.

name: Colin Walker
review:
At the beginning of June a group of 4 of us stumbled across this restaurant which was less than 10 minute walk from our hotel, on a highly competitive passageway of inviting restaurants. The food here turned out to be very tasty, and I would have liked to revisit if we were in Venice for longer. Two of us ordered T-bone steak mains - 1kg steaks were presented to us before cooking. I've never seen anything like them before and I guess our eyes were bigger than our bellies when we went ahead with the order.   What we hadn't realised was that for steaks you pay for the weight: €110 per 1.1kg! We live and learn, but at the same time the food was high quality. As I like my steak well done though it's nigh impossible to achieve that throughout such a thick steak. So it was unfortunate to leave a lot of the steak uneaten because of the rareness.
Other dishes were fine: mussels in a wonderful tomato sauce, prawn salad , lasagne, roasted veg.

name: Shikun Shen
review: One of the best food experience I had in my 3 days’ stay in Venice! The food was very delicious and the price was very reasonable. The service was also top notch. The atmosphere was comfortable (with A/C on) and quiet.

name: Mina Pham
review: I loved this restaurant! Spacious, has A/C, especially with the heat wave, and very hospitable and attentive. We are a family of 4, so we did not want to order too much, and shared our dishes. The portions were plenty of for us. We ordered two prawn salads, spaghetti with clams and squid ink spaghetti. So so good!

name: Ma Ann
review: Very good food. Service is excellent. Staffs are so friendly. Will definitely recommend!

name: Rafael
review:
A gem closed to such San Marco! And they even open before 7:30pm! What a nice feeling for early eaters, and it’s not a tourist trap at all!

Nice fried seafood with even fennel bulb underneath! Taste on point and decent portion! Paired with prosecco suggested by the professional and welcoming waitstaff team. So good!

Had pistachio semi-freddo for dessert, not really pistachio-ish but it’s tasty. However I will order tiramisu or panna cotta next time.

My best dining experience in Italy so far, who can imagine such a gem in a tourist destination? Such a blessing!

name: Justin H
review: The best place we found in Venice. Service, food quality, and quiet. Will be back on my next trip.

name: Luis Borjas
review: Excellent food with exceptional service. One of the best carbonara pasta in Italy so far.

name: Enjoy Life
review: Foods are good. Black ink pasta is a bit salty however the mixed shellfish pasta is excellent. Great service.

name: Javier López Franco
review: The pasta was very good and the waiters were very nice. Moreover the shrimp was cooked to perfection and the guanciale was golden crispy. The only reason I am not giving it 5 stars is because we wanted potatoes as a side for our lamb chops, and we received frozen fries. If I am paying 9€ for a side of potatoes, I expect them to be of a higher quality than McDonald’s fries, and this was not the case. I will return next time I come to Venezia and hopefully this issue has been fixed so I can give it 5 stars.

name: Noranice
review:
It was my first time in Venice and a local friend took me here. I don’t eat that much, so we shared a seafood platter and also tried two pastas: tomato crab spaghetti and spaghetti with clams. Honestly, everything tasted amazing!! The seafood was super fresh, full of flavor, and probably the best thing I’ve had on this trip.

The tiramisu was another highlight, the cream was clearly hand-whipped, rich but not heavy. If I hadn’t been so full, I would have ordered a second one.

Even the spritz was just right, not too sweet, I really appreciated since I try to avoid too much sugar. Overall, a really satisfying meal. I’d love to come back next time I’m in Venice to try more dishes.

name: William Guthrie
review: We were only in Venice for two nights and ate at this restaurant 3 times. The food was that amazing. Our last night, we had the T-bone steak and fish and were blown away by our meal. The food was phenomenal, the service was outstanding and the staff was so welcoming. Leo and Halim were wonderful! Once you eat here, it will be hard to try anywhere else.

name: MARLENE LOPEZ
review:
Perhaps is the best seafood fritti I had in many years! Fresh, crunchy y full of flavor.
It’s located in Campo S.Margarita to have one of the best night life in Venice.
The restaurant open until late (11pm) do high recommend for a good dinner.

name: Jenny Roche
review: Had a great experience here! The staff were super friendly and attentive, which made the visit even more enjoyable. The food was absolutely delicious — I ordered the pasta, and wow, it was the spiciest I’ve ever had! But don’t get me wrong, it was incredibly flavorful and I loved every bite. Definitely coming back!

name: Aditya Tanwar
review:
Staff member "Mayank" was very helpful and knowledgeable about the menu, greatly appreciated his work.

Had our dinner at this restaurant and everything was absolutely delicious! The food was full of flavour and perfectly cooked. We ended up getting a pasta for takeaway for later munchies — and it was just as amazing! We had 2 drinks, 2 pasta dishes and Tiramisu. Money well spent!

name: Adelina Lupu
review: A true gem in a quiet but lively square in Venice. We ordered a starter a main course and a desert and each one was so good that we will be surely coming back tomorrow! A friend recommended this place to me and I will surely recommend it further to anyone who wants to have a true culinary experience in Venice!

name: JCV Walks
review: We stopped here on our first evening in Venice for dinner. My wife and I both had pasta. Both dishes were delicious, the price was reasonable and the portions were generous. The waiter was friendly and helpful. The outside seating on the square was pleasant. There was a lot of lively activity nearby. We were happy with our choice.

name: Philip Z.
review: Amazing typical Venetian restaurant. Showed them a picture of a dish from before but turned out they didn’t have it on the menu anymore. But they still went out of their way to make it for me which totally made my night. Nice owner too.

name: Kristi Simone
review: We wandered down this street and were so glad we found this amazing little restaurant. It is one of the oldest in Venice. We met the owner. And our waiter was so incredible. The restaurant itself is so incredibly charming wood brick just really old and warm. Our food was absolutely fantastic. We had spaghetti with clams and linguine with fresh crab. We had the scallop appetizer. Our waiter is the person who actually made the homemade tiramisu. He says he makes it every morning fresh. And it tasted fantastic. Definitely highly highly highly recommend.

name: Caelin Laron
review: Ristorante Da Carletto has been my favorite restaurant so far during my trip to Italy. The food was delicious, and the restaurant felt very warm and welcoming. I also really adored the staff. I didn’t catch the name of the server that helped us before his shift ended, but we were also attended to by a waiter named Arsany. Arsany was very kind as well as funny, and we loved having him as our waiter and getting to talk to him as well. Definitely a highlight of our trip, and we will remember Ristorante Da Carletto well! Thank you for taking great care of my mother and I :)

name: rhan x
review: So amazing! Everything is very good, especially the fish, which is really tender, and the wine is also very good. I really like it. It's the best restaurant I've ever had in Venice.

name: Suzy
review: This restaurant has been my favorite one in Venice! Amazing seafood pasta especially frutti di mare! Sea bass was great and all the foods we ordered were just incredible. After the food, make sure you order one tiramisu! 😉

name: Jane Peshkova
review: Amazing find near St. Mark’s Square! The scallops appetizer was incredible, the spinach & cheese ravioli and beef fillet Rossini were outstanding, and the sea bass was perfect. Even the tiramisu (and we don’t usually like desserts!) just melted in the mouth. Their homemade limoncello was the perfect finish. Highly recommend! 🤩🤤🤤🤤

name: Simon Shu
review: An exceptional dining experience! The dishes were perfectly prepared with generous portions, and the wine complemented everything beautifully. Truly satisfying.

name: Katelyn Smith
review: The best food and service I’ve received during my trip across Italy. We had the smoked goose breast, traglioni with scampi, calamari and tiramisu. The waiter said it was the “best tiramisu in Italy” and he was right. Service made you feel like royalty, and the location in a 1500s palace with original architecture was romantic and intimate. Cannot recommend enough.

name: Matthew Toepfer
review: Our last night in Venice was amazing! Our dinner and service were some of the best in all of Europe. The food was full of flavor, and our server was very welcoming. This little family owned restaurant is a true gem. Thank you

name: Tayla Cvitanich
review:
We loved the €3.50 aperol deal and great food so had to come back after our first visit. Excellence service. Great atmosphere.

Carbonara was very tasty, pesto gnocchi was incredible (with a bit of extra salt added) and my partner loved his lasagna and pizza. Overall a really solid option and pretty affordable for Venice.

name: Jen Ferris
review: This is one of the best meals I have had so far in Italy. The lasagna was absolutely delicious, the grilled vegetables were good and the tartufo Classico dessert was delicious. Fastest service I’ve received as well, and a lovely outdoor area.

name: Hongyang Pan
review: The food was very good, staff very friendly. A great experience for our trip in Venice!

name: Savannah Laporte
review: This was the first place we ate in Venice and it was amazing. The food was great and Paulo and Ahmad were excellent servers. They were super friendly and attentive. We came back the next day to get drinks and see them again. 10/10 would recommend.

name: Zhenhui
review: This is my first time in Venice. I actually had difficulties in choosing a restaurant because I am alone and the weather is not good. But the waiter is very warmhearted and arrange me a nice table besides the window. Also The seafood spaghetti and tiramisu are amazing! I will definitely come again in future❤️

name: Lou Francavilla
review: Amazing hospitality and equally amazing food.  My wife and I visited Venice in the summer of 2022 and while walking around we stumbled upon this cozy restaurant.  When we walked in we were greeted with a genuine welcome and immediately we felt welcomed.  The beer was super cold and refreshing (especially on a hot day).  Dinner was amazing….the mussels were the best we ever had.  They were so good, we went back the next night for more.  We also had pasta, king fish, chicken cutlet and don’t forget to order the seasonal greens…..delicious!  Can’t wait to go back in 2023!

name: Alan Tan
review:
My wife and I stumbled upon this cozy looking restaurant 9 years ago during our first visit to Venice.
The delicious food and fantastic service left such a mark in our memories that we made it our first stop for lunch after coming back to visit Venice 9 years later.
Despite the change in restaurant staff, the food and service remains unchanged, still as great as we remembered it, especially the lasagne and fried seafood platter. 👍🏻
We will definitely visit again the next time we are in Venice!

name: Robert Carmichael
review: Venetian oysters were good but a bit pricey at 5 euros each. Had spaghetti with seafood mix. Very Good. Service was average. Had to call down to remind them of drink order with meal they forgot to bring.

name: Maria Mamouzelou
review: Dinner at an unforgettable restaurant at Lido island!!We have visited Lido a couple of times but it was the first time we had dinner at this traditional restaurant, mid Oct. After reading the reviews I tried ”hard” to get them on the phone since there isn’t  any reservation form but finally I reserved our table!!! Everything was perfect. Warm welcome (and goodbyes), friendly  waiters, reasonable prices, beautiful wall decoration, colourful venetian glasses all round us but most of all quality of all the tasty seafood dishes we order, exceptional! Grappa for the end is on them!!! Thank you for a lovely evening.

name: Herbert Lust
review: I love this restaurant. The chef is a great guy and his wife is an excellent hostess. The servers were prompt and joyful. Incredible meal.

name: King Miu
review: We were in Venice for two days.  Day 2, decided to do a ferry trip round the islands.   As we felt hungry, googled some nice seafood place and stumbled upon this Italian fish and seafood restaurant.  Did not have a booking but somehow, we were accommodated to a table for 2.   Pleasantly surprised by the clams pasta, which I will definitely recommend!   Loved the ambient atmosphere and the Murano glassware that were used to serve drinks!

name: David Molin
review:
Lovely pasta dinner and great service

We got a quick and friendly treatment by the daughter of the owner. Inside it's light and quite big but with that also somewhat noisy. We choose a table outside. We where there early, around eight and the restaurant was almost full when we left. We ordered three different pasta dishes and all came out same time. Very well made and lovely taste. The cheesecake for dessert was to dry and sweet for our taste. Got some nice grappa as an bonus.
Atmosphere: 4+
Service: 5
Food: 4+
Worth the money: 4+
Overall experience: 5

name: Adam McCluskey
review: Tasty Venetian food with a fun interior. My kids were able to get some delicious gnocchi al ragu and one with a pomodoro sauce. I got a delicious seafood salad to begin, followed by Seppie al Nero which was amazing. My wife enjoyed a grilled swordfish with polenta. We ordered a crisp bottle of a Venetian Pinot Grigio which was refreshing on a summer night. The staff was extremely friendly and helpful.

name: Monica Negrea
review:
Delicious food, very nice personnel and great atmosphere!
P.S. Need reservation

name: omar zela
review: This beautiful family restaurant, adorned with exquisite art and Murano glassware, offers a delightful atmosphere. However, the highlight for me is undoubtedly the exceptional food, which makes every visit truly memorable. The risotto although none “instagrameable” was so delicious and cooked to perfection.

name: Excel Coaching
review: We recently stayed in Venice for 5 nights, and decided to take a ferry to Lido on a spur of moment to get away from crowds and enjoy the beaches. Upon googling for a nice restaurant on the island, ended up at Trattoria Andri without a booking. The staff were very accomodating, was able to arrange a table. The food was fantastic, particularly the fish selection. We enjoyed it so much that we ended up going there for 3 nights during our visit! Can’t recommend this gem of a restaurant high enough!

name: Laura Pariso
review: A local friend recommended this spot to me. It’s tucked away behind a fish market, so it can be a little tricky to find, but once I stepped inside, I instantly loved the atmosphere. I ordered the raw bar platter, it was fresh and beautifully presented, and the squid ink pasta was absolutely delicious.

name: Bosun Alexandra
review: Awesome place and atmosphere! The service impecable, very friendly and the food delicious. They prepared the Tiramisu in front of us and it was very good. A bit pricey, but it is totally worth to try at least once when you visit Venice.

name: Nisha N
review: This place was amazing!!! The service, the atmosphere, and the food were all excellent. I’d recommend trying to venetian appetizers! Every servers were extremely nice and treated you like royalties. They even had a welcome drink and complimentary dish to try. Don’t miss this place when you’re in Venice!!

name: suleewan sueram
review: There is one staff, the food is delicious, and they also have a welcome drink service. They are very kind. The atmosphere in the restaurant is very good, but the entrance is a bit difficult to find because if you are not a local, you probably won't know this restaurant because you have to pass the fish market.

name: Maria Norton
review: The food was not great - expected fresh Italian seafood and what we got was overpriced chewy lobster. Sat to a really loud table and were moved when complained about it. If you’re thinking about coming here to taste Italian food? I recommend you pick elsewhere! Even after leaving most of our meal, we were still charged full price. At €150, I’d expect much better!

name: Emma Moulton
review: We had a lovely lunch at this historical restaurant, Poste Vecie, Davide and his team were very friendly, attentive and helpful. Davide was very kind to give us a brief history on the property and the noteworthy pieces. The food was delectable. Highly recommend!

name: Sean
review: This is a trattoria where you can enjoy Venetian cuisine. It's very delicious. A surprising for Japanese tastes is that they serve dishes like Nanbanzuke (apparently called Saora in Venice) and taro. This is a unique taste from a different angle from popular Italian. The squid ink pasta is also delicious, with the flavor of the ingredients soaked into it. If you include this restaurant in your trip around Italy, you can enjoy new food tastes.

name: Mariana Lopez
review: Tucked away in the fish market area, you can easily walk by and miss this cute restaurant. The service was absolutely spot on, and every dish was prepared perfectly. The servings allowed for my husband and I to order each course, and they were so good we licked the spoons in the end. We had the oysters, fish stew, ravioli, eel, and fried seafood plate. We ended with the tiramisu and bruleed pineapple. Everything was so good, I can't recommend one dish over the other!

name: Michael Carusone
review:
What a delightful night we had at Trattoria Altanella! Alessandro was a wonderful server and told us his family has owned the restaurant for 5 generations. We had an incredible Spaghetti a le Vongole, fried cod, gnocchi, and scallops.

Can’t recommend this place enough and would love to visit again some day. Absolutely sublime.

name: Phil P
review: The Mother’s Day special Italian burger was quite exceptional. It’s about 10 story high with super thick pattern. Treatment was rather unique in Venetian style.  The owner served us and I showed him a Google photo of himself telling them. He was famous and he was embarrassed.  Overall, it was a pleasant dining experience.

name: Lionel Anidjar
review: A true Venetian gem. Altanella combines a gracious, old-world welcome with a refined yet unpretentious cuisine that captures the essence of the lagoon. The dining room, adorned with mid-century works by Venetian painters, exudes understated elegance. A place for those who appreciate authenticity, heritage, and perfectly executed simplicity.

name: Jose Mario Hank
review: The service was good and the food was fine. The view and the patio here were definitely the best features though. The only remarkable dish was the octopus starter, perfectly cooked and well balanced with the salad. The fried fish, veggies, and purple pasta with shrimp were all okay.

name: Juno
review: Best view, away from crowds and top position. Super kind professional and nice service with the food with passion. Good vine which is impressive. My favorite restaurant in Venice.

name: Stephane KILLIAN
review: A legendary address, once frequented by politicians and artists — you can still feel the history in the atmosphere. However, the meal itself was rather unremarkable unless you go for the signature dishes, which we didn’t. Without those, the experience felt quite ordinary, with nothing particularly memorable on the plate. To truly enjoy what this place has to offer, it’s probably best to stick to the classics that built its reputation.

name: MYR
review: Absolutely a must do on Giudecca island in Venice for a nice family Sunday lunch , very discret door to access a very romantic diner place and a beautiful patio terrasse at Altanella Trattoria. The best hand made gnocchi so far .

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
This trattoria is a hidden gem, tucked away on a narrow street — and I’m so glad we followed the recommendation and came here. The place is cozy and full of character, with an authentic bar counter and a wonderful view of a quiet canal away from the crowds.

The staff are exceptionally warm, attentive, and professional — their hospitality and charisma make the whole experience unforgettable, and tipping here truly feels like a heartfelt thank-you. The food and local wine were outstanding. I tried the Venetian specialty Bigoli di Bassano alla salsa di acciughe, while my husband had Gnocchi di patate fatti in casa con carciofi e mazzancolle — both dishes were excellent and gave us a deeper appreciation of Venetian cuisine.

A place full of charm that we would happily return to again and again.

name: Erika Wu
review: Amazing restaurant in Murano. We got the spaghetti with clams and mussels, the caprese salad and fried calamari. And of course we had to try the tiramisu, which was so amazing we ordered a second one! The service is friendly and the prices are very reasonable. If I ever come back to Venice I will definitely make the trip to eat here again.

name: Jasmine Tran
review: Nice and peaceful location. The clamps spaghetti was one of the best I had had. The staffs were considerate and efficient. Note that they accept payment by cash but not card.

name: Катя Катерина
review: Excellent Italian restaurant. We stumbled upon it by accident during the hot lunchtime. It was a real immersion into Italian cuisine, all the dishes were delicious, the portions were large, the dessert was simply magnificent. The service made a special impression, our waiter Simon was very friendly and despite the restaurant being fully booked, he managed to get everywhere. I would like to come back here again and will recommend it to my friends. Thank you❤️

name: Total Medical & Dental Supplies
review: I came to this wonderful restaurant 20 years ago and it's still the best one. I know you have to walk around Murano to find it but believe me, will be the best experience ever. Special mention to the bacala mantecata a typical Venetian dish. It's prepared daily here with great taste, for me is the best. This is where locals come to eat, don't expect a typical turist restaurant. This is what we call cucina de la nonna (Grandsma cooking style). Don't miss spaghetti alle vongole and the tiramisu which is hand made. Every time I come to venice this is a must-visit. Away from the crowd and perfect for the real italian experience

name: Gina Gontero
review: It was amazing! We love to cook and the food was so fresh and tasty! The staff was very pleasant. We are so glad we found this place.

name: Andrea Cayero Clausell
review: Best experience in Venice! The service was fast and very kind, and the food was amazing, Silvia also recommended a great option to observe the glass blowing. Would recommend 100%.

name: Benny CHAN
review: Excellent service, very nicely cooked seafood, reasonably priced, they well deserved all 5 stars!

name: Nick Tehrani
review: You know your in the right place when you see local Murrano residents daily.  The moment we walked in, they welcomed us like family. The cuttlefish pasta was the best I’ve ever had perfectly al dente with rich, briny depth that is romatic to the senses. The risotto changes daily and ours was silky and beautifully balanced. Fresh fish caught the same day and served as if it jumped onto the plate from the ocean.  Lovely attentive service and excellent food all around an easy five stars with a smile. We will come back again and again.

name: Rachel S
review:
This is honestly the best gluten free pizza I've had in Venice! No way could I tell the difference in pizza base.
The service was attentive, the food came out quickly and was heavenly.
If I had more time in Venice, I would be going back.
The location is perfect. The outside seating was very picturesque and not on the main thoroughfare.
5 stars all round.

name: Erik Thorberg
review: The best lasagna in Venice or anywhere for that matter. We have dined here on 5 different trips to Venice over the past 8 years and it never disappoints. The lasagna noodles and the freshest and softest. Highly recommend!!

name: Joanna Ustupska
review: Cosy restaurant, quiet but without passersby. Lovely waiters, food is delicious although the portions are not too big. Gluten-free cannelloni with ricotta and spinach (delicious) or lasagne. Copertto included in the price of meals. Restaurant run by the Italians (not so common in Venice).

name: Mikhail Kazansev
review:
It’s probably the most “italiano vero” place I’ve ever visited. Many thanks to the wonderful Marco, who gave us great advice and even helped us improve our Italian. It was a truly fabulous experience.
The food was absolutely delicious, and we had never seen desserts like this before. It was a truly fabulous experience.

name: Robert Artac
review: Cuttlefish ink pasta was good. I wouldn't do it again but has nothing to do with how it was prepared. The pasta was bang-on al dente. Perfectly prepared. Wine selection was excellent. Very kind waiter and loved his impromptu whistling. Overall would definitely go again.

name: Eugene Ch'ng
review: If you want local, and real Italian food served by seasoned chefs at an affordable price, try this restaurant. Avoid the Main Streets where the touristy restaurants are!

name: Girl Dissolved
review:
Outdoor seating tonight at peak hour .

It took a while for us to get the attention of the  2 floor staff.

Ordered the Eggplant 🍆 pizza and the GF porcini mushroom 🍄‍🟫 with speck .

This pizza was yum , and not too salty.
The mushrooms 🍄‍🟫 gave it heaps of umami flavour . They were very  generous with the speck topping .
However the eggplant pizza only had 7 pieces of the eggplant 🍆 , would be good to see more topping .
Overall good pizzas and an enjoyable dinner .

name: Wiktoria Jankowska
review: Very delicious food made with fresh ingredients and Italian soul(we had lasagne,pizza, tiramisu and pistachio mousse) which we struggled to find in the rest of the city. Also very good alcohol. Special thanks for great service, especially to a man who my boyfriend called Italian version of Robert de Niro, really appreciate you:))) 🇮🇹 🔝

name: Bana Joha
review: I highly recommend this restaurant to everyone! I stayed in Venice for 4 days and only discovered it on my last day – I wish I had known it earlier. I ordered seafood pasta and it was full of seafood, unlike many other places where you only get a few pieces. The waiters were very friendly, and even the chef also , he’s very generous. Prices are very reasonable and local, not tourist prices, even though the restaurant is right by the canal with a beautiful view.

name: Karolina Szwejkowska
review: If you're in Venice, don’t miss Trattoria Agli Amici. I found it too late, —it’s a hidden gem. The seafood is fresh, the pasta is homemade, and the staff are incredibly welcoming. The lively vibe makes every visit feel special. Easily one of the best meals I’ve had in Venice.

name: Oli Wka
review: we stopped here for food with my boyfriend who was celebrating his birthday. We ordered pasta (bolognese and carbonara) which were delicious. and when I asked for some cake for my boyfriend we got a warm crossainte with honey from the restaurant! Thank you very much. Very nice lady who served us!! The food was very good!

name: Skutela
review: Really nice restaurant, staff very attentive. Nice view of the canal with prices not being insane. Was €5 for my lemoncello spritz so pretty decent price. If you want to people watch whilst having a relaxing dinner I would recommend this restaurant.

name: Dermot Breen
review: Cant fault the location as you are literally sitting on the edge of a small canal in a quiet square, so the atmosphere is unbeatable. The food was decent. A few of us had pizza and pasta and one grilled chicken. Id say it was good but not overly exciting, but very reasonable in price. The staff were friendly, and provided good service,  but like what someone said previously they tend to "hang around" inside. and you nearly have to disturb them to get their attention. But again for location this was a really lovely place to eat.

name: Melanie P
review: We randomly stopped here for breakfast when we first arrived in Venice. I really enjoyed the English breakfast and loved the cappuccino and croissant was fresh. A lot of locals stop here for coffee. My husband had the pizza and did not like it. If you speak Chinese, or any Asian language, it's wonderful because they have their menu translated into almost every language. I thought this was wonderful as they are very welcoming to travellers.

name: Shichen
review: Amazing salmon steak. Top quality service! Would recommend.

name: Matija Dimov
review: This is the first restaurant that we visit in Venice and maybe the best. Woman that work there was really friendly. And also its wasn't expensive, I can't remember, but it was around 30 eur. for two drinks, pizza and pasta. Best carbonara that I try. Highly recommended (We was hungry so I didn't take a picture)

name: Амина Горб
review:
We had an amazing experience at Trattoria del Local!
The food was absolutely delicious — full of flavor and beautifully presented. The service was top-notch: friendly, professional, and made us feel truly welcome. A perfect spot for anyone who appreciates great cuisine and warm hospitality.

And a fun detail: be prepared — the owner herself might be serving you, and you won’t even realize it! 🙂

Highly recommended!

name: Antonio Carbone
review: Benedetta and Manuel took care of us! The Paccheri and caponata were phenomenal. The dining room is cosy, thoughtful and beautiful. This is a standout for the Venetian dining scene!

name: İsa Bektaş
review:
original and amazing food. we went here on their second day of opening. everything was great. especially the butter and bread. the butter was something else.

cheese selection is good nice with wine. definitely try the cauliflower. the nuts on it taste amazing. simple dish, maybe, but very tasty. also, obviously, try the pasta.

my only complaint would be the requirement of a cover charge on top of the bread being paid… do i pay the cover charge to be served the fork and spoon? just choose one. if they’d ask for a service charge on top of these, i think i’d not be surprised.

all things aside, great food, lively service, nice atmosphere.

name: B P
review:
I’ll admit, I was sceptical about food in Venice: too many places trading on location rather than care. But Trattoria del Local completely changed my mind. The cooking here is thoughtful, generous, and full of quiet confidence.

The fegato alla veneziana was, without exaggeration, the best I’ve had in my life …perfectly cooked, deeply savoury, balanced with just the right sweetness. So tender. And the selection of amari and digestivi was extraordinary; clearly curated by someone who loves flavours and knows how to end a meal beautifully.

The team were warm, knowledgeable, and relaxed in the best way. They didn’t perform hospitality… they simply were hospitable. The key ingredients to my favourite places. I’ll be back, and will rave about the place. Siete bravi!

My friend and I left feeling genuinely looked after and so happy. Thanks for doing what you’re doing. Greetings to Salvatore!

name: Alexey Shtatnov
review:
White fish rillett starter was fantastic, highly recommend!
Pomodoro mozzarella salat was great as well.
Stewed white fish was ok but much smaller than I expected for main plate))

name: Tanya Myers
review: We came here for lunch a few weeks ago when we first arrived in Venice.  Staff were very friendly and helpful; Everything was absolutely delicious!

name: Alexandros Margaritis
review: Benedetta and Manuel provided an outstanding experience! Their warm hospitality, attention to detail, and expert knowledge made our visit truly special. From the exquisite food to the perfectly paired wines, everything was flawless. Highly recommend for an unforgettable experience!

name: Chiara Di Chio
review:
Just amazing- Giovanna Benedetta and Manuel are incredible hosts taking the atmosphere to another level. Salvatore and Wally in the kitchen brought us back to the authentic tastes of our Italian grandmas - Venice was missing a place like this, the world needs more Trattoria del Local!!
We’ll definitely be back!!

name: Stefanie Shiva
review:
We came here solely because another commenter said that they had the best carbonara of their life here - and we were not disappointed!! Generous portion, yummy, creamy, and also the best we’ve ever had!! We weren’t even that hungry but wolfed down the whole plate. We will be remembering this carbonara by the water for a long time, thank you!! :)

Staff is friendly, restrooms and restaurant is very clean.

name: Alexis Bryant
review: Liton was a phenomenal server! He made the whole night a great experience. The food was amazing. I would definitely recommend the spaghetti bolognese it was wonderful

name: Elena Sorokovaia
review:
Restaurant was recommended to us by our hotel.

I took seafood pasta, and it was good to the taste, BUT it had only clams, mussels and prawns (whereas in other places they add squid and octopus), and the prawns were overcooked. Which is why 4 stars.
Otherwise nice pasta and the sauce. Price 18,50 (cheaper than many other places)

The waiter was pretty occupied, it looked like he was working alone. However, there were not many customers when we came, but we still had to wait a while for the food, and for the check I asked him 3 times. Otherwise ok and polite

Beware that there is a service fee of 2,5e per person.

name: M D'Silva
review: This was my first stop straight from Marco Polo airport. Was too hungry to check reviews and ratings. After getting a table, I happened to check reviews of the place and seeing the negative reviews, I was apprehensive about eating here. But still ordered flat tagliatelle with shrimp (tagliolini ai gamberi) and it wasn't bad at all. It tasted good and I had no complaints about the service too. The person who served me was very polite. They also have an English section in their menu. They have both indoor and outdoor seating (facing the canal).

name: Ashley Mason
review: By the water so it has a cool vibe but a very busy street so be prepared for lots of foot traffic beside your table. We got the Carbonara and the Lasagna and an aperol spritz and a bottle of water all that was 50€. Fairly average price for the area 😀 the Carbonara was 10/10 on the deliciousness scale but the lasagna left a bit to be desired for flavor but bare in mind that lasagna it tough at a restaurant because it has to sit and be ready for customers, so with that in mind I'd give it a 7/10 above average but not the best I've ever had. They provide rolls and some cheese for your pasta. Both the servers that helped us were wonderful and spoke English (helpful if you haven't bothered to learn a little Italian for your trip) so all in all would come again for the vibes but not for the lasagna 😉

name: Kaleb Kemper
review: Love Liton , he convinced us to try this restaurant out .. and I’m so glad he did. Excellent service. Excellent food. I love this place!

name: Irma Pukki
review:
I recently dined at the restaurant and unfortunately experienced a very uncomfortable situation.

While we were eating, a street performer approached our table, stood right next to us, and asked for money. When we did not give him any, he remained by our table and became increasingly insistent, eventually resorting to verbal insults.

What made the situation worse was that the staff witnessed the incident but did not intervene. As paying customers, we expected to be able to enjoy our meal without being disturbed or harassed by someone not affiliated with the restaurant.

We hope that in the future, similar situations will be handled more proactively by the staff to ensure a safe and pleasant dining environment for all guests.

name: Alex Fonseca
review: Excellent service and food. We had their pasta and it was delicious. Drinks were really good as well. Ronnie was an excellent and super friend's host. We came back the next day and he remembered all of our drink orders. Would recommend

name: Carrie (キャリー)
review:
Absolutely amazing!!!
I encountered two italian people while waiting and they said it's the place where local loves coming.
They were so right!!!!!
We loved the food!!! Sooooooo delicious and even though they were extremely busy, they were so friendly!!!! Thank you so much!!!!!!

name: Ricardo Gonçalves
review: Great place, pasta Al Nero sim sepia is beautiful, smooth , velvety and really flavorfull. The house wine is really nice , the value is fair , for the food quality , it's truly worth the detour out of the more turistic areas

name: Chris Hutton
review: My partner and I have been traveling around Europe (we're from NZ) we like to do our research because Venice can be super tourist trapped. But we decided to take a punt on this place after watching someone go here on YouTube. The surrounding restaurants were gasping for customers but there was a line for this place. Everyone that left while we were waiting in queue said "this is Venizian Food for Venizians" and so we we were getting our hopes up for what to expect. There was a 20min wait for a table so we were happy to stand and wait for quality. The gentleman at the bar was so nice, I ordered 2 Spritz' at the bar while we waited for our table. When we got a seat, the food was no nonsense, delicious wholesome food at only 15 Euro per plate. We had their signature plus catch of the day which was kinda what we wanted from Venice as wed heard how good their seafood was. Overall a great experience, yes you can wait a little bit, but this is Italy, im on Vacation, im happy to people watch and drink the delicious house barrel wine while I wait. My only criticism is that they weren't open for dinner that night as def would go back.

name: Jiří Vojtíšek
review: Nice restaurant with a great location near a canal. Oftentimes, you can see a grocery gondola parked nearby. The staff was very friendly and helpful. At peak hours you might have to wait a bit to be served and before your food is ready, otherwise, it was a good experience for an affordable price.

name: Mind Journeys
review:
Really deeply very super good Squid ink spaghetti and also venetian spaghetti!
I'll come back again if I have a chance.
Bro hours service, friendly and clean.
Really recommended!!!

name: Lee Argyle
review: Awesome place. We had a great meal. We visited as a party of 4 including 2 vegetarians. All were catered for. This place is packed with atmosphere and the staff work extremely hard at keeping everyone happy.

name: Moon Moon (Nguyệt Hằng)
review:
It was a little local restaurant with crowded of local people, and the food was just simply amazing. We shared Tagliatelle al Ragù, Polenta e baccalà and the taste was really very wonderful. Fresh bread provided.
Dessert and coffee is also good.
The dishes and the setting are rustic but very well-made.
It was nice to find a not touristy place, if you are visiting this part of Venice, this trattoria is worth a try. Popular to locals, home style cooked food.

name: ulrika skalberg
review:
Best meal in Venice, great atmosphere,  super location.
Very genuin and throughly recommend it, we will be back. Xx

name: Kuan Hou “Angus” Lee
review:
After getting ripped off in other places in Venice, this restaurant was amazing in both value and flavour of food. Highly recommend the squid ink pasta and the bream pasta. The souffle for dessert was really good too!

We arrived at 6.30pm when it opened and the restaurant was immediately filled. Since there's only one staff taking orders it took awhile to get our order in but the waitress was super helpful in recommending a wine. Food was quick to come after that!

name: Cecile Biezanowski
review:
The best restaurant we have eaten in Venice. We are really picky with the food. Quality and price are very important for us.
The menu is short which is also a good sign. The boss is very nice and friendly. The food quality was great  Very tasty and original. Small restaurant in a nice and quiet area. We really recommend the place !!!

name: CL Chen
review: Ordered the squid ink pasta and the seafood big plate. Squid and octopus and scampi were nicely grilled and tender though the mussels are quite tiny and would be a lot nicer if they were bigger. The fish could have been clean better so that it does not taste bitter. Squid ink pasta was very nice and delicious.

name: Hedvig Olofsson
review: I really loved this restaurant! We ordered the duck ragu and the squid ink pasta (I’m sorry I don’t remember the actual names in Italian). The food arrived super quickly after ordering and the waitress was friendly despite it being busy. No reservations and it got quite crowded quickly, so come early if you want to be sure to have a table:)

name: Drew
review:
Authentic delicious food.
We visited for lunch on our last day in Venice and had to go back again for dinner!
Small restaurant off the main areas . Open kitchen so you can see the chef preparing g the meals.
We had the Pistacio Ravioli, lasagne and the beef ragu Parpadelle which was sensational.
The soufflè dessert was one of the best ive ever had. Its just a shame we couldn't fit it in for the dinner visit as we were so full!
Definitely recommend.
Also the Aperol sprits was just the right ratio.
House white wine also very nice and not expensive.

name: Al Michael
review: Short wait, but with no reservation I was seated within 5 minutes of arriving. Do not skip this restaurant! Go figure the best food I have had in Venice was a traditional Venetian restaurant. All the ingredients are sourced from Italy, and the dishes are all authentic. I had the squid ink ravioli, and the platter of Adriatic fish and shellfish. Both were truly heavenly! Service was friendly, professional and efficient. Oh and how could I almost forget, the Tiramisu!!! Homemade with love, the best I’ve ever had. 5 stars, 10/10, bravo, grazie mille!!!

name: Caroline Bonin
review: We honestly don’t understand the good reviews for this restaurant. The food was very average, overly salty, and lacked real flavor. Prices were extremely high, and we didn’t see any locals dining there. We suspect they may have changed owner or chef recently. Definitely not a place we would return to.

name: Tracy Sung
review: Super good pasta! We stopped here for a late lunch and was seated inside. Ordered the clam spaghetti and squid ink pasta and both were superb. Staff was super friendly despite being super busy. Loved that there was a clean (but tiny) bathroom for us to use as well. Great place to eat lunch!

name: Gregorius Deus
review: This was a very good experience for us. A tattoria with a heart. We felt very welcomed and were served with utmost care, we were treated as regulars from the very beginning. Everything went smooth and the food was really good. We've been there almost every day during our stay in Venice so you can imagine that we really liked the place. Mussels and Pizza with speck were our favorite. If you want a good food and experience you most definitely should visit Pan e Vin.

name: Bonnie Barnette
review: This was such a lovely Italian restaurant.  I got the lasagna, and my husband got the fettuccine with swordfish. The portions were generous, and the food was delicious. The host was welcoming and was able to recommend the best dishes suited for our preferences. They made sure to bring us extra bread and desert wine too! We loved it so much that we are going back again tonight!

name: Helen Hutton
review: Absolutely fantastic night at this restaurant. We were welcomed very warmly by the lovely staff whose experience showed when they understood what we wanted and made excellent recommendations. As a group we tried for our primi - the spaghetti with clams (delicious just what i have been dreaming of), black spaghetti with shrimp and porcini, the children had melon and prosciutto for antipasti and then we had secondi of fegato alla veneziana with polenta (liver with delicate italian polenta), mouthwatering steak grilled with potatoes and Angus steak with porcini and we shared some grilled vegetables. The children had some delicious pizzas for their mains (really good long menu with loads of varieties). Then for dessert it was 2 x chocolate cake for the children (so good - warm and soft with a good dollop of cream!), Catalan creme (Italian take on a Spanish version of creme brulee!) and then we shared the most delectible tiramisu which was so light and perfect followed by super chilled sweet and tart limoncello. Our drinks started with an Aperol and a Campari spritz and then followed such a good suggestion of red wine from our lovely waiter who was simply best! Sorry i don’t know what the wine was - except it was a soft and velvety Sicilian red - but put yourself in their hands and you won’t be disappointed. Our waiter’s humour and experience showed so well. 10/10 - so worth it and a good price considering everything we had. Absolutely recommend this restaurant, laid back friendly and lovely - they also they have a gluten free menu if you want this too! The price per person reflects three courses and plenty of drinks including wine, aperitif and digestif

name: Ella Burton
review:
50% discount with the fork app but this is limited menu. Prices are very reasonable though so wouldn’t make much of a difference.
Staff very friendly and welcoming. Super super fast service.
€37.50 for 1 starter 2 mains 1 dessert and 2 alcoholic drinks cant complain really.
Tortellini and lasagna were really tasty but portions could be larger.
Would recommend

name: Patrick M
review: Even though we were only in Venice for a few day, we loved it so much that we had to come back! The food was incredible both times, especially the fresh pasta, the lamb and the eggplant. The service was warm, and very welcoming. A cozy, authentic spot that deserves every bit of praise.

name: Andrew Mariani
review: We had a good experience here. Excellent food and excellent service. Leo himself is the most hospitable and welcoming Italian man I have met on the island. I definitely recommend supporting them! Not to mention the prices were great and food was honestly spectacular. Beef filet might have been the best one, melt in your mouth. Everything else was also delicious. We could not complain about a single thing!

name: Angelika Barola
review: We had such a wonderful time at this Italian restaurant! We ordered pizza, appetizers, dessert, and cappuccinos—and everything was delicious. The owner was incredibly kind and welcoming, offering thoughtful recommendations not only on what to eat and how to enjoy it, but also giving us great local tips for our trip. His hospitality truly made the experience feel special and authentic. We highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the area—this spot is a gem! We’ll definitely be back.

name: Ecesu Olgun Kesici
review: The owner of this trattoria is a gentleman, kind and very hospitable man. He really hosts us comfortably. At the same time, pizzas are very delicious and their sizes very enough for one person. If we come to Venezia again, definitely prefer that place.

name: adam
review:
Friendly restaurant with excellent food and professional waiters.
We went there in the evening. The only menu was a Seafood set menu: 50 euro. All plates were very tasty and big portions. Inside was very loud because the restaurant is small and narrow. Outside was a bit cold but that was fine with us. I would love to go back here to have only one or two dishes. It was impossible to make the card payment and we had to look for cash from some of our bags!

name: SAMAN MOFTAKHARIANASORIMOVAHED
review:
For me the pasta with ragu was too oily. The pasta was literally floating in oil. The meat texture was tough and the taste was bland.

Here  there are not many options to choose from and for us it was between three types of pasta: pasta with ragu, pasta with tuna, and pasta with tomato sauce.

It seems like a family business and there were 5 people all cramped up inside the kitchen and it was so crowded that for a bottle of water we had to wait a long time and repeat it and after when we wanted to take a coffee they completely forgot about us and only remembered when we decided to leave without the coffee and they decided they wanted to make money on that coffee and in a sloppy move they offered us the coffee on the counter which was rude. Not recommend.

name: Ethan Carpio
review: Great local restaurant right next to the water. Away from the commotion at the city center. Was recommended to me by my local friend who lives in Venice. Food was amazing.

name: Caryn Suehowicz
review: Local fixed menu restaurant that everyone should take the opportunity to experience. All locals and university conference goers hoping to get a seat for lunch, but our party of 21 had a long anticipated reservation. We are so glad we booked that lunch. The pasta was fresh and the sauces were exquisite. The meat options were varied and all were delicious.

name: Paul H.Y. Cheung
review:
I have the fish menu. Everything is great. Food is delicious and the view is great. People are nice, too. I believe that price is also reasonable, compared to so many things that they offer.

My only complaint is that... please have a print-out of the menu. I didn't expect the fried fish was coming and I was so full!!!  If the logistics is slightly better, the experience will be amazing. With that said, they did help me pack the fried fish so I guess I'll 5-star them anyway.

name: Cat Ramen
review:
The second to seventh photo is only starters for 5 people. My family and my friends ordered fish fixed menu, it is 45€ for every person. Each of the starters were unique.

The octopus was soft with tomato sauce. The fish’s are cold but tasted fishy. Clams were quite good, and the tuna and mayonnaise were very good.

Then, we have fish lasagna. Cheesy and creamy. I had a lot of very delicious dishes.

recommended but not the best.

name: Andrew Witts
review:
Don’t eat here.

I was recommended by a friend who lived locally, but as so many reviews warn, this place is a disappointment. It may once have had charm, but that’s long gone. The food is cold, bland, and completely uninspired, while the service is careless, rude, and painfully slow.

The wine selection is laughable, apparently the only option is terrible Prosecco, and don’t expect wine glasses unless you specifically ask. Want seasoning for your tasteless food? Good luck, most staff will tell you they only have pepper, and only one particular person seems to know otherwise.

The rudeness when attempting to pay was the final straw. I couldn’t pay without a receipt, and thankfully I didn’t - when finally given a card reader, the total was more than my bill, and they tried to overcharge me.

Miserable food. Miserable service (apart from the one female staff member who was actually trying to be helpful and smiley - Thank you!). Overall a miserable experience. Don’t waste your time or money here.

name: P. Hung
review: While this place is being touted to tourists as a traditional local experience, I would not recommend this place for tourists. The service was abysmal, and being local or even ‘family run’ should not be an excuse. They do not have a written menu, which would be fine if the waitress/owner could be bothered to explain the menu to us. We speak Italian and even then, were only told about the pasta (primi) options and nothing else. We eventually noticed other ‘more local looking’ patrons having meat and sides dishes, but we were not told about it. We were in the dark of the pricing until it came time to pay, and there was no itemised billing, so they could have charged us whatever they wished. The food was fine, but not good enough to make us forget about the poor service and experience.

name: Mavzuna Melikova
review: All the positive reviews about the service are truly justified. The staff were incredibly friendly and really did their best to make our time there wonderful — they were very attentive to us. My pasta was juicy, full of flavor, and the portion was generous. The lasagna portion was a bit smaller, in our opinion, but it was also very tasty. We finished everything and left completely satisfied.

name: Stephen
review:
I first visited this restaurant 6 years ago and it immediately became my favorite. I came back here to try again in the first night on my second trip to Venice. It still tastes the same! If you’re looking for authentic, traditional Italian seafood and pasta, surrounded by the local neighborhood while dining in, this is the place to be!

I haven’t found any same type of Italian bistro is better than this in Venice. Considerate services, not overpriced menu, great wine list. What more could you ask for as a tourist in Venice?

name: Alex
review: My first dinner in Venice. This trattoria was recommended by our hotel (which was right by the corner). It was popular with Italians, which is always a good sign. I had spaghetti with cuttlefish ink with a glass of wine, and tiramisu for dessert. I also shared a cheese platter as a starter. Everything was delicious and I’m happy to recommend this place to others. We paid 40 euros each.

name: Anni Damerow
review: We visited this restaurant as it was close to our hotel. The service was fine, and the food ok. The seafood salad was average, we’ve had better elsewhere for the same price. The squid with polenta was rather plain and didn’t feel worth the €3 more than the black squid pasta. The pasta was really good!! Overall, not a bad experience, but nothing particularly memorable.

name: Harry T
review: Away from the touristic hustle and bustle of endless poor and tasteless pizza/pasta restaurants, this Venetian restaurant with a seafood focus is centrally located with a variety of dishes and welcoming staff and service. No pizza here, but you will find family style cooking and home made deserts in a calm and relaxed environment - really recommend stopping by for a very enjoyable moment.

name: L C
review: Amazing seafood and pasta! Very attentive and friendly staff. I absolutely loved the spaghetti with clams and grilled baby scallops. It was so flavorful and tender.

name: J Wu
review: Delicious food. We had a party of 10 for lunch and our hotel concierge called ahead so they could start setting up the table. Location was very close to St. Marks square. Food was excellent. Especially the seafood salad; it was a real salad!  The pastas were not too salty which seems to have been a trend with recent local restaurants.

name: Brian McGauvran
review: Had dinner at the best restaurant in Venice.  They graciously seated 8 of us at a late seating. The food was excellent.  Can’t say enough about the staff and service. We closed the place.  Lol. , met great people at the next table. Great experience and atmosphere.  We will be back.

name: Pettin Brathwaite
review: I was truly looking for an authentic pasta meal in the middle of Venice and I was walking by this wonderful restaurant. The host Luca had such a welcoming smile, I went in without hesitation and said I was hungry! He suggested a very delicious Primi Piatti named Spaghetti A La Busara! It was truly delicious and a great size serving to fill my large appetite! The atmosphere is the restaurant is very cozy and warm! Luca made me feel like he was my uncle by the time I left! Visit this restaurant when in Venice, delicious tasting food at an affordable price and homely feeling with a cherry on top!!

name: Sara Matulonis
review: Perfect canal view with shade - also indoor and garden seating available. Staff is kind and welcoming. We had fresh seafood apps, a carbonara and squid ink pasta for dinner, and the best meringue dessert I’ve ever had. Highly recommend if you want a fun canalside dinner in Cannaregio.

name: Darryl Viegas
review: Our family has nut and shellfish allergies and we were concerned about eating out in Venice. The reassuring sign at the front gave us confidence. The waiter advised which foods should not be considered. Even bread was provided separately to avoid cross contamination. The food was delicious! We'll definitely be back!

name: Janeal Maxfield
review: Delicious meal! Our last night in Venezia and we had a fantastic meal. We had the turbot and it was so tasty. Friendly service, we felt very welcome. Highly recommend!

name: Jennifer E-F
review:
It’s a lovely canal side restaurant with indoor and outdoor seating. My husband loves arrabbiata and has eaten dozens of various styles of arrabbiata and this definitely hits his top three of all time. I had the potato crusted orata (sea brill) which was extraordinarily fresh, crisp, and flavorful!
We’re ordering the house made desserts and I expect them to meet the level of deliciousness.
The service is wonderful, and the people are so warm and friendly. Highly, highly recommend; come here for dinner and leave full and satisfied!

name: Penelope Roth
review:
Bellissimo! This restaurant was my favourite in Venice for so many reasons, I even went back two nights in a row which usually I would never do when on a short holiday.

The food is excellent quality, value and authentic Venetian cuisine. The location of the restaurant along the canal is so beautiful, and they have a garden out the back for a tranquil and pleasant outdoor meal.

We were a group of 15 travellers and the service was so friendly and helpful. This is a family run restaurant and the owner Luca was very accomodating and also went out of his way to make it a memorable night for one of our group members' birthday.

The second night I went back in a duo we were sat outside next to a couple who said they had been coming here for many years, and it is easy to see why!

name: marcy mccreery
review: Luca is amazing! Such a wonderful experience. Food was great and Luca’s recommendation for wine was superb! Our price person was very reasonable and we had three appetizers a bottle of wine and two cocktails. Will definitely be back 😎

name: Rebecca Huang
review: A fantastic meal. 100% recommend. The family who own this restaurant are so welcoming. We loved coming here and enjoyed the hospitality and fun!

name: Joel José Ginga
review:
During my four days in Venice I ate at this restaurant two times, wish I had more. It is really an experience.
The staff is very nice, especially one of the Ladies whose laughter and good mood fills the place.
The food is very tasty and good and the serving sizes are way bigger than in other places in Venice. Also, I didn't feel overcharged by the drinks and services as in other places.
Don't be fooled by the decoration that seems a little bit outdated it just adds to the mood.

name: Baseem Abdel Samad
review:
Our first restaurant to try during our visit to Venice, and I must say it couldn’t have been better.
Starting with the joyful greetings of Daniella, to the home felt food prepared with love.
This place is a must try, trust me, we went more than once to try different things from the pastas to the pizzas.

name: Van Thanh Tran
review: Our family with a young kid was offered two dinners here during our stay in Venice at the restaurant's owners' hotel. The restaurant is located in a small, quiet ally inside a crowded, touristic area and is not difficult to find. It was an interesting experience to us as tourists trying local dishes that we did not fully understand what they were before ordering, dishes that otherwise we would not have ordered. So we came here twice. The restaurant was not very busy at the time we visited, and we were served by the restaurant owners. They are happy people and there was nothing to complain about the service. The food came out quick and was delicious. The portions were large and we had to try to finish them to avoid food wasting. They speak English well.

name: Yang Huang
review: It's been a year and a half since I've had their pasta a la vongole and I'm STILL thinking about it. I've tried elsewhere in Italy and cooking it back home, but can't get anywhere as good. We saw huge sacks of flour being brought in while we were there, so they clearly are making everything fresh in the kitchen and the quality shows.

name: Ingrid
review:
Went to dinner with my boyfriend, both had the tourist menu.
We ordered wine, but it took a very long time to arrive. Shortly after the wine arrived, we got our first course. Portion of the fish me.u was good and tasty. Portion of the meat menu was small but tasty.
The second dish was okay for the fish, but not all the fish was cleaned very good. The poop part was still in the fish. The meat dish was very small and the side dish, fries, was very small. The table next to us had a full plate of salad, the other option as a side dish.

All together not a place to go again. Including the copperta this is to expensive for what you're getting served. But if you choose to go and want a tourist menu, you better take the fish.

name: Elise Cole
review: Had an amazing birthday dinner at Trattoria Alle Lance! Excellent service, delicious food, I came back two days later for lunch. Good fixed menu that comes with a pasta, a main dish (meat or seafood) and a side salad for €13/€16 plus a 2 euros head charge. So €15 total if you get the meat and €18 for the seafood. The gnocchi was really delicious. As was the spaghetti with the spicy sauce. And the rosemary beef was great. And the tiramisu. And generous portions. A good price for the high quality of food!

name: Kevin Hing
review:
Perfect hideaway from the bustling tourist street. Just a slight turn into an alley and it is there. Like a Harry Potter hidden gem. Serving yummy lunch and dinner sets.
The portion is huge for each item in the set.
For €13 set or €16 set it is totally worth it.
Seafood set example - squid ink spaghetti and fried calamari and fries
Meat set example - Lasagne, beef stew and fries
As the choices available are everything we wanted to taste in Venice.
Yummy and affordable
A perfect hidden gem

name: jardine
review:
this place definitely deserved a 5 star rating for the price to quantity ratio! one of the better food we ate in venice for the price point. dont be fooled by the interior which may look abit dated, the portion size was much better than expected that we went back twice!

really enjoyed the spaghetti with mussels qnd gnocchi was pretty good too! the set meal is really a bang for your buck and the fries were hot and fresh which was a good change from the other restaurants

name: Kevin Allen
review:
Terrific for the price, would be first on our list to go back to next time in Venice. We did a 5 course menu and added on two little finger sandwiches with a cod cream and a beef tongue/salsa verde, which were both delightful. Menu highlights were the green & white asparagus with bottarga and egg yolk, fantastic flavor, then the tin can full of seafood and squid ink and beans and sea foam, super interesting. The risotto with artichoke and shrimp was very good but not as interesting/memorable, then cuttlefish polenta with corn chips was terrific. Lastly the tiramisu with zabaglione was by far the best version of my favorite dessert I've had, really a new level.
$105/person, 22/20 score, priced great

name: Ann-sofie Spitaels
review: A hidden gem in Venice where hospitality and gastronomy is the heart of everything. The hostess was incredibly friendly, and together with her husband (the chef), they create an unforgettable dining experience with impeccably prepared dishes. We went for the 7-course meal but unfortunately we had to stop because I was feeling ill. We will definitely come back!

name: Mihaela Lungu-Tranole
review: Only had a delightful glass of Prosecco, late in the evening, but the food looked delicious. Very cozy place by the water, close to both bus and train station, though away from the tourist crowd. The waiter was very friendly and the price fair.

name: Miriam Oehme
review:
Zanze XVI is just a short walk from Piazzale Roma, the main entry point into the city if arriving by bus or car. It is located right at a small canal. It is considered one of the oldest restaurants in Venice, of course run by many different owners. The current owner kept the historic look and feel widely untouched, and the atmosphere as casual and relaxed as a traditional restaurant would be. Service is highly professional, and the food very inventive although based on local tradition and focused on locally sourced products.
The tasting menu contained very flavorful dishes centered around vegetables and fish. The pace was just about right, as were portion sizes. While there was no wine pairing available, the very knowledgeable sommelier presented a list of wines by the glass so that you could create your own pairing based on your personal preferences. Many wines were from the region, but also from other parts of Italy or even other wine-making countries.
Overall a great dining experience - flavor-forward food, yet light and elegant, and a very good selection of wines.

name: Sophie Pouteau
review:
We literally stumbled upon Zanze XVI, a hidden gem in Venice that didn’t look like much from the outside. But as soon as we stepped inside, we were transported into a world of Venetian gastronomical excellence. We opted for the 5-course prix fixe menu, which began with an array of flavorful amuse-bouches that awakened our palates. Each small dish offered more depth and complexity than the last.

As each new course arrived, our delightful hostess described the flavorful journey we were about to embark on, making the experience simply magical. Every dish was meticulously crafted to tantalize the palate, featuring fresh, local ingredients that were unfamiliar to us as non-Venetians. Chef Giovanni Rigoni’s cuisine is inspired by Venice and its surroundings, focusing on the freshest seasonal ingredients to continually renew the menu.

This is a culinary journey you must experience during your travels to Venice. The young and beautiful staff will guide you through this unforgettable adventure. Just remember, reservations are a must.

name: Lochana Ponnambalam
review: An absolutely amazing dining experience. The food was creatively presented, and absolutely delicious. The 5 course tasting menu was a very good amount of food, leaving us quite full in the end. The wine pairings were also perfectly selected for each course. The staff were incredibly friendly and made the experience that much better. Definitely worth the hype and would recommend making a reservation (though we saw a few walk-ins while we were there).

name: Laura Rigitano
review: Expectations were very high… and the experience was even better! What an amazing selection of dishes: creative, tasty, perfectly executed, with great fit of ingredients and wines. Definitely recommend the tasting menu and location - just in front of the canal, creating a very nice environment. We went in a lunch with friends and was difficult to select our favorite dish as all were a great surprise. Congratulations to all the staff, that are highly skilled.

name: Av. Umut Koray Tuncay
review:
It was our first Michelin Star/Fine Dining experience and boy it was really something out of this world!

I mean it’s not easy to remember all the unusual food with very distinguished ingredients but simply everything we tried was spectacular! The “wine flight” is a spot on choice as well, because every wine is pre-matched with the food served and you can easily understand why.

The staff was very very respectful, involved, helpful, kind and welcoming too. So we had an amazing night altogether!

name: Holly Marland
review: I discovered this gem by accident 5 years ago, just 2 months before we were all brought to a standstill during lockdown. I’m so happy I managed to return and have another truly wonderful meal there last night. I was blown away by the flavours - each course matched perfectly with different wine thanks to the recommendation of the gentleman serving who seems enthused by his craft and was more than happy to discuss the flavours and produce. Top marks for table presentation, atmosphere and creating wonderful dishes using local produce. I can’t recommend this place highly enough. I will certainly return, hopefully in less than five years!

name: Leyani Redditi
review: Wonderful seafood dinner! Warm welcoming service, delicious food and wine. A lovely evening out in Venice! Make sure to try the Cantina Gibadda Vermentino di Sardegna wine the owners make and the pickled vegetables for an antipasto. We will be back the next time we visit Venice for another special evening.

name: Eve Hester-Wyne
review:
An authentic, family-run restaurant in Venice can be hard to find, and this is absolutely the real deal. We go to Venice a lot- this week was about the 7th time we've visited. Every time, we eat at Trattoria Anzolo Raffaele because it's amazing. It's off the beaten track in Dorsoduro, run by a husband and wife, and the food is absolutely wonderful. Fresh authentic ingredients, prepared with skill and heart. The seafood is amazing, but we also love their steak with rosemary. We keep coming back for more!
Just to manage expectations: they don't do pizza or kids' menus. But they do amazing gluten-free pasta if anyone has allergies!

name: Matthew Van Zuylekom
review: Had the most special dinner here. The food was simply the best we had in Venice where everything was home made and cooked to order! The staff were beyond accommodating and created such an ambient atmosphere where you felt like you were dining in someone’s home. The interior and exterior are tasteful and well preserved. Would recommend 100 times over!!

name: Ngayazi Elliott
review: Hello, we came here last night and we were blown away by the team here. The food was so delicious, especially the pasta dishes and the dessert, highly recommend! We had one vegetarian and they catered their dishes especially towards her. As well as the food, the owners and our male waiter here were so friendly and lovely, very attentive. The interior of the restaurant was cosy with a classic italian feel,  we were the last ones in and we were very happy this was our last stop before we left Venice. Thank you very much!

name: Audrey MORON
review:
First restaurant to visit during our stay in Venise, it was perfect.
On a very calm place away from the loud touristic alley of Venice, this place is a very charming, quite stop to enjoy traditional and local food from a couple that offer you healthy, fresh, local and tasty Italian food. Their vegetables coming from their own garden shared with another restaurant close to Venice, we really enjoyed this stop, thank you for your team work, warm welcome and lovely traditional Italian food an place.

name: Ben Middlemiss
review:
After 5 days in Venice being constantly disappointed, this place was the antidote for our trip.  The restaurant is located in a quiet off the beaten track Square and they set up a table outside, next to the wall of the church, for us at lunch because the sun was shining even though they were not prepared for outdoor dining.  Very kind.

The food was delicately prepared, fresh and flavorful.  Truly one of those experiences we will be talking about for years to come and recommending to everyone that comes to Venice to get away from the other nonsense that's so easy to experience here.

They make their own small batch wine in Sardinia along with their olive oil and homemade bread.  Truly, exquisite a genuine culinary triumph and reasonably priced for how good the food and overall experience was.

name: David Chapple
review:
This is a "must eat at" restaurant in Venice. The owners are charming and understandably proud of the quality and taste of their amazing menu.
I would have wanted to go everyday so I could try every dish.
You are also away from the crazy crowds and feel this has a genuine local feel and flavour.

name: Sameeha Ahmed
review: We tried the seafood pasta, salmon pasta and tomato pasta. Everything was so good. The tiramisu is the best I have had! The service is very good, and there is a cute cat that lives at the restaurant. The views are also amazing. Definitely the best place to come if you’re in Venice!

name: Chris and Stephanie Ashley
review: We found this charming restaurant as a way to get out of the rain.  Once inside we found a charming restaurant with friendly staff eager to guide us to some of their house specialties.  We had Calzone, pizza, cabonara.  Loved it.  Great prices.  Warm and cozy.  They have inside and outside seating.

name: Supis Pat
review:
The seafood spaghetti was so tasty and full of flavor!
The squid was fresh and made the dish feel light, not too heavy.
The grilled fish was also really fresh and delicious. Loved it! 😋

name: Monica
review:
I discovered this restaurant during a leisurely stroll through Venice, and it turned out to be the highlight of my day.

Imagine sitting just steps from the sea, feeling the gentle breeze on your face, that's exactly what you get at this beautiful spot.

I tried their special Spaghetti Do Mori, and honestly, it’s the best spaghetti I’ve had in Venice! Perfectly cooked, rich in flavor, with some crispness ,I absolutely loved it.

If you’re looking for delicious food in a relaxing setting with a sea view, don’t miss this restaurant.

name: Irina Popova
review:
A very nice place right at the Palanca stop on the quiet street along the pier. We ordered salami pizza, French fries and beef steak. All was good! The host was very polite and nice. We got complimentary tiramisu which was really delicious.
The price is affordable with no extra fee.
Definitely recommend the place.

name: Аліна Ткаченко
review: A charming and cozy place with very friendly staff. Great location, both convenient and beautiful. The food is about a 4/5, but overall we really enjoyed it.

name: CRISTINA Colilla
review: I went this summer to eat with my family, we were seated in a table next to the canal having an amazing view.The food has good quality and was well presented. Overall the restaurant  has well maintained facilities and offers a view of the city of Venice wile you enjoy  your food.

name: Mohamed Aly
review:
Amazing atmosphere and food! The pasta was incredible and the tiramisu (without alcohol) was a great end to the meal.

We kept coming back because of the service and delicious food.

Highly recommend !

name: David Dais
review: Went here for dinner with my wife and we enjoyed the food a lot. I ordered a pasta dish with lobster in it along with a pizza and my wife ordered a pesto pasta dish and she enjoyed it as well. Overall, the food was good and I would come back there again.

name: TJ
review: Very attentive service. Home cooked great food and lovely atmosphere. A real traditional trattoria experience

name: Elmira Mammadova
review: We had a lovely time! The food was delicious and the prices acceptable. The waitress is a very kind, friendly and attentive lady. We had ordered some black tea and she offered to bring as extra hot water which was very sweet of her. Overall, I highly recommend Trattoria Pizzeria!

name: John G
review: Recommended by my B&B host and it did not disappoint. Awesome pizza, very good Venetian beer and prompt service. A spot popular with locals so you can expect good things.

name: Jan Kovanic
review: Great pizza, pasta, seafood. Nice and friendly staff. Good prices, just a few steps from the main street. This place was recommended to us by our host and it was truly great, we ended eating most our meals in Venice in here.

name: Nata Pie
