review: Really great food, super nice service, usually a place for locals that was recommended to us by our hotel, has more to offer than the classic go-to italian dishes. Really worth a visit!!

name: wind and wheels
review:
One of the last true italian osteria of the city! With a rich Venetian menu as well as pizza fatto a mano along with a great wine selection, what more can you want!!
We always leave so happy!

name: Desiree Flores
review:
Food was excellent. We had spaghetti a la busara, pizza Diavola and a Margherita pizza. Service was good, they kinda leave you alone, but good service overall.
They take visa as a form of payment.

name: Massimiliano Riposati
review: real home made food

name: Georgian Bica
review: Super

name: Evgeniia Lavrenets
review: Дуже прикольне і класне місцеве кафе! Знайшли випадково, але саме таке і хотіли. Щоб максимально відчути не туристичний колорит, а справжній локальний. Тут обідають працівники промислової зони, що поруч, тому дуже смачна локальна місцева їжа, доступні ціни. Охайний інтерʼєр, чисто. Дуже рекомендуємо !

name: Giuliano Porcelli
review: Trattoria che sorge dove non te lo aspetti, in un panorama di ciminiere ed edifici industriali, alcuni dei quali in decadenza. Ci siamo capitati per caso e abbiamo scoperto una realtà singolare. L'ambiente è luminoso e molto pulito, i tavolo sono ordinati e viene immediatamente voglia di sedersi e mangiare. La cucina è semplice e casalinga e i prezzi sono più che onesti, con menù a prezzo fisso ma flessibile nella composizione e nel prezzo. Il menù è diverso ma fisso per ogni giorno della settimana. Gli avventori sono principalmente operai della zona, ma non mancano anche i colletti bianchi. L'accoglienza è amichevole e attenta al cliente, senza esagerare. Oggi ci è capitata una pasta e fagioli veramente ben fatta e un secondo di carne che non avrebbe sfigurato in un ristorante di più alte pretese. Il locale è chiuso la sera, ma è prenotabile per serate familiari o tra amici. Il gestore ci ha detto che la trattoria è gestita dalla sua famiglia ininterrottamente dal 1913, ed è molto fiero di questa tradizione. Il locale è anche una specie di galleria d'arte, perché le pareti sono impreziosite da molti quadri - non in vendita - di pittori soprattutto veneziani del '900, alcuni dei quali molto conosciuti ed apprezzati. In definitiva, una piacevole scoperta.

name: Sarah Marx
review:
Super nettes Restaurant.
Da gehen wohl alle Arbeiter des Hafens hin, aber ich habe noch nie bisher so leckeren Spaghetti aglio e olio gegessen !!! Bevor wir bedient wurden, gab es erstmal 2 Karaffen mit Wasser und Brot aus der Küche. Preislich solide. :)

name: Antonello Balestrieri
review: Da oltre due mesi questa semplice e deliziosa trattoria mi fa sentire un po’ meno lontano da casa. Atmosfera famigliare. Cibo “come a casa tua”. Servizio eccellente. Grazie 🙏

name: Jose Ballester
review: Buena tratotia italiana con menú del día. Tanto para obreros como gente de oficina. Comida rica y variada, aunque poco pescado. Varias pastas a elegir cada día. Menú abundante

name: Matteo Esposito
review:
5 lettere, fa battere forte il cuore: è Pilla.
Convenienza, cortesia, convivialità... mi vengono in mente migliaia di aggettivi per descrivere questo locale ma non li elenco perché sarebbero una mortificazione dell'effettiva esperienza che vivrai.
Menzione d'onore per i tramezzini, belli paffuti, goduriosi... da mangiare in compagnia all'esterno come vuole la tradizione.

name: Katie Goll
review:
This place was a recommendation from a local gentleman and it was wonderful. Sitting next to the water and watching the world go by with a glass of wine and some good food is the best.
Even our kids ate all their food (a rare occurrence).

name: O Fournier
review: This is the WORST macho rude disrespectful agressive waiter I’ve ever seen (with a long pony tail) who told me after 30min wait for a simple dish of pastas that it was normal in Italy and Venise as its not Mc Donald. THANK U, I know what a good normal restaurant is about and what Italian proper service should be: definitely not here! Fast food service is 10 times better ahah

name: Frank Enstein
review:
Some of the tourists are experiencing a cultural shock. They are bot used to the fast service and the rough but professional way of the waiters. They are amazing and I love it.
The food was good and the service top. The tiramisu should have rested a little bit longer before it was served. But it shows that they are making it fresh  which is a big plus.
I would say it's the perfect place for good food with middle budget.
Enjoy!

name: Cristi Timofte
review: Food was terrible. Service was terrible, we waited 40 minute to order.

name: Natalia Dorofeeva
review: It’s a family restaurant, local people love it. You can taste and Venetian food , and Sicilian. Also ask about specialties of the day. Prices are not high. Lunch is served until 3 pm. Then they will open only for dinner. I had cuttlefish with ink and pasta with gambas. Both dishes were delicious.

name: Jabir Majeed
review: This was by far the worst dining experience I’ve ever had. The service was appalling, and the waiter was unbelievably rude and racist. We were made to wait unnecessarily, and when we tried to order, she bluntly told us that we could only order certain items or leave and go to another restaurant. It was clear she had no interest in serving us and was incredibly disrespectful. I would never recommend this place to anyone. Save your time and money – there are far better Italian restaurants where you’ll actually be treated with respect.

name: b rokash
review: we showed up here ready to recharge, we were greeted with such good energy and humor. Francesco was amazing, food outstanding, and the wine was delicious. i recommend you come here and enjoy Murano.

name: Anusha Gupta
review: I had the tiramisu here in Jan 2024 and I haven’t stopped talking about it since. My husband and I had decided to not visit a city twice, but we’re certain we’ll need to visit Murano again just for this Tiramisu.

name: Eloy Yip
review:
Situated on Calle Crosera in the Venetian’s Castello neighborhood, my partner, our friends, family members, and I decided on Trattoria Alla Fonte as our first meal last Saturday off the beaten path.

In Italian, “trattoria" (plural "trattorie")
means a type of informal, usually small, restaurant that serves traditional, hearty, home-style meals, often with a regional focus.

We came there by chance and didn't regret it. Upon arrival, we were welcomed by the staff and got a table for six outdoors in a pleasant and cozy vibe among other patrons.

The menu has multiple language options, offering a variety of traditional, genuine Italian dishes, i.e., pizza and pasta, and has something for everyone, including vegetarians and vegans.

We decided on the Grilled Vegetables, Mixed Salad, Luna Salad, Chicken Pizza, Funghi Pizza, Margarita Pizza, and Caprese Pizza. Complimentary bread started our meal.

Every single bite was delightfully tasty, using only the freshest ingredients, ensuring that every dish was bursting with flavor. The cost was slightly cheaper than other places in the area with a perfect portion size. The staff was friendly, and the service was fabulous.

With its convenient location and delicious menu, it's the perfect spot for a memorable dining experience in the City of Canals, which we can recommend.

name: Karina Chow
review: Very unique pizza offerings with a nice thin (albeit slightly burnt) crust. We got an interesting one with clams and mussels. Had a great salmon/tuna/swordfish carpaccio for €25. Great price for what you get. If you're on a budget definitely come to this cute tucked away place!

name: Nursena U
review: Everything is so delicious and fresh. Best pizzas in Italy!  Suvo and his team are so friendly. Best place in Italy ! Our favorite 🩷🩷

name: Sarah M
review:
The most amazing pesto pasta I have ever tasted!!! Handmade and fresh - it certainly showed!! Every single bite was so full of rich flavor and delight. Perfect portion size - it was very filling! I usually experience unpleasant symptoms when eating gluten, but I was able to eat this without negative effects!

They also do have quite a few gluten free options. My husband got a GF pizza, which was delicious. Staff were cordial and looked like they actually enjoyed working there! Nice atmosphere inside and out. I am still missing that pasta!!
(We ate lunch here on 5/19/25)

name: Kasia Kaminska
review: We came there by chance and didn't regret - the food we ordered was really good, fresh and tasty. The waiter was very polite and professional. The area was quite cozy, not loud, great to sit outside. Definitely worth visiting.

name: Shahed Mazumder
review: Speechless. No words. This is the best authentic Italian restaurant you will come across in Venice. This place is a seafood heaven, and if you’re a seafood lover, you should consider this a must. The freshest seafood in northeastern Italy. Not to mention, the service is amazing, and the restaurant has 5 star ambience and decor. Venice, you have done me well. Readers, do not worry I am not biased although I received a feast of seafood for free.

name: Nancy Ong
review: Finally, we have an authentic restaurant serving simple but tasty food. The fish was fresh. The pastas were al Dente with good flavors.   Location is further but worth it.

name: Carmen Chilson
review: Food was good but nothing special. Literally everyone dining there that evening was speaking English so it doesn't seem like a place locals eat at. Their menu has multiple language options which is useful. Slightly cheaper than other places in the area

name: T D
review: One of the classic Trattorie in Venice, mentioned in many guides and highly recommended. Pictures of celebrities on the walls (Woody Allen, Lionel Messi and so on) but none of the pictures are from recently. Fun fact: „ Carampane“ used to be the prostitutes you could find around here when this part of town was the red light district. Have a good look at the logo of the Trattoria and you will notice the reference. They serve only sea food, daily dishes dependent on what’s available at the Mercato Rialto nearby. We had the mixed sea food salad for starters, then the fried mixed seafood and the monkfish (that was a daily dishes). The kids had spaghetti with tomato sauce at 18 EUR (!) per dish not much else for kids. Quality was ok, the monkfish was dry though and some of the fried fish had an odd soapy taste. Service was friendly but not warm. The Interieur is classy, clean and you feel the history of the place.

name: Braden Hahn
review: Went on a recommendation from a friend.  Very nice, tucked out of the way on a quiet alley.  I had high expectations.  Not sure about the Michelin rating, I expected a bit more attentive service only because of that, but I am spoiled in that sense.  Food was very good.  They sent reminders of my reservation several times.  Interior was nicely done.  This is a small restaurant, you would need a reservation.

name: Jackson Huynh
review: Awesome lunch spot with affordable wines by the glass to enjoy. Fried seafood platter was very delicious you can tell the quality of seafood used. Also highly recommend getting the crab pasta the flavours were on point!

name: Alex
review: An excellent restaurant in Venice, recommended to me by a local. I’m glad I’d made a reservation to dine here. The food was delicious and the environment delightful. I had cuttlefish with polenta, a glass of wine and tiramisu for dessert. I also shared an antipasto - a selection of fish/seafood.

name: A Mok
review: Love this restaurant. Booked for lunch, was seated outdoors in a quiet and breezy alley. Food was spectacular, seafood was so fresh. Loved their glasswares too! Highly recommend and do book in advance.

name: Michael Hickey
review: One of the best meals we had in Venice!  We are foodies from Brooklyn and we’ve had our fair share of delicious dinners. Don’t miss the grilled octopus, and the rest of the meal was very well prepared. It was a very full and somewhat noisy restaurant, and service could be a bit slow, but in our opinion worth it and overall highly enjoyable.

name: QC
review: Came for dinner and it was delicious. We received a welcome fried ball that was delicious. We ordered the grilled octopus (very good), fried shrimp and calamari (the wasabi mayo was yum), and a pasta dish in crab sauce. The restaurant is cozy. Highly recommend.

name: Tanya
review: Huge disappointment. Pretty presentation, but bland food, cold service, and when I dared say the swordfish in the pasta was dry, the manager (I think?) was just offended and told me it was made that morning and everyone likes it.  The pasta itself was not particularly flavorful, nor was the sauce. The frito misto my husband ordered was fine but nothing incredible, and way overpriced for what it was at 35 €. Red wine by the glass selection was very low quality and served way too warm! Room temperature wine in August at this type of restaurant?? The glass of local Venitian white Tai was very nice, as was the foccacia. This place was recommended by Conde Nast Traveller, and has pictures of a few famous people on the walls, but seems to have become a tourist trap! We’ve had a great number of amazing meals in Venice with incredible service, but this was all the opposite.

name: Loo Yeo
review: Busa alla Torre is a Murano institution, named for the vanished belltower of San Stefano. Set in a sunlit square, it’s famed among locals for Venetian authenticity and fresh seafood. I began with moeche fritte – soft-shell lagoon crabs, a rare spring and autumn delicacy, sweet, salty, and egg-crisp, offset by fried seasonal vegetables. Then came spaghetti alla marinara: pasta perfectly al dente, with fresh shellfish, ripe tomato, and a lift of parsley – proper delicious. Espresso closed with traditional biscuits: buttery esse Buranei and golden zaeti. Honest, vibrant Venetian cooking.

name: Randy Hamm
review: Finally got black squid pasta (cuttlefish). A fun experience. Nice campo to eat at, with good shade, food and drink!

name: AmirHossein Jamshidi
review:
I didn’t expect to have one of my best restaurant experiences in Murano, but this place truly surprised me. The food was excellent — the best meal I had during my entire trip to Milan and Venice.
The owner is a great and kind man who served us himself with such respect and warmth, which made our experience even more enjoyable.
We tried the grilled salmon and tagliolini ai funghi with a salad — both were absolutely delicious. Also I tried tiramisu which was the best I have ever tested.
I definitely recommend trying this place — I’m sure you won’t regret it.

name: lea harb
review: Very good service, professional staff who is very friendly and welcoming. The food was AMAZING! The grilled prawns and shrimps were great and so was the fresh fish of the day!! Highly recommended

name: James Middleton
review:
Plates for the outside area are stored outside, uncovered with pigeons walking all over them, wouldn’t be a problem if the top plate wasn’t used but it was handed to a customer who we then informed.

Pigeons were also in the bread bin which the staff we’re leaving open, hard to tell from where I was sat if the bread was covered or not within the basket.

Just got drinks, decided not to eat.

name: Jeffrey Andrews
review: Lovely unpretentious place. Authentic and appreciated by the locals who recommended it me.  Inexpensive by Venice standards with a lovely view of a church.  Seafood is a focus but everything I had was superb.

name: Sheyla Arango
review: The best: outside lunch with a view. Tables outside surrounded by the beautiful church and the canal are priceless. They offer a fixed menu (15€) that is a good deal actually: first dish, second dish and side dish. I enjoyed the pasta alle cozze, it was the best of what I've tried. As second, I had fish that was no good no bad. No taste at all I would say. House wine was good too. Clean bathroom.

name: Carlo P.
review:
How nice to find such an authentic restaurant! Great traditional food, cordial atmosphere and homey decoration.

The antipasto was excellent, as well as the pasta and the grilled calamari cooked to perfection.

Outstanding almond cake and tiramisu! Strongly recommended

name: Jennifer Joy
review:
We had a wonderful meal at Trattoria Ca’ Foscari al Canton! The atmosphere was cozy and authentic, making us feel like we had discovered a true local gem in Venice.

The food was excellent — fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. From the starters to the main dishes, everything was cooked with care and full of that traditional Venetian taste you hope to find. The pasta was especially delicious, and the portions were generous.

The staff were warm, friendly, and attentive, adding to the overall experience. Their recommendations were spot-on and made our meal even more enjoyable.

If you’re looking for authentic Venetian food in a welcoming setting, Trattoria Ca’ Foscari al Canton is absolutely worth a visit. Highly recommended!

name: Ronda Smith
review: My pizza was delicious and I really enjoyed it! The steak was a smaller portion and my partner would rate it a 6.5/10. It was good and above average but I think could add a garlic butter on top to give a little something. The calamari were good but we would’ve enjoyed a bit more salt on top!

name: Haarika Srinath
review: Gnocchi was very bad, the worst I’ve ever had in a restaurant. The marinated sardines are ok. Service is nonexistent even though there weren’t that many diners. It took a very long time for me to actually get the entree. Tourist spot. Has cover fee

name: Danielle Martin
review: The mixed vegetable appetizer was very good. I would skip the lamb, it was a small portion for how expensive it was, I can make it much better at home. The lasagna was good but not the best I’ve ever had.

name: Heather B
review: For not knowing anything about this place and just stumbling across it, we couldn’t complain. Everything was decent and they even cooked the pizza to our “thinner” crust request. The pasta dishes are available gluten free which was a big plus for me.

name: J
review: Exceptional food. Seafood pasta was delicious and good portion. Salmon gnocchi was exceptional but small portion. Added 4 euros to our bill for stale bread we didn’t ask for. Overall a great dinner.

name: Mihaela Boka
review: Cute, quiet and affordable place outside the touristic area. The food is good, although the portions for tortellini and gnocchi are quite small, same goes for the tiramisu. The service is nice. They accept cash, card payment and you can book in advance and pay with The fork app! 👍🏻

name: Chuck Zich
review: Lovely place with great food, prompt service and fair prices.  There is no outdoor seating but the temperature inside was fine.  It was a lovely place and we had a great meal.

name: Kirk Colledge
review: We stopped for some lunch and a few drinks while walking around in Venice. The service and food were outstanding. We had the Risotto, pizza, lasagna and Bruscetta. Everything was great. Would highly recommend.

name: Fatemeh Ebrahimi
review: I had a wonderful experience at this restaurant. Everything from the food to the service was beyond expectations. Paris made the dining experience even more enjoyable with her excellent hospitality. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to friends and family, and I look forward to visiting again soon!

name: Sofia Senchina
review: The food was scrumptious. We got two tomato and basil pastas and one with clams and lime. Both of those dishes were good. Drinks were fine. Nothing extraordinary or too tasty. I would recommend to go to the bar across the street for the drinks. The staff was okay. Not too friendly, but definitely not rude. Just there. Overall, it’s a good place to eat.

name: S. 21lva
review: The menu has nothing to offer for its price. I don’t fault food quality much but could be better especially the pizza after having a board 100 % italian which i didnt felt so but the service is not good at all. Mushroom risotto was good though. The waiter doesn’t bother to give the change back even after charging 10% service charge .

name: behnam afsari
review:
We visited this place to grab couple of drinks as we were not sure if this is a good place to eat or not, but the service was extraordinary our server
Pardis ( Paris ) made our day , so we decided to give their food a try and food was excellent like the drinks, really appreciate Paris for her kind and great service and will recommend this place and we will go back for sure, nice view and great food!!

name: Balazs L. 3Dpano (Panoinsight)
review:
A True Taste of Italy in the Heart of Venice – Da Roberto

Ristorante - Pizzeria da Roberto is a charming and authentic Italian gem nestled in the heart of Venice. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with warm hospitality and a cozy, inviting atmosphere that made us feel right at home.

The menu offers a wide selection of classic Italian dishes, from freshly made pasta to seafood specialties and, of course, delicious wood-fired pizzas. We tried the seafood spaghetti and a traditional Margherita pizza — both were absolutely fantastic. The ingredients were fresh, the flavors perfectly balanced, and the portions generous. The house wine paired beautifully with our meal, and the homemade tiramisu was the perfect sweet finish.

Prices were fair, especially considering the central location and the quality of both food and service. Whether you're a tourist or a local, Da Roberto is a must-visit for anyone looking to enjoy genuine Italian cuisine in a relaxed and friendly setting.

name: Tom C
review: We weren't sure if we wanted to try this place based on the reviews. After we did we were pleasantly surprised. Yes, more expensive that other meals we had, but the food was very flavorful. I had the squid ink pasta and my wife the lasagna. We shared a spinach dish as a side. The place has a nice atmosphere too and was warm and welcoming in a cold December night.

name: yasaman ahmadnia
review: I went there in happy hour and it was really nice place with a nice and kind staff the beauty young lady also suggested one of pizzas and the taste was heavenly . Everything was so nice there.

name: Dattatreya Roy
review: We visited Murano for a day and decided to stop by for lunch at this place. The food tasted amazing especially the scallop pasta, cuttlefish tagliolini and the grilled sole fish. The location must be one of the best in the whole of Venice - right at the intersection of multiple canals and comparatively less crowded as compared to canal side restaurants on the main island. Would definitely recommend!

name: Yasha Taylor
review: Out of all the places we visited and enjoyed it. This by far was the favorite. Such a lovely family restaurant and the owner ... He's a great guy. The food was delicious everything you could imagine.

name: Roger Parramore
review: Excellent in all respects, service, fresh food, and traditional Muranese fare. We go here often. A true winner. It can be tourist infested, just pick your time and season. If all else fails, bear the tourists and go anyway. It is well worth it.

name: Michael W.
review:
We walked all day and every one of my friends was tired. We saw Trattoria Ai Frati in the middle of the water. It looks so good. We want to stop here for a break with food and drinks.

The food is very fresh and tastes good. Drinks are much better after we walk for a long time. The services are a little slow. But I love the feeling of eating and drinking in the middle of the canal.

name: Kateřina Osmiková
review: Perfect food and service, excellent views, sitting just above the water. Highly recommended!

name: Wolfgang (Lupo)
review:
Simple, but delicious food like a grilled fish or cooked shrimp. As far as I can tell, local and very fresh. For sure not frozen and carried around the world. Presentation is honest, no fuzz. If you love original taste then you are in the perfect place. Wine? One red, one white. That’s it. The white was excellent and just right for a weekday lunch. I give 5 stars because everything was so tasty. No distraction on the plate.
The back room has an impressive painting based on da’Vinci Last Supper showing the owner, family, suppliers and regular guests feasting at a long table. Funny folk art. It was the first time we could get a table in that room.
Lunch only these days. We will be back.

name: Cherie Kennedy
review: We were booked here by our concierge on Venice main island. It is an absolute gem. We let our waiter guide us on menu selection based on fresh catch of the day, and it did not disappoint. Highlight of our visit to Murano

name: Joaquín LASA
review:
I ordered the pasta with scallops and it was really good and tasty.
It has a nice outdoor place to eat in front of the canal.

name: Alam “Zorro4ever” Ababei
review: Semplicemente super!

name: Andy Britner
review:
A great experience at this restaurant.
Very attentive and friendly staff, such a nice visit.
Our table had a whole selection of food, but we didn't take pictures of everything, just 2 dishes as the others were dived in to right away!
All pastas were perfect, sauces delicious and fresh and plentiful.
If back in Venice, we would visit here again.

name: Adem Tat
review: We ordered prawns, risotto (with mushrooms), spaghetti (Arrabbiata), pizza (margherita). All of them were delicious. We had a tiramisu and panna cotta. Good atmosphere and great food! But the portion  of food were big, actually two children can finished only one portion, be careful! Overall, it is highly recommended!

name: Lou
review:
We were looking for this place for two days after accidentally passing it during day walks around town. Found by by a chance - Thank you Universe! 🫶🏻 really wanted to try cuttlefish ink risotto and yes it was very much worth the try. Fantastic bruschetta’s for a starter sealed the deal.
A big plus - Italian music in the background which was so rare to find everywhere else!

name: Quing_刘妈妈
review:
📍 Da Cherubino – Venice, Italy 🇮🇹
A cozy local restaurant 🍝🍷 tucked in the quiet streets of Venice, away from the main tourist crowds. The menu offers authentic Venetian and Italian dishes 🇮🇹—from fresh seafood 🦑🐟 to homemade pasta 🍜 and traditional desserts 🍮. The atmosphere is warm and intimate, with friendly service 😊 that makes you feel welcome. Prices are fair compared to many tourist spots, and the quality of food is excellent. A hidden gem worth discovering if you want a more authentic dining experience in Venice. 🙌

name: Shaun Matney
review: This place was great. We were seated immediately, services was friendly and entertaining. Food was great. Plan to visit again next time we go to Venice.

name: Weiying Gao
review: Overall good experience. Food is good, although the fish soup I ordered was a bit too salty. The appetizer Cichetti is very good, a little too pricy though for the amount served.

name: Tina Yeung
review:
I ordered dried salted cod Venetian style. It did not taste salty at all. But clearly from the texture it had been salted. Served with arugula and lightly toasted baguette slices. Very nice.

Sea bass Burano Style. The fish was well done, served on a warm plate. Served with slithers of aubergine, courgette, red bell peppers, capers and olives.

The vegetables were fresh. As you know, sometimes you can eat this kind of vegetable and feel like you are going to choke on the skin.

I anticipated the capers and olives might overpower the palate and leave me unable to taste anything else. But surprisingly, it didn't. It all blended nicely. I can only guess that it was down to the quality of the ingredients.

Pannacotta. This was well done, too. Sometimes the berry sauce served with it can be so sharp or leaves an acidic taste in the mouth.

This was sweet and complemented the pannacotta nicely.

name: Avani
review: THE FOOD IS AWESOME! The customer service is super nice. My favorit dish I definitely love the fetticuine with crab😁. The atmospher issuper cute and small and i just love it!! Dima is a great server!

name: Courtney Farmer
review: We ate here two nights in a row while we stayed in Venice. Delicious pizza, and the house white and red wines were scrumptious..  prices were good. Staff was very welcoming! Would highly recommended!

name: Elena Guerrero Madejón
review: Incredible food. Impecable service. While the name promises great pizza, a visit here is incomplete without trying one of their signature seafood dishes, particularly the Sepia (Cuttlefish). This item stands out as a beautiful example of quality ingredients handled with respect.

name: Daniel Valchev
review: We liked it so much, that we visited it twice during our stay in Venice. The food, the service, the atmosphere - everything was very good. Good place to try the local cuisine.

name: Wyatt Cuddington
review: Go here. So delicious. Had sweet and sour seafood, spaghetti, and duck ragu. Mezzo mezzo spritzes and affogatos for dessert. Every bite was fantastic and the service was great. Lovely local neighborhood off the beaten path.

name: Konstantin Knauder
review:
during our last stay in venice, trattoria ae do marie turned out to be our new favourite place for having a great apperitivo, lunch or dinner.

as a quite large group of 12 friends, we were having two very nice evenings, enjoying amazing traditional food and wine.
the whole team is absolutely lovely, creating a warm and familiar atmosphere we already miss. <3

looking forward to coming back again soon! :)

name: Jenny Betzen
review: Beautiful garden dinner. Most welcoming staff. Delicious pizza and bruschetta. Perfect aperol spritz. Could not recommend more. So thankful we found this restaurant!

name: Katiusca Irismar Peek Rondon
review: The experience at this restaurant in Venice was wonderful. Delicious food, charming atmosphere, and excellent service. Truly one of the best experiences I’ve ever had.

name: Fredi Pançi
review: This is amazing place, very good service, very kind hospitality, clean and quiet, diversity kitchen, amazing staff. If you are looking for  a wonderful place, delicious food, affordable prices and maximum quality of service, this is the correct place.  I strongly recommend this place to everybody.

name: vaggelis diapoulis
review: Sat there for lunch and firstly I have to say that the staff is very friendly. The atmosphere was nice and quiet inside, proper for some rest after the crowded city. The food is decent but I think you find better else. The pasta with squid ink was pretty good but the pizza didn’t really impress us

name: Lukas Nussbaumer
review: The food was okay, but not amazing. The carbonara was too thick and egg-heavy. The ravioli was better. The service was poor. The wait staff was friendly and attentive, but otherwise inadequate. Very slow, didn't clear away used dishes and glasses, and forgot to bring a mineral water order. There are better restaurants in this price range.

name: Anh Nguyen
review: We have lunch at this restaurant, Venetian style decoration inside and some nice tables outside on the calle del paradiso. Despite having some groups of tourists, the waiters served us nicely and very prompt. We were super hapy! Highly recommended!

name: Ergys RROGA
review: I will give 5 start if they were more faster for the order and if they have prices not very expensive, the food was good and place is nice.

name: H. E.
review: We thought about eating there, but the waiter attacked our dogs. No sorry no stopping. Went somewhere else

name: Kevin Simmons
review: Well priced pizza was fantastic, had to ask specifically for the pizza menu though. Was given bruschetta and prosecco as a welcome but then charged for it. The charge was only 6 euros total so still worked out as a relatively cheap and filling lunch for central Venice.

name: Cheryl Maplesden
review: We had one of our best meals at Trattoria de Bruno. The service and food were 5 stars. We had a great pasta dish as the first serving, followed by delicious steak tips in an incredible gravy with peas and carrots.  For dessert, we enjoyed a refreshing flan-like dessert. Also,  the restaurant was well air conditioned.  Anyone who has experienced the heat and humidity of Venice knows how important air conditioning is.  I would highly recommend this restaurant.

name: Indra Baļķīte
review: Wonderful service, prosecco as a welcome drink, compliment from the kitchen, food great, prices adequete, and limancello at the and, according to Italian traditions. Very cosy athmosphere! Recommend!

name: Rick Rappleyea
review: So good, we went twice! The food and service were excellent, with reasonable prices and a great wine selection. The atmosphere strikes a perfect balance between elegant and cozy. Everyone was incredibly friendly and accommodating—even when we showed up without a reservation. Highly recommend!

name: Konstantinas Jacikas
review: Me and my wife were recommended to this place by local gondolier as authentic and affordable restaurant to eat. We are really glad that we took his advice as our visit was amazing. Great atmosphere, beautiful view outside and indoors, attentive service and wonderful meals. The seafood was so fresh and tasty and I would definitely recommend to try! Also we were very happy with waiters who made our dinner to feel very exclusive. A+++

name: lee jones
review: This is a lovely and popular little restaurant with both indoor and outdoor seating, the food is always excellent, steak cooked to perfection, lamb chops juicy and tasty. .. the service is amazing and this restaurant is really worth a visit. Gnocchi is great and the parma ham with melon starter is a light option very good.

name: Saraid Cann
review: Lovely authentic trattoria, great food, good buzz, refined and yet homely too - where the locals eat. The waiters were friendly and welcoming having gentle banter with all of the diners. The food was lovely with great portions and a pizza, wine and water came to aroybd €25 inc service. The place was pristine with spotless tablecloths and elegant glassware while the children's drawings behind the bar added family charm 🤩. Very buzzy but not overwhelming and no one (literally no one) had their phones out - everyone just enjoying the experience. Added bonus was that next to us was a table full of gondoliers eating heartly after a hard day's work. Best in Venice, will definitely go back.

name: Russell Barnier
review: Stumbled upon the square by accident and returned later in the evening for dinner.  Very enjoyable - great atmosphere in the twilight with table lights shining on the tables.  Mix of locals and tourists happily eating their meals.  Food was good, service was good and the weather was perfect.  A great mix.  We enjoyed our evening very much.

name: Katianna Filiou
review: The food was fantastic, both meat plates and pasta. The pizza and the tiramisu were the best we tasted in Italy. The prices also really good. The service is nice and all the waiters really polite and helpful. It worths visiting.

name: Omar Almesri
review: This place is a hidden gem in venice that everyone should enjoy. The food is from heaven beautifully seasoned and presented and also tastes amazing. The staff are very friendly and welcoming they will make you feel as you are the most important person in the place. The atmosphere is sensational the have a beautiful patio with small lamps in the evening to add a romantic touch also it’s located right in front of St. Toma chapel. I can’t say enough about how good this place was it was so good that I went there twice. Would definitely recommend going there.

name: Julia Dunbar
review:
We came across this trattoria when looking for a simple lunch. We struck lucky!
Delicious food, efficient and staff, fine wine. But don’t just take our word for it
-ask the several tables of gondoliers who ate here during our visit- I assume that they know a good, value for money local restaurant. I’m not sure if some of the reviewers here actually visited the same place! Highly recommended.

name: Grace Li
review:
The BEST clam pasta we’ve ever had.
The razor clam and scallop appetizer was delicious. Our server recommended the risotto to us which is only made a certain way on Bruno islands. A lot of menu items have a minimum 2 person order so the risotto was 60€ 😳 which was good but nothing of wow factor like the clam pasta was. If we could go back, we’d get the clam pasta and another pasta dish instead. You must make a reservation in advance or get lucky like us and go right off the water taxi to see if any reservations are left. Luckily we got one of the last walk-ins for lunch at 2pm. Total for an appetizer, the risotto for 2 and 1 clam pasta dish which we split was 170€.

name: Nikos Tsironis
review:
If there were 10 stars I would put them, 5 stars are not enough for this place!!
I did my daughter’s christening and it was the best place we could choose!!!
The chef, the people and the food are on another level !!
The place serves every desire and exceeds them !!!!
Highly highly recommended to go !!
Whoever hasn’t been there MUST go :))

name: Cherry
review: This place has juicy, fresh and delicious seafood! We loved the razor clams especially. My husband also loved the clam spaghetti. The ragu pasta was of good quality too. We had the traditional tiramisu for dessert and it was lovely.

name: Mary N
review: The clam pasta (vongle) dish was a standout and a must have. We had so many average to poor pasta dishes throughout Italy until we reached this place. The signature antipasto dish and mixed grilled fish plates were good. Manuel our server was excellent and made it a great experience.

name: Sailor Pear
review: Exceptional food and service. We had made a reservation as recommended and got the seafood special appetiser and risotto. The seafood was super fresh and seasoned beautifully. A very special experience. We paid €121 for the food and water.

name: Ush K
review:
It’s a bit pricey, but absolutely worth it - this was the best restaurant we visited during our trip to Venice. In fact, it was because of this meal that Burano became our favorite island.

We had lunch here without a reservation, but the staff were incredibly kind and offered us a table in 40 minutes. The bread with balsamic vinegar and olive oil was delicious. We’re still wondering where I can find the same vinegar they use!

The seafood appetizer was fresh and flavorful, and the pasta we ordered was rich and perfectly cooked. While waiting, we chatted with a lovely older couple (in their 60s/70s) next to us, who told us it was their sixth time coming back to the island just for this restaurant.

I hope that one day, when we’re their age, we can return to this little island and enjoy another meal here.:)

name: Ruth Smoother
review: The best eating experience in all ways I had in Italy. From the kind personalized service, to the atmosphere, the crockery, cutlery, all give an amazing vibe before even the food arrives. The food was just delectable. I had the spider crab tortellini. And it was divine. The paired wine suggested complimented it. It was extremely reasonable in cost also! I cannot recommend this treasure more - just make a reservation so you don’t miss out on this once in a lifetime experience.

name: Maddy Rueda
review:
Genuinely the best restaurant I’ve ever had the pleasure of dining at. Massi was so kind and truly made me feel like a member of the family.

I’d emailed the restaurant in advance to reserve a table, which I highly recommend doing ahead of time. In the nicest way possible, they had to turn away many walk ins because they simply didn’t have the capacity.

I was seated in the back garden, which felt like its own little slice of heaven. It was quaint and made me feel there was not a worry in the world.

The service was so special. I was greeted with a glass of white wine upon arrival (not sure if this is standard or because this was a kind gesture for their being a queue at the host stand) and was visited multiple times by  Massi and another waiter to ensure everything was to my liking.

I ordered the clams to start and the fish as my main. Both were fresh and flavorful, and they filet the fish table side for me so that I didn’t have to pick around any bones myself and had the fish presented in such a memorable way.

The food, service and atmosphere were all impeccable. I live halfway across the world and can’t wait for the day I get to return here again.

name: Kotaro Nakata
review:
The best restaurant in Murano!!!
Sea food Carbonara and Mussels and tomato pasta were amazing. I love them and would like to have it again.

Atmosphere is perfect. There are three area to have lunch or dinner, outside next to river, inside, and garden place.

The service is amazing too. The staff are attentive and realize when we wanna order.

I fully recommend to come here even if you are in the Venice main Island. It's a bit expensive for students like me but get a great experience.

name: jiaxin zhang
review: I want to say that this is the best in town. We tried other places. It was very salty and had very little portion, but look at the four main dishes we got for 90 euros! It's really delicious. I recommend beef and grilled fish!

name: Russ Pasley
review: Great evening meal after a tour of Venice. Good service, white wine and pasta. Desserts were also excellent. Price was half what we'd pay at another establishment down the way. Highly recommend.

name: Vlada Ryzhko
review: We found this place on google maps, one that hasnt closed during the afternoon hours. The atmosphere was amazing, the garden was cool, packed with a lot of fans and the staff was very kind to place the fans towards where we were sitting. The food was delicious, smartly and well portioned, I really dont understand the comments saying that the portions are tiny. We were all full and satisfied, the seafood tastes fresh, delicious, the tiramisu (mentioned in some other review) was scrumptios. The staff was friendly, attentive, we got our food in 15 min. Would recommend and hopefully visit again.

name: Лэ Сун
review: I think this was the best place I ate in Venice during my four-day trip. The food was fresh and the service was excellent! I highly recommend this place to everyone I meet for a good meal!

name: Franco Saspe
review: Great food, Great service, Great atmosphere. Came here two days in a row, very rare !

name: GREG AKRIDGE
review: We had dessert and drinks on the canal just after sunset and it was quiet and everything you could hope for away from the Venice crowd.

name: Lisette Marin
review: Such a great place to eat, especially if you want a break from pasta and pizza. We had the chicken cutlet; it was delicious & filling!

name: Paula Micaela de Bruijn
review: This restaurant delivers a sensational and authentic taste of Venice. The waiters were all lovely and engaging, offering service with a bit of fun and cheekiness. The cuisine was absolutely delicious. I highly recommend starting with the superb octopus and the fresh scampi and artichoke appetizers. The clam pasta was also a standout! This was hands down my favorite dining experience while visiting Venice.

name: Myra R
review: The photos speak for themselves. It was an absolute joy to dine here four times — the best representation of Venetian food we've had. The off-menu seafood gnocchi is a must-try! We're really particular when it comes to Italian cuisine, but this experience was truly a gem. Pure bliss. The sardines were piccolo, but niceee (an inside joke with the owner). Ciao, till we meet again!❤️

name: Giulia Brioschi
review: Bepi Antico 54 da Loris delivered one of the most memorable meals we had in Venice. The spaghetti alle vongole was a perfect balance of freshness and depth—tender clams, fragrant garlic, and just the right touch of heat and parsley. The tagliatelle alla granseola was equally outstanding, with sweet, delicate crab meat folded into silky, house-made pasta. Every bite reflected the freshness of the ingredients and the chef’s refined touch. A true gem where tradition meets perfectly balanced flavor—absolutely worth seeking out.

name: Chloe Tay
review: We arrived at this restaurant promptly as it opened after reading really good reviews about it. We ordered their spaghetti ale vongole which we were craving, however it was unfortunately disappointing. Out of the clam shells provided, there were only 6 clams, much less than received at other restaurants. Furthermore, the pasta lacks depth and flavour. We also ordered the fried mixed seafood as a main, which although tasted nice, could not be justified by its price point as this was similar to the fried calamari appetisers back in Australia. Based off the reviews, we had expected food of better quality and portions, but perhaps we just ordered the wrong things.

name: Gregg
review: Wonderful restaurant! We had several dishes including the artichoke and lobster, potato ravioli with spinach and ricotta cheese and a shrimp dish. All were very good. We sat inside towards the back. It was quiet and a perfect setting. There was outdoor seating but a wedding was going on next door so kind of noisy with kids out front. There was just two of us and a great place for lunch. Seemed to be a

name: John Anderson
review:
Now, it makes sense why they force gratuity.
Arrived at 7:40. We were  seated quickly with no menus, no offer for drinks or water. We waited 15 mins, then my wife had to get up and go around to the front and ask for menus. 15 minutes after that, a woman came (who we never saw again), and we ordered water and fried calamari.
10 minutes later, she took our order.
By 8:30, no bread was offered, and no one came by to check on us.
Finally, at  8:45, our meals showed up. We had to ask for bread. The bread was sliced and stale, we could hardly bite it.
I got the spaghetti with clam sauce with the smallest clams I had ever seen. The spaghetti was undercooked.
30 minutes go by, and after having to remove the pinky nail sized clam meat from the clam, I realized, "we never got the fried calamari."
I then taped my wine glass and said loudly,  "I have an announcement to make! I want to get the he'll out of here!".
Then we waited and waited and waited. Finally, a guy came by and offered coffee or dessert.
I laughed and said, "No, we'll take the check.
Also, like i said before. we never saw the original woman again...

name: Amal Hadiya
review: The pasta with clams was so fresh, I think they just pulled from water, really salty sea taste but in good way, not heavy. Then fish on grill, simple, no too much oil, just nice. The guy who served us was joking around, he said try the local wine and yes it was good with seafood, I liked that part. Tables are close, everyone speaking loud italian, sometimes hard to hear but it gave me feeling like real place, not tourist. Prices ok for Venice, not cheap, but with the portion and quality I didn’t feel robbed.

name: Kira Zatsepina
review: What an amazing place! The food was delicious, the service very friendly and the atmosphere very special. The recommended wine was great. The prices are very fair too. There’s a lot of locals here. We had a reservation from a few weeks before, and I think it’s best to try to have one. Dinner here was the perfect final chord for our magical day in Venice. Thank you! I cannot recommend this place enough!

name: Ofer
review:
We ordered 2 pizzas, which were very good!
Besides, the happy hour cocktails was bellow avarage, small and seems like have not enough alcohol, and more ice/water. And also calling it happy hour and charge 4.5€ instead of 5€ isn't a real happy hour.
And of course the sign that says that there is no service charge - they do have cover charge of 2€ (that's OK), but nothing special than all other restaurants in Italy that justify the sign.

name: Edgars Matisans
review: We stopped by for their €4 Aperol, which, while cheap, seemed to be missing enough prosecco. Overall, it was a pretty average happy hour cocktail. The place was empty, and we didn't order any food, but we were a bit put off by the personnel smoking on the guest terrace.

name: jonathan swaby
review:
We ate here for lunch. The place was filled with local workers taking their lunch break.
My wife and I had pizza and my son had some sort of pasta dish.
I ordered a pizza with raw ham, but when it was served there was no ham. So, it was a cheese pizza. The crust was really good. The cheese to tomato sauce ratio was also good. The flavor in general was good. Not the best pizza I have ever had, but also not the worst.
Neither my wife not soon comment on their meals, so I will assume they were average.

name: The Moistmaker
review: Attentive staff, but the squid ink pasta was just so-so. The sauce was quite dry. Still, it is better value than a lot of places in Venice. It was the first restaurant we visited upon arrival in Venice. We were exhausted, and found it to be a great choice due to the relaxed vibes. The happy hour drinks are great value, too.

name: Eliza Korpalska
review: We were there for a dinner. Place has good food and we could feel comfortable. We ate bruschetta (the only i made foto of), pizza with braseola and gnocchi 4 fromaggi. All with good quality.

name: Goshgar Huseynli
review:
Pizza was ok, but 8 euros for a 0.5l beer is crazy. Just because of the food I ordered it, otherwise would never pay that amount for a beer🤦🏻‍♂️

Edit: The reply to my review was wrong because I had explained the situation. You must satisfy the customers not argue with them. For that reason I'm omitting 2 more stars. You need work on your marketing.

name: Melanie Brazier
review: Because of the previous reviews, I decided to try this place. I enjoyed it! We sat outside, and the weather was perfect for it! I would visit again if in the area.

name: Myra Giordano
review: Great food and attentive service, the best pizza we had in Venice and their hospitality was amazing, serving us near closing time as we were tired from a long day of travel and making us feel like family. A great experience overall.

name: John Owen
review:
The owner seemed nice, but the service wasn't the best and neither was the food.
For the starter we had a a plate each which had different kinds of fish and shell fish on. The scallop felt like it had been microwaved and the other bits lacked seasoning.
The owners seemed nice, but our waiter I felt was quite intimidating.. standing over us and honestly it felt like we were an inconvenience to be there.

The restaurant itself is really nice, but sadly the food wasn't the best and for the price, I'd expect a lot more

name: Bull Dogg
review: Venice is known for substandard food (by comparison to other Italian tourist destinations), but this beautiful restaurant proves this hypothesis wrong. A bit hard to find, the restaurant is tucked away in one of the hottest tourist areas in Venice. This is a more expensive but great price/value restaurant, featuring linen table cloths and a nice an quiet outdoor dining area. The food is absolutely fantastic. We had sea bass carpaccio, clam spaghetti, and shrimp/crayfish pasta and it was great! The servings are traditionally small since you to through all four courses, but the preparation is fantastic. The owner couple are very nice and friendly and accommodating. Highly recommend this restaurant is you want to pay a little bit more than average for a fantastic dining experience.

name: Caesare Torres
review: Came here with my wife and had an awesome experience! Order the Fritto Misto as an appetizer, Tattalgia di manzo, Tiramisu for dessert and a bottle of red wine, all of which were delicious! The waiter was super friendly and kind.

name: Amalia Helmis
review: A very quiet restaurant with a garden in the back. It's well hidden between these narrow streets. The pasta was amazing and we also received a gift, a small cube of ice-cream covered in chocolate. It was AMAZING!

name: silvio garola
review: Wow. Great! Five stars. They have delicious food and the service is great. I reccomend

name: Taj Ratta
review: Super lovely.  Should be much busier than it is and the waiter was exceptional in directing us to the best dishes

name: D H P
review:
The reviews depend on the writer and place in time, so....
Food was fresh and very well prepared, tasty!
The service was very good, 20 min to prepare at late evening time, on Saturday night, with half of the tables in use.
Waiter patient, pleasant, helpful.
Restroom area could use attention.
Also the overall cleaningness.
The atmosphere was what time in a restaurant in Venice should be.

name: PickfordStudios
review:
This is our favorite restaurant in Venice.  We come to Venice every two years, and we always get a warm welcome here.  Fausto is just the best waiter.

The capesante (baked scallops) are the best you will find anywhere, as is the seafood risotto.  Really comfortable restaurant with inside dining and a lovely garden.

We’ll be back again in two years!!!

name: Ekaterina Sterkhova
review: This is a wonderful place in streets of Venice. Enough space, a lot of waiters from whom nothing will escape us. You don't notice but they make sure everything is on the table, there are no dirty plates, very attentive waiters. We celebrated the daughter's birthday and of course they sang congratulations to her. The food was great, amazing tiramisu, gorgeous pasta and caprese. We were delighted.

name: Daniel Benson
review: Authentic Venetian food! We had the caprese salad, squid ink pasta, meat ravioli, and grilled monkfish. All of it was fantastic. Our server Gabriel was amazing and made great recommendations. He even threw in a couple free after dinner drinks. I’d recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for an authentic dining experience in Venice.

name: Carlos Pereira, Jr.
review: My wife and I were here from Alaska on our EuroHoneymoon and I can tell you that this place deserves more than 5 stars. Don't expect a chic, luxurious atmosphere. It is your typical Italian mom & pop trattoria with homemade, made from scratch Italian food and VERY DELICIOUS. Yes, it takes a while to get your food but that is typical Italian cuisine style - you are in Italy, not America. The seafood salad was amazing and the spaghetti with squid black ink was to die for, especially when paired with our wine that our server recommended. If you're in a hurry to get to places, don't stop here. If you want REAL Italian cuisine at the pace of REAL Italians, alla Madonna is the place for you. We will definitely stop here again when we visit Italy!

name: miss4elle
review:
A typical Italian trattoria and fish restaurant, no pizza here, which we actually appreciated, but it’s worth mentioning for those expecting it.

Our kids had gnocchi with meat, which was very good. We ordered the sole fish, caprese and the fish soup, both were delicious and full of flavor.

The restaurant is tucked away from the busy streets, so it’s calm and peaceful. Just be aware that they charge a ‘coperto’ of €2 per person, and also add a service fee to the bill.

If you want to enjoy authentic Italian food at an authentic Italian pace, Alla Madonna is definitely the place to go. Even some gondola drivers were eating here during our visit, which says a lot!

name: Blogger Review
review:
Avoid unless you are looking to spend lots of money on salty and low quality seafood.

Ordered a mussel spaghetti (look at the photo and see if you can spot the mussels) - 18 euro. Food was salty without any mussel fragrance in it. Felt like I’m eating a salty tomato spaghetti.

There is also a cover charge of 3 euro per pax and an additional 10% service charge for the total bill.

name: Thilo Kä
review:
The Spaghetti was served without oil or any kind of sauce - no discount offered, still 18€

The Lasagna looked like somebody threw up on the plate - 17€

What a scam! It was very noisy inside and the service wasn’t friendly. Can not recommend at all!

name: Bratislav Džombić
review:
Tried Trattoria alla Madonna while in Venice for some authentic local cuisine. The black squid with polenta was decent—classic and traditional. The atmosphere has a nostalgic charm, and you get the sense that this place once had a strong reputation. However, it seems to be resting on that past a bit too much.

One downside: they charge both coperto and servizio, which is uncommon even for Venice. That felt a bit excessive. Also, the staff could be more welcoming—service was brisk, almost cold. Overall, not a bad meal, but there’s definitely room for improvement if they want to live up to their name.

name: Chatchapong Samutalai
review: Trattoria alla Madonna is an Italian restaurant that truly shines when it comes to seafood! The dishes are fresh and flavorful, capturing the essence of authentic Italian cuisine. If you’re a seafood lover, this place is a must-visit. The atmosphere is cozy and charming, making it perfect for a delightful dining experience.

name: Cassie Noah
review: We had dinner here the first night in Venice and it set the bar high! I had the seafood pasta and my husband had the carbonara both were equally delicious! The bread and wine were also fantastic. Highly recommend a visit. It is a small space so advise to make a reservation.

name: Leah M
review: Lovely food and cozy seating. We got the bruschetta as a starter then a diavola pizza and carbonara for mains. the tiramisu was also good. friendly staff and quick service. very cosy seating so you’ll be very close to your neighbours but we loved our whole experience!

name: Pinar Oezalp
review: The food was delicious, yet we were sitting super tight with other folks. Some waiters looked somewhat tired of their lives, some friendly, the decoration was nice, we liked the glasses and silverware. It’s ok to drop by and try! Reserve before!

name: Gabriel Brus
review:
Location good.
Atmosphere is really good.
Service is good.
It is heavily focused on Pizzas, but pizzas are very average, pizza base was too crusty and hard to cut. Burrata for starters was good, especially the dried tomatoes.
Would not come back here second time.

We got recommended this place by our hotel who they apparently co operate with and was told, we get some kind of discount. Mentioned it to the waiter in the end and got told, that should have told them at the start and cannot do anymore.
Had to pay the bill, which is fine. Keep in mind, they charge 12% service fee on top!!!

name: J. Ce
review:
The pizza was decent, better-than-average I could get around touristy spots in Italy. Fairly crispy and looked nice. However, I had to wait 30 minutes for it after I ordered.
What also left a bitter taste, they charge 12% "service fee", but then also bring you the bill with "Tips not included" plastered all over it in multiple places...

name: Niamh McCormick
review: We had a lovely dinner at Da Mamo. I had some of the most delicious pasta, it was the Spaghetti alla chitarra with garlic, chilli, burrata and tomatoes. As a vegetarian, this was a delightful find. The atmosphere was perfect, cosy and authentic. Also a delicious aperol spritz! 🍊

name: Irene Zhang
review: I ate at this restaurant 8 years ago and enjoyed the food and wanted to come back to relive the experience. Sadly, food didn't taste the same and as great. The Da Mamo pizza was overly salty due to the salami they used. And the seafood pasta was just okay; nothing to write about. We received bread and packaged breadsticks. Service was fine. I had a resy so was seated quickly. If you choose to eat here, I recommend the resy because it's really small inside with limited seating. I think the waiter was annoyed when I went up to him to pay. He said he would circle back around, and I said, okay, but like right now? We were waiting to pay for a while and just wanted to leave. Not sure what the problem was for a two second transaction. FYI there were no locals dining in this restaurant, at least the time when I went. And any restaurants with a service fee is a red flag for tourist trap. Again, I wanted to go to relive the experience, but won't be back again, unfortunately.

name: Ryan Hughes
review: Superb pizza at a reasonable price. This place is awesome. We visited a decade ago and came back again - ten years later. Some of the pizza is bizarre, but that’s what makes it great!

name: Stacy
review: This was an amazing dinner! The service is just another level! So kind and attentive! Thank you very much! Our last dinner in Venice couldn’t be better! They recommended us a very nice wine and meals! The seabass with strawberries…you should try it! So delicate and delicious! And big thank you for a birthday song for my fiancé, it was such a special moment for us!  You should come here!

name: Philine Cloet
review: I had an amazing dinner at this restaurant! The food was really amazing, especially the ravioli with shrimps. The staff is beyond friendly and welcoming. I would definitely recommend this place & come back the next time I visite Venice.

name: Zoë Lamming
review: Absolutely beautiful seafood! Very small and cosy, staff were excellent- attentive and knew the menu very thoroughly. The menu is small and there are daily dishes based on what seafood they can get locally. I would thoroughly recommend for some delicious local and inventive seafood.

name: Kirill
review: Great place to have a seafood themed dinner. Prices are fine for these dishes. Personal is great but on Wednesday the place was quite empty.

name: Catherine H
review: Brilliant little place on the canal. Lovely waiting staff who were very knowledgeable and helpful. Dishes were perfect and very creative, enjoyed this a lot. Ask for a table by the window to watch the canal life go by.

name: Colin Smith
review: The whole menu is Venetian style seafood and the seafood here was quite good.   The branzino and sea bass both tasted very fresh and had very nice cream/butter/herbacious sauces to go with them. The tiramisu was delicious. The service was friendly and helpful. The location outside was right on one of the waterways. My wife and I thoroughly enjoyed the meal, I'd recommend to anyone.

name: Theo Bell
review: Delicious food and fantastic service. Inventive dishes that change based on what they get from the Rialto Market. Would definitely come back!

name: Julie Snyder
review: Best food we had in Venice by far. Quiet and beautiful setting with just 10 tables inside and ac! You could also sit outside on the canal. The waiter, Diego was incredible. We started our meal with a complimentary glass of presseco. Every dish was fresh and delicious and they were very accommodating of my son’s allergies. Highly suggest going here. Made a reservation in advance although was probably not necessary.

name: Pat Z
review:
Great restaurant in Burano. Shrimp risotto and calamari were both great. Really enjoyed the calamari as it was a perfect amount of breading and had great flavor w lots of meaty pieces.

Came for Lunch on a Sunday and no wait to sit outside (inside was pretty empty). Outside service could use another waiter as I could tell the two working there were very busy.

name: Loo Yeo
review: Da Romano is more than a restaurant: it’s a Burano institution. Four generations of the Barbaro family have run it since Romano turned his bottega into a trattoria around 1920. The walls are crowded with paintings, a legacy of the artists who dined here from the 1930s onwards. The food lives up to the history: a wood-grilled plate of sea bass, bream, monkfish and prawn (8.5/10); a briny-fresh frittura mista of Adriatic fish and vegetables (8/10); and the signature risotto al nero di seppie - deep, poised, umami-rich (8.5/10). Tiramisu was lighter, coffee-shy (7/10). Bussolai biscuits, meringue cake and a rare santonego grappa closed the meal - rooted in Burano, straight from the lagoon.

name: Katja Jenič
review: A charming, classic restaurant with a refined, old-world atmosphere — walls adorned with vintage photographs and paintings, waiters serving with true gentlemanly grace. The menu offered a wide selection of traditional dishes and wines. The risotto, though a bit mild in flavour, was cooked to perfection, while the fish was simply outstanding.

name: Katrina Cordero
review: Wonderful food and experience. The risotto was one of the best we’ve had! A must try. We also had the mixed fish grilled and fried. You can taste the freshness of the food. Delicious all around.

name: JG Fit Singapore
review:
Had the most enjoyable lunch at Italy yet

A great restaurant with 100+ years of history

This particular gentlemen, assisted us for lunch and we really felt the pride and joy he has.

Risotto was good
Lemon Meringue was good
Food was perfect.

Most important the people, would recommend you to come !

name: Danielle Martin
review:
We took a water bus over to the island of Burano to eat at a local restaurant that has been in the family for 4 generations, since 1920. We heard about it from Anothony Bourdain, where he went nuts over their seafood risotto, which has been made the exact same way since they opened, passing the recipe down the family line. When they first opened they just served fishermen and lace makers, but now the island is popular with tourists.

On the walls of the restaurant were many famous people who had eaten there, including Robert De Niro, Keith Richards, Charlie Chaplin, Sylvester Stallone, Jane Fonda, and a very long list of other famous people.

The risotto was pretty good but my favorite part was the lemon cake with meringue, it was without a doubt one of the best things I’ve ever eaten. It might inspire me to make more meringues. The sea bass was good but expensive.

If you’re staying in Venice a few days then you should get the multi day boat pass, it’s a lot easier and more economical.

name: Dávid dr. Pongrácz
review: A great place in the heart of Burano. The trattoria is big but busy, we had to wait on arrival to get a table but it was worth it. Anyone can find a place here, there is a terrace, a stylish and a family-friendly section. The seafood and the artichoke was amazing. Prices are high, but so is the quality. Professional service, friendly staff. Recommended. 👌🏻

name: Yumi Noh
review: A hidden gem on this island - the warm staff made me cared for, the food was unforgettable, and the atmosphere was the perfect mix of elegance and comfort. I truly hope to return one day :)

name: Ilina Dimitrieva
review: Outstanding service and an unforgettable meal! The server was incredibly attentive, always checking in to make sure everything was perfect. We had the mixed seafood, which was absolutely delicious, along with spaghetti meatballs and a mozzarella pizza. Every bite was bursting with flavor — honestly, this was the best meal I’ve had in Venice. We’ve been to more expensive places during our stay, but none of them came close to matching the quality, taste, and service here. This place truly fulfilled every need. Highly recommended! ☺️

name: David Miller
review: As a tourist restaurant my hopes weren't high but the pricing was fair, no scams except encouraging me to get a beer on tap which was more expensive by volume (4cl) than the large bottle I'd asked for (6cl, same price) The food was the most disappointing thing we ate all holiday and I cooked better meals while self catering. My sea bass on the set fish menu was rubbery and my wife's chicken escalope was very dry.

name: Scott Percival
review: We highly recommend an evening at Trattoria Della Maddalena while visiting Venice. Our server, Hewar, was delightfully friendly, knowledgeable, and he cleaned the fish skillfully right in front of us. We devoured the freshly caught sea bass and turbot. You may also choose to finish the night with authentic tiramisu and limoncello, cheers!

name: Borut Zavrašek
review: Most other places were pretty full on saturday evening, so we let ourselves be convinced into eating here by the lady inviting people in. Had aperols and margheritas. Prices aren't horrible and the service was pretty quick, but the pizza was nothing special, they used some very chewy cheese and i couldn't detect any basil on it.

name: Ellen Tan
review:
We went into this restaurants as we thought there may be some locals but realised everyone in there are tourists.
Food was absolutely disappointing. The steak was chewy but what was odd was that the source was very milky. It's like eating steak with thick warm milk.

My husband's lamb racks looked miserable. When it was served, I thought to myself that didn't look good. Sure enough, my husband confirmed it. It was dry and skinny. Feels like they must have gotten the skinniest lamb in the country.

When my husband feedback to the waiter, the waiter replied that he doesn't think the lamb rack is bad. Well, maybe my husband wasn't lucky and for all the lambs that they had cooked which might be beautiful, fact is, waiter should not rebuke that the lamb that my husband had was good unless he took one rack before serving and tasted it to determine that.

The young couple besides our table ordered calamari with chips and when served, they looked disappointed. It was really from a low end chip shop. It just looks so bad.

name: Soumya Ghosh
review: Open till late on weekends. We thought about giving a try on way back. Food was excellent and reasonably priced. Sea food quality and quantity was great. Meat/steak was ok. The main waiter was patient and helpful. Although some info he provided was confusing and contradictory like tap water is not good in Venice or olive oil in Venice may not be flavorful. Forgot our bread as well 😀. Nonetheless, it was good experience and definitely recommend.

name: Andrea
review: Can’t say anything about the food, since we only had drinks. The drinks were a great price and the staff was kind. One of the waiter was truly having a great time, made great jokes and kept an eye out for our needs. The next day he recognised us on the streets again, which shows how much attention he pays to his guests.

name: Libni Munoz
review: Our family of five had dinner here and everything was delicious. We ordered Cozzo e Vongole alla Marinara but substituted for white wine and lemon, Lasagna, Spaghetti Al pomodoro, Spaghetti alla carbonara, and Pizza a la diabla. The waiter was polite and friendly. Everything was great! Also, reasonably priced.

name: Ozge Ustundag
review: A warm, elegant restaurant perfect for a special dinner — we celebrated our anniversary here. The staff were incredibly kind, especially our waiter, who turned out to be married to a Turkish flight attendant and chatted with us warmly. Everything we tried was delicious: oysters, grilled octopus with pumpkin, baby calamari, and crab pasta. Two glasses of white wine, tiramisu, and prosecco to finish — the meal cost €132, and it was worth every cent. Refined yet unpretentious — one of the best dining experiences we had in Venice.

name: Natalie Park
review: I came here because I was looking to have an experience with the fegato alla veneziana AKA venetian style liver and onions and found them highly recommended through my Google research. Honestly, it was a life changing meal - I never knew liver and onions could taste this good. The liver was so soft and flavorful. With that said, I usually do enjoy liver, and my fiance who hates liver tried it and noticed it was good but he still didn't like the texture/ taste of the organ meat as he kept chewing it. My fiance had a pasta dish - perfectly cooked. The table bread was better than it looked, but it doesn't come with any butter or oil, so I used the bread with my delicious dish to soak up all the flavor and enjoy it more fully. I'm not a big dessert person, but the tiramisu was very good also. The service was excellent! We were very lucky to get there last open table without reservations as we rushed to get there right when they opened, but probably would recommend having a reservation ahead of time as we watched them turn away several unreserved customers while we were eating.

name: NIKOLAOS TOMAZINAKIS
review: We had an unforgettable dinner at Vini da Gigio in Cannaregio. The octopus with pumpkin was absolutely delicious, the tagliolini with spider crab was a true revelation, and the linguine Malamocchina style had a perfectly balanced, slightly spicy kiclllpWhat really impressed us was how this meal felt like an escape from the overly touristic side of Venice — here we enjoyed genuine, authentic Italian food that exceeded our expectations. My wife and I were thrilled with everything we tasted. No surprise that locals recommended this restaurant to us — and we couldn’t agree more. Highly recommended!

name: Lola
review:
Had an awesome experience in this typic venetian restaurant.
Ambiance was great and service really amicable.
Food was really good and wine selection is exhaustive.
Perfect restaurant away from the crowds and perfect if you’re looking for an authentic experience.

name: Jure Tuš
review: The most average restaurant I’ve been to in my entire life, and for 80€ per person. Wouldn’t recommend a single thing I had. Maybe the oysters and the wine, but they didn’t really have to do much with those. The service was good though.

name: Francis Gingras
review: We had a wonderful experience. The food was very good, the crab pasta was outstanding. This is a real restaurant owned and operated by a family which is un uncommon in Venice.

name: Lynne Friedli
review: Returning here after more than 30 years, we were delighted that Vini da Gigio is still run by the same family and the food is still wonderful. Everything is done well: sardines in saor, tagliatelle with truffles, the fritti Misti is a triumph - not showy just sparkling fresh and fabulous. Order sole al brachio and they take it off the bone for you in the traditional way. Lovely bread, fairly priced wine and above all the great atmosphere of a well run restaurant that continues to care about food, customers and Venice. One of the best!

name: Alena
review:
A delightful place with an excellent view.

A huge selection of wines and a small food menu. My favorite is the crab pasta! 🍝💔 It's just perfect.

Also, the pasta with broccoli and anchovies was delicious, but the risotto was a bit too firm.
The risotto is served for two, but the portions are quite small.

Pleasant atmosphere and a very beautiful view of the canal from the restaurant.

Service is quite slow.

It's better to book a table in advance; we were lucky to get the last table available, as the place was full.

name: Nasif Siddiqi
review: We were suggested to come to this restaurant after our show by the Gritti concierge as it was close by and would be open until later in the evening. After our show we walked passed many open restaurants to get to Do Forni and wondered why he had sent us here rather than one of the others. We soon found out! From the moment we arrived the entire staff welcomed us and provided exceptional service. The food was spectacular. I had the lobster linguine which was the best lobster dish I’ve ever eaten. The sea bream was also amazing as were the oysters and the tiramisu. All exceptional quality and taste. Thank you for a wonderful meal!

name: Hannah Lang
review: Highly Recommended! The food was amazing and the staff are wonderful! We first went and sat in the front of the restaurant for some whiskey and came back later for dinner! They also have Gluten Free Pasta Options!

name: John G
review: The food was excellent!  I had the mixed fried fish and it was incredible.  You could not taste the oil.  It was fried perfectly.

name: MIke Burke
review: The food was amazing, every dish was amazing. The service was next level friendly, knowledgeable and great recommendations. The wine was amazing and priced right. Coming from NY and family originally from Italy we love food and wine.  Felt like home when we walked in the door. I am a retired NYC firefighter and own 5 restaurants in the States, so when we find a gem we like to share it.

name: Ronette S
review:
Trattoria Do Forni

That your hubby, your wife, partner, beastie, best or worst daughter or son, anniversary birthday special occasion, date night or treat yourself and by all means leave those kids at home. I empathize no children for this experience.

Birthday dinner for Hubby at Trattoria Do Forni. He checked menus online to find what he wanted to eat. We spent so much time hopping from restaurant to restaurant it was tiring.

This is fancy like no other restaurant we have been to. Attentive!! We had 5 waiters waiting on us, white and black coat. I am here for it!!

This restaurant is an experience, for real.

Come with your chips and dip (coins and money). They are a little pricey but OMG!!  the food is scrumptious.

I ordered the lamb chops, hubby got the lasagna. I did not get pictures sorry, by the time I remembered I was half way through and my plate was a mess. Trust and believe I don’t lie about my food.

I am not recommending I am telling you to go!  5 stars across the board.

Per person per entry 30 to 40 Euro. We had dessert. Coffee. I got a complimentary dessert. Strawberries and whip cream. Fresh🌺🌺🌺.

Got a few pictures of food around me.

Before I return home I want to say. You can spend Euros or American money. You can exchange or use your card. Most places take both but you came say Euro conversion which is better for you. I have a some Euro left from our other destination so I used the rest here for local shop. I don’t think you need to exchange unless you want to. Or use your card. Happy travels.

name: Luke Han
review:
Delicious Italian food in Venice, Italy. Ate a nice Venetian dinner our first night in Venice after a walking tour of the city. Very good service and attentive servers. Good wine to complement the meal.
Enjoyed eating in the Orient Express dining car.

name: Dimitris Taloumis
review:
From the first bite to the last, the lobster spaghetti was an absolute delight. The freshness of the seafood and the expertly crafted sauce made for an unforgettable meal.

Thank you Mateo!!!

name: AllAttiCO casa vacanze Matera
review: Trattoria Do Forni in Venice offers a serene escape from the bustling streets, perfect for a leisurely lunch or dinner. While we opted for a quick meal, it’s evident that this establishment thrives on a slower pace, allowing guests to savor the flavors of their expertly crafted dishes. Specializing in seafood, every bite is a testament to the freshness and quality of the ingredients. The ambiance exudes charm and tranquility, providing an ideal setting to unwind and indulge in a culinary journey. Whether you’re seeking a quiet escape or a memorable dining experience, Trattoria Do Forni delivers delicious delights that linger on the palate long after the meal is over.

name: Gabriella Winniman
review: Small place, not fancy, but delicious food. My husband and I both had seafood risotto. It was a very generous portion with the seafood perfectly cooked. The service is friendly and efficient.

name: Nadia Guzun
review:
My husband and I ordered pasta and drinks, and a couple of minutes later, we decided to order some grilled vegetables. First, our drinks were served, followed by the pasta. When we asked for the bill, I noticed that the vegetables were included, even though they hadn’t been brought to our table.

After mentioning this to the waiter, he spoke with his colleagues and suggested that we could pay the full price and settle the cost of the vegetables in cash. I agreed, but I was surprised that he didn’t even apologize for the oversight.
