What if I hadn't checked the bill? I would have ended up paying for food that I hadn’t been served. Overall, I would rate the service as 2 stars. The food itself was okay.

name: 顾无语
review: Does not let us seat after we bought ice cream and pizza at the counter. Does not kindly tell us to leave but to shoo us. Waitress tells us to wait for the second person in line when we are the first. Ngl the price was affordable but was  bad. The pizza was still cold after heated.

name: M.A. Bailey
review: This place is small but it had very good food. I had tortellini Bolognese it was really good tasting. I also had a side of spinach which tasted very fresh. My wife had a vegetarian lasagna. It was very good and had a flavor. She also had a salad with tomato. Very nice size.

name: Agata Połomska
review: Pyszne cappuccino i lody 😍

name: Isabel Meyer Asesora de Imagen
review: I loved this restaurant because they serve  fresh fish and it was deliciously prepared. They also have all sorts of pasta but I did not try them therefore I can't an opinion. I can only say that the fish was absolutely delicious.  The vegetables antipasti was good too.  The service is really good and it is a very cozy restaurant.

name: Scott F
review: Incredible food, incredible service, and great vibes. I stood up to ask for the WiFi password and the staff was there to help me before I had my chair pushed in. Very catering to your taste as well with great recommendations. My friend and I only ordered their first recommendation for appetizers and entrees and we both loved it.

name: VanMoTaN
review:
Thought we’d stumbled into a historic trattoria… turns out we just stumbled.
•	€58 for a “mixed fish” plate that tasted like it had been deep fried last week and reheated. Dry, salty, and definitely not the Venetian seafood experience we came for.
•	Dessert? A sad, dry almond cake that felt like punishment, not dolce.
•	Asked for a non-alcoholic beer — apparently a foreign concept here.

Staff weren’t rude, but the food quality and prices are a mismatch of epic proportions. Venice has plenty of authentic gems — do yourself a favor and keep walking.

name: YY
review:
Was looking through the menu when we got approached by the service staff to be seated at the mini-square which was well ventilated - promptly handed the menu which came with English translations, and another menu with multiple photos illustration.

🍲 ordered the “local fish soup” and gotten a seafood soup that comes with mussels and clams which we don’t eat (though there were also fish and prawns too), only realised today that fish actually meant seafood after encountering the same at another restaurant

🐖 the escalope was actually a grilled variant (when I expected a fried one like it was in Lucerne), and the French fries came in grilled potato chunks too - pretty much not what I was used to

Whilst the menu wasn’t really what we had assumed like many other reviews mentioned, there were no complaints on the quality of the food - soup was clean and good and the pork was super tender.

💰 Another point to note like what others mentioned is their lack of clarity in the receipt, with no mentions of the dishes and purely the value amount of each dish and the total bill correspondingly. That said, if you don’t touch the bread and limoncello isn’t served, you won’t be charged the cover fee.

All in all, not the best meal we had in Venice, but still an acceptable place to dine in a nice environment - definitely go with fishes like what the service team mentions though.

name: Jordan Williams
review:
Finally had terrible "tourist" food, and Antica Torre was the "trap". Came here at night,  the place looked nice with a decent ambiance. However the bread was stale, tougher to chew than shoe leather. The food I ordered had the single flavor of tomato and nothing more. The lasagna had the mono-taste of "cheese". The most disappointing aspect was the service. Servers tried to rush us to order and only showed up to drop off the food and bill. The staff missed the most basic hospitality standards with us, and never ONCE came buy to ask how anything was going. I would have loved to order a beer but never got the chance; though the staff floated around other tables like butterflies. The complementary wine was added to the bill as a blank €5 charge, until we brought it up and had it removed.

Without a doubt, I felt like a number and not a guest at Antica Torre. As a tourist, you have hundreds of other restaurants in the immediate area where you can spend your money while you're in Venice; don't let my mistake be yours.

name: William Poon
review:
Little restaurant in the corner of the square next to Rialto Market. They had a ton of seats outside and we sat out at a table for lunch.

We ordered three pizzas and a lasagna. The pizzas were okay, pretty normal. The lasagna was really good though.

Service was good and it was nice being able to sit outside and enjoy the weather and people watch.

The price for food was decent considering it was close to Rialto Market. The soda price was crazy though.

The only thing was they charged a service fee, about 12%, on top of having a cover charge. I told the waiter we hadn’t seen this at other restaurants, which we still haven’t.  I’m just a tourist so I might be wrong but just a heads up.

name: Kemuel Valdes
review:
Whomever gave this place anything less than 5 stars, doesn't know food or came with the previous chef. This is among the best restaurants in Venice. From service to food. Absolutely top quality and flavor.
Do yourself a favor and taste the almond cake.

name: Bruno Santos
review: Restaurant very good Italian food, with fresh products. Good wine list. Excellent terrace. And excellent service from Luca, very attentive and concerned with our satisfaction. I strongly recommend.

name: Sheyla Arango
review: The best: the food and eating outside. The food is super good, and eating in the tables outside with the view of the canal gives all the Venice vibes. Prices are high/medium but house wine is cheap. Loved the risotto. Attention is good and fast.

name: G Whizz
review: We stopped for lunch at this small lovely typical local restaurant with our boat on a friend's suggestion. Good thing we had a reservation as it is a very popular restaurant. What you get here is honest, very tasty home style Venetian food at excellent prices, with good old school service to boot. Everything we have tasted was just right. This one is a keeper.

name: D Lav
review: Absolutely fantastic experience! The food was delicious — every dish was full of flavor and beautifully presented. Try the ravioli with the chefs sauce it was awsome.The atmosphere was great, staff were friendly and attentive without being intrusive. I fully recommend this place.

name: Berlin Gal
review:
We liked the fritto misto, and mixed seafood starter. Despite the simple plating, the baccalà mantecato was very tasty, though the grilled squid could do with a little more love. Basically it was v rustic, bland squid on plain arugula.
It's a convenient spot right before Murano with a great lagoon view. Worth a visit, perhaps with some dishes being stronger than others.

name: Hans Lind
review:
Absolutely fantastic! Calm spot a bit away from the selfie sticks. Marinated sardines first - really really good and then sole as mani course. And a splendid Americano to start with.
One of my best lunches ever.

name: Joseph Edmondson
review: Best seafood we had in Italy!  Very fresh and the grilled cuttlefish was amazing!  All the food and the house wine was delicious.  Very friendly and helpful waitstaff.

name: Gayla Bejczak
review: Grazie! Thank you for a wonderful experience today. Lunch was amazing. Would definitely recommend making this a stop on your Venice vacation. Remember to make reservations, it’s always full!

name: Vruti Sivakumaran
review: Amazing food in a quiet corner. We didn’t make a reservation but managed to get seated after waiting for 30 min. The seafood was really fresh and well flavored, especially the shrimp and squid. The grilled branzino was delicious and the house white wine was amazing. We got the seafood antipasti, the risotto burano, mixed fried fish, grilled branzino and the grilled artichoke.

name: Catherine Chin-Khanijow
review: local and traditional trattoria serving hearty fresh cuisine at very reasonable prices

name: Krzysztof Maziarz
review: I can recommend that restaurant.

name: Siguang Qi
review: Amazing restaurant, delicious food, excellent service, and English friendly

name: Augustin Onaciu
review:
+ excellent local food
+ very nice staff, service was fast
+ cozy and relaxed atmosphere
+ prices are affordable

name: annina ve
review: mmm bono

name: Deane Crabb
review: Robbery.

name: marco giurizzato
review: Top

name: Tomasz Zawadzki
review: Przemiła obsługa i bardzo smaczne domowe jedzenie 👍

name: Miguel Vargas
review:
High prices vs food quality.
I ordered a pasta with Parmesan, 25€. The taste wasn’t great. As the grilled cheese neither.
I don’t feel like the price worth the taste of the food.

The only good thing was the service.

name: Susan Kenard
review: Service and food were excellent. Dined alone outside along the canal watching gondolas and water taxis. Side salad and pasta carbonara with house red were an excellent choice after a busy day. I will be back.

name: Timothy Hiesterman
review: The food was amazing...our kids are kinda picky eaters and they ate everything and loved it.  This is right on the canal with great atmosphere - not too busy and not too noisy.  Gentle breeze with no direct sun.

name: bartika dutta
review: This restaurant has a outdoor seating area right next to a canal, where gondolas glide by and the city’s charm hums in the air. Each dish we tried had fresh ingredients and authentic flavors. I had ordered a pasta with pesto sauce, and the server was nice enough to suggest that I could have it without the pesto sauce. Nevertheless I tried the pasta with the pesto sauce and decided I should have listened to him! The scallops with garlic sauce were tender, perfectly seared and buttery The service was impeccable, attentive and friendly without being intrusive.

name: Maxence Rousson
review:
Please do not make the same MISTAKE. Make sure to AVOID this restaurant, this is a SCAM.
1/ One-liter beer served when asked for a « large » beer: we’re not in Germany and would expect a 50cl pint instead. The least the waiter could do is to clarify this point when taking our order and not smile sarcastically at us when bringing the beers.
2/ There was a SLUG in our ceasar salad and the staff found it « normal in a green salad » - not to mention the industrial dressing and croutons.
3/ The 12% service charge was not explicit and in any case the staff should have offered a discount re 1/ and 2/.
After we shared these comments, the owner accepted to revised downwards the bill from 77.90€ to 67€ but - despite the good location - we still believe the experience was overcharged. Better places can be found in Venice.

name: Julie Dickson
review: 4th time to this place and what a disappointment. Awful service with a surly waiter. Had an average pasta meal - lasagne was ok and carbonara was good, with mixed salad. We ordered a bottle of wine and the waiter didn’t have the one we wanted so the he tried to push another bottle that was almost twice the price. We then asked for something different, he came back with two different wines, arguing they were the same, when they absolutely weren’t. Different labels, different years, different alcohol content. One was a Chianti Classic and was a Tuscan red. After asking to taste them it became clear he had tried to push the cheaper one on us for the same price as the other we ended up choosing. Their wine list doesn’t specify what you’re getting so be careful. We asked questions and the waiter became rude and aggressive when we kept calling him out on his repeated attempts to swindle us. I come to Venice often and won’t be coming back here. A shame because they used to be better.

name: Mike Niebling
review: Obviously there are places everywhere to eat in Venice. We stumbled upon this gem. Food was excellent. Lori’ our server was friendly and gave good advice. Right on the canal. View of crooked church. Highly recommend.

name: Polo Zheng
review: Had a delicious lunch while watching gondolas go by. Try the pizza with seafood on it, so tasty! We enjoyed our time relaxing here and service was great!

name: PRS
review:
Excellent food and excellent service ✨✨

We had 2 pasta dishes (Tagliatelle with Shrimp and Zuchini & Tagliatelle with pana (creme)), and one beef & sauce dish.

We were very satisfied with the quality, quantity, and price of the food. 🙌

No reservation required, and they are open till late (23:00).

The highlight of our visit was our waiter ☀️Albano, who was extremely welcoming, polite,  accommodating and told us all about the amazing artwork displayed inside the restaurant. Amazing guy !

The tiramisu was good, and the overall atmosphere of the restaurant was very cozy.

Ask the waiters to point out their best dishes and you won't be disappointed. ✨✨✨

10/10 recommended

name: Минато Намикадзе
review:
We ordered a pizza here and were shocked. It was a disgusting frozen supermarket pizza, absolutely tasteless! I live in Rome, have been to Naples, Florence, Milan, and Turin, and I can say for sure that real Italian pizza should NEVER be like this.

The waiters kept poking their fingers at the menu, invading personal space, and one of them didn’t even understand either English or Italian. The Aperol Spritz was so watered down that it felt like drinking soda, not even a slight buzz.

And on top of all that, the prices are the same as in a proper osteria or even trattoria, plus they have the nerve to charge €2.5 per person for coperto. Be careful and don’t fall for this scam.

name: Heather Bruemmer
review: We had a lovely meal on the evening of our arrival in Venice with very attentive service. It's a very cosy atmosphere tucked away from the main tourist areas, but still easy to get to. They have a very extensive menu - including one with photos of all the dishes which was helpful to send to our friends who were too tired from traveling to come out. We had assorted starters, pizza and pasta and enjoyed everything. I ordered an Aperol Spritz (very typical for Venice) and he assisted me with a tasting of 3 different kinds of Spritzes. I am eager to visit again next time I am in Venice.

name: Fernando Contreras
review:
I must admit, I was a bit hesitant to try Trattoria Marciana after reading some negative reviews from a few months ago. But, I'm thrilled to report that my experience last night was absolutely perfect!

From the moment I walked in, the ambiance was warm and inviting. The staff were incredibly friendly and attentive, making sure every aspect of our meal was enjoyable. They received my two boys like kings and made them feel very important. Especially one of the main guys there, "Mateo."

The food was the highlight of the evening. Every dish was expertly prepared and bursting with flavor. We had the spaghetti frutti di mare, the seafood tagliata, the spaghetti carbonara, the fried seafood. Our appetizers were the shrimp cocktail and the seafood mix. For dessert, we had the panna cotta and the chocolate cake (be mindful that this last one may have some sort of liquor). Overall, it was simply divine. In general, the presentation was not majestic, but it was not bad either, and lastly, the portions were generous. (see pictures)

I went ahead and commented to Mateo about the reviews, and he mentioned that they are currently operating under new administration, and it's clear that Marciana had undergone a remarkable transformation. Whether it's the improved service, the elevated menu, or the overall atmosphere, they have truly turned things around. I highly recommend giving them another chance—you're in for a delightful experience!

name: Валерия Бурышева
review: They're cheating! The photo doesn't match what they actually brought. We ordered baked fish with potatoes, but they brought boiled fish with zucchini and carrots. As for the salad, it's embarrassing to even think that such a thing could happen in Italy. I just ate boiled fish for 160 euros.

name: Jesse and Rosaliana Smith
review:
Very bad experience! Unfortunately.
Meat wasn’t seasoned at all. Service was bad, very slow. And of course a surprise bill +$100. .

name: A Kanjani
review:
Nice restaurant, we went in as the looks were of an authentic restaurant.

The staff were happy in serving, but the pizza quality was supermarket pizza.

We ordered a lot of different foods including pasta, salads, for the price paid and expected ambiance, the restaurant well way short of delivery sadly.

name: Hady Go
review:
We visited this restaurant recently, and it was one of the worst dining experiences we’ve ever had. All three of us got food poisoning shortly after the meal, which clearly indicates serious hygiene or food handling issues.

To make things worse, the bill was much higher than expected, we were overcharged, and the food was definitely not worth the price. There was absolutely no value for money

name: mirka amrit
review: It's a very clean restaurant,  they serve  great food , lunch specials are offered too.

name: Josh C
review: Nice pizza! Service was great! Staff was friendly and felt really well in this restaurant. Pizza Quattro Stagione was really delicious!

name: Andrés Fdez
review:
Craso error entrar aquí. Comida muy mala y encima a un precio elevado... pedí una lasaña con boloñesa y la pasta era chiclosa, muy pasada, no había a penas carne, poco queso y algo quemado. Mi novia pidió gnoquis 4 quesos y estaban al mismo nivel, malos.
La lasaña que no pude ni comer la mitad 20 euros (ponía 18 en la carta) y los gnoquis 15 o 16 fueron.

name: Opiekun Medyczny
review: Super obsługa, kelner Badi bardzo przyjazny. Polecam serdecznie ❤️‍🔥

name: elena coccato
review: Ho mangiato in questo ristorante una sola volta, forse non è sufficiente per dare un giudizio, ma l'ho trovato un buon locale, dove si mangia bene ed il personale è stato gentile.

name: Jose Maria Sanchez Perez Alonso
review:
Comida mediocre y carísimo
En general como todo Venecia
No pongo foto no merece la pena

name: Iury Mata
review: buonissima

name: Phil Lessa
review:
Mediocre food, dirty and dark ambience with loud Chinese screaming staff

Pros
- Ordered a pizza to go and it came out in about 10mins
- Low prices

Cons
- Staff is constantly yelling back at each other in Chinese
- Dark ambience with sticky tables
- Mediocre food. Our pizza tasted like pre-made, store bought dough, had little to no cheese and was missing components

I would skip this place, there are many other options nearby with better quality.

name: Michael Webster
review: Very fast service, great food options and very friendly. We’ve now been here twice for breakfast and it’s been great. Simple delicious Italian food that is just perfect for any meal.

name: darsh mishra
review: Enjoyed the food, got good amount of options, even considering veg. , atleast the food we ordered was good. The staff is very good and friendly. The atmosphere might not be the best out there, but still great nonetheless

name: Joseph John
review: Very friendly and polite food restaurant near the Venice bus station. The day starts with  Cappuccino and Pistachio Croissant which was delicious.

name: Gayaneh Mankikyan
review: Very good pizzas! Though, the place is not the cleanest and most comfortable one, there's a €1.50 cover charge per person.

name: Camilo Santa Cruz
review: Very good Price-Quality Relation

name: Michael Beckman
review: Great place to have some drinks when you wait for the bus or before you head into Venice to do your sightseeing. The staff is very friendly and the menu and food looks good, even when i didn't try it myself. I was staying with the beer which comes with s small bowl of crisps every time, what makes you wish your bus will be late ;)

name: Iga Gł
review: The food was horrible. Every single dish , and we tried a lot of them was terrible. Pizza had canned mushrooms on , the meat was so hard you could break bricks with it and I won’t even mention backed potatoes that were made out of potatoes boiled at least a week before :/ Prices completely inadequate to the quality of food. In general , the worst food I have eaten in Venice.

name: Toaster Täubling
review: You get instantly seated, which is a good thing and its not to loud in there so you can actually unterstand each other, but the prices are a bit high and the ambiente is very akward. The food is alright, nothing to special. The waiters are very respectless, they took our plates while we still ate.All in all it’s not that good.

name: Anton Nikolov
review:
TAX SERVICE CHARGE!!! 6-7 €
SCAM!!!!!

I visit many pizzerias, but this experience was disappointing.
When I write this review, I’m still frustrated with my recent dinner at [name of pizzeria]. The pizza was at a regular price, and the taste was good, but when the bill came, I discovered that they had added a service charge of 7 euros, which was not mentioned beforehand.
While it’s common practice for restaurants to charge a service fee, it’s important that this be clearly stated when placing the order, or at least on the menu, so customers can make an informed decision. In this case, there was no mention of it on the menu, nor was I told about it by the staff when I ordered. This is very disappointing and creates the feeling that something unreasonable is being "slipped" into the bill without the customer knowing.
Such practices are not only unpleasant but also unacceptable for establishments that care about their reputation and customer trust. If this charge had been disclosed, I would have had the choice to accept it or not, but it was hidden, and that will definitely impact my decision on whether to return in the future.
Recommendation: I would recommend the restaurant’s management to be more transparent about their pricing and to inform customers of any additional charges before placing the order. This will avoid unpleasant situations and improve the overall customer experience.

name: joe none
review: a decent choice for food in venezia.. good house wine. but do get the desert at the suso gelato next to it.

name: Vuk Mitrovic
review: We ordered pizza, and it was terrible. The dough was frozen, and the price of the food and drinks is outrageous. We paid 8 euros for one beer. Aside from that, they also charge you a serving fee of 3 euros per person, which is a lot. They also advertised that they had WiFi, but it wasn't working. The bathroom was also filthy, and you couldn't properly do your business. My warning is to not come here due to the fact that almost everything about this restaurant was terrible.

name: Ilona Nurmela
review: We enjoyed the tiramisu here - it was excellent, but the mille fiori (Italian version of Napoleon cake) was too dry. Coffee was alright but I was disappointed that they don’t serve any tea here. Service was friendly, but a bit slow. Good for an afternoon sweet snack off the main road, but we did not try any of the mains.

name: Edvinas Kanazauskas
review: This place is so just cozy . Service at first looks really not welcoming , but a few minutes later it feels like home. The food it feels like you eat and eat and it never ends just like magic . Atmosphere its cozy like Italy is . And there was a man who came and played some beautiful music . I cant say that this isn’t italy , it just feels like Italy. And for the hate come here at evening it just perfect. The music the service and atmosphere ten out of ten. Love it!!

name: Colin Kleinmann
review: Nice place, but they could do with improving the recipes. Might possibly be a turn/burn with not much attention to quality. Definitely not worth the prices they charged. We gave them a fair shot even though their reviews were a little low. That being said the drinks were good and so was the liver and onions.

name: André Santos
review: Unfortunately, the pizzas are not up to standards (and I mean undercooked dough) and definitely not what you expect for the price. The place probably survives because of the location and the vibes, but could do so much better with a proper pizzaiolo. It's hard to ruin a pizza, but this place seems to achieve the impossible.

name: Natalia QUEREL
review:
Eating in Italy is always an art, but dining at Ristorante da Mario alla Fava is truly like visiting a museum, from the moment we arrived until we said "arrivederci."

The two owners are continuously monitoring each table, ensuring every guest was having a wonderful experience. The maître d'hôtel Massimiliano is the real master chief on the ground, offering excellent suggestions with wonderful Italian humor.

We thoroughly enjoyed all the dishes; all were prepared with high-quality ingredients and beautifully presented. The tiramisu dessert was a perfect ending, served in a half sphere of dark chocolate—a unique presentation I've never seen or tasted anywhere else.

In short, a visit to Venice is incomplete without experiencing your restaurant.

Grazie mille Massimilano

name: Sylvie Armand
review: Run, don’t walk to da Mario alla Fava. The food was SO GOOD! And the owner is super kind. My partner is vegan and he had a ton of great options, as they have a vegan menu. I had omnivore dishes, which were fabulous. Frito misto was delightful, the pasta was solid, and the Bacala was yummy. I enjoyed this place thoroughly. We debated going back our last day for lunch but we ended up eating pizza at our hotel instead because we were lazy. Strong recommend!

name: Emily Harmon
review: Amazing family owned business. Seriously - the owners & the staff are so kind, warm, & welcoming! We have been vegan for several years, so we came for the vegan options & they did not disappoint! Everything was absolutely delicious. 10/10 recommend!

name: Michel Desjardins
review: A nice restaurant, recommended by the concierge of our hotel. Went for lunch. Outside sitting.  Ordered baby octopus (8/10), seabass milllefeuille (7/10) and black ink cuddlefish (calamari family) with polenta (6.5/10).

name: CHRIS L
review: Pricey for the smallest portions ever.  Decent taste, nothing special. Waited a very long time for 10 pieces of pasta.  Maybe they had to catch the smallest shrimps from the sea?  Had to get McDonald’s after.

name: Stuart Hoegner
review: Amazing trattoria -- food, drinks and service all wonderful. Fish definitely worthwhile. One of the best meals I had in Venice.

name: New Orleans Silver and Gold
review: We were recommended this place by our hotel concierge and it did not disappoint! I asked for a recommendation to a restaurant that was traditionally Venice cuisine and this was the spot. My fiancé and I chose exactly what the overall recommendations were on the menu. I added some photos for reference! This place was BOMB! Another really amazing thing about the place was how reservations started at seven. You are not able to walk in off the street, because all of the food is prepared that day. Overall was a wonderful experience.

name: Aric DiPiero
review: If you ever wondered whether there were still unique and exceptional restaurants in Venice, this is the place! We had their Vegan tasting, and it was Michelin-grade amazing. Fantastic service and a great atmosphere completed the experience. We may just return here every night we are in town. Bravo!

name: Olena Lukashenko
review: A very charming family-run place where you’re greeted with a smile and genuine warmth. The staff are friendly, and the seafood is exceptionally fresh and delicious — perfectly cooked and full of flavor. Definitely a place I’d recommend! 🐚✨

name: levar parkinson
review: The Risotto Di Go is pure perfection. The sea bass and scallops were amazing. The service was everything! Slipped us to grab her name but we remember that the dishes we had are her mom’s recipes so I’m assuming she is the owner but nonetheless, if you read this, just know you are an absolute gem and the sweetest person. Thank you! Zoom into the window on the photo of the restaurant to see the nicest hostess smiling for the photo!

name: Ray Miranda
review:
€3 Cover Charge – As explained by the waitress, the cover charge includes the bread placed on the table (whether you eat it or not) and a few other items. Even she, as a local, pays it—so it’s not just a tourist thing, so they claim.

For my appetizer, I ordered an assortment of sliced meats (€18), followed by a house special not listed on the menu: seafood lasagna (€22). I also had a glass of white wine (€9). I initially thought the wine was €5, but wine isn’t exactly my area of expertise—perhaps I ended up with a different pour. Ignorance is bliss?

The meats were solid. The seafood lasagna was tasty, but the portion was noticeably small—especially compared to the generous pasta plates I saw being served around me. To be honest, photos of the lasagna elsewhere looked more impressive than the one I received.

Service was warm and attentive, and the ambiance was pleasant overall.

name: Jim Elsenbeck
review:
Amazing.  This is a dining experience.  The food is incredible, the family that runs the restaurant is wonderful.  We completely enjoyed ourselves and were treated like family.

The homemade tagliolini scampi and risotto were what my wife and I shared, the rest of our group demolished their food and we were too busy eating to get more pictures!

The traditional Burano biscuit was the perfect ending to our meal.

Definitely on my "need to return" list!

name: Nicola Ventre
review: What a wonderful family run restaurant serving fresh seafood from nearby waters.  Each dish is explained in detail with love.  Each dish was fresh with new flavors we never had before.

name: Andrzej Lisowski
review:
Waitress was nice, and seafood spaghetti was also nice.
Unfortunately carbonara was a disaster - it was basically pasta with scrambled eggs and no taste of real carbonara... and it was expensive, I've had much better carbonara for 12 euro, this one was not worth even 8 euro (and it did cost around 16 euro)
Pasta itself felt like something you can get at every store.
Overall the dinner (2 pasta dishes, 1 beer and one drink) cost us 57 euro, but it was all worth maybe 40 euro

name: Sim Oz Y
review: Friendly family run restaurant. Very efficient service. A few sisters run around entertaining guests. All very friendly. The owner is also a fisherman and serves catch of the day as daily specials. We had the baked brim with potatoes. The mussels were super fresh and the vongole had a generous amount of clams. The sauce and flavor was spot on. Wished I had more spaghetti to mop up the sauce. Top 3 meal of my Italian trip.

name: Preeti Varma
review: We had such a lovely lunch here with our two children. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely kind. The food was outstanding - my husband had the fresh catch of the day, recommended by the owner, and it was fresh and perfect. I loved the local preparation of liver, while our kids happily tucked into grilled scampi and a seafood platter. Every dish was delicious, and the atmosphere made us feel so welcome. We left very happy and very much hope to return.

name: L V
review:
A Hidden Gem in Venice – Authentic Food & Wonderful Hospitality
We stumbled upon Trattoria Tosi's by chance while wandering through the old streets of Venice – and what a lucky find it was! Tucked away from the tourist crowds, this place is a true gem. We ordered traditional pasta with mussels and fish, and the food was absolutely delicious – fresh, flavorful, and beautifully cooked. The atmosphere is calm and authentic, and the staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful. A special thank-you to the gentleman (probably in his 40s or 50s) whose kind manners and attentive service really made our evening. He was an absolute star! Highly recommended if you're looking for a genuine Venetian experience.

name: Stefan Hiensch
review: We were here today for dinner, have had some fish and seafood as well as dessert. The food is decent for the price that you pay, however everythingwas delicious. The waiter was very nice and welcoming and capable to give a recommendation in English, but don't expect a written menu in English. The hause wine was ok, and the coffee good. You should definitely come over if you would like to be in a more quite part of Venice, they have a nice garden but you can also sit outside at the street.

name: Howard Marchant
review: Salt is not as salty as the dish served. Wine was good. Horrible dish of ravioli.  Way too much salt . Place is clean. Pizza oven was broken.

name: Dan Iliuță
review: We had a very pleasant experience with the service, extremely friendly and accommodating, would definitely recommend.

name: Mike Gingerich (MikeG)
review: Loved our pizza. Large menu, not just pizza. Based on how good the pizza was I would like to go back and try more of their offerings. Convenient location away from The Tourist. Friendly service. Would have given another star if they were on a canal.

name: Markus Kalgar
review: We liked the place, cause it was not so crowded. The pizza was quite ok. Prices have been quite good for Venice. The service was friendly. The toilets havent been too clean, esp. when you are with kids. That's maybe normal in Italy, but  we will never be ok with it.

name: Kent Galloway
review: I have food allergies to the nightshade family of plants which includes tomatoes potatoes and all the peppers including red and green peppers hot peppers paprika, but excluding black and white pepper. I speak Italian reasonably well and explain to all this to the waiter in Italian and he made some excellent recommendations. We ended up having the cream pasta salmon dish, green salad, bread, cheese and mineral water. It was an excellent meal and the service and help with my food allergies was top-notch. Unfortunately I did not get the name of the waiter that helped me, but he did an excellent job.

name: Greg Niemeyer
review: This is one of the chillest and tastiest restaurants in Venice. Down to earth, friendly, and very authentic. It caters primarily to locals. As a repeat visitor, I feel at home here more every time I come back. Bravo and Grazie!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Wouter van der Veek
review: Absolutely fantastic food! Nothing like any other camping restaurant. We had the scampi and beef, which were both perfectly cooked! Great and friendly staff which makes the night!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Alex Kovács
review: Wasn't there, just in the camp but its still open at 9 pm

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Joanna Andrysiak
review: Mieszkając na campingu wybraliśmy się na kolację - ciepło, smacznie, duże porcje. Obsługa miła i pomocna, mówiąca po angielsku.

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Claudia De Santis
review:
Locale esternamente informale, internamente curato.
Abbiamo cenato con una tagliata e insalata, una zuppa di cozze davvero buona e un fritto misto croccante e patatine !
Avevo puntato un dolce ma ero sazia ! Peccato.
68 euro  in tre. Servizio super veloce e la cameriera che ci ha servito, insieme al ragazzo giovane, sono stati super gentili e devo dire sempre sul pezzo ! Bravi ! Avanti così

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Leonie SCHERER
review:
Hébergée dans le camping avec un groupe de 27 personnes, le restaurant n’a pas hésité a nous accueillir. Tous les plats que nous avons choisis étaient délicieux (mention spéciale pour les pâtes aux fleurs de courgettes et burrata, c’est un délice absolu).
Le service est extrêmement agréable, le personnel est souriant, les plats sont délicieux et copieux, j’aurais aimé tout goûter tellement tous les plats que j’ai vu sortir de la cuisine m’ont donné envie !

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Ute Böhm
review: An unserem letzten Abend in Italien wurden wir herzlich empfangen und bekamen die leckersten Pizzen unserer Tour. Dazu gab es einen leckeren Hauswein und Espresso zum Abschluss. Alles topp, wir können die Pizzeria am Campingplatz guten Gewissens weiter empfehlen.

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Niklas Stenskog
review:
Absolut det sämsta restaurangbesöket jag varit på, jag och min fästmö är här på plats med husbilen på campingen och skulle äta middag. Campingen är superbra och ligger väldigt bra men resturangen är så dålig, väldigt otrevlig personal som inte är serviceminded alls och otrevliga när man försöker betala!
Försökte få betala i ca 20 min och sen fick vi gå till baren och betala efter mycket frågande! Kommer aldrig rekommendera denna resturang till någon då vi äter på flertalet restauranger varje vecka och aldrig blivit mottagna så illa!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: RaWi
review: Die Bedienung war sehr schnell und angenehm. Die guten Pizzen zügig auf dem Tisch und auch heiss. Einzig das Tiramisu hat mich nicht ganz überzeugt. Die Preise sind sehr intressant.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: CRISMA GHOSTINE
review: I am surprised by the high rating, ravioli was overly eggy, the ricotta lacked authenticity, and the artichoke salad was tough and inedible. That said the service was excellent, and the wine outstanding. Wouldn’t recommend the dishes above.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Leah Groat
review: Amazing food! Needed a quick dinner upon arriving late into Venice. Had gnocchi with bolognese and tiramisu for dessert. Couldn’t have been more delicious and the waiter was very patient with my non-Italian!

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Ingrid Bergheden
review: Nice, good food, portion sizes vary a lot depending on what you order (gnocchi was a big portion, tortellini was a very small one).

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: carol tsang
review: Staff was nice but sadly they cannot speak fluent English .. I have to google translate the menu to order my food ...but to be honest..the environment of the restaurant is so comfy and it’s good to have a sip of wine enjoying the dinner with your another half ..food is freshly cook and it’s quite hot when it’s served ..the octopus salad is also amazing ...a bit pricy for dinner but I believe it’s still cheaper than on the island side

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Enrico Carollo
review:
Amazing dinner into this restaurant with typical Venetian heritage cuisine. Optimal raw materials and a delicate-yet-tasty preparation will make your dinning experience superb. Kind service in a quiet and gently surrounding environment.
Will recommend to anyone looking for a special evening, and for sure I'll be back there!

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Janez Nučič
review: Amazing home made pasta. Try the Ravioli with bruscandoli, carletti, agretti sautéed with butter and sage. Amazing.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Sofie
review: We visted Italy in June and this was most definitely one of the best restaurants we ate at during the entire trip. We ordered a bunch of food which turned out way too much, but it was so good that it was difficult to stop eating. Staff was very friendly and accommodating as well, especially since we really don't speak Italian. I would highly recommend this restaurant.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Maria de los Angeles Freire
review: We were two people. We had to ask for the menu because nobody helped us. Then they took a long time to take our order. My husband asked for a plate and I asked for a dessert. The waiter told us that we both should ask plates or he couldn't serve us. The restaurant was almost empty and they prefer not to serve us because we weren't going to ask for everything in the menu!! Unbelievable!! We left. Update.- thanks for your reply, I would never ask for a plate divided in two, my husband wanted a main dish and I wanted a dessert. If your rules are that each person should ask "for full course" it should be informed previously because we have eaten in another restaurants in Italy or trattorias and the service has been excellent without exception. Thanks anyway!

name: David Majinian
review:
Real Local restaurant. Almost no tourists. If you want to taste real Italian cuisine, this the place. Pizzas, calzones are unbelievable. We also ordered Branzino which is most expensive entree -28 euros. Pizzas are from 8-16 euros.
Most expensive wine Pinot Noir 35 euros.
2 local beers , 2 bellinies, branzino and pizza were 77 euros. They not adding Tip like other tourist based restaurants.
They served fresh bread to begin and lemonchellos at the end.
13-15 minutes walk from St Marks square.
Enjoy !

name: hungming lai
review:
Enjoyed the vibe, food, and the weather here! Had a four cheese pizza, green salad, and lasagna. Love the pizza the most! The crust is soft and crunchy at the same time!

We stayed nearby and this is definitely an underrated restaurant! Would recommend to anyone who comes by this area.

name: Deog-Kwan Kim
review: Delicious taste and pretty outside view. cost effectice in viewpoint of taste, good location, kindness.

name: maria.krupskaya
review: In love with this place! We spend all day in Venezia and accidentally found this restaurant only at the end of our journey. We were surprised by the location, Arsenal is so beautiful place 😍 The service, food and local atmosphere were so wonderful (and limoncello as compliment was too 😋). I will highly recommend ‘Da Paolo’ to my friends, who are only planning to visit Venezia. Thank you, guys, you have made our evening perfect! I will also share the restaurant and my Italian journey in Instagram: @marie__kru. Grazie mille! ❤️🙏🏼

name: Sandra Moriarty
review: Our hotel recommended this restaurant for our first meal in Venice. I had the scampi tagliatelle and it was to die for. OMG, amazing. The wine, the location, the service was incredible. We were the only tourists, lots of locals picking up pizzas. When in Venice, you must come to Da Paolo!

name: Andres Molano
review: We’ve been to this restaurant a couple of times before on our previous trips to Venice, but this time was really disappointing. We came in the afternoon, after the lunch rush, just to have a quick coffee and tiramisu, but the staff was incredibly rude. One waiter (a tall guy in his 30s) didn’t even let us sit, even though another one was about to seat us, just because we weren’t planning to order more food. It was supposed to be a quick 20-minute stop — I honestly don’t understand how that could be such a problem for them. Awful experience.

name: Angel Semerjiev
review: Very bad and rude service, order two pizzas which were made with obviously cheap ingredients. Our cola was served poured into a glass and was not even carbonated. Upon complaining about the bad pizza the waiter rudely claimed they make the best pizza in Venice and that we don't know anything about pizza. The mozzarella on it wasn't even real mozzarella and had the consistency of chewing gum, also the pizza was overall entirely deprived of any flavor whatsoever. Would not recommend stay away. We had much better pizza in the are  for 8-10 euros, whereas most pizzas here were 15. A very disappointing restaurant.

name: Sol
review: We really liked the food and the atmosphere, but what I would like to highlight is the attention of the chinese waitress who served us and especially her coffee. We can have coffee anywhere, but a really good coffee is another experience. Thank you for sweetening our day on our trip!

name: Andreea Sarca
review: Tasty lunch deal at only 15 euros! The lady that served us was very friendly and sweet. The food and the wine were tasty. The location is very close to Piazza San Marco

name: Zuzanna Kantor
review:
Delicious pasta with seafood.
I feel like it's one of the less touristic/fancy places. I liked the vibe, much more authentic than most restaurants in venice. Can be a bit too noisy/hectic for some people, though and the interior is not the best. Good food though!

name: Lucas
review:
Worst restaurant ever! Everything was just microwaved! Tomato sauce straight from the can and also very small portions. People who walked by literally started laughing at us. There must be some fake reviews, because this cannot be over 1 star. If you are looking to get salmonella or food poisoning this is the place to be. Don’t waste your money on it.

PS. I gave 1 star because a 0 is not possible.

name: Vivien Travel guide
review:
Mid.
They make a deal of 18€ for both "primo" and "secondo".
The reality is that they serve you small portions (I asked for pasta with mussels and they gave me pasta al pomodoro lol). The seafood was laughable. Literally As big as the palm of my hand.
Paid 18€ for something that is at most 10€. It's a no from me.

name: Alin Tintea
review: Worst italian food...prison like. Bad pasta, bad liver - i guess they served us the dog food. Frozen french fries. Supermarked frozen pizza. Half an hour for service. The sodas were poured from big plastic bottles ( problably opened days before). Crowded, the waiter always shouting. For an hystorical restaurant...very dissapointing. My kids asked me to live this review - you left a very bad taste. Although we were hungry as can be, your food still tasted bad

name: Zhang Aileen
review:
Awful experience, we ordered 3 cappuccino and one pasta, the handwriting bill says 12 euro for pasta and 12 euro for 3 cappuccino and 6 euro for unknown fee (Service fee? Coverage? Idk) then we realized that cappuccino is 3.5euro instead of 4 euro, i reminded their staff and they did corrected the price, but i got yelled ("ok!??")

I paid the money eventho i still dont know what that 6 euro for

Wouldnt recommend tourist go any restaurant that has less tourists.

Pasta we ordered was not bad, but i dont recommend the cappuccino

name: Paulo Barbaresi
review: The Trattoria is close to a busy area which solves the problem of having lunch for tourists. If you know Italian cuisine you realized the quality of the food is medium but they offer a menu which for approx 20 eur you lunch with first, second, and desert.

name: Aurore Viola
review: We entered this restaurant because a blonde waitress was on the street and entreated us to come in, we thought it was welcoming. We sat down, had a drink. We waited for her to come and give us the menus but after 20 minutes we had to call her. There were not so many customers. She showed the menu and my friend chose, I was not ready so she took it for a no and didn't wait for me. She came back and nearly wrenched the menus from us, not even looking at us or listening to our protests. Then another one arrived with the order of my friend. I guess I might have had something later. We were dismayed and my friend decided we should leave though I was ill at ease because the dish had been served. The problem is that she didn't take the time to speak, we're foreigners, she must be used to this in Venice but she didn't respect us at all. And I add that I'm French and my English is good enough, so this isn't even a  problem of language. Or maybe she doesn't speak English at all in such a touristic place???? Her behaviour is a shame for her job. Fortunately, we went to Osteria ae spezie, they were very nice and it was tasty. Don't go there!!!

name: K
review: I guess I had a different experience here. The service and ambience is great. The food was not. Not that it was horrible! I ate it all because I was starving. Maybe I got the wrong thing…. But i definitely would not come back to try again.

name: Imelda C.
review:
We are tourists and we arrived at this restaurant by accident when we were searching for a place to eat as we went on a road trip. The restaurant seems like a local one and mostly of the people were wearing elegant clothes.
The food was good, nothing else to say. We ordered clamps and they were small, so if you decide to order them be ready to struggle to eat them.

name: Water Bottle
review: Do not go if you are a tourist. They treat you terrible and give you a different price for what you get than the local customers. I understand enough Italian to know that they scammed us. Luckily we realized we were being scammed before we ordered so we only ordered one thing. A small plate of pasta a water came out to be 20 euros whereas I saw 2 locals sitting next to me get 3 plates of food each plus wine plus coffee and payed a total of 30 euros. They give the locals the “menu” deal and allows tourists to order it but charges you for their separate prices. In other words they would charge a local 15 euros and could charge a tourist up to 100 euros for the same thing. Disappointing because I lived in Spain for a year and similar restaurants do not scam tourist. Unfortunately these Italians are backwards minded and treat tourists awfully and makes them not want to return.

name: Alvise Fontana Z
review: Fish fish fish old style trattoria with one big room and squares tables....they don't make them any more ! It is not fancy  you probably do not notice it from the outside but the menu is very good. Local Venetian style dishes. The quality of the raw material is good likewise the service. Yes go for it. 5 star food 3 star place...make it 4

name: G LENCER
review:
They cheated us with the price. a shame. Don't go or at least ask twice about the price.
We order the daily menu.  They served it us, but when we asked for the bill,  they said that they forgot that there was no menu on Saturdays and we had to pay everything separated. We paid three times more that they said us at the beginning.  And what could we do? They take advantage that you are from outside. .... incredible

name: Shane Davis
review: Open until 11 unless you're American then your turned away at 930 on a Saturday with 10-15 tables currently seated and even after explaining you just want dessert. I hope some day they visit America and realize they will always receive the same service as everone else.

name: James Maitland-Smith
review: Superb service with a smile - the seafood is some of the best in the area and well worth the drive from Venice.

name: Zak Kohler
review: Incredible. Full of locals. Everyone was really friendly and the pizza was great. Go here rather than the Ristorante.

name: Shiv Shetty
review: I totally love their pizza and the pasta, and dined here twice. The atmosphere is decent. They charge 2,5 euro per person (including kids) as coperto (fixed cover charge) so for me it was 7,5 euro of additional surprise in the total bill. Can't help, it's similar in most restaurant here. I had funghi pizza and araebita pasta. Btw, funghi is mushroom in Italian:)

name: Marcelo Fraia
review:
The atmosphere was good.And the lasagna was too cheesy,there was not enough meat . But my experience was a lot better than the other reviews.I also agree  that the service is good
In the other hand there is not kids menu . Plus I didn't see any vegan food but on the door it said "pizza and pasta able to have no gluten". Overall it was a good experience and I would like to eat here again.

name: V7890 K
review: Great tasty food and quick service. Pasta bolonese was very tasty and house wine superb.

name: Michael Saveljev
review: Very unpleasant experience in this restaurant. Waiter is so rude, doesn’t really know the details of what he offers, can’t remember the order correctly, then blames us for it, moreover follows us outside shouting “blessings”. We paid in full, don’t see a reason for this behaviour. No apologies were offered. I didn’t find this to be regular in Venice, as we dined in 4-5 different restaurants (all with reviews less than 4, found them very nice) and I left reviews.

name: Devin Wadkins
review: Our AirB&B was directly across from this restaurant, so it was very convenient for us. The only 2 staff we ever saw was a Man and his Fiance. They were both really nice and spoke good English. The food itself was great. We had a variety of things at our times there. We had pizza, pasta, seafood, all of it was great. The prices were also very reasonable for what you got. The sea bass for example, was less than 10€ and fed 2 people. Overall, this restaurant was great, the staff were amazing, and for me, it was one of the most memorable places in Venice.

name: Wes
review: Worst experience I’ve ever had in Italy. The waiter was mentally unstable. He was extremely condescending and kept telling us “this isn’t the Olive Garden.” Asked for a basket of bread and he responded “this isn’t America, what do you think? How about you pay for some apprizers..” Ended up walking out and the waiter followed us into the street screaming at us. My parents are in their 70s and extremely kind. Do not go to this place.

name: Raul Chavez
review:
Been in Venice for two days and have eaten here twice. The food is amazing. The customer service is great.

Highly recommend the shrimp scampi. Wine was amazing and our waiter brought us a lemon cello. Amazing time and amazing food.

Prices are way better than in the Saint Mark square. Way better service and food by far

Maximus our server was awesome

name: Javi Dub
review:
Nice place ,, nice music .. very friendly waiter … food arrived fast and it was tasty !!!!😋

I had a  decent gluten free pizza and the rest of the group were happy with their choices

Surprise with the bill .. beers 11 euros .. and 5 euros extra for the no gluten pizza

name: mirka amrit
review: It's a very clean restaurant,  they serve  great food , lunch specials are offered too.

name: Josh C
review: Nice pizza! Service was great! Staff was friendly and felt really well in this restaurant. Pizza Quattro Stagione was really delicious!

name: Andrés Fdez
review:
Craso error entrar aquí. Comida muy mala y encima a un precio elevado... pedí una lasaña con boloñesa y la pasta era chiclosa, muy pasada, no había a penas carne, poco queso y algo quemado. Mi novia pidió gnoquis 4 quesos y estaban al mismo nivel, malos.
La lasaña que no pude ni comer la mitad 20 euros (ponía 18 en la carta) y los gnoquis 15 o 16 fueron.

name: Opiekun Medyczny
review: Super obsługa, kelner Badi bardzo przyjazny. Polecam serdecznie ❤️‍🔥

name: elena coccato
review: Ho mangiato in questo ristorante una sola volta, forse non è sufficiente per dare un giudizio, ma l'ho trovato un buon locale, dove si mangia bene ed il personale è stato gentile.

name: Jose Maria Sanchez Perez Alonso
review:
Comida mediocre y carísimo
En general como todo Venecia
No pongo foto no merece la pena

name: Iury Mata
review: buonissima

name: Toaster Täubling
review: You get instantly seated, which is a good thing and its not to loud in there so you can actually unterstand each other, but the prices are a bit high and the ambiente is very akward. The food is alright, nothing to special. The waiters are very respectless, they took our plates while we still ate.All in all it’s not that good.

name: Anton Nikolov
review:
TAX SERVICE CHARGE!!! 6-7 €
SCAM!!!!!

I visit many pizzerias, but this experience was disappointing.
When I write this review, I’m still frustrated with my recent dinner at [name of pizzeria]. The pizza was at a regular price, and the taste was good, but when the bill came, I discovered that they had added a service charge of 7 euros, which was not mentioned beforehand.
While it’s common practice for restaurants to charge a service fee, it’s important that this be clearly stated when placing the order, or at least on the menu, so customers can make an informed decision. In this case, there was no mention of it on the menu, nor was I told about it by the staff when I ordered. This is very disappointing and creates the feeling that something unreasonable is being "slipped" into the bill without the customer knowing.
Such practices are not only unpleasant but also unacceptable for establishments that care about their reputation and customer trust. If this charge had been disclosed, I would have had the choice to accept it or not, but it was hidden, and that will definitely impact my decision on whether to return in the future.
Recommendation: I would recommend the restaurant’s management to be more transparent about their pricing and to inform customers of any additional charges before placing the order. This will avoid unpleasant situations and improve the overall customer experience.

name: joe none
review: a decent choice for food in venezia.. good house wine. but do get the desert at the suso gelato next to it.

name: Vuk Mitrovic
review: We ordered pizza, and it was terrible. The dough was frozen, and the price of the food and drinks is outrageous. We paid 8 euros for one beer. Aside from that, they also charge you a serving fee of 3 euros per person, which is a lot. They also advertised that they had WiFi, but it wasn't working. The bathroom was also filthy, and you couldn't properly do your business. My warning is to not come here due to the fact that almost everything about this restaurant was terrible.

name: Ilona Nurmela
review: We enjoyed the tiramisu here - it was excellent, but the mille fiori (Italian version of Napoleon cake) was too dry. Coffee was alright but I was disappointed that they don’t serve any tea here. Service was friendly, but a bit slow. Good for an afternoon sweet snack off the main road, but we did not try any of the mains.

name: Edvinas Kanazauskas
review: This place is so just cozy . Service at first looks really not welcoming , but a few minutes later it feels like home. The food it feels like you eat and eat and it never ends just like magic . Atmosphere its cozy like Italy is . And there was a man who came and played some beautiful music . I cant say that this isn’t italy , it just feels like Italy. And for the hate come here at evening it just perfect. The music the service and atmosphere ten out of ten. Love it!!

name: Colin Kleinmann
review: Nice place, but they could do with improving the recipes. Might possibly be a turn/burn with not much attention to quality. Definitely not worth the prices they charged. We gave them a fair shot even though their reviews were a little low. That being said the drinks were good and so was the liver and onions.

name: André Santos
review: Unfortunately, the pizzas are not up to standards (and I mean undercooked dough) and definitely not what you expect for the price. The place probably survives because of the location and the vibes, but could do so much better with a proper pizzaiolo. It's hard to ruin a pizza, but this place seems to achieve the impossible.

name: Natalia QUEREL
review:
Eating in Italy is always an art, but dining at Ristorante da Mario alla Fava is truly like visiting a museum, from the moment we arrived until we said "arrivederci."

The two owners are continuously monitoring each table, ensuring every guest was having a wonderful experience. The maître d'hôtel Massimiliano is the real master chief on the ground, offering excellent suggestions with wonderful Italian humor.

We thoroughly enjoyed all the dishes; all were prepared with high-quality ingredients and beautifully presented. The tiramisu dessert was a perfect ending, served in a half sphere of dark chocolate—a unique presentation I've never seen or tasted anywhere else.

In short, a visit to Venice is incomplete without experiencing your restaurant.

Grazie mille Massimilano

name: Sylvie Armand
review: Run, don’t walk to da Mario alla Fava. The food was SO GOOD! And the owner is super kind. My partner is vegan and he had a ton of great options, as they have a vegan menu. I had omnivore dishes, which were fabulous. Frito misto was delightful, the pasta was solid, and the Bacala was yummy. I enjoyed this place thoroughly. We debated going back our last day for lunch but we ended up eating pizza at our hotel instead because we were lazy. Strong recommend!

name: Emily Harmon
review: Amazing family owned business. Seriously - the owners & the staff are so kind, warm, & welcoming! We have been vegan for several years, so we came for the vegan options & they did not disappoint! Everything was absolutely delicious. 10/10 recommend!

name: Michel Desjardins
review: A nice restaurant, recommended by the concierge of our hotel. Went for lunch. Outside sitting.  Ordered baby octopus (8/10), seabass milllefeuille (7/10) and black ink cuddlefish (calamari family) with polenta (6.5/10).

name: CHRIS L
review: Pricey for the smallest portions ever.  Decent taste, nothing special. Waited a very long time for 10 pieces of pasta.  Maybe they had to catch the smallest shrimps from the sea?  Had to get McDonald’s after.

name: Stuart Hoegner
review: Amazing trattoria -- food, drinks and service all wonderful. Fish definitely worthwhile. One of the best meals I had in Venice.

name: New Orleans Silver and Gold
review: We were recommended this place by our hotel concierge and it did not disappoint! I asked for a recommendation to a restaurant that was traditionally Venice cuisine and this was the spot. My fiancé and I chose exactly what the overall recommendations were on the menu. I added some photos for reference! This place was BOMB! Another really amazing thing about the place was how reservations started at seven. You are not able to walk in off the street, because all of the food is prepared that day. Overall was a wonderful experience.

name: Aric DiPiero
review: If you ever wondered whether there were still unique and exceptional restaurants in Venice, this is the place! We had their Vegan tasting, and it was Michelin-grade amazing. Fantastic service and a great atmosphere completed the experience. We may just return here every night we are in town. Bravo!

name: Olena Lukashenko
review: A very charming family-run place where you’re greeted with a smile and genuine warmth. The staff are friendly, and the seafood is exceptionally fresh and delicious — perfectly cooked and full of flavor. Definitely a place I’d recommend! 🐚✨

name: levar parkinson
review: The Risotto Di Go is pure perfection. The sea bass and scallops were amazing. The service was everything! Slipped us to grab her name but we remember that the dishes we had are her mom’s recipes so I’m assuming she is the owner but nonetheless, if you read this, just know you are an absolute gem and the sweetest person. Thank you! Zoom into the window on the photo of the restaurant to see the nicest hostess smiling for the photo!

name: Ray Miranda
review:
€3 Cover Charge – As explained by the waitress, the cover charge includes the bread placed on the table (whether you eat it or not) and a few other items. Even she, as a local, pays it—so it’s not just a tourist thing, so they claim.

For my appetizer, I ordered an assortment of sliced meats (€18), followed by a house special not listed on the menu: seafood lasagna (€22). I also had a glass of white wine (€9). I initially thought the wine was €5, but wine isn’t exactly my area of expertise—perhaps I ended up with a different pour. Ignorance is bliss?

The meats were solid. The seafood lasagna was tasty, but the portion was noticeably small—especially compared to the generous pasta plates I saw being served around me. To be honest, photos of the lasagna elsewhere looked more impressive than the one I received.

Service was warm and attentive, and the ambiance was pleasant overall.

name: Jim Elsenbeck
review:
Amazing.  This is a dining experience.  The food is incredible, the family that runs the restaurant is wonderful.  We completely enjoyed ourselves and were treated like family.

The homemade tagliolini scampi and risotto were what my wife and I shared, the rest of our group demolished their food and we were too busy eating to get more pictures!

The traditional Burano biscuit was the perfect ending to our meal.

Definitely on my "need to return" list!

name: Nicola Ventre
review: What a wonderful family run restaurant serving fresh seafood from nearby waters.  Each dish is explained in detail with love.  Each dish was fresh with new flavors we never had before.

name: Andrzej Lisowski
review:
Waitress was nice, and seafood spaghetti was also nice.
Unfortunately carbonara was a disaster - it was basically pasta with scrambled eggs and no taste of real carbonara... and it was expensive, I've had much better carbonara for 12 euro, this one was not worth even 8 euro (and it did cost around 16 euro)
Pasta itself felt like something you can get at every store.
Overall the dinner (2 pasta dishes, 1 beer and one drink) cost us 57 euro, but it was all worth maybe 40 euro

name: Sim Oz Y
review: Friendly family run restaurant. Very efficient service. A few sisters run around entertaining guests. All very friendly. The owner is also a fisherman and serves catch of the day as daily specials. We had the baked brim with potatoes. The mussels were super fresh and the vongole had a generous amount of clams. The sauce and flavor was spot on. Wished I had more spaghetti to mop up the sauce. Top 3 meal of my Italian trip.

name: Preeti Varma
review: We had such a lovely lunch here with our two children. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely kind. The food was outstanding - my husband had the fresh catch of the day, recommended by the owner, and it was fresh and perfect. I loved the local preparation of liver, while our kids happily tucked into grilled scampi and a seafood platter. Every dish was delicious, and the atmosphere made us feel so welcome. We left very happy and very much hope to return.

name: L V
review:
A Hidden Gem in Venice – Authentic Food & Wonderful Hospitality
We stumbled upon Trattoria Tosi's by chance while wandering through the old streets of Venice – and what a lucky find it was! Tucked away from the tourist crowds, this place is a true gem. We ordered traditional pasta with mussels and fish, and the food was absolutely delicious – fresh, flavorful, and beautifully cooked. The atmosphere is calm and authentic, and the staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful. A special thank-you to the gentleman (probably in his 40s or 50s) whose kind manners and attentive service really made our evening. He was an absolute star! Highly recommended if you're looking for a genuine Venetian experience.

name: Stefan Hiensch
review: We were here today for dinner, have had some fish and seafood as well as dessert. The food is decent for the price that you pay, however everythingwas delicious. The waiter was very nice and welcoming and capable to give a recommendation in English, but don't expect a written menu in English. The hause wine was ok, and the coffee good. You should definitely come over if you would like to be in a more quite part of Venice, they have a nice garden but you can also sit outside at the street.

name: Howard Marchant
review: Salt is not as salty as the dish served. Wine was good. Horrible dish of ravioli.  Way too much salt . Place is clean. Pizza oven was broken.

name: Dan Iliuță
review: We had a very pleasant experience with the service, extremely friendly and accommodating, would definitely recommend.

name: Mike Gingerich (MikeG)
review: Loved our pizza. Large menu, not just pizza. Based on how good the pizza was I would like to go back and try more of their offerings. Convenient location away from The Tourist. Friendly service. Would have given another star if they were on a canal.

name: Markus Kalgar
review: We liked the place, cause it was not so crowded. The pizza was quite ok. Prices have been quite good for Venice. The service was friendly. The toilets havent been too clean, esp. when you are with kids. That's maybe normal in Italy, but  we will never be ok with it.

name: Kent Galloway
review: I have food allergies to the nightshade family of plants which includes tomatoes potatoes and all the peppers including red and green peppers hot peppers paprika, but excluding black and white pepper. I speak Italian reasonably well and explain to all this to the waiter in Italian and he made some excellent recommendations. We ended up having the cream pasta salmon dish, green salad, bread, cheese and mineral water. It was an excellent meal and the service and help with my food allergies was top-notch. Unfortunately I did not get the name of the waiter that helped me, but he did an excellent job.

name: Greg Niemeyer
review: This is one of the chillest and tastiest restaurants in Venice. Down to earth, friendly, and very authentic. It caters primarily to locals. As a repeat visitor, I feel at home here more every time I come back. Bravo and Grazie!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Wouter van der Veek
review: Absolutely fantastic food! Nothing like any other camping restaurant. We had the scampi and beef, which were both perfectly cooked! Great and friendly staff which makes the night!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Alex Kovács
review: Wasn't there, just in the camp but its still open at 9 pm

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Joanna Andrysiak
review: Mieszkając na campingu wybraliśmy się na kolację - ciepło, smacznie, duże porcje. Obsługa miła i pomocna, mówiąca po angielsku.

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Claudia De Santis
review:
Locale esternamente informale, internamente curato.
Abbiamo cenato con una tagliata e insalata, una zuppa di cozze davvero buona e un fritto misto croccante e patatine !
Avevo puntato un dolce ma ero sazia ! Peccato.
68 euro  in tre. Servizio super veloce e la cameriera che ci ha servito, insieme al ragazzo giovane, sono stati super gentili e devo dire sempre sul pezzo ! Bravi ! Avanti così

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Leonie SCHERER
review:
Hébergée dans le camping avec un groupe de 27 personnes, le restaurant n’a pas hésité a nous accueillir. Tous les plats que nous avons choisis étaient délicieux (mention spéciale pour les pâtes aux fleurs de courgettes et burrata, c’est un délice absolu).
Le service est extrêmement agréable, le personnel est souriant, les plats sont délicieux et copieux, j’aurais aimé tout goûter tellement tous les plats que j’ai vu sortir de la cuisine m’ont donné envie !

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Ute Böhm
review: An unserem letzten Abend in Italien wurden wir herzlich empfangen und bekamen die leckersten Pizzen unserer Tour. Dazu gab es einen leckeren Hauswein und Espresso zum Abschluss. Alles topp, wir können die Pizzeria am Campingplatz guten Gewissens weiter empfehlen.

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: Niklas Stenskog
review:
Absolut det sämsta restaurangbesöket jag varit på, jag och min fästmö är här på plats med husbilen på campingen och skulle äta middag. Campingen är superbra och ligger väldigt bra men resturangen är så dålig, väldigt otrevlig personal som inte är serviceminded alls och otrevliga när man försöker betala!
Försökte få betala i ca 20 min och sen fick vi gå till baren och betala efter mycket frågande! Kommer aldrig rekommendera denna resturang till någon då vi äter på flertalet restauranger varje vecka och aldrig blivit mottagna så illa!

place_title: Trattoria Bar Pizzeria at Camping Rialto
name: RaWi
review: Die Bedienung war sehr schnell und angenehm. Die guten Pizzen zügig auf dem Tisch und auch heiss. Einzig das Tiramisu hat mich nicht ganz überzeugt. Die Preise sind sehr intressant.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: CRISMA GHOSTINE
review: I am surprised by the high rating, ravioli was overly eggy, the ricotta lacked authenticity, and the artichoke salad was tough and inedible. That said the service was excellent, and the wine outstanding. Wouldn’t recommend the dishes above.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Leah Groat
review: Amazing food! Needed a quick dinner upon arriving late into Venice. Had gnocchi with bolognese and tiramisu for dessert. Couldn’t have been more delicious and the waiter was very patient with my non-Italian!

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Ingrid Bergheden
review: Nice, good food, portion sizes vary a lot depending on what you order (gnocchi was a big portion, tortellini was a very small one).

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: carol tsang
review: Staff was nice but sadly they cannot speak fluent English .. I have to google translate the menu to order my food ...but to be honest..the environment of the restaurant is so comfy and it’s good to have a sip of wine enjoying the dinner with your another half ..food is freshly cook and it’s quite hot when it’s served ..the octopus salad is also amazing ...a bit pricy for dinner but I believe it’s still cheaper than on the island side

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Enrico Carollo
review:
Amazing dinner into this restaurant with typical Venetian heritage cuisine. Optimal raw materials and a delicate-yet-tasty preparation will make your dinning experience superb. Kind service in a quiet and gently surrounding environment.
Will recommend to anyone looking for a special evening, and for sure I'll be back there!

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Janez Nučič
review: Amazing home made pasta. Try the Ravioli with bruscandoli, carletti, agretti sautéed with butter and sage. Amazing.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Sofie
review: We visted Italy in June and this was most definitely one of the best restaurants we ate at during the entire trip. We ordered a bunch of food which turned out way too much, but it was so good that it was difficult to stop eating. Staff was very friendly and accommodating as well, especially since we really don't speak Italian. I would highly recommend this restaurant.

place_title: Da Vittoria La Trattoria - Ristorante Campalto (Venezia)
name: Maria de los Angeles Freire
review: We were two people. We had to ask for the menu because nobody helped us. Then they took a long time to take our order. My husband asked for a plate and I asked for a dessert. The waiter told us that we both should ask plates or he couldn't serve us. The restaurant was almost empty and they prefer not to serve us because we weren't going to ask for everything in the menu!! Unbelievable!! We left. Update.- thanks for your reply, I would never ask for a plate divided in two, my husband wanted a main dish and I wanted a dessert. If your rules are that each person should ask "for full course" it should be informed previously because we have eaten in another restaurants in Italy or trattorias and the service has been excellent without exception. Thanks anyway!

name: David Majinian
review:
Real Local restaurant. Almost no tourists. If you want to taste real Italian cuisine, this the place. Pizzas, calzones are unbelievable. We also ordered Branzino which is most expensive entree -28 euros. Pizzas are from 8-16 euros.
Most expensive wine Pinot Noir 35 euros.
2 local beers , 2 bellinies, branzino and pizza were 77 euros. They not adding Tip like other tourist based restaurants.
They served fresh bread to begin and lemonchellos at the end.
13-15 minutes walk from St Marks square.
Enjoy !

name: hungming lai
review:
Enjoyed the vibe, food, and the weather here! Had a four cheese pizza, green salad, and lasagna. Love the pizza the most! The crust is soft and crunchy at the same time!

We stayed nearby and this is definitely an underrated restaurant! Would recommend to anyone who comes by this area.

name: Deog-Kwan Kim
review: Delicious taste and pretty outside view. cost effectice in viewpoint of taste, good location, kindness.

name: maria.krupskaya
review: In love with this place! We spend all day in Venezia and accidentally found this restaurant only at the end of our journey. We were surprised by the location, Arsenal is so beautiful place 😍 The service, food and local atmosphere were so wonderful (and limoncello as compliment was too 😋). I will highly recommend ‘Da Paolo’ to my friends, who are only planning to visit Venezia. Thank you, guys, you have made our evening perfect! I will also share the restaurant and my Italian journey in Instagram: @marie__kru. Grazie mille! ❤️🙏🏼

name: Sandra Moriarty
review: Our hotel recommended this restaurant for our first meal in Venice. I had the scampi tagliatelle and it was to die for. OMG, amazing. The wine, the location, the service was incredible. We were the only tourists, lots of locals picking up pizzas. When in Venice, you must come to Da Paolo!

name: Andres Molano
review: We’ve been to this restaurant a couple of times before on our previous trips to Venice, but this time was really disappointing. We came in the afternoon, after the lunch rush, just to have a quick coffee and tiramisu, but the staff was incredibly rude. One waiter (a tall guy in his 30s) didn’t even let us sit, even though another one was about to seat us, just because we weren’t planning to order more food. It was supposed to be a quick 20-minute stop — I honestly don’t understand how that could be such a problem for them. Awful experience.

name: Angel Semerjiev
review: Very bad and rude service, order two pizzas which were made with obviously cheap ingredients. Our cola was served poured into a glass and was not even carbonated. Upon complaining about the bad pizza the waiter rudely claimed they make the best pizza in Venice and that we don't know anything about pizza. The mozzarella on it wasn't even real mozzarella and had the consistency of chewing gum, also the pizza was overall entirely deprived of any flavor whatsoever. Would not recommend stay away. We had much better pizza in the are  for 8-10 euros, whereas most pizzas here were 15. A very disappointing restaurant.

name: Sol
review: We really liked the food and the atmosphere, but what I would like to highlight is the attention of the chinese waitress who served us and especially her coffee. We can have coffee anywhere, but a really good coffee is another experience. Thank you for sweetening our day on our trip!

name: Andreea Sarca
review: Tasty lunch deal at only 15 euros! The lady that served us was very friendly and sweet. The food and the wine were tasty. The location is very close to Piazza San Marco

name: Zuzanna Kantor
review:
Delicious pasta with seafood.
I feel like it's one of the less touristic/fancy places. I liked the vibe, much more authentic than most restaurants in venice. Can be a bit too noisy/hectic for some people, though and the interior is not the best. Good food though!

name: Lucas
review:
Worst restaurant ever! Everything was just microwaved! Tomato sauce straight from the can and also very small portions. People who walked by literally started laughing at us. There must be some fake reviews, because this cannot be over 1 star. If you are looking to get salmonella or food poisoning this is the place to be. Don’t waste your money on it.

PS. I gave 1 star because a 0 is not possible.

name: Vivien Travel guide
review:
Mid.
They make a deal of 18€ for both "primo" and "secondo".
