review: Excellent food! Great ravioli stuffed woth buratta cheese, delcious salad with feta and nectarines ... Friendly and fast service!

name: Bahattin Çiniç
review:
We had a salty croissant, a focaccia sandwich, and two sweet croissants.
Everything was great! The focaccia sandwich was the best one, but the others were also really good.

name: Jože Habinc
review: All three pizzas we tried tasted great, the service was solid and the price, considering the location, was also good. However, there were a lot of leftovers on the tables (but everyone could clean up after themselves, as they have trash cans installed), the pigeons between the tables were not pleasant to watch, and what bothered me the most was the strong operation of the air conditioners and ceiling fans. They really overdo it with the cooling.

name: Sarah Petry
review: We are staying in Venice and wanted a nice easy dinner. We found Farini, they were friendly and welcoming to some tourists with little Italian. We ordered and range of pizzas and the boys ordered some tarts for dessert.

name: Bhawana Agarwal
review:
🌟 5 stars

One of the best pizza places you can try—an absolute hidden gem! The pizza is a must-have, even if you’re not hungry. It’s that good! A total steal for the quality you get. Perfect for dine-in or takeaway, and the coffee is just as amazing. Friendly and welcoming staff make the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit—Bon appétit! 🍕

name: Moji gh
review: We ordered two caffè lattes and two croissants. The coffee was average but acceptable compared to other places in Italy. The croissants were fresh and tasty. The main downside was the service — the staff member who served us was unfriendly and unwelcoming.

name: Ari Sastre
review: My uncle says that the best pizza starts with the crust, and Farini's crust is superb. Simplistic homegrown ingredients combine together to make a pizza that you think would be found in an expensive celebrity chain. Prices are affordable and reasonable too.

name: Kaitlyn Affuso
review:
The BEST pizza I had while in Italy.
Went to Venice, Florence, and Rome and this was by FAR my favorite. We visited several times while we were in Venice for 3.5 days.

Highly reccommend. Get several different slices and share because they're all delicious!

name: CCG G
review: Good place for croissants and fresh yogurt with fresh fruit and granola. The coffee was honestly one of the grossest coffee ups I’ve ever tasted - it was very burnt I just threw it away. Place is small with limited seating so it’s chaotic and not a good place to sit and eat. Highly recommend if you’re craving yogurt though.

name: Facundo Cesa
review:
Our experience wasn't as bad as others I've read. They didn't try to con us at least.

But it's true, this was the absolute worst cannolo in the world. If by accident you try this disgusting thing filled with a sugary hydrogenated vegetal crap, please, don't believe you've tried an ACTUAL cannolo.

The waitress didn't understand our Italian. Which, come on... Isn't THAT bad...

Maybe Italian wasn't her first language either. Maybe she comes from a country without formaggio grattugiato or smiles.

name: Joana Pack Melo Sousa
review: The worst coffe shop ever!! The taste of the cannolli was greasy. The price of the cappuccino outside says 3,50. They charge 6,00 inside + 2 euros of service. They were rude when we asked about the price.

name: Mitch Tornquist
review: DO NOT be fooled by the great location and beautiful window display. Absolute worst cannolo I’ve ever had in my life. In fact I’m not sure how they could have made it any worse. The filling tasted like icing and sugar. Sickeningly sweet. I did not finish it, and that’s crazy, cause I eat everything.

name: F.M. Zowczak
review: Terrible service, you have to pay table cover fee - 2€ per person (even though you’ve ordered 2 small cookies). Food is very poor quality. Cookies are extremely sweet without any flavor (it’s so sweet that you want to vomit). Toilets are not clean, tables are placed to close to each other so you can’t barely move. Do not recommend…

name: Giovanni Balzi
review: Don’t fund this kind of business, the guy overcharged a spremuta just because we sat down and this extra charge was not specified anywhere for the client to see. The person at the counter was very rude when he confronted us. I would say to stay away from this type of establishment.

name: Leo Babayan
review: Place was one of the only spots open late so we went without checking the reviews, we spend $49 euros for 3 ppl and we all got sandwiches, let me tell you the sandwich’s at the gas station and train stations taste way better. It was plan and 1 start out of 5. The workers were nice but food sucks l.

name: john fing
review: We stopped in for coffee and tea very early one morning. Now asking for a black tea with milk on the side is not a usual Italian request. Accomplished, water in a pot steaming hot, good quality tea brand and milk on the side in a little jug. Came with a little biscuit on the saucer and a sachet of honey. The coffee was, well, coffee, not great but also not bad, a well made average coffee.

name: Megan Prosise
review: We sat down and enjoyed two coffees, three cannolis, and another little pastry. Everything tasted amazing. This was about 22 euros. The restaurant is very close to the Rialto Bridge, so I imagine that's why the price is higher because of the tourism aspect. We thoroughly appreciated the very kind service provided by the ladies working there. The only part that was a bummer was that they asked us to pay with cash instead of card. I'm not sure if card would've been accepted or euros just highly preferred.

name: Ficho Stainov
review: I sat down in the Café on a weekday in October and I had to work for a few hours on my laptop. The coffee, food and Aperol were great and the staff was friendly and did not object me working there. The prices are also reasonable for Venice. All in all a great café and good for home office.

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
We stopped at this café for a quick coffee, even though we had seen some negative reviews beforehand. The prices for coffee at the table are on the higher side (I know it’s common to have a difference compared to the counter, but it still felt a bit much). The coffee itself was fine, though not among the best we tried in Italy.

The atmosphere was lively, and we noticed that local regulars were greeted very warmly by the staff, which gave the place a certain charm. Our own experience was more neutral — the service was efficient but not particularly attentive. Overall, it’s a stylish café, though perhaps better suited for locals who already know it well.

name: Sebastian Römer (sibbedaiah)
review:
We had breakfast at this café in Venice, and while the food and atmosphere were decent (3/5), the service was unfortunately one of the worst experiences we’ve had during our trip.

The waiter was incredibly unfriendly towards tourists, even though we were polite and courteous the entire time. What made it even more uncomfortable was seeing how warmly he treated the locals at the nearby tables — a stark contrast that left us feeling very unwelcome.

Despite our friendly attitude, it felt as if he had taken a personal dislike to us for no reason. Even when we left a tip (which, frankly, wasn’t deserved), he accepted it only very reluctantly, almost as if it bothered him.

Such a shame, because the café itself has a nice setting and the breakfast was fine. But the poor attitude and lack of basic respect from the waiter completely spoiled the experience

name: David Cho
review: I liked the food and Morning staffs were great but one of the Afternoon staff came and threw the menu on the middle of table and slammed the tissue box on top of it. Was light throw and slam but was not necessary because I already finished my order. Still giving 3 stars for Morning staffs were very friendly with big smiles

name: Saffron Koinch
review:
Extremely rude staff – completely ruined our morning!

This café is close to our hotel and looked like a nice, local Italian spot, so we decided to have breakfast here. Big mistake. The staff was unbelievably rude — honestly on another level.

I simply asked if they make a flat white, and the man’s response was shockingly rude and dismissive. As a tourist, I was just trying to understand their menu, but he acted like my question was ridiculous. He’s a local and obviously knows what coffee they serve, so he could’ve easily explained it politely instead of responding with such an attitude.

I was so shocked and upset that I just stood there in silence, not wanting to argue or let it ruin our morning.

name: Elizabeth Heiner
review: Delicious food. Ignore the comments regarding rude service. We did our best to speak Italian and it’s amazing what happens when you try to speak the native language. We saw some very rude Americans which was embarrassing for us as fellow Americans. We ordered cappuccinos, caffe, and hot chocolate along with a myriad of sandwiches. Highly recommend!

name: Hiten Umarnani
review: Firstly served cold food despite clearly stating on the menu that the food was hot. Secondly, it was incredibly overpriced with the menus online being cheaper clearly lying about the prices. Thirdly, we specifically asked for non seeded bread as a member of our group had a severe food allergy, it wasn't even a modification as on the menu the bread was non seeded. Yet still they served our freezing cold sandwiches with seeded bread because they ran out apparently. After this attempted homicide, they refused to give us a new sandwich and that member had to go outside. There's specifically a bearded waiter/manager who was incredibly rude. He shouted at us  to not drink outside drinks despite the fact that we only had the bottles in our bags and gave us a darn ultimatum to leave despite people on our table already having food. Genuinely, one of the worst experiences I've ever had in a restaurant. This is worse than some  inner city Denny's I've been in.

name: Martin Kluk
review: Not sure how anyone could complain about this shop. The service and food/drinks were great and the prices were very reasonable compared to the insane prices of the square down the road. Thanks for having us!

name: Noor Yassine
review: We dropped by this cafe in Venice for a mid-afternoon coffee break. Their Cappuccino was delicious. Their veggie pizza was average, kinda disappointing how it was not wood fired & we’re literally in Italy. Their outdoor seating was open with plenty of fresh air & pretty views. Only con was that the owner was kind of rude & wasn’t as welcoming when we placed our order.

name: Nadya Fetisova
review: We stayed next door, and came for breakfast the day we were leaving. Esther, the owner, was very nice and welcoming, and explained the menu for us. Service was fast, coffee was great, all good impression. I also liked how they bring coffee in big bright cups all colour mixed, very cheerful. They also sell packaged coffee, we got it as a nice souvenir from Italy.

name: Jennifer Claros
review:
DO NOT go to this place. They are very rude. Specially the owner. We were ordering capuchinos inside and the guy told us to go outside and get a table, the owner grabbed my 9 year old daughter and pulled her outside and told her they don’t serve capuchinos for 5 people. My husband tried to ask why and told us to leave. This is the guy who grabbed my daughters shirt. YOU DON’T TOUCH, you need to be respectful.
To the old owner guy- if you have a business with a lot of tourists you need to be respectful. If you are not respectful then close your business because we as tourists are giving you money we are not begging you for anything.

name: Craig Bentley
review:
Valerio  and Ricardo hosted us today on2 separate sittings.

You could not ask for a nicer or more welcoming restaurant to eat and drink.

The drinks are fresh and fast as soon s you order. As well as the foot and ice cream.

I will always come back here.

Highly recommend!

name: Noemi Garcia
review:
wish I could give this place zero stars. From the second we sat down, the staff (guy in the picture) was unwelcoming. Our group of five simply wanted to order cappuccinos, but we were told that they “don’t serve cappuccino to five people.” They refused to explain and instead told us to leave. The guy put his hand on my niece. YOU DON’T DO THAT, especially to a CHILD.

This was hands down the worst service I’ve ever encountered. If you expect even a basic level of respect as a customer, do yourself a favor and take your service where you’ll be respected!

name: tokozon
review: The group of young girls may be treated ok here. Outside seatings are nice. There are several kinds of coffees with photos in the menu. That was thoughtful, but young waiter was super rude. After seeing the other reviews, it did not surprise me. Just to be aware of not getting a friendly service. They may be tired of dealing with tourists.

name: Yuriy Yakovenko
review: I should have checked google reviews prior to going there. They brought me a cold cappuccino. When I pointed that out, they just added some hot water, so it didn’t taste like anything at all. No apology. Paid 24 euros for just two croissants, watered down cappuccino and horrendous service. Avoid this place. There are a lot of good places around.

name: Qatar4ever
review: Very good coffee & dessert, but the chairs for seating outside a bit uncomfortable

name: Rak P
review: A very lively and popular bacaro. Prime position. Location is picture perfect, opposite church, across ponte/bridge overlooking canal. Popular with locals and workers but also passing tourists coming into the area. Stopped over in the morning for coffee but the real unexpected star were the croissants,  had these wirh chocolate,  orange and pistachio fillings, they are delicious.  Best croissants we had whilst staying in Venice. Prompt and efficient service. Chichetti looked good, nice selection and popular as well. Good selection of local wines. Nice vibe and ambience, both mornings and evenings, people in and outside the bar. Very reasonable prices. If you are visiting, come over and have a slice of typical Venice.

name: Sebastian N
review: We stopped by this bar while strolling through Venice and noticed a great selection of cicchetti displayed in the window. We decided to stay and try a few, along with a couple of drinks. We sat by the window with a lovely view of the city — such a pleasant spot! Delicious cicchetti, friendly service, and a wonderful atmosphere. Highly recommended!

name: A L
review: This was our favourite bacaro in Venice. We returned here a few times as it was so central and lively in the evening. The bar is right on the steps of a bridge, next to large open area and church which makes it the perfect spot for a select spritz and cicchetti. Well recomended.

name: Patrick Kluver
review: Fantastic experience. Great value and quality for money! Best priced cicchetti in town! Meat 2€, fish 2.50€. We’ll be back!

name: Ricky Lamar
review:
This little bar and cicchetteria has it going on! Georgia with her mischievous twinkle will mix you up a traditional Italian spritzer offering up tasty tapas to tempt those taste buds.
And Lorenzo, in charge of the fun made this a night to remember.

name: Nima Lama
review: We made a quick stop for some drinks and ended up trying their cicchetti. It was delicious! The staff were super friendly, and we were lucky to get a seat right by the airco. Grazie mille!

name: Rhakria and or Embisch
review:
Impeccable bacaro experience.
I'll list some positives :
- Wine selection, as well as enough other drinks for those who don't want wine
- freshly prepared Cicchetti with enough variety to suit all tastes
- very stylish music playlist of classic 80-90s disco
- lovely / lively people behind the counter

The negatives :
- can't think of one...

Thank you for the well spent evening 🤗

name: David Wilson
review: We stopped in for a coffee and water.  Everyone was so friendly and the cappuccinos were VERY tasty. Grazie Mille!

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
A lovely place to have coffee and a variety of pastries and baked good such as a surprisingly delicious croissant.
Food is delicious and fresh.
Service is polite and friendly.
Atmosphere is modern and clean and merchandise is clean as well.
Reasonably good value.

Great place to nosh

name: Sara
review:
I was looking for a good pistacchio croissant and couldn't find it...One older lady told me there is pasticceria so I checked and it was amazing!
I tried pistacchio croissant and also frittella, they were absolutely great!

name: Valeriya Smirnova
review: Awesome lemon roll cake - very tender cream, not too sweet - just perfect

name: Alexis Kokonuzz
review: Cant speak about the croissants, maybe those are good, but the pistacchio cannoli is disappointment made pastry. Terrible artificial cream flavor, horrible value for money, sad sad sad.

name: RnV Marty
review: We tried a few flavors of cannoli and they were all delicious. Glad we stopped in.

name: Alex O.
review: No pistachio in my cannoli, only sugar

name: David Zemdegs
review: Lovely place for coffee and pastries.

name: Mike Tretola
review: Wanted to try the cannolis that were in the window and were highly disappointed. The filling was like a whipped cream, not traditional ricotta or mascarpone. Cappuccinos were excellent, though.

name: Oren Bar
review:
Great food, fantastic service and good price!
The toast / focaccia was super fresh and very tasty! Would definitely recommend! Not a tourist trap! I came here 4 years ago and it is still amazing!

name: zeke monroe
review: Fantastic spot, the first night we arrived in Venice we had arrived late and even though they were in the process of closing the man running the shop, Sandro, sold us some water. The next day we came up he remembered us and set us up with a fantastic meal and even set some roses and a lamp on the table. Ordered the Focaccia Ricotta E Spinaci and the Tiramisu. 10/10 recommend. You will not be disappointed.

name: Allie Arnaiz
review:
Great food for the price, meals came with so much. A coffee, entree, and a bread. Really good food. Good place to stop at if you're hungry in the morning before going further into the city.
Great view
Don't specifically remember the service but I feel like that is a good thing. Not exceptional but not bad.

name: Gabriela Gisca
review:
J’ai bien aimé les pasta 😍😍 it was so delicious
I recommend it for price and for its taste

name: Nonso Come
review:
A nice little spot in Venice
Stopped by for a quick coffee and snack. The service was friendly and the atmosphere cozy. Prices were reasonable for the area. Nothing fancy, but a pleasant place to take a short break while exploring the city.

name: adam schonert
review: We were here everyday during our trip to Venice. They have reasonable prices, great quantity, and kind and fast service. They have a comprehensive menu with quality items. We would get eggs, bacon, toasts, croissants, and coffee in the morning. We had a Malibu coke in the afternoon, and it was wonderful. Highly recommend!

name: Bosanski Ljiljan
review: Great Pizza, low Prices and a great Customer Service

name: Susanna Kantelinen
review: The Bellini was beautiful and the vegetarian chicetti delicious and fresh. My waitress, Alice, was just wonderful.

name: Ken Tao
review:
Walked passed many many local cafes, looked at many gorgeous food items on display, but didn't like sitting outside in the steamer and watched by countless other tourist like animals in a zoo, dodged into this Starbucks and went upstairs to hide out in the most precious resource in Venice - AC.
There were 2 staff called Ana, one is a cashier and the other is supervisory.  Both were helpful and able to converse in English.  The food there was local, forget about everything you see in the US.  I had a club sandwich!  It was good despite it might have sat there from morning until noonish when I ordered it.  From the display to the sandwich toaster, grilled until there was a crosshatch.  Cut in half and shared.  The bread was regular white bread size.  Costed only 7 Euro, it didn't have the raw ham but can't complain.   Separately ordered a raw ham croissant, that was small but it was good.  There was this melon pearl iced drink that I was told it was pretty good.  Not here in the US yet.
There were power outlets everywhere upstairs.  The setting has a couple large tables for 5-6 and then lots of smaller table for 4 and then some small rectangular bar tables.  It has the best of the local Los Angeles store setups (regular plug + USB power, chairs, napkins, restroom, inside seating).  No refill, Starbucks App does not work- asked in detail with the Ana's.  DO NOT remotely think about half and half in your coffee.  Polizia might show up.  You won't find it either anywhere.  The tables were all bolted down to the floor.  The two lay back chairs upstairs were not recommended for it's low to the ground/hard to get up and lack the length to support your head weird low angle but you'd need to hold you head/neck strain.  Ground floor is extremely crowded because of the constant long line for the restroom. Americano was superior than the ones here.  Can't comment on the other drinks as I didn't try.
One thing that is somewhere alarming but inescapable was the sewage smell that was Venice in hot weather.

name: Melanie Sarah “LiA” Eggink
review:
Overcrowded, super loud.
What’s somehow ok for the only Starbucks in Venice. But how the situation is handled by the staff is not appropriate.

The line for the toilet is through the whole restaurant between the tables. Tables are not cleaned and dishes and plastic cups from 2 earlier guests piling on tables.

Staff doesn’t care, but tells me to not place my bag on the stairs to pack the mug I just bought, for safety reasons.
It took literally 30 seconds. 😜

name: Dione
review: I speak English & staff were very understanding while I ordered. I young lady remembered me from the day before & knew my order, so excellent service

name: Paulina 89
review:
We had a very disappointing experience at this Starbucks in Venice.
We only had a couple of hours in the city, didn’t want a big lunch, and decided to stop here just for a coffee and  food and to use the facilities. The place was understandably busy, as Venice is full of tourists, but the service was extremely poor.

The coffee arrived quickly and looked nice, but the toast we ordered took over 20 minutes to arrive, by which point the coffee was already cold. When the toast finally came, it was half cold, with artificial-tasting cheese — honestly inedible.

To make matters worse, there was only one toilet in the entire  large) Starbucks, and it was out of order. We only found out after ordering, as the tiny sign was hidden behind products at the counter. Considering they serve drinks and food, having no accessible toilet is absolutely unacceptable — especially in a city like Venice, where public toilets are very hard to find.

Overall, this was a poor experience. We came to Starbucks expecting reliability and basic comfort, but instead left disappointed. I will definitely avoid this location in the future.

name: Monika Esseová
review: The coffee was good, but the toilet could have been cleaner, and there was only 1  available.

name: Cellist ESTHER
review:
The steps are really friendly and fast. I was happy with the service they provided.
The inside of the store was pretty clean and well organized. If you hate waiting in line, I recommend you go in the morning.

name: Fi Lip
review: Franchise owner, can you please review the toilet maintenance in this starbucks. Very inconvenient and totally visitor unfriendly to keep the toilet closed for a long time

name: SaliiH
review: Starbucks Venezia Rialto is often bustling with tourists, making it challenging to find a seat. Despite the crowds, the staff are impressively quick and efficient, so the lines move fast. Great spot for a coffee break if you don’t mind the busy atmosphere.

name: Bryan
review: Tiny place with fresh gelato with delicious flavors. This was probably the best gelato I had in Venice. The owner was very friendly. Definitely recommend, if in the area and it was fun to eat next to a not-so-busy canal.

name: Bernardo salcido
review:
A lovely spot in a quiet neighborhood.  Right by the gondola repair place.

The marscapone with fig was fabulous.

Salted caramel was great with a stronger flavor.

The little bits of crunchy fruit in the Chantilly were delicious.

name: Kris
review: Easy 5 stars! Friendly, reasonably priced, no queues and… amazing ice cream!! The best we had all summer!

name: G M
review: What to say? A very good ice cream spot in Venezia: great location near the gondolas workshop, many students in the area as well, great ambiance and of course very tasty ice cream: few choices for an efficient selection with creamy and tasty ice creams. Very nice welcoming and service level, I recommend the place.

name: Moon
review: This was such a nice gelato shop. It was so tasty and creamy. The guy was also very friendly. Wish I could come back here again.

name: na na
review: Maybe I would have rated this place more highly were it located anywhere else other than the country of amazing gelato. The texture was too icy and hard, more like regular ice cream and not really like gelato — the picture and shape of the scoop already shows this is not a regular gelato. The flavors we got, pistacchio (recommended by other reviewers) and nocciola (always a safe choice) tasted way too artificial. I’d gladly go to a chain before coming back here.

name: Daniel D.
review: Best ice creams in Venice and we have tried all other „best” places. This place is the one you don’t want to miss🔥💥🧁

name: alan chang
review: Amazing flavours! The owner is super friendly. I got the salted Carmel and pistachio.

name: Jim Elsenbeck
review: We booked a cicchetti tour and this was the first stop.  We ended up sitting down outside because we were right on the Grand Canal with an incredible view.  This was also our introduction to spritz, so we had to try the aperol, the hugo and the cynar.  They weren't our last spritz's for this trip.  ;)

name: Oliver Wehner
review:
Never go there with children.
Very unfriendly.
Children where sitting calmly on the table playing some Table games.
After the second warning we were requested to leave.
They were never loud nor disturbing someone.
Just ridiculous.
Never again

name: Eric3 Skywalker
review:
Sat down at a table at 2pm to have a dessert and a drink. Most of the other tables were empty. We got sent away because "they only accepted people for lunch".
Clowns...

name: Gábor Majer
review: We bought 2 ice coffee. One of them was delicious, other one is not. Everybody was kind and you can enjoy the coffee next to the Grand Canal. Price is 10-20€ if you buy coffee for 2 person. If you wanna it somethinh then 20-30€.

name: Esra Zengin
review: Very close to the Rialto Bridge, with a beautiful ambiance and a location near the waterfront. Our coffees were delicious, but the tiramisu was average. The waiter who assisted us was very polite. Recommended for those who want to enjoy a pleasant time with a view.

name: Taylor Zang
review: We stumbled upon this place while walking on the grand canal at Venice. The place is perfectly located in front of the canal very near to Rialto bridge. We ordered one of their vegetarian pizzas called Pizza alle verdure. It was perfectly served with right amount of vegetables. It was delicious and filling. In Venice they already charge sitting fee so people normally don’t tip here, which we found out towards the end of our trip :-)

name: Alan Hewitt
review: Lovely atmosphere by yhe Rialto bridge with the water right next to you. The service was very friendly, but the food was average. Not enough flavor or brightness in the pasta dishes. Better value for similar prices elsewhere.

name: Katie Hall
review: So good we came here twice on the same day! Great coffee and cakes, good service, and a really nice casual and cool atmosphere inside. Or you can sit outside by the canal. If you stand at the bar you can get much cheaper coffee, but if you're walking around Venice you need to sit - and this place is great.

name: Rak P
review: A very lively and popular bacaro. Prime position. Location is picture perfect, opposite church, across ponte/bridge overlooking canal. Popular with locals and workers but also passing tourists coming into the area. Stopped over in the morning for coffee but the real unexpected star were the croissants,  had these wirh chocolate,  orange and pistachio fillings, they are delicious.  Best croissants we had whilst staying in Venice. Prompt and efficient service. Chichetti looked good, nice selection and popular as well. Good selection of local wines. Nice vibe and ambience, both mornings and evenings, people in and outside the bar. Very reasonable prices. If you are visiting, come over and have a slice of typical Venice.

name: Sebastian N
review: We stopped by this bar while strolling through Venice and noticed a great selection of cicchetti displayed in the window. We decided to stay and try a few, along with a couple of drinks. We sat by the window with a lovely view of the city — such a pleasant spot! Delicious cicchetti, friendly service, and a wonderful atmosphere. Highly recommended!

name: A L
review: This was our favourite bacaro in Venice. We returned here a few times as it was so central and lively in the evening. The bar is right on the steps of a bridge, next to large open area and church which makes it the perfect spot for a select spritz and cicchetti. Well recomended.

name: Patrick Kluver
review: Fantastic experience. Great value and quality for money! Best priced cicchetti in town! Meat 2€, fish 2.50€. We’ll be back!

name: Ricky Lamar
review:
This little bar and cicchetteria has it going on! Georgia with her mischievous twinkle will mix you up a traditional Italian spritzer offering up tasty tapas to tempt those taste buds.
And Lorenzo, in charge of the fun made this a night to remember.

name: Nima Lama
review: We made a quick stop for some drinks and ended up trying their cicchetti. It was delicious! The staff were super friendly, and we were lucky to get a seat right by the airco. Grazie mille!

name: Rhakria and or Embisch
review:
Impeccable bacaro experience.
I'll list some positives :
- Wine selection, as well as enough other drinks for those who don't want wine
- freshly prepared Cicchetti with enough variety to suit all tastes
- very stylish music playlist of classic 80-90s disco
- lovely / lively people behind the counter

The negatives :
- can't think of one...

Thank you for the well spent evening 🤗

name: David Wilson
review: We stopped in for a coffee and water.  Everyone was so friendly and the cappuccinos were VERY tasty. Grazie Mille!

name: Bahattin Çiniç
review:
We had a salty croissant, a focaccia sandwich, and two sweet croissants.
Everything was great! The focaccia sandwich was the best one, but the others were also really good.

name: Jože Habinc
review: All three pizzas we tried tasted great, the service was solid and the price, considering the location, was also good. However, there were a lot of leftovers on the tables (but everyone could clean up after themselves, as they have trash cans installed), the pigeons between the tables were not pleasant to watch, and what bothered me the most was the strong operation of the air conditioners and ceiling fans. They really overdo it with the cooling.

name: Sarah Petry
review: We are staying in Venice and wanted a nice easy dinner. We found Farini, they were friendly and welcoming to some tourists with little Italian. We ordered and range of pizzas and the boys ordered some tarts for dessert.

name: Bhawana Agarwal
review:
🌟 5 stars

One of the best pizza places you can try—an absolute hidden gem! The pizza is a must-have, even if you’re not hungry. It’s that good! A total steal for the quality you get. Perfect for dine-in or takeaway, and the coffee is just as amazing. Friendly and welcoming staff make the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit—Bon appétit! 🍕

name: Moji gh
review: We ordered two caffè lattes and two croissants. The coffee was average but acceptable compared to other places in Italy. The croissants were fresh and tasty. The main downside was the service — the staff member who served us was unfriendly and unwelcoming.

name: Ari Sastre
review: My uncle says that the best pizza starts with the crust, and Farini's crust is superb. Simplistic homegrown ingredients combine together to make a pizza that you think would be found in an expensive celebrity chain. Prices are affordable and reasonable too.

name: Kaitlyn Affuso
review:
The BEST pizza I had while in Italy.
Went to Venice, Florence, and Rome and this was by FAR my favorite. We visited several times while we were in Venice for 3.5 days.

Highly reccommend. Get several different slices and share because they're all delicious!

name: CCG G
review: Good place for croissants and fresh yogurt with fresh fruit and granola. The coffee was honestly one of the grossest coffee ups I’ve ever tasted - it was very burnt I just threw it away. Place is small with limited seating so it’s chaotic and not a good place to sit and eat. Highly recommend if you’re craving yogurt though.

name: Ficho Stainov
review: I sat down in the Café on a weekday in October and I had to work for a few hours on my laptop. The coffee, food and Aperol were great and the staff was friendly and did not object me working there. The prices are also reasonable for Venice. All in all a great café and good for home office.

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
We stopped at this café for a quick coffee, even though we had seen some negative reviews beforehand. The prices for coffee at the table are on the higher side (I know it’s common to have a difference compared to the counter, but it still felt a bit much). The coffee itself was fine, though not among the best we tried in Italy.

The atmosphere was lively, and we noticed that local regulars were greeted very warmly by the staff, which gave the place a certain charm. Our own experience was more neutral — the service was efficient but not particularly attentive. Overall, it’s a stylish café, though perhaps better suited for locals who already know it well.

name: Sebastian Römer (sibbedaiah)
review:
We had breakfast at this café in Venice, and while the food and atmosphere were decent (3/5), the service was unfortunately one of the worst experiences we’ve had during our trip.

The waiter was incredibly unfriendly towards tourists, even though we were polite and courteous the entire time. What made it even more uncomfortable was seeing how warmly he treated the locals at the nearby tables — a stark contrast that left us feeling very unwelcome.

Despite our friendly attitude, it felt as if he had taken a personal dislike to us for no reason. Even when we left a tip (which, frankly, wasn’t deserved), he accepted it only very reluctantly, almost as if it bothered him.

Such a shame, because the café itself has a nice setting and the breakfast was fine. But the poor attitude and lack of basic respect from the waiter completely spoiled the experience

name: David Cho
review: I liked the food and Morning staffs were great but one of the Afternoon staff came and threw the menu on the middle of table and slammed the tissue box on top of it. Was light throw and slam but was not necessary because I already finished my order. Still giving 3 stars for Morning staffs were very friendly with big smiles

name: Saffron Koinch
review:
Extremely rude staff – completely ruined our morning!

This café is close to our hotel and looked like a nice, local Italian spot, so we decided to have breakfast here. Big mistake. The staff was unbelievably rude — honestly on another level.

I simply asked if they make a flat white, and the man’s response was shockingly rude and dismissive. As a tourist, I was just trying to understand their menu, but he acted like my question was ridiculous. He’s a local and obviously knows what coffee they serve, so he could’ve easily explained it politely instead of responding with such an attitude.

I was so shocked and upset that I just stood there in silence, not wanting to argue or let it ruin our morning.

name: Elizabeth Heiner
review: Delicious food. Ignore the comments regarding rude service. We did our best to speak Italian and it’s amazing what happens when you try to speak the native language. We saw some very rude Americans which was embarrassing for us as fellow Americans. We ordered cappuccinos, caffe, and hot chocolate along with a myriad of sandwiches. Highly recommend!

name: Hiten Umarnani
review: Firstly served cold food despite clearly stating on the menu that the food was hot. Secondly, it was incredibly overpriced with the menus online being cheaper clearly lying about the prices. Thirdly, we specifically asked for non seeded bread as a member of our group had a severe food allergy, it wasn't even a modification as on the menu the bread was non seeded. Yet still they served our freezing cold sandwiches with seeded bread because they ran out apparently. After this attempted homicide, they refused to give us a new sandwich and that member had to go outside. There's specifically a bearded waiter/manager who was incredibly rude. He shouted at us  to not drink outside drinks despite the fact that we only had the bottles in our bags and gave us a darn ultimatum to leave despite people on our table already having food. Genuinely, one of the worst experiences I've ever had in a restaurant. This is worse than some  inner city Denny's I've been in.

name: Martin Kluk
review: Not sure how anyone could complain about this shop. The service and food/drinks were great and the prices were very reasonable compared to the insane prices of the square down the road. Thanks for having us!

name: Oren Bar
review:
Great food, fantastic service and good price!
The toast / focaccia was super fresh and very tasty! Would definitely recommend! Not a tourist trap! I came here 4 years ago and it is still amazing!

name: zeke monroe
review: Fantastic spot, the first night we arrived in Venice we had arrived late and even though they were in the process of closing the man running the shop, Sandro, sold us some water. The next day we came up he remembered us and set us up with a fantastic meal and even set some roses and a lamp on the table. Ordered the Focaccia Ricotta E Spinaci and the Tiramisu. 10/10 recommend. You will not be disappointed.

name: Allie Arnaiz
review:
Great food for the price, meals came with so much. A coffee, entree, and a bread. Really good food. Good place to stop at if you're hungry in the morning before going further into the city.
Great view
Don't specifically remember the service but I feel like that is a good thing. Not exceptional but not bad.

name: Gabriela Gisca
review:
J’ai bien aimé les pasta 😍😍 it was so delicious
I recommend it for price and for its taste

name: Nonso Come
review:
A nice little spot in Venice
Stopped by for a quick coffee and snack. The service was friendly and the atmosphere cozy. Prices were reasonable for the area. Nothing fancy, but a pleasant place to take a short break while exploring the city.

name: adam schonert
review: We were here everyday during our trip to Venice. They have reasonable prices, great quantity, and kind and fast service. They have a comprehensive menu with quality items. We would get eggs, bacon, toasts, croissants, and coffee in the morning. We had a Malibu coke in the afternoon, and it was wonderful. Highly recommend!

name: Bosanski Ljiljan
review: Great Pizza, low Prices and a great Customer Service

name: Susanna Kantelinen
review: The Bellini was beautiful and the vegetarian chicetti delicious and fresh. My waitress, Alice, was just wonderful.

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
A lovely place to have coffee and a variety of pastries and baked good such as a surprisingly delicious croissant.
Food is delicious and fresh.
Service is polite and friendly.
Atmosphere is modern and clean and merchandise is clean as well.
Reasonably good value.

Great place to nosh

name: Sara
review:
I was looking for a good pistacchio croissant and couldn't find it...One older lady told me there is pasticceria so I checked and it was amazing!
I tried pistacchio croissant and also frittella, they were absolutely great!

name: Valeriya Smirnova
review: Awesome lemon roll cake - very tender cream, not too sweet - just perfect

name: Alexis Kokonuzz
review: Cant speak about the croissants, maybe those are good, but the pistacchio cannoli is disappointment made pastry. Terrible artificial cream flavor, horrible value for money, sad sad sad.

name: RnV Marty
review: We tried a few flavors of cannoli and they were all delicious. Glad we stopped in.

name: Alex O.
review: No pistachio in my cannoli, only sugar

name: David Zemdegs
review: Lovely place for coffee and pastries.

name: Mike Tretola
review: Wanted to try the cannolis that were in the window and were highly disappointed. The filling was like a whipped cream, not traditional ricotta or mascarpone. Cappuccinos were excellent, though.

name: Jess Owen
review: If you want good chocolate in Venice this is the place. The cashier was so friendly and was more than happy to talk me through the flavours and give her own recommendations. They all looked so delicious I couldn't choose and had one of each and some chocolate bark and it only came to €25 which is an insanely good bargain. I'm a patisserie chef myself and after trying them I was immediately wondering if I had time to go back!

name: Beverly Zygmunt
review: While there is a beautiful selection of chocolate candies in the shop, I only had the hot chocolate and pastries. The hot chocolate is thick, dark, and delicious. I had it twice, with whipped cream. One day I had it with a piece of dense chocolate cake. For my second outing, I had a salted-caramel topped brownie. Everything was excellent and the staff are very friendly.

name: Lisa Pertl
review: Amazing experience! We accidentally stumbled upon this little place. They have amazing homemade chocolates, cookies, and ice cream, and they also sell great coffee. The owner is absolutely lovely and more than happy to explain the store and have a chat.

name: Pavee Suvagondha
review: Great place for chocolate, gelato (including vegan options), tiramisu, and cakes.

name: Krzysztof Jankowski
review: Very nice, kind staff. Tasty pastries and coffee.

name: Tati Costa
review: Hot chocolate was AMAZING. Service was very friendly. The only reason atmosphere is not 5 stars is that it could have more seating options. There is space for at least one more table.

name: Alice Hsieh
review:
A tiny hot milk chocolate costs $3 euro. Such a tourist trap lol The lady is very friendly.

We tried both hot milk chocolate and “spicy fusion” hot chocolate. Not that impressive, and the hot chocolate we ordered was cold because there was an issue with the machine. We did receive another one though.

name: Sarah
review:
If you're even the slightest bit fond of chocolate or hot chocolate, you must stop by Vizio Virtù! We were in Venice for a week and I came back three times after my first visit 😅

The hot chocolate is phenomenal, the richest and thickest I've ever had, really worth it for €4 in my opinion. And the truffles and pralines tasted every bit as good as they looked — my favourites were the truffle with rum and maybe the peperoncino-infused praline. We also tried the chocolate tart, and it was deliciously moist and not too sweet.

They have a good selection of other confections ranging from chocolate-covered nuts to single-origin bars.

I cannot recommend them enough! Would give them 10/5 stars if I could. 😊

name: Deepak Deshpande
review: Amazing falafel wraps. The falafels were sooooooo good and fresh. Super good for vegetarian vegan folks. Gujarati, Tamil Brahmins, Jain folks from India who are strict vegetarian will love this place as the kitchen is vegetarian (no meat but they instead use substitute like beyond meat).

name: Daisy
review: Got a cappuccino and an English breakfast tea (I've been wanting one for days and this is only place I found that had). Also got pizza a different day (€12 for kosher Margherita) and it was really good. Staff were very nice, especially Angie who helped us multiple times. Fyi this is a takeaway, no seating.

name: Julia Fleisher
review: So grateful Gam Gam Goodies exists! It’s the only “dairy” kosher in Venice. They have some gelato, a fridge section of some pre packaged kosher grocery goods (like salami, cheeses etc), wine and snacks. This is a take away place, you order and go, but sooo good! We got pizza after Shabbat (which was totally worth sending my husband while I stayed in the Airbnb with our sleeping toddler) and in the morning got a breakfast pita with fresh pita. The dough is made in house in front of you and it is a delicious! The cookies and baked goods are also amazing. They are recipes from the Rebbetzin of the community and so so good!

name: Daniel Heideman
review: This pizza GETS THE JOB DONE. When you’re walking around Venice taking in the scenery and you suddenly get hungry, this is a great place to go. It’s not near the shops but it’s a good walk and the pizza is worthy of the walk. They also sell funky packaged salami and other meats. The guy making the pizza was super nice and suggestive, and the pizza was good. Not the best, but does the job! Pizza flop was 5/10 and slice overall was 6.1/10

name: Y B
review: Pretty good food here, but not great. The pizza was alright, the falafel was pretty good! They only take cash in this place, although their sister restaurant down the alley takes credit card as well. This is a combination of restaurant and bakery and kosher shop.

name: Shmuel dovid Uhlman
review:
Tasty kosher pizza, falafel and a wide variety of freshly made pastries. 🍪
You can also purchase wine beer or kosher basics like cheese and cold cuts. 🧀
Take out only-but you can eat you food in the nearby chabad house 🏠.

name: Rivky
review: Such a plus to have delicious food with high kashrus standards , was delicious there pizza on late motzia shabbos , kids loved there gelato, they made us very good egg scrambled sandwiches, . They were unbelievable accommodating when we asked they should prepare us 8am our sandwiches , everything was ready , neat and gusto.

name: Yisroel Cohen
review:
Love this place. Nothing fancy.  But solid plain old pizza.
The falafel isn't where it's at though. Stick with the pizza, coffee, and pastries.

It's also fairly cheap

name: Patti Nault
review: We stumbled upon this gem on a Venice street while wandering around. The menu looked delicious and not too pricey. We chatted with Tony at the door, and he was super helpful with all our menu questions. The food was absolutely fantastic, and the service was just as great. After dinner, Tony even suggested some places to get gelato. We can’t recommend this restaurant enough!

name: Pagriima
review: Waiters were tires and sad, as we came late before closing. Still the service was good. Portions moderate, not big for the price. Minestrone tasted more like from frozen vegetables.

name: Nicholas
review: Mediocre at best. The bruschettas were nice With the cocktails really bad… a limoncello spritz without any soda and a mojito that felt flavoured water

name: Kellyanne Matt
review:
Most amazing cicchetti and aperol spritz
The prawns are so fresh and delicious. The bruschetta is amazing. The mozzarella incredible. The speck was delicious. The cannoli the kids went mad for them !  Just is such a lovely little Hidden gem in Venice. Wonderful staff. Amazing food!
We came one day in the morning and absolutely loved it so we came back in the evening the next day

name: Sara Fischer
review: Overall, meh. Bread was very fresh. Cannelloni was alright and served in a weird white sauce. Should have been in a pomodoro sauce or alfredo. We also ordered breaded fish and fries. Fries were good. Fish looked like it was out of the box. Waiter was a bit lax, so had to go to the bar to get my own ice. Overall, edible and cheap.

name: Carlos C.
review: Best place to Drink to go un Venice, also nice food, highly recommended nero di seppia e salmone

name: Noodle Soup
review:
Did I just get a frozen, out of the box pizza?

Yes, yes, I did!

Came here with some friends, decided to order a Davola pizza. And what I got seem to be an out of the box, undercooked, pepperoni cheese, pizza.

Now,I just spent weeks traveling  all around northern Italy, eating pizzas, and this was by far the worst pizza.

I've had better pizza from Aldi's

Now, I rarely give a one star rating, unless it's truly deserved.

When I asked the waiter, if this was a Davola pizza, he just shrugged it off and said yes.

You need to do better!

Now, the good part... The restaurant itself is cute, laid-back, and just off the beaten path.

My friends had seafood pasta, which they said it was OK to good. I didn't dare try it.

Overall, you get the one star. My only one star in Italy on this trip.. congratulations.

name: Elleevy
review: Actually, a pretty decent place! The staff was friendly, the food was delicious and the prices were normal. Total VFM. It could be a bit more polished.

name: Antonio Calabrese
review: Great place in San Marco square in Venice to have a bite or coffee or dessert. Great vibe and live band was amazing taking and playing requests from everyone. Enjoyed my time there and it’s my third time being there and that should explain it all!

name: Le Dang
review: A great hot spot with a fantastic atmosphere! We spent about €50 for two drinks and a coffee, with snacks included. The music was awesome — plus, you can request your favorite songs.

name: Jonna
review:
One of the most expensive meals of our entire Euro trip — dropped €200 on just a few sandwiches and cappuccinos (yep, €18 per cappuccino 😳).

But honestly… nothing beats the view. The cafe is right in the heart of Venice, in front of St. Mark’s Campanile. You’re paying for the experience, the atmosphere, and that iconic Venetian charm.

Oh — and as a bonus? You even get serenaded by a very posh live band while you sip that overpriced cappuccino. Pure main character energy. No regrets.

name: Blue Bean Photography
review:
Like plenty of others have said before me, this ended up being the most expensive coffee stop of our entire Venice trip. Sixty-two euros for two cappuccinos (yes, including tip), which is borderline outrageous, but would I do it again? Absolutely.
Because here’s the thing, nothing beats the view or the atmosphere. The café is smack in the middle of Venice, right in front of St. Mark’s Campanile. You’re not paying for the coffee, you’re paying for the experience, the iconic setting, and that picture-perfect slice of Venetian life. We were super lucky with fabulous weather at the end of March and zero crowds.

And to be fair, the staff were brilliant. We had 1-to-1 attention the entire time and our waiter guarded our bags from the pick pockets, which definitely helped soften the financial blow. Pricey? Without a doubt. Worth it? Absolutely.

name: Вікторія Воропаєва
review:
Good place with music.
Some workers not so friendly
But Garson Luca,he so good in work,smile a lot.I like how he work!!
I travel alone, and this guy just take my phone and start make photos for me.
He made compliment for me as chips,I had one and he give me another plate with chips.It was very kind!

name: Preeyakorn Palm
review: Travelling all over the world, this is probably the most expensive Cafe ever. Coffee and ice-cream are good by Italian standard. It's just way over priced. Even with all the services included in the prices as indicated on the menu, the waiter still reconfirmed if additional tip was to be added at will.

name: Manish Ghatke
review:
We spent a few hours in this cafe on the famous St Marks Square on a beautiful sunny afternoon.

The highlight of this place is obviously the lively music and the views of the expansive square and the cathedral.

The price is definitely on the higher side, but the experience was a good one.

name: Sales LLC
review:
Caffè Lavena was one of the worst experiences I’ve had in all my travels across multiple countries. The staff—especially one particular lady—was unbelievably rude. I had just bought a small cheesecake and a coffee to-go. Carrying several shopping bags, I sat down briefly to organize my things. Within seconds, she yelled at me: "Stand up and leave now, Sir!" in the most aggressive and disrespectful tone imaginable.

I wasn’t trying to dine in—I simply needed a moment to manage my bags. As a paying customer and international visitor, I’ve never been treated with such hostility anywhere else. It’s no surprise this place has a 2.6 rating. Honestly, it’s a disgrace to hospitality. Avoid this place at all costs.

name: Facundo Cesa
review:
Our experience wasn't as bad as others I've read. They didn't try to con us at least.

But it's true, this was the absolute worst cannolo in the world. If by accident you try this disgusting thing filled with a sugary hydrogenated vegetal crap, please, don't believe you've tried an ACTUAL cannolo.

The waitress didn't understand our Italian. Which, come on... Isn't THAT bad...

Maybe Italian wasn't her first language either. Maybe she comes from a country without formaggio grattugiato or smiles.

name: Joana Pack Melo Sousa
review: The worst coffe shop ever!! The taste of the cannolli was greasy. The price of the cappuccino outside says 3,50. They charge 6,00 inside + 2 euros of service. They were rude when we asked about the price.

name: Mitch Tornquist
review: DO NOT be fooled by the great location and beautiful window display. Absolute worst cannolo I’ve ever had in my life. In fact I’m not sure how they could have made it any worse. The filling tasted like icing and sugar. Sickeningly sweet. I did not finish it, and that’s crazy, cause I eat everything.

name: F.M. Zowczak
review: Terrible service, you have to pay table cover fee - 2€ per person (even though you’ve ordered 2 small cookies). Food is very poor quality. Cookies are extremely sweet without any flavor (it’s so sweet that you want to vomit). Toilets are not clean, tables are placed to close to each other so you can’t barely move. Do not recommend…

name: Giovanni Balzi
review: Don’t fund this kind of business, the guy overcharged a spremuta just because we sat down and this extra charge was not specified anywhere for the client to see. The person at the counter was very rude when he confronted us. I would say to stay away from this type of establishment.

name: Leo Babayan
review: Place was one of the only spots open late so we went without checking the reviews, we spend $49 euros for 3 ppl and we all got sandwiches, let me tell you the sandwich’s at the gas station and train stations taste way better. It was plan and 1 start out of 5. The workers were nice but food sucks l.

name: john fing
review: We stopped in for coffee and tea very early one morning. Now asking for a black tea with milk on the side is not a usual Italian request. Accomplished, water in a pot steaming hot, good quality tea brand and milk on the side in a little jug. Came with a little biscuit on the saucer and a sachet of honey. The coffee was, well, coffee, not great but also not bad, a well made average coffee.

name: Megan Prosise
review: We sat down and enjoyed two coffees, three cannolis, and another little pastry. Everything tasted amazing. This was about 22 euros. The restaurant is very close to the Rialto Bridge, so I imagine that's why the price is higher because of the tourism aspect. We thoroughly appreciated the very kind service provided by the ladies working there. The only part that was a bummer was that they asked us to pay with cash instead of card. I'm not sure if card would've been accepted or euros just highly preferred.

name: Kiara Charlier
review: just an incredible bakery, the girls and i came back like 4 times during our 5 days in venice!  any and all cornetti are amazing, honestly pick any pastry and you’ll have the best time ever! heads up: lots of pigeons outside!

name: Magdalena
review:
We ate pizza by the slice, croissants with pistachio and almonds, a ham sandwich and drank coffee, everything was delicious and fresh. The only downside was the pigeons that tried to eat our breakfast..
P.S. in the middle of the cafe we ​​left our notarial deed in a white folder, please destroy and throw it away.

name: Jason Lin
review: Amazing, definitely worth the walk. After a gorgeous walk along the south coast of Venice and then along this equally gorgeous street there’s this wonderful spot. The tea smelled so good and tasted amazing with a packet of sugar and the pastry was just as good. So cheap too. I got the “red tea” some type of fruit tea made of wild berries, hibiscus, and rose hips. There’s also outdoor seating so you can enjoy the wonderful atmosphere.

name: Sozaev Uruzmag
review:
Absolutely great 10/10.

Very tasty food. Especially pizza with sausage and margarita
Great staff, welcoming, fast serving and heartwarming

Prices are affordable, low compared to similar places close to tourist spot.
I recommend the place 10/10

name: Michael Stavrakakis
review: Very tasty brasserie in Venice Italy. We stopped there for breakfast one morning and were not disappointed. The coffee was excellent and both the sanwiches and the sweets were tasty. The service was great and we enjoyed many small delicatessen.  We recommend this place for breakfast whole in Venice.

name: Erik B.
review: Very nice cafe/snack bar. Early in the morning you can have breakfast and they have pizzas and paninnis during the day. Definitely worth it.

name: Jacek Lasota
review:
It is a noce place for a quick coffee with some cakes. They also have good drinks.

When I was there there were 3 ladies working. 2 of them were really nice ,but the one who was taking orders and payments were giving an impression like she didn't care, and she was also ignoring me for a moment. I don't speak Italian, and maybe this was a language barrier but I think the staff should be nice to customers.

name: Sirodjah Datzkey
review:
Absolutely best sweets and cakes. Not extremely sweet and balanced textures.
Frendly and experienced staff. Guys if you read it - thank you very much!
Strongly recommended place.

name: Max Urazaev
review:
We spent great time having coffee with astonishing views on Ducale Palazzo, Basilica San Marco and the bay.
The price is higher than average but I believe you pay here for the atmosphere and splendid views. Staff was respectful and caring.
The only thing we could advice for the future is just make a clear disclaimer about price difference to have drinks here or take away.

name: Mirella Tabib
review: The place is quite nice, we stopped for a coffee before we take our boat tour, surprisingly we paid 43 💶 for 2 cappuccino (mini size) & 2 hot chocolate 😏😏. It is soooo expensive and not of value for price. You can have a full breakfast for 4 people for not more than 20 euros. Not recommended honestly

name: DeeDee Cooney
review:
Great views but itineraries crowds are!!!!  Food was good quality.  Coffee quite good.  Waiter seemed to hate his job or the tourists or both!!!!!
There are better places to try particular done in around the small channels.  Great vibe there.

name: Luis Arturo Macias Padilla
review:
avoid it at all costs!
Biggest TOURIST TRAP. Do not eat here.
Beer for more than 15 euros?
Food is tasteless, worst food I have had in my Italy trip. Yes the view is nice but they are ripping people off.

name: baby shark
review: Everything was perfect — delicious food, wonderful wine, and excellent service. The staff were elegant and attentive, and the view of the lagoon made the experience unforgettable. Highly recommend this place if you want to feel the real Venice 🇮🇹✨

name: Jasmin Colello
review: The atmosphere is very nice, regardless of the amount of tourist flooding the square 😅 The cicchetti was okay... One of them was just tuna fish and mayo, which I didn't realize till I bit into it.

name: Marta Skorubska
review: Nice place , friendly stuff . Nice Aperol , croissants delicious,  but Tiramisu was very dry. Beautiful views.

name: Ariadna Elias
review: Bad service, bad atmosphere, and not to mention that LITERALLY 2 simple coffees cost us €20, and the table next to us paid another €20 for just a water and a Coke. Inhumane prices you don’t expect, since you don’t stop to check the menu because you assume the prices, and then you’re hit with this outrage. I do not recommend it at all!!! It’s a tourist scam!!!

name: Julia Lee
review: Prob one of the best croissants I’ve had ever. It was warm and slightly sweet from the brioche dough. I’m def going to go again tomorrow before we leave Venice bc it was that good. I highly recommend it!

name: Alexandra Woolsey-Puffer
review: My favorite food shop in the neighborhood - great breads and pastries, good selection of wines and local spirits, plus choice meats and cheeses to make it easy to put together a snack to eat back at home or at the canal

name: Berlin Gal
review: This delicatessen shop became instantly my go-to-place for the most delicious artisan made cookies (!! highly addictive), small batch olive oil, craft beer, wines and so many other tasty products that are carefully curated from small and very often local makers. I also enjoy chatting with Luisa and Alessandro, the owners, about locally made and sourced foods. A hidden gem in Cannaregio!

name: Yasmin
review: Fresh and crunchy pastries. The employee working there is super sweet and was helpful in explaining each item with the origins and ingredients. We ended up buying pizza, salty and sweet pastries and got free tasting pieces.

name: Grace
review: Cute little Italian “grocery store” with freshly baked bread she sells by weight, coffees you can order, wine bottles, and other foods. The owner/staff was super friendly and made my morning so lovely. I tried one of the freshly baked breads and a cookie with a coffee and I wish this was near my place (I was only there on vacation) so I could go all the time.

name: Farrah Mojabi
review: Great bakery we found in Venice!! Friendly staff and wonderful baked goods. We went there several times and even picked up some necessaries that she  Carrie’s such as milk, eggs and bottled water!!

name: Paul Adelman
review: Delicious bakery and coffee in Cannaregio neighborhood of Venice! And very friendly!

name: Ray Hubley
review:
My wife and I stopped by earlier this week before heading home to New York and wanted to share how taken we were with your shop, the people working there and the great selection of local and artisanal products.
Tonight, we will drink the Maramao Pinot Grigio we brought home with us and make a wish to return — not just to the store, but also your beautiful city. (Hopefully, the olive oil will last for a few weeks!)

Thanks also for steering us to VENETIKA — where we had two unforgettable dinners.

Warmest regards,
Ray & Deborah / New York City

name: Ken Tao
review:
Walked passed many many local cafes, looked at many gorgeous food items on display, but didn't like sitting outside in the steamer and watched by countless other tourist like animals in a zoo, dodged into this Starbucks and went upstairs to hide out in the most precious resource in Venice - AC.
There were 2 staff called Ana, one is a cashier and the other is supervisory.  Both were helpful and able to converse in English.  The food there was local, forget about everything you see in the US.  I had a club sandwich!  It was good despite it might have sat there from morning until noonish when I ordered it.  From the display to the sandwich toaster, grilled until there was a crosshatch.  Cut in half and shared.  The bread was regular white bread size.  Costed only 7 Euro, it didn't have the raw ham but can't complain.   Separately ordered a raw ham croissant, that was small but it was good.  There was this melon pearl iced drink that I was told it was pretty good.  Not here in the US yet.
There were power outlets everywhere upstairs.  The setting has a couple large tables for 5-6 and then lots of smaller table for 4 and then some small rectangular bar tables.  It has the best of the local Los Angeles store setups (regular plug + USB power, chairs, napkins, restroom, inside seating).  No refill, Starbucks App does not work- asked in detail with the Ana's.  DO NOT remotely think about half and half in your coffee.  Polizia might show up.  You won't find it either anywhere.  The tables were all bolted down to the floor.  The two lay back chairs upstairs were not recommended for it's low to the ground/hard to get up and lack the length to support your head weird low angle but you'd need to hold you head/neck strain.  Ground floor is extremely crowded because of the constant long line for the restroom. Americano was superior than the ones here.  Can't comment on the other drinks as I didn't try.
One thing that is somewhere alarming but inescapable was the sewage smell that was Venice in hot weather.

name: Melanie Sarah “LiA” Eggink
review:
Overcrowded, super loud.
What’s somehow ok for the only Starbucks in Venice. But how the situation is handled by the staff is not appropriate.

The line for the toilet is through the whole restaurant between the tables. Tables are not cleaned and dishes and plastic cups from 2 earlier guests piling on tables.

Staff doesn’t care, but tells me to not place my bag on the stairs to pack the mug I just bought, for safety reasons.
It took literally 30 seconds. 😜

name: Dione
review: I speak English & staff were very understanding while I ordered. I young lady remembered me from the day before & knew my order, so excellent service

name: Paulina 89
review:
We had a very disappointing experience at this Starbucks in Venice.
We only had a couple of hours in the city, didn’t want a big lunch, and decided to stop here just for a coffee and  food and to use the facilities. The place was understandably busy, as Venice is full of tourists, but the service was extremely poor.

The coffee arrived quickly and looked nice, but the toast we ordered took over 20 minutes to arrive, by which point the coffee was already cold. When the toast finally came, it was half cold, with artificial-tasting cheese — honestly inedible.

To make matters worse, there was only one toilet in the entire  large) Starbucks, and it was out of order. We only found out after ordering, as the tiny sign was hidden behind products at the counter. Considering they serve drinks and food, having no accessible toilet is absolutely unacceptable — especially in a city like Venice, where public toilets are very hard to find.

Overall, this was a poor experience. We came to Starbucks expecting reliability and basic comfort, but instead left disappointed. I will definitely avoid this location in the future.

name: Monika Esseová
review: The coffee was good, but the toilet could have been cleaner, and there was only 1  available.

name: Cellist ESTHER
review:
The steps are really friendly and fast. I was happy with the service they provided.
The inside of the store was pretty clean and well organized. If you hate waiting in line, I recommend you go in the morning.

name: Fi Lip
review: Franchise owner, can you please review the toilet maintenance in this starbucks. Very inconvenient and totally visitor unfriendly to keep the toilet closed for a long time

name: SaliiH
review: Starbucks Venezia Rialto is often bustling with tourists, making it challenging to find a seat. Despite the crowds, the staff are impressively quick and efficient, so the lines move fast. Great spot for a coffee break if you don’t mind the busy atmosphere.

name: Giovanna Luca
review: I ordered a sugar free number 7 (strawberry, blackberry and raspberry). The menu states it as 100% natural. They handed me a smoothie made with box apple juice as base. Nothing natural about it and super sugary from the apple. The apple juice is not stated anywhere in the menu, water would have been my preference.

name: Shah K
review:
Bar Gelateria Al Ponte: A sweet treat in Murano!
This charming gelateria offers a delightful escape in the heart of Murano. The gelato is absolutely delicious, with a range of flavours that are a must-try. Prices are reasonable, offering great value for money. Definitely worth a stop when visiting Murano.

name: Isaac Melendres
review:
The best Café in Murano for good price!
The the only problem is that you need to pay for the table an extra

name: Kevin McCabe
review: No complaints here. Just popped in for gelato and a coffee (and Prosecco!). Friendly service. The place was clean. Reasonable prices. The gelato was delicious according to the kids. I enjoyed an espresso, and the wife a Prosecco. They had a number of food offerings as well. Also some canned and bottled beer in a cooler. This was a diverse little restaurant. Really enjoyed the convenience of it and the quick and friendly service at the counter.

name: PN
review:
Original Italian little restaurant that has great food and Gelato.
Prices are average and quite a busy spot.
How nice it was to see a toilet inside and no need to pay.

name: David Beres
review: Perfect cappucino here, tiramisu and the pistaccio cornetto are also super

name: Susan
review: Not the most delicious gelato ever, but definitely the best value we’ve had in Italy - €2 for a small cone/cup. The best thing however is the value for money - plenty of seating inside and outside, with a super clean bathroom (since public toilets in Italy can cost you €1 alone!!). Really friendly service as well. Would highly recommend as a quick break while exploring Murano.

name: Lars Thomsen
review:
Very fast service. Fair prices, delicious Aperol Spritz, food and draft beer. Perfect spot for a quick snack and to escape the heat outside.
Even has a terrace in the backyard.

name: K. F.
review:
This is a nice spot for a quick meal. They’ve got a great selection of andwiches, pizza, pastries, coffee, plus beer or wine if you want.

Ordering is simple-Look around and identify what you want. Pay at the cashier counter in the center (cash or card’s fine). Give your receipt to the counter, and they’ll get your food ready.

The staff speak enough English to make it easy, and if you’re not sure what to order, just take a photo of the item and show the cashier—it works. Food comes out fast, and I found the portions are big.

There is seating for around 60 people upstairs. Just clean up your own tray when you’re done.
Don’t come expecting five-star service—it’s a train station, not a fancy restaurant. Place your order, grab your food, and move on.

Heads-up: the toilets are paid, but they’re clean enough.

Iced Caffe Latte 3/5
Caffe Latte 5/5
Conchiglia Latte Panna 5/5
Croissant Simplice (Plain) 3/5
Tonno e Cipolla (Pomodoro, Tuna) Pizza 5/5
4 Stagioni (Ham, mushroom, artichoke) Pizza 4/5
L'Audace (Roastbeef) 3/5

name: Lonnie McDonald
review:
Relaxation & Coffee at the train station Santa Lucia on the way to Venice Italy

This is a food court area

Note: one of the few places that would not take American Express Credit Card

The area was bright and clean.

The staff was not friendly.

The atmosphere was get your stuff and move on.

The trains were close and the Venice Grand Cannal was outside.

It was raining outside , the water was choppy and water taxis aren’t inexpensive.

name: Hugo Krajnc
review: Quite good spot for food at the Venice train station. It’s not restaurant-quality food or service, but to avoid having to go to an overpriced restaurant in the city itself, or to get a quick meal to eat while on a train it works perfectly well. There’s enough variety of food and sandwiches, and a sufficiently large seating area upstairs

name: Wisanu Tuntawiroon
review:
*Relaxation & Coffee* at Santa Lucia train station is the perfect spot for a quick break amidst the hustle and bustle of Venice. Conveniently located right at the station, it's an ideal stop for travelers needing a moment to unwind before or after a journey.

The coffee here is excellent, offering rich flavors that truly hit the spot. Whether you prefer a strong espresso or a creamy cappuccino, you'll find something to enjoy. The ambiance is laid-back and casual, providing a relaxing environment where you can recharge with a drink or light snack.

name: Stephanie
review:
A clean and serviceable area to wait for your train. Prices are what you would expect for a train station.

For the food stations, you have to pay at the cashier first who will then give you a ticket to take the kiosk to have your food items prepared. We had a prosciutto panini and it was actually very good. We enjoyed it on our train along with some cold beverages they have available.

There is seating both outdoors and indoors on the upper level. Toilettes available upstairs to customers.

name: Lara Li
review: I bought €5.90 panzerotto and it was empty inside. The guy said it has cheese and tomato. Scammers

name: AN LU
review: Seeing that I was Asian, they deliberately didn't take my order. Then I said I came first, but they still toasted the bread first and gave it to others. The attitude when serving the food was very bad. Disgusting.

name: Justen Benck
review: Extremely over priced disappointment. And rush payment.  Thought it would be a nice place to relax and eat before a train a little over an hr wait but was presented with the bill and card reader before taking menu away. Food came super fast but that's because it's cheap  . My wife's food came in a Takeaway bowl of sadness. I had the have panini wrap of despair. The smoothies we got were the only good thing. 2 drinks bowl of sadness and wrap of despair( 1/2 wrap Not even whole wrap loaded with wet mushy lettuce and small clump of cheese little bits if ham?   A slice of tomato)  served on paper plate and plastic ware, we finished neither because it tasted so bad. 51.50€ for 4 s.all items. Save your money buy a train ticket somewhere new instead.

name: Yana K
review:
One of the best cappuccinos in Venice! Price just 1.80 euros.
Great local cheap baked homemade goods.

Advice to pay a visit to try local fresh pastries.

In this review I highly discourage you to go for “the best coffee” to the place called “Caffe Del Doge” where cup of coffee is around 6€. Save your time and energy.
Cheers!

name: LJ
review: We made a special trek over to this part of Venice to visit this place. It has been run by multiple generations in one family. It is so endearing to watch nono making the donuts. Speaking of the donuts, some of the best we have ever tasted. A perfect combination of crispy outside and soft and doughy inside. Worth the visit. Plus, you can spend time in a less populated neighborhood of Venice.

name: Lily Fryer
review: We tried their fugassa, Bussola buranello, and classic krapfen, all really nice! The bakery smells incredible when walked in, owner very friendly!

name: E R
review: Friendly service, great coffee (one of my favorite from today in Venice). Really tasty. I recommend!

name: POP-UP HOUSE
review: During last few years the quality of krapfen has dropped dramatically low, unfortunately:(

name: Andrew
review: Really nice bakery, worth a trot. Happened upon this shop after eating across the street. I forget the names of the three items we purchased but all were delicious and cheaper than they could get away with. They have a donut-shaped regional pastry that was really great and happened to be vegan. My very selective vegan partner loved it. We wish we bought more of that and would welcome another vegan item or two.

name: G B
review: Smaller pastry shop with less diverse of a selection as the other shops. However, they make up for this with their dedication to quality. They are family owned and operated. It's not as busy as tonolo but it is a bit better in my opinion. Definitely worth paying a visit if you're looking for authentic venetian pastries.

name: David MH
review: Wonderful pasticceria in Dorsoduro - lovely coffee and pastries as well as homemade jams etc. Very friendly. The fritelli with zabaglione we had during the carnevale were the best things we ate in Venice during our trip.

name: Noor Yassine
review: We dropped by this cafe in Venice for a mid-afternoon coffee break. Their Cappuccino was delicious. Their veggie pizza was average, kinda disappointing how it was not wood fired & we’re literally in Italy. Their outdoor seating was open with plenty of fresh air & pretty views. Only con was that the owner was kind of rude & wasn’t as welcoming when we placed our order.

name: Nadya Fetisova
review: We stayed next door, and came for breakfast the day we were leaving. Esther, the owner, was very nice and welcoming, and explained the menu for us. Service was fast, coffee was great, all good impression. I also liked how they bring coffee in big bright cups all colour mixed, very cheerful. They also sell packaged coffee, we got it as a nice souvenir from Italy.

name: Jennifer Claros
review:
DO NOT go to this place. They are very rude. Specially the owner. We were ordering capuchinos inside and the guy told us to go outside and get a table, the owner grabbed my 9 year old daughter and pulled her outside and told her they don’t serve capuchinos for 5 people. My husband tried to ask why and told us to leave. This is the guy who grabbed my daughters shirt. YOU DON’T TOUCH, you need to be respectful.
To the old owner guy- if you have a business with a lot of tourists you need to be respectful. If you are not respectful then close your business because we as tourists are giving you money we are not begging you for anything.

name: Craig Bentley
review:
Valerio  and Ricardo hosted us today on2 separate sittings.

You could not ask for a nicer or more welcoming restaurant to eat and drink.

The drinks are fresh and fast as soon s you order. As well as the foot and ice cream.

I will always come back here.

Highly recommend!

name: Noemi Garcia
review:
wish I could give this place zero stars. From the second we sat down, the staff (guy in the picture) was unwelcoming. Our group of five simply wanted to order cappuccinos, but we were told that they “don’t serve cappuccino to five people.” They refused to explain and instead told us to leave. The guy put his hand on my niece. YOU DON’T DO THAT, especially to a CHILD.

This was hands down the worst service I’ve ever encountered. If you expect even a basic level of respect as a customer, do yourself a favor and take your service where you’ll be respected!

name: tokozon
review: The group of young girls may be treated ok here. Outside seatings are nice. There are several kinds of coffees with photos in the menu. That was thoughtful, but young waiter was super rude. After seeing the other reviews, it did not surprise me. Just to be aware of not getting a friendly service. They may be tired of dealing with tourists.

name: Yuriy Yakovenko
review: I should have checked google reviews prior to going there. They brought me a cold cappuccino. When I pointed that out, they just added some hot water, so it didn’t taste like anything at all. No apology. Paid 24 euros for just two croissants, watered down cappuccino and horrendous service. Avoid this place. There are a lot of good places around.

name: Qatar4ever
