review: A hidden gem in Venice. Definitely check out the store. They got cold drinks and a great staff.

name: Jason
review: They charge tourists more money than what is posted on their menu. Food and drinks were very bland also and the employees were unsanitary, I saw them grab food with bare hands after touching money.

name: Federico Ascolani
review:
Small bar near the station. Service was good and fast.

Food was aceptable. Good variety of food and drinks for a small bar.
Prices were high.

I don't recomend buying Orange juice, is just is a small glass of juice for 4,5 euros.

The place was normal. Noisy. Very concurred.

name: Darren Cox
review: Didn't have food. 11:15 am. 3 coffees and a can of pop, €10.30. Staff were friendly. Handy rest stop waiting for coach to the airport.

name: Elias C
review: The menu prices are half of what they charge you if you are a tourist. They make prices up when you check out.

name: Byron Khan
review: Great service fresh Juice was serve with the smile ! Fair price the location is right near to the parking

name: S_Zeel Pdf
review: Cocktails are bad, the service is worse and they charge you more just because you take a seat, they argue that the prices are different in the menu, however there are no extra fees advertised anywhere. THEY JUST CHARGE YOU MORE FOR BEING A TOURIST.

name: Casper Singh
review:
To the naked eye this looks like any other corner store. When we walked in we were greeted with weird aggressive looks from two of the customers eating and from the worker.

We wanted to buy 4 waters and the guy working there looked at us like we had just insulted his whole family tree. When we paid and said ‘thank you’ he turned around, rolling his eyes. Guy eating near the exit stared at us like we were breaking the law.

After seeing the reviews, I’m glad we only got water. Don’t go. Buy your things somewhere else.

name: Chan Le
review: We had a horrible experience here, first they lied about the prices on the menu. When I confronted the lady she just pretended not to understand. I was going to let it go but I got very sick after from the food I ate. The place was very dirty with no soap in the bathroom, do not go!

name: Marlon Blake
review: This place was solid. We randomly found this spot and glad we did. While the food was just ok the wine selection was amazing. I can see why folks like this place especially during warmer times. We traveled during the winter so seating was only inside. The staff was awesome.

name: sunisa wanmuen
review: Cozy café with a mix of modern and vintage style. Friendly staff and affordable drinks. Great vibes!

name: Ben Carey
review:
Nice a quite place for a drink. You get chips with the drinks
Food also looked very nice.( people.sat next to us looked like the enjoyed what they ate.)

name: Dennis H
review: Tasteful, good ambience and quick service. Communication was clear, we were warned that 1,5 hours later the table was reserved so we were happy that they let us sit and did not rush us. Good food, drinks and view. Everything necessary for a solid Venice experience

name: Natalie Perme
review: We ate here for lunch today and enjoyed it so much! After coming from touring Doge’s Palace, we were looking for a quiet, scenic spot to eat and get away from crowds - and this was perfect! Just saw it on Google maps and it had good reviews, so we sat down to give it a try. The cicchetti platter was delicious, as were the meatballs. We got glasses of wine after asking the waiter for recommendations, he was very helpful. He also brought extra non-fish cicchetti to our table after hearing that my friend wasn’t a huge fan of seafood (unlike me)- very sweet of him and it was also on the house which was extra generous. We watched a painter by the canal making lovely art and enjoyed our peaceful lunch tucked away in the canals. We were the first people at the restaurant for the day (arrived just after 12pm), but so many people sat down in the time we were there or took cicchetti to go, seemed like many locals. Can’t recommend enough!

name: Rebecca Hanley
review: Nice setting but the service was so terrible. We were in the middle of asking the waiter for a wine recommendation when he suddenly began helping another table. We had to call him back after waiting 10 minutes to order, we ordered and we were left 30+ minutes waiting for a beer and a wine only to be told there was no beer left (fine we ordered two wines) we were then left another 10 minutes waiting for 2 glasses of wine? The waiter didn’t even acknowledge the delay. Honestly would not recommend given the extremely poor service.

name: Jordan Byrne
review: Staff really friendly and attentive. Lovely spot for a wine away from the hustle and bustle.

name: Hilda Obeng-Kingsley
review: The service and the drinks were amazing. It's a great place to relax and have some reasonably priced cocktails. The servers have great attitudes and are very friendly.

name: Yana K
review: I cannot judge anything else’s mentioned in reviews, but cannoli at truly the best I’ve tried. With actual mascarpone and not some cheap oiled cream.

name: A L
review: A decent bar and well worth a visit if in the area. Our cocktails were not the best we had enjoyed on this trip though. Close to a canal where you can sit and chat.

name: Lily Mai
review: Servers were incredibly nice! The restaurant is small but clean and cozy, and the pizza my friend and I shared was a perfect snack/lunch during our day in the city. Thin and crispy pizza if you like that :)

name: Akınalp Fidan
review: We had aperol from here two times. Can easily say that it was the best aperol we had in italy and we had aperol in lots of cities and places. Its cheap, just enough ice( not filling entire cup with ice) and delicious. The servers were also very nice too

name: Montserrat Islas
review:
I want a picture, but we ate it so fast. The service was so good and attentive that the girl at the front explained everything patiently. We tried Cicchetti, and they were delicious, €2.50 each. We also had coffee, even though the cups were small.
The place is clean, and we didn't have to wait for seats. I recommend this place!

name: Michelle Reindl
review: Stayed close to here during my recent visit to Venice. Pizza, pasta and drinks were great. The spritz window was so fun! Staff was wonderful and made us feel at home.

name: Oksana A
review: Food was fine, but there're complete unsanitary conditions. The cutlery smelled of a dirty rag. I saw that the waiter went to the restroom wearing gloves, which he then used to serve drinks

name: Britt Furlanis
review: Great air conditioning, clean toilet, yummy Aperol Spritz and pizza. Thank you, Grazie!

name: Vuscor V.
review: they dont do separate checks, filthy cutlery straight out of the pack, overpriced. service charge is normal, but they also include a mandatory tip. i believe it's listed as a "beauty salon" to avoid legal troubles so i requested an edit. hopefully they will be closed for their hygiene problems and fraudulent fees. the food is not worth the money, the service is terrible, the place is dirty.

name: Daniel Jonsson
review: Bought a small pastry that tasted OK. However, the staff felt rude and stressed, so it wasn't a pleasant experience to visit this cafeteria. They made me feel like I was in the way for other customers.

name: jernej zivic
review:
The worst service in my life!
The waiter was agressive towards guests and potential guests. He blew them away with: WE DON'T SERVE COFFEE OUTSIDE....

name: WAR Al
review: The food is good ,and the staff are nice

name: Antonio Grana
review: 16€ for two warm cappuccinos and two day old pastries. Not only theft but the toilet is not cleaned. Don’t think because you are stopping there before going into the city that it will be cheaper. Up the road are a dozen cafes with the same drinks for 1/3 the price. Disgusted!

name: Alexander Bayona
review: The waiter took out the garbage and didn’t wash his hands before serving us. So disgusting. We asked to change or food and drinks and they refused, so, also, too expensive. No recommended at all

name: Daniel
review: Very expensive, not worth it.

name: SIMIUC LIVIU DANIEL
review:
Te clava el cafe a 6€ como les da la gana , no llevan los precios puestos.hay sitios con vistas mejores en el centro y 1.80€ capucino.
NU VA DUCEȚI, 6€ PE UN CAPUCINO ,

name: Matteo Mikulić
review: Sas Bar is an absolute gem! The coffee is exceptional – rich, smooth, and perfectly brewed every time. Their selection of pastries and sweets is equally impressive: fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is cozy and stylish, making it the perfect spot to relax or catch up with friends. The staff are friendly, welcoming, and clearly passionate about what they do. If you’re a coffee lover or have a sweet tooth, this place is a must-visit!

name: Jairo Lopez-Sierra
review: Honesty prob the worst gelato I’ve eaten, theirs so many other real authentic Italian gelato that’s so much better than this one

name: Tom Joyce
review: Great value and a friendly atmosphere.

name: Santos Alvarez
review: La trabajadora emplea el 100% de su tiempo en mirar y escuchar el teléfono móvil. No recoge, no limpia, no coloca… no hace nada salvo usar el móvil.  Y tampoco es amable… ¡ un auténtico desastre !  Después apareció otra trabajadora con, exactamente, la misma actitud.   Las posibilidades de que el negocio sobreviva con semejantes empleados son nulas

name: Korneliusz Dziki
review: Nowo otwarty klimatyzowany lokal, espresso 1.20€, Cappucino 2€, Limoncello 3.50€, smaczne przystawki, bardzo miła obsługa❤️ Warto wstąpić na poranną przekąskę przy kawie.

name: Krzy
review: Pyszna kawa, aperol. Miła obsługa, polecam :)

name: francois brigo
review:
Le café est bon mais la serveuse n'a pas l'air heureuse d'être là.
Dommage la place est jolie et elle pourrait en faire un lieu chaleureux.

name: Louis BURCK
review: Établissement géniale. C’est très simple mais fait l’affaire or les toilettes ne ferme pas à clef dommage mon ami aurait voulu aller lâcher les gosses à la piscine

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Rocío Serrano
review: Avoid at all costs! We stepped in to escape the cold — rookie mistake. I wish I had checked the reviews first. The owner was shockingly rude and unkempt, with a sink full of dirty dishes right in sight. When I politely asked, “How are you?” he replied, “Not well, what do you want?” The food was overpriced and downright awful. I genuinely hope you don’t make the same mistake I did.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Pola Zaki
review:
Food was not fresh and we waited 15 min although no one was there in the place except us.
He charged extra €1 when we ate the food inside

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Kamil Zdziennicki
review: Extremely rude owner. You cannot even touch the chair if you order takeaway. Canollo di pistachio was expensive, artificial, not very tasty. Staff talking behind the back about customers (one of us speaks Italian). Not recommending this place.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: V YNWA
review: I was served by a nice lady with tattoo. Not sure if she work there or she is the boss. Another man has a poker face and I think they are Father and daughter. Anyway, the lady was nice, service oriented. Recommended.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Josh Funnell
review: Absolutely disgusting place, dirty and rude owner. Wouldn’t even let me go to the toilet despite ordering. Would never return.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Kristin Shanahan
review: Lovely man behind the counter, delicious coffee and sandwich

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Malen Pugliese
review: We were treated rudely by the staff. We felt unwelcome and uncomfortable the entire time. Definitely not a place I would recommend or ever return to.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Aarne Raudik
review: Price what you see is not true. They will charge you an how they feel like it.  Espresso was 1.50 charge 2.50. Did say if you sit down every item is an extra charge, espresso + extra, Coca-Cola + extra, slice of pizza + extra, slice of pizza + extra. Don't buy anything from there

name: Sarah Petry
review: Great little spot for some gelato and bruschetta, it was great to sit outside and watch the world go by.

name: Shaen Catherwood
review: Lesson from reviews seems to be: focus on the drinks. We paid €36 for two beers, two generous glasses of white wine, and a very (very) generous cocktail. Comfy seats, lovely setting plus the added interest of it being beside a gondola stop. We were very happy. Can’t comment on the controversial cannoli…other than to say that they’re from Sicily and we’re in Venice.

name: Kevin McCabe
review: Grabbed a coffee and some pastries. I don’t remember anything particularly bad about this spot. They had some cold drinks and fans going to chill everyone off. Looked like ample seating outside. No complaints really. Just your average Venetian cafe.

name: xristina_eski
review: We ordered a beer and an Aperol, which were both very nice and refreshing! I loved this place because the view was stunning, and it’s perfect for a quick drink after a walk!

name: Roland Csontos
review: Confusing business with multiple names (Anastasis S.a.S., Bar Ai Miracoli, Antichità). No menu or prices shown, total for 4 gelatos + 1 cannoli was €19, far above local norms. Felt misleading, be aware before ordering.

name: Sierra Shellabarger
review: TOURIST TRAP!! Didn’t read the reviews beforehand, just walked in and ordered because I was exhausted and desperately needed a coffee and didn’t want to have to walk another block lol. My mistake. Paid €9 for an overpriced Latte macchiato and the worst cannoli I’ve ever had the displeasure of eating. That was not even a cannoli, it was a cannoli shell filled with frosting that tasted worse than canned Betty Crocker. Couldn’t eat more than a bite. Even the cherries on it tasted horrendous. I wish I could give it 0 stars. View of the church was nice though

name: Juan Antonio Garcia-Mendez
review: The best mojitos we’ve had in Venice.  Would definitely come back for another.  Oh we’re back already after our Gondola ride that we picked up just next door.

name: michel azzam
review:
Overpriced, not worth it at all, not recommended!

We ordered 2 hot chocolate and 2 Fornarina with a total of 19€!

On top of it, the Fornarina was extremely bad, that I had to ask the server how often do you change them since it’s made with creme, he said: “every 1 week!!”
It was awful!! I Informed him about that and he couldn’t care any less.

Not recommended at all!

name: Shayla Raine
review: First time trying an aperol spritz and it did not disappoint. The bruschetta was fresh and just the right snack on the beach. Decent prices for good quality.

name: Emma Nedelcu
review: A lot better than expected vs reviews. Seems like last year’s reviews complained about poor/no table service. Well now they just have big signs saying “no table service”, problem solved (the staff certainly don’t seem happy to be there and won’t spare a smile or eye contact for you). We ordered and got one of those buzz back things for when the food is ready. The food is better than it has any right to be for a place where we were surprised they even cook anything there, but that’s Italy for you!

name: catrine Waltersdorph Hansen
review: It seems like this bar doesn't like to serve anything for anyone who is a tourist. The attitude is so bad and poorly. When ordering it feels more of a pain than anything else. The service is shameless. I gave them 3 different opportunities, over 4 days, just to let the benefits come to the dough. Unfortunately, same bad poorly attitude

name: Zoltán András Dániel
review: Everything was delicious, the staff is cool and kind

name: Jules Philpott
review: Scruffy place, no atmosphere, but cocktails were good!

name: Buck
review:
We didn't eat here, just had drinks.  But I can understand staffs frustration with tourists as British couple in front of us had come back to cancel a lasagne order (who orders take away lasagne on the beach ?).  Still the Italian histrionics of bar staff were fantastic, particularly as they confirmed they had not started cooking the lasagne.
Points awarded for "basil faulty" style !!

name: Andrea Plesarová
review:
I highly do not recommend this bistro, if you are a foreigner. We left without any service provided. We came here with my husband just to enjoy a drink and some finger food. We were sitting at the table quite a while,  no one came to take our order. Later, we just went to the stand for order. Bartender spotted us, asked, whether we came for just drinks or for food too, we said food too, so he give us sign to sit at the table.
We were sitting at the table for like 20 more minutes, still no service. The bartender, meanwhile, took order from a couple sitting right next to us, served them food and drinks, and totally ignored me and my husband. After more than 30 minutes of waiting,  we just left without any service.

name: Mark Dunne
review: Good vibes on the beach  in Lido... managed to find a San Miguel beer and the cocktails are good as well, prices reasonable

name: Ruslan Salikhov
review:
🛑 Very disappointed with Bialetti moka pot (€100 model)

I trusted the Bialetti name, but the experience was frustrating.

The interior coating is difficult to clean, and residue builds up quickly. Even after careful use and gentle cleaning, the inside never looks clean.

The outer finish started fading and changing color after just a few weeks.

Coffee started tasting metallic, which is worrying for a high-priced item.

I contacted support — their advice? Clean it with baking soda (which is abrasive) and “watch the temperature”, but they gave no specific guidelines.

Honestly, this product isn’t worth the money. It looks great out of the box, but quickly loses quality. I expected better from Bialetti.

Wouldn’t recommend based on my experience.

name: J. Lepianda
review: Great selection of moka pots and kitchenware. A must-visit for coffee lovers in Venice.

name: Yixuan Lu
review: I had the worst shopping experience in the 2 stores of them during my 2 months travel in Italy. To be fair, I like the products very much, but the staff really need better training. Firstly, they didn’t remind me that I can do tax refund when I spent 90€ there for the first time. I came there again for buying gifts for my friends, but the staff in the bigger shop was VERY passive, she didn’t even answer or smile at me when I said hi, and was giving quick answers like “no we don’t have” in a cold way, instead of introducing the products. So I went to the smaller store, where the staff was nicer and did tax refund for me this time. But when I arrived at the airport they told me the passport number was wrong - the staff in Bialetti wrote I for 1… I was literally speechless… My experience told me this is not an occasional case. If you ever visit their store, be sure to skillfully dodge all the staff’s traps and obstacles before finally locating what you came for.

name: Yuşa Nuri Güngör
review: Super super friendly staff. I didn’t buy moka pot because I just bought my bialetti a few months ago. Next time I will buy moka pot with italy 🇮🇹 colors.

name: Sofía Piña Félix
review:
All the things they sell are divine, but the service is beyond terrible, the girl that was there didn't answer any of my questions, didn't help us at all and when I pay she took the money rudely and barely said a word.
TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE

name: George StandingBear
review:

name: Nariné K. Q.
review: Very unkind and impolite saleswoman, being a loyal customer of Bialetti I don’t recommend at all this shop. The shame for the brand !

name: Abelardo Villarosa, Jr.
review: Beautifully designed store with a complete array of coffee brewers and kitchen equipment. The famous Bialetti Moka Pot in different variants are available. Got e 6-cup Dolce & Gabbana Limited Edition Moka Pot and Espresso Wafer Cups. Worth a visit.

name: Arevik Ford
review: Far from your typical tourist menu, this corner spot overlooking a piazza serves up delicious food and great service. Good variety on the menu and even entrees are well portioned. Would come back here again!

name: Williamson Transport, LLC
review: we're sitting inside and saw some bread drop on the floor,  next to the blue glasses.  They picked them up and put them back into a bread basket.  YUK!  Guess it's better not to know what happens to your food sometimes.

name: Quentin Brandwood
review:
Fantastic food in the centre of Burano. We had the fried sardines (with a side of "French fries" which were fried thin cut potato, I was a bit skeptical when they were suggested but yes order these) and lasagne both were exceptionally good.
The menu is in multiple languages and the waiter that we had spoke good English. There is a small cover charge (1,8 euro each) which is on the menu. This was in a nice out door setting in the middle of Burano. Easy to find and well worth the visit.
Highly recommend eating here.

name: Karel Petrášek
review: Nice looking place at the square, so we decided to celebrate our engagement there. At first, we read through the menu and decided to get drinks only, maybe dessert. Although there was nobody inside, and at the time maybe 5 tables occupied, nobody noticed. So we picked our seats and sat down. In about a minute or so waiter came and asked if we’d like to eat. As soon as we told him we are there probably for drinks only, he immediately asked us to move to a different area of the restaurant, as “our” seats were for lunch customers only. (No sign or obvious hint to assume such thing). As politely as he asked us to move, we stood up and left and took our business to much nicer establishment. Not surprised the place was almost empty.

name: Carmen Macota
review:
The food is good here, we had fried fish plateau, spaghetti with squid ink, spaghetti bolognaise and spaghetti with sea food and their famous biscuits.
The food came pretty fast and the waiter was nice.

name: Steven Cheng
review:
Great food, great drinks and Great service!

Very inviting of the outside seating area and wonderful seafood selections !

We stopped by to having lunch, very friendly staffs speaking enough English for you to order your meals!

We hope we can visit this little island and this restaurant again!

name: Fiamma Seda
review:
Let me start with the warmth and happiness we received as soon as we approached the restaurant, Fabio (the owner), his family and employees super respectful and 100% attention to details. Their food is extremely fresh, from their cocktails, to their fish tapas all the way to they heart melting FIG CAKE (i wanted to steal the recipe!!!) no joke the BEST experience by far.

They let us sit were we wanted, we love a specific cocktail and they made it for us, introduced us to an unfiltered local beer, brought us some tapas for my mom and myself to share & finally a fig cake (you’ll want to take home)

Please have in mind these islands depend 100% on tourism and the pandemic hit them hard so please go support and tip these lovely people.

Thank you Fabio, Emely and the team.

name: Adelina D
review: The restaurant is exactly in the main square. The service is impeccable, the waiter was very polite, and the order came quickly. The food was very good, big portions. The sepia ink pasta are delicious, I recommend.

name: Jenique K
review: Food is reasonably priced and nice for the price we paid. Coffee was good too. We enjoyed our breakfast here and we came here for 2 of the mornings we were in Venice. The cafe is modern. And the owners were nice. Do consider coming if you are looking for somewhere to grab a snack or breakfast!

name: Gianfranco Alvarez
review: Great place to have breakfast in Mestre. Very nice people and good selection. The bar itself is very modern and cozy, and the bathroom is extremely clean. Good prices too: Each Brioche was €1.20, each cappuccino €1.50 and fresh orange juice for €3.50. Great place, very satisfied.

name: mahsa shirmohammad
review: I tried this place a few times. I like the vibes and the staff are nice. I had breakfast several times and everything is always fresh. Prices are fair and in the usual range.

name: Sandy Lam
review: Food was good but pizza likely frozen or pre-made. Decent portion size and Price around 7-8 euro for pasta/pizza. Great place to sit and have your coffee.

name: Naveen Agrawal
review:
Didn’t expect the best spaghetti pasta I will eat during my trip in Italy will be at a Cafetaria and not at a restaurant or trattoria!!!
The tomato sauce was really well done! flavorful and tasty! Hidden gem!

name: Karol Adamiak
review:
Had really bad experience here. All of the food is prepared from frozen food. You can actually see it haw they are preparing it.
Pasta I have ordered it wasn’t cooked properly and some of the bits were hard...
Good prices for coffee.

name: Maciej Blacha
review: Nice tasty sandwiches,  very good coffee

name: Laura Valenzuela
review:
Unpretentious cafe with a limited selection of dishes, but that doesn't mean is anywhere ordinary.
We had a great dinner with 3 bottles of Pinot Nero, from Pasta Carbonara to Lasagna and Risotto plus Charcuterie and Cheese platter.
And the Brownie was to die for!
Had a blast and staff was so attentive! Thank you for your hospitality!

name: Bia&Pedro
review:
The Pizzeria Birreria Barbanera is simply incredible! If you are looking for a complete gastronomic experience in Venice, it is a fantastic choice. The cost-effectiveness is excellent and the price is unbelievable for the quality of the food. The dishes are delicious and served with perfect service!
​A major highlight is their attention to tourists: the staff speaks English, Spanish, and other languages, and the menu is available in Portuguese, English, Spanish, and other languages. This makes things much easier! It's the ideal spot to eat well, be well attended, and save money. I highly recommend it!

name: Debra Colbert
review: We were so impressed with everything that we came back a second night. Fatima Al was our server. So friendly and accommodating, making suggestions for food and drinks. Our table had so much food, I’m not sure how we managed. The interior of the restaurant was nice, cozy, and perfect for our group of 4. We plan to visit again the next time we return to Venice.

name: Kelsey
review: Found this place on Google and was not disappointed, very glad we came.  Service is great (they even bring hand wipes after you finish your food!!), food was very tasty and a great price.  One of our servers recommended a white wine (the Soave) and we thoroughly enjoyed.  I personally loved the music that was playing too - some good throwback songs in there! We were €66.70 for two mains, a Coke Zero, still water and a bottle of white.  No service or cover charge included by default.  You will enjoy your time here!

name: The LightHouse Porto Santo
review:
A magnificent restaurant in the heart of Venice. A pleasant, welcoming atmosphere. The service is excellent, and the staff are very friendly. The food was a delightful surprise! The best food I've had in all of Italy! The flavors were spectacular, with excellent presentation—a marvel! It was so good that we returned to this restaurant for another experience, and once again, it was worth it. Everyone was very satisfied.

I recommend this restaurant to everyone, and when I return to Venice, it will definitely be a must-visit! 🫶

name: Izy
review: Service good and food arrived quite quickly. Spag Bol was nice. The ravioli was good but the sauce for the dish and for the shrimp pasta wasn’t the best, very strong tomato flavour like a can of canned tomatoes had been emptied onto the plate. Chips nice.

name: Cesar Canales Hortelano
review:
Amazing place with nice people and nice food. It is very special that everything is included on the prize. Not extra charge! It's very important in Venezia where in every place try to get more money for nothing. All the staff are very friendly and kind. For sure all my family will come back!!
Tiramisu and Panacotta are the best!

name: Kristi Simone
review: Nasir was Our later. He was so fantastic and helped us understand the menu. The food here is absolutely amazing. Amazing. Definitely come here. It looks like it is all locals. I feel like we were the only Americans in here. Truly fantastic food. Such a delightful experience.

name: Victor Araujo
review: I'm not one for leaving bad reviews but the salami pizza looks like one I would see at my high school cafeteria. The fungi pizza was better, but the crust also seemed cheap. Taste-wise it was alright but I expected much better, especially in Italy. Service was fine and quick, just the food wasn't good.

name: Nick Razdevsek
review: A true hidden gem in Venice! Stumbled upon it by chance and had the best carbonara and pizza I’ve tried in Venice so far. Authentic flavors, great service, and fair prices..no tourist trap, just real Italian food done right.

name: Vee Baginski
review:
The most delicious food that stood out amongst all the touristic restaurants in the area. Their tiramisu was so satisfying and perfect, had it every day while in Venice guilt free. Not to mention the impeccable arancini, reasonable price points when you take away- and Hugo spritz converted me to enjoying an alcoholic drink- and an individual who’s typically alcohol free.

I have to recommend their pistachio muffin- when warm it just tastes like heaven

name: Ree
review:
We stopped for some food in Venice - which can be very overwhelming for places to try as there are so many options. This place had an extensive menu with some great options.

We got mushroom pasta, pizza, antipasti boards, burrata salad & caprese salad. Everything was fine, the cheese and salad a highlight! The pizza was also lovely.

Definitely by no means a groundbreaking meal but a good place to stop for food if you need somewhere!

name: Stefan Tivodariu
review:
The quality of the food was satisfactory. However, the experience was severely marred by issues regarding price transparency and dishonest service.
The waiter refused to provide a menu with current pricing and instead presented us with a different, more expensive menu than the one available online, resulting in inflated costs. A specific instance involved an order for rum, which was doubled in price and the incorrect brand was served. Furthermore, we encountered problems with the "plate of the day," which was not listed on any menu, and the staff were unhelpful in clarifying its cost. An unrequested and unannounced extra charge for burrata was also added to our bill after the waiter suggested adding it to a dish without disclosing the additional fee. Ultimately, when we questioned the bill, the waiter admitted to using a completely separate menu (not the one intended for customers) to determine our charges.

name: Paul Anderson
review: One of my favourite meals I have had while in Italy! Everything was delicious and the staff were friendly and helpful. I ordered the catch of the day and our server skinned and deboned the cooked fish at our table right in front of me which was really cool to watch! Also had my favourite tiramisu I've tried so far. This place is wonderful. 💖

name: samira
review: We went there like nearly everyday, one time for dinner, breakfast and lastly for lunch. The service is really good, they understood my  mom, even though she spoke French, which is kind of impressive. The prices are OK, the place itself is nice decorated and that comes from a picky person so yeah. It is also not really located near the tourist places so it's not that busy. Overall I recommend this place to ya'll.

name: Andrei Mihai
review: A ok Dinner. The waiter tryied to make us pay more for our order. He tried to play stupid after answering our questions and deliberately hiding the costs... we understood that at the end, and then he tried to make us pay extra for our drinks, bringing a menu from inside... while he told us to look on the internet menu, where the prices were different (half).

name: Sebastian Weißinger
review: nice little restaurant in a quieter area in Venice.  Aranginis taste good and so does the pizza.  For 8€ such a delicious margarita and only 1€ cover price, you can't complain. Value for money is fair, but please clean the water glasses better or polish them dry.

name: Kimberly Briscoe
review: By far the best food we had during our trip to Venice. The carbonara was beyond delicious and may have ruined pasta for me as now nothing can ever compare. The tiramisu was delicious, as well - the best I've had. I love how it was served in a little mason jar; it gave it more of a home-prepared and less of a mass-produced vibe. It was a wonderful atmosphere and the service was great without being overbearing. When I come back, this will be my first stop for food!

name: Liv Anderson
review:
Ask for Fabio as a server if you can!!! 5 stars for him- not so many for the other guy on day 1.

I visited this restaurant twice in our 3 day visit to Venice. One night 1 we had a mojito (best I’ve had all trip), and an Aperol spritz. We ate a capri salad and shared a calamari plate. The capri salad was amazing, the calamari was a large portion and had a thick crumbing which isn’t our favourite, it was still a great meal. At the end our serve was relatively aggressive regarding a tip. He kept pushing and pushing for one. In the end we gave a very small tip… he was not fabulous- BUT Fabio on night 2, he was a great server.

Night 2 we ate the lasagna and a sea food boil bag in which were both good meals, followed by tiramisu and a cake type moose with cream, which I forgot the name. That cake was INCREDIBLE- the server Fabio suggested it to me. I would go back just for that dessert!! He did not disappoint! The service by Fabio was awesome and much better than the first night. He was happy, bubbly and just an overall good vibe!

To top the night off, Fabio gave us limoncello shots on the house. It was a great atmosphere, beautiful views and yummy food and drink. I would recommend this place to anyone visiting Venice- ask for Fabio!!

name: Lars Van der baan
review: Staff was really friendly, our waitress (Paula) was super sweet and gave us really good recommendations. She wasn’t pushy about it and gave us time to think. The view was gorgeous! We had the calamari to share and a bottle of wine which our waitress refilled when she saw that our glasses were empty. A really enjoyable experience, would recommend 🩷

name: Zosia Pietrzak
review:
Risotto was amazing, pizza was delicious.
We love the atmosphere here
Waiter Fabio is the best!!!
We recommend it very much! 🤍

name: MerijnGarry Home
review: Looking out over the water what an amazing location.  So friendly so professional food and service first rate…just relax and enjoy

name: Thao N
review: Great service even in a super busy evening with full of people waiting for firework. Everyone there including the lady asked what would we like til the staff who made drink for us were kind and smiling all the time. They were very helpful answering my questions related to my mocktail drink. The drink was yum too! Thanks team!

name: Cheryl Affleck
review: Had amazing meal tonight, food and service excellent. Our waitress was Paulo, she was really friendly and helpful. Put heated lamps on outside and brought us blankets when it turned cold outside. Beautiful food would highly recommend.

name: Cintia Szíjártó
review: We had a pleasant dinner here today. Our waitress, Paula, was very kind and helpful. The food was delicious, we were satisfied with everything. Thank you again, Paula!☺️

name: Omar Patel
review: I've been all around the world and I've never had someone be such a completely horrible person. I'm sure he gets asked about information all the time, but he could have just said "sorry, I think you should ask that person there". Instead, he gets all mad and starts getting upset that I have the audacity to ask him anything and I should only talk to him if I want to buy anything. He says he's super busy when there were no customers either. I also wasn't talking to him, but another person working there. Just a terrible dude.

name: Олег Подолян
review: They start work on time, delicious croissants

name: Elian Ozen
review: El boss of cafe is agresive, becose i didn't see that u need to pay with a card 5€ minimum. And he threw away my café

name: shahar tamir
review:
Eddy is doing the best fresh food!
Highly recommended!!

name: Jakob Kremling
review: Uno dei migliori panini che ho mai mangiato, veramente gustoso e saporito, pure veloce, e per i prezzi di Venezia economico, poi anche il venditore era gentile

name: il boss dei lupi (gianluigi)
review: Ho preso un cappuccino e mi è stato fatto direttamente da asporto, ho pagato 2.20€, prezzo un po’ più alto della media, il latte leggermente bruciato, ma tutto sommato bevibile, servizio super veloce e gentile

name: Jacopo Celesia
review: Orario di apertura alle 6.00 - bar aperto - proprietario scontroso che mi dice: "chiuso, apro tra venti minuti". Risultato: 4 ore di autobus a stomaco vuoto. Complimenti!

name: Giovanni Compagno
review:
Let me just tell you that after eating a hot dog from them, probably either the mustard or the ingredients of the sandwich themselves spoiled; I stayed 2 days with stomach cramps and regular diarrhea every hour; excellent experience and staff always on the ball !

Vi dico solo che dopo avere mangiato da loro un hot dog, probabilmente o la senape o li ingredienti stessi del panino avariati; sono rimasto 2 giorni con crampi allo stomaco e diarrea regolare ogni ora; esperienza ottima e personale sempre sul pezzo !

#reffevefora

name: Kristi Simone
review: Nasir was Our later. He was so fantastic and helped us understand the menu. The food here is absolutely amazing. Amazing. Definitely come here. It looks like it is all locals. I feel like we were the only Americans in here. Truly fantastic food. Such a delightful experience.

name: Victor Araujo
review: I'm not one for leaving bad reviews but the salami pizza looks like one I would see at my high school cafeteria. The fungi pizza was better, but the crust also seemed cheap. Taste-wise it was alright but I expected much better, especially in Italy. Service was fine and quick, just the food wasn't good.

name: Robin G
review:
AVOID LIKE THE PLAGUE!!!!

Lady with fake red hair was so MISERABLE and RUDE. I ordered a cappuccino. She decided for me that I would have a latte instead because cappuccino is apparently only served before 11 am.
While you are deciding for me what I will drink, I’m living my best life traveling the world and you are stuck  behind a sandwich counter heating sandwiches for tourists like me! BOOYAH!

name: Taisiya Taisiya
review:
Discrimination of tourists. The same order, 2 coffees, ordered at three same time with the locals, costed us €2,5 each. While for locals it costed €3,5. After my question if the prices are different for locals and residents the bartender just lied in my face and said “no”.
Also there is no difference between the price for coffee on the bar and coffee at the table (which is usually so in Italy). So do not recommend if you do not want to be cheated.

name: Christian Gagnon
review:
Avoid this horrible place
They just charge you what they want for coffee… we paid 5 euro for ordering a cappuccino and were given a latte

The staff is very rude and thieves… she asked for cash pretending the card machine was not working which was false

Hope they get arrested and shut down

There are other options

name: Max
review: If you’d like to experience the depths of hell on earth, look no further than Bar Cafe Palmisano. The wrap was inedible. Rubber meat and brown lettuce wrapped in raw pizza dough. The workers didn’t care to be there, and why would they? I couldn’t imagine working at such a train wreck of a place. Pigeons, live pigeons, INSIDE THE RESTAURANT AND ON THE TABLES INSIDE!!! This place is a disease. A filthy and wretched excuse for a bakery that genuinely detracts from the beauty of burano.

name: Tesha Blair
review: Great coffee, patio, and atmosphere! Plus a clean restroom! Also, Jesus is coming back soon! Pray and ask Him to come into your heart and repent of your sins to be saved. 💜

name: Stephen Bressi
review: Had a nice lunch. The service was prompt and the food good. My Ham & Cheese Panini was nice and hot. We also had a Burano biscuit. They also have a nice selection of baked goods. I do not understand the negative reviews.

name: Sarah
review:
Went their for lunch and only sandwichs have prices. Staff are very unpleasant as when you ask prices for the drinks look annoyed and complained about it in Italian thinking we won't understand ! Super unprofessional !
Sandwich wasn't bad but small left us hungry still so 8€ for it. Then cappuccino 3€ for half a cup being mousse... Prices as expensive as in London for poor quality food and service. Again could of stay in London for that rather than coming all the way to Italy. Definitely won't go there ever again.
Positive points toilets were clean, and they don't tax you to seat at the table

name: MS P
review: Best for tiramisu. But we went twice it was out of stock. Better to reach before 3 pm

name: Nick Razdevsek
review: A true hidden gem in Venice! Stumbled upon it by chance and had the best carbonara and pizza I’ve tried in Venice so far. Authentic flavors, great service, and fair prices..no tourist trap, just real Italian food done right.

name: Sebastian Weißinger
review: nice little restaurant in a quieter area in Venice.  Aranginis taste good and so does the pizza.  For 8€ such a delicious margarita and only 1€ cover price, you can't complain. Value for money is fair, but please clean the water glasses better or polish them dry.

name: Brandy
review: Best kept secret in Venice. Had dinner in the fairytale setting of the restaurant. Gorgeous views of the grand canal, amazing service and the most delicious meal we had in Italy. Highly recommend the octopus salad, sage butter ravioli, sea bass and tiramisu. All from scratch. It was the perfect evening.

name: Hovig Manjikian
review: We had the most magical post-wedding dinner at this charming restaurant in Venice with our family and friends—truly a night to remember! The staff were amazing with their warm, attentive service, even squeezing in an extra guest at the last minute without missing a beat. The food was exquisite, the ambiance cozy and romantic, and the value was incredibly reasonable for such a special occasion. We couldn't be more thrilled and highly recommend this gem to anyone celebrating in Venice! 5 stars all the way!

name: Mert Gündüz
review:
This was a true hidden gem of Venice. Our waiter was kind, fast, responsive, clear and had incredible knowledge of what he was serving.

Balcony was scenic and comfortable. It’s silent and had very beautiful looking interior. For a relaxed, romantic dinner this restaurant has everything to offer.

Food was delicious and light, fairly priced, drinks have vast and authentic choices.

At the end of our dinner, it started rain, our waiter asked if we have coats or umbrellas, when he learned we don’t have neither they supplied us with an umbrella. Incredibly kind again, thank you for this amazing experience.

name: Lindsey Martin
review: Gorgeous property that was once a private home. Didn’t stay at the hotel but popped in for a dinner. Took some great photos on the balcony and watched the sunset! We had to move inside because of the mosquitos, which the staff was happy to accommodate. The food was not the best, so I would say to come here for a lighter bite, a drink, and the view/checking out the gorgeous interiors!

name: Tony McBass
review:
We dined at Palazzetto Pisani on the 7th of July 2024, and it was an experience we can't stop raving about! The food was absolutely divine, and the view over the Grand Canal? Simply breathtaking. But the true highlight of our evening was the host, Carlo, this guy is just unbelievable. He was a star, very polite, proactive, and radiated warmth and friendliness. Don’t miss out on his special olive oil dip for your bread—it's a game-changer. We’re thrilled we stumbled upon this place; it's a true hidden gem in Venice! Worth a try...

@namhla_mbawuli

name: Juigné Nicolas
review:
Just amazing!
We've been served by Carlo and this guy is just unbelievable.
Thanks again for the moment and the view.
Take the balcony view, you won't be disappointed!

name: Rozy Floratou
review:
We truly enjoyed our dinner, the atmosphere was elegant and relaxed, the staff very kind, and the food was delicious. We chose seafood dishes such as shrimp tartare, cod, and umbrine, and everything seemed perfect at the time.

Unfortunately, just a few hours later, we started experiencing severe symptoms of food poisoning, which required medication and rest. The vomiting and diarrhea episodes were intense and continuous, leading to serious dehydration and extreme fatigue (we were vomiting all night and couldn’t sleep at all) .The situation was even more difficult because we had to travel the next day in this condition, and the whole experience was exhausting and upsetting.

It’s a real shame, as we were initially very pleased with the place. I hope the restaurant takes this seriously and improves food safety standards to protect future guests.

name: Daniel Leigh
review: We ate here a couple weeks ago and haven't forgotten about it. The food was incredible, the view over the grand Canal spectacular, but the real star was the host Carlo. Polite, proactive and very friendly and helpful, ask for his special olive oil dip creation for your bread, amazing. We are so glad we ate here, a real hidden gem in Venice

review: By far the best food we had during our trip to Venice. The carbonara was beyond delicious and may have ruined pasta for me as now nothing can ever compare. It was a wonderful atmosphere and the service was great without being overbearing. When I come back, this will be my first stop for food!

In the end we gave a very small tip… he was not fabulous- BUT Fabio on night 2, he was a great server.

Night 2 we ate the lasagna and a sea food boil bag in which were both good meals, followed by tiramisu and a cake type moose with cream, which I forgot the name. That cake was INCREDIBLE- the server Fabio suggested it to me. I would go back just for that dessert!! He did not disappoint! The service by Fabio was awesome and much better than the first night. He was happy, bubbly and just an overall good vibe!

To top the night off, Fabio gave us limoncello shots on the house. It was a great atmosphere, beautiful views and yummy food and drink. I would recommend this place to anyone visiting Venice- ask for Fabio!!

name: Lars Van der baan
review: Staff was really friendly, our waitress (Paula) was super sweet and gave us really good recommendations. She wasn’t pushy about it and gave us time to think. The view was gorgeous! We had the calamari to share and a bottle of wine which our waitress refilled when she saw that our glasses were empty. A really enjoyable experience, would recommend 🩷

name: Zosia Pietrzak
review:
Risotto was amazing, pizza was delicious.
We love the atmosphere here
Waiter Fabio is the best!!!
We recommend it very much! 🤍

name: MerijnGarry Home
review: Looking out over the water what an amazing location.  So friendly so professional food and service first rate…just relax and enjoy

name: Thao N
review: Great service even in a super busy evening with full of people waiting for firework. Everyone there including the lady asked what would we like til the staff who made drink for us were kind and smiling all the time. They were very helpful answering my questions related to my mocktail drink. The drink was yum too! Thanks team!

name: Cheryl Affleck
review: Had amazing meal tonight, food and service excellent. Our waitress was Paulo, she was really friendly and helpful. Put heated lamps on outside and brought us blankets when it turned cold outside. Beautiful food would highly recommend.

name: Cintia Szíjártó
review: We had a pleasant dinner here today. Our waitress, Paula, was very kind and helpful. The food was delicious, we were satisfied with everything. Thank you again, Paula!☺️

name: Amindha Fernando
review:
We accidentally stumbled upon this place trying to escape the crowds in central Venice. Glad we did as it was a highlight meal of our stay here.

Our intention was only to stop for a drink but when the apertivos arrived, we knew the food had to be good too. Highlights were the bruschetta and the meatballs with polenta. Service was great too and being on the quieter side of the Venuce, it was a beautiful spot to unwind.

name: Stella Gonzalez
review: Stopped here for lunch and it was pretty good! Would definitely recommend the lasagna. Service was great and we got our food and bill very fast. It’s also on a scenic yet not overly crowded street. Definitely stop by here for drinks or lunch!

name: erdal bana
review: We ordered spagetti with mixed seafood. İt was literally magnificient. Very delicious. And the waiter named NILOY was very kind and helpful. Thanks to the waiter and the chef. İ advise everyone this place. İf i ever come Venice again for sure i will visit this place and eat the same food.

name: Алеся Слюз
review:
It was amazing
Thank you very much for the delicious lunch
Very tasty food and extremely nice staff.
I tried the pasta, it's the best pasta I've ever had.

name: Joan Skelton Smith
review: We enjoyed the menu of the day for lunch, 2 courses with several choices of first course for 15.90€ plus coperto of course. The starter bruschetta and rigatoni were both excellent. The salad with the grilled chicken was great, the chicken breast nice but could use more spice.

name: Sadie Coulianos
review: Food was so delicious and well-priced! Amazing mozzarella. Service was very nice. Free limoncello shot. And the best tiramisu I’ve had in my life!

name: Ryan Hough
review: Thank you for a tasty meal in a lovely location with a beautiful sunset.

name: Stacie Givens
review: Tapas was our waiter and made our dining experience even more enjoyable. The food was incredible and I am proud to say I ate too much and enjoyed every bite. Would eat here again if it wasn’t our last night in Venice!

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Bohdan Shchensnevich
review: The only bar that truly feels Venetian. The tramezzini were delicious, and they offer a great variety of spritz. A perfect spot for an authentic local experience. 5/5

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Éric Tournie
review: Lovely, 16€ for 4 drinks, totally fair prices. Lots of locals too :)

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Joe Ross
review:
Total rip off. They will over charge you if you have a drink there.
Don’t mind paying a bit of a premium for Venice but when they blatantly over charge the listed price it spoils the whole experience. Local clientele were lovely. Such a shame the lady owner has no moral compass what so ever. No shame

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Marco Repola
review: Pretty small, very local spot for drinks and snacks. This bar has a lot of heart, the drinks are solid and reasonably priced, the tramezzini hit the spot, and the staff are lovely.

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Justin Laughlin
review: I ordered an Aperol spritz. I watched him make it and he put a literal splash of prosecco. All Aperol and water. I tried to make small talk and was told that only venetian and Italian are spoken in a very rude manner. There are a bunch of small bars around here, I'd go to one of them.

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Chelsey
review: Very beautiful bar in Venice:) Stopped for an espresso and a snack, both were delicious. I couldn’t speak Italian, but the keeper was very kind

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Leeann Cross
review: AVOID lady totally overcharged on the beers 2 small & 2 regular beers, 22 euros rip off steer clear if you are a tourist such a shame a nice little quaint place where we met a lovely local man & his dog

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Markus Christensen
review: Tremendously bad experience for something that should be rather simple – a cup of coffee and some tour planning at my pc. Apperently using modern technology at your own discretion is against the store policy, and they do not tell that to you in a nice way.

name: jasmine xu
review:
Fresh air, beautiful and musical!
This famous and historical icon is located inside of the San Mark's  Square.  It has a lot of outdoor seatings and tables,  all year long and for many years.  It also has a great passion for classic and contemporary music in a formal and professional level!
I have seen those well dressed musicians playing high class music in the warm afternoon sun,  and I really admire their dedication and wonderful work!  This famous,  " World Living Room " ---San Mark's Square, has come to live when the live band and music started to play,  it makes you feel that this Square is full of the life,  music,  and fun!
An open air bar with concert,  playing all year long,  who doesn't want to come to San Mark's Square?

name: berke ayrancioglu
review: A fancy restaurant/cafe located right in the middle of St. Mark's square. Desserts and coffee are nice and waitors are kind but very expensive. Can be experienced once if you are visiting Venice

name: Patrick BICHET
review:
Yes we are in San Marco square
Yes, there are many tourists (including us)
Yes, there are establishments waiting for tourists...
We spent an hour quietly, in the sun, having a drink, eating a tiramisu and it was a very good time.
The service was neutral and without special attention it is true, but very professional...
The rest doesn't matter

name: Farzaneh Niazi
review: Everything was professional, everyone super friendly just overpriced. It was very hot outside in the morning so I asked if i could sit inside even though it was closed, he let me . I liked my order.

name: Ciara Walton
review: The food is obviously super pricy, but the location and the live music make it worth it!!

name: Jumana Amar
review:
The process in the invoice were double the ones in the menu , i was surprised with the prices in the invoice but was in hurry to argue with them but raised to them my concern.
Staff and management are rude, We were 5 people , so once we entered the cafe we asked to merge two tables (cause the tables outside are all for 1 or 3 people mqx) but waiter refused Rudy. Once we ordered he brought us another table and said now i can give you another table! What a rudeness! We understood that this is the policy of restaurants in the area… but still rude and effort to move the table will much less to loss reputation. I hope they can consider this

name: Pablo Balbuena
review: Slow and rude staff, they see you there and they don’t give a s*** about it. We finally got a table, the guy who served us was nice (Adriano). When I paid, I found a difference between the price on the website and what I actually paid. We asked the waiter, he excused the restaurant cause they have prices pre-pandemic and they have a “very complex system” that didn’t update the prices. It’s not his fault but…BASTARDS! NEVER AGAIN.  The live music was good (I means that other people will record the musicians on my face and it’s a bit uncomfortable). Location is great. #neveragain

name: Tobias Herzog
review: It's a trap and really untrustworthy! Higher prices for such a location are ok, but a Sprite for 14€, a Cappuccino for 14€ is ridiculous. This location is ruin the reputation of Venice.

name: Pichet P
review:
Ordinary cafe in my opinion. The cafe location is prime as it is about 100-200 meters from the metro station.

We did dinned in around 10am, it was crowded. Their staff is quite good, but everything is too salty for us.

Fresh fruit seems not fresh, a lot of water is in the plate and it tastes weird.

There is a lot of tables available, toilet is good (nice and clean).

name: Antonio Calabrese
review: Good place for a quick bite! Place was clean and had a good vibe. Location was great right by our hotel and across the street from the train station.

name: Tristian H
review: Really good food and service from Tanvir. I was staying at the hostel (Anda) around the corner and they offer a 10% discount for guests from the hostel! Excellent value for money. And the food was delicious!

name: Eva Batres
review: We were greeted and seated right away. Tanvir was wonderful to have as a waiter. He was very attentive and kept making some jokes :) he also helped me a little bit with my Italian since I’m trying to learn. The food was delicious and we received complimentary cookies at the end of our meal that were really good.

name: Lisa Suen
review:
We had lunch here just because of the location.
The manager is not nice at all. Cabonara spaghetti tastes awful. It’s the worst I have never had. Salmon salad has only a few lettuces with a few salmon, no salad dressing nor any other garnish.
There’s a €2 per person head charge if you dine in.
Overall, Not recommended.

name: Mel Fleming
review: Julia was very friendly, very attentive and the biscuits were delicious!  Food was fantastic, thoroughly enjoyed our meal, excellent price and value for money. Would recommend anyone coming to Venice to check out Sould Kitchen Cafe!!

name: Peter Muraglia
review: Amir is a wonderful waiter! He made perfect recommendations for us from the extensive menu. We thoroughly enjoyed our dinners and look forward to a return visit soon.

name: Lily V
review: A group of us dinned in here tonight and it was a 10/10 experience for us! It’s our first day in the area and we stumbled upon this absolute gem of a spot. As someone who is gluten free going out for dinner is often a hard task especially in other countries but the staff here were so helpful and accomodating. They have multiple gluten free options as well aa all the pastas can be made gluten free, in the back of the menu they had an allergy chart which made our experience so much easier. I can’t recommend this place enough. The overall look and vibe of the place is amazing the staff are so lovely especially Donald, and the food/drinks comes out quick as well. Definitely a place we will be coming back to :)

name: Betül yangöz
review: I used to go there almost everyday. I really like this place. The stuffs are so nice.

name: Isabelle
review: Good and tasty breakfast, in a nice part of Mestre. However staff not very nice.

name: Mona Abbas
review: Delicious Pastries, good coffee, Plesant environment.

name: Iraklis Zaimidis
review:
The place was excellent!
Helpful staff, good prices and excellent products!
We had four days in a row our breakfast there!

name: A S
review: Gorgeous fresh pastries and coffee. Very friendly and accommodating staff as well. I would recommend this shop over all the others in the neighborhood.

name: Alberto Antinucci
review: Great place, great atmosphere, great food, great pastries and, best of all, great looking and extremely efficient and gentle girls working there. ☺️

name: Jan Samek
review: Great place, very good coffee, kindly staff.

name: Viktorija Klepsiene
review: Very good coffee, friendly stuff and delicious pastry

name: Ina Amyza
review: forgot to leave a review for the place that made me fall in love with any pistachio filling! the croissants are flaky and filled, coffee tastes bold which is to our liking. love the ambiance and interior though it is quite crowded and u might have to wait longer for seats and to be attended, but staffs were great regardless and still answer to ur queries!

name: Jen Ticzon
review: Simply amazing. If you are in Venice, this is the only place to get quality coffee. Our family made it a point to make it here every day while we visited. The quality of the espresso and pastries is simply unmatched. For drip, you can even pick your beans, bring it to the counter and they’ll make it for you. Pastries are fresh and when they run out, they’re gone for the day so best to get there earlier if you want to try them all. Brother got a mango smoothie and loved that as well. Friendly staff that help guide everyone through the process of ordering and then paying after you are finished. Helps make you slow down and enjoy— I loved it. The line is nonstop so I think everyone else does as well :) I love everything about Venice but would actually come back just for this coffee shop alone. 20/10 recommendation.

name: Dmytro Sobokar
review: One of the best coffee shops I ever visited. They have great variety of different kinds of coffee beans. I tried everyone and everyone is awesome! Taste is so full of flavour. The beans are amazing quality and they are roasted perfectly. If you are coffee lover you must go here. You can also buy  some beans to have this perfect coffee at home. Extremely recommended.

name: Jenevieve Hornsby
review: We have been here twice and I have a hard time reading it because the food and coffee is good. The style of the place is decorated to be very cute and cozy, but unfortunately, both times we have been it seems like the customer race is a little pushy and rude before they ordered and just overall made the space a little uncomfortable. The reception was a little more welcoming. I think it would be a five star, but the feelings of this place is just off. Pricing of items varies based on if it’s takeaway, eating standing at a counter or sitting down to eat. This can be hard to tell so be aware if you care. So far the cappuccinos I have had here were good and they have the best cereali and wheat croissants I’ve ever had. I definitely recommend this place. I just wish that the vibes there were more friendly.

name: Shruti Dhoot
review: Make sure to join the right queue ‘coz the service can be slow. Need to first order and then pay before leaving. Still dreaming about the pistachio croissant I had here. It was just the right amount of flaky and the filling was delicious. Don’t miss out!

name: Rylan Verge
review:
Excellent spot to go for your coffee and baked good when in Venice. It's best to go early in the morning as it starts to fill up throughout the afternoon. The service staff here are incredibly efficient and are always cleaning the few tables available so that everyone can have the chance to sit down and relax -  very hard workers.

Enjoy your light breakfast by the canal and soak up the sunshine before your big day of exploring!

name: dixie509
review:
I’ve visited 6 coffee shops in Venice. I’m not sure to rank this top or bottom. It’s complicated and personal.

I like flat whites - a rare species in this town. According to smiley barista, only here and ….Starbucks (! Ergh) make them. Great start.

Lots of attention to the coffee, these guys really seem to care. But - and I’m so sad there’s a but! - the milk was, IMO, awful!  The first cup I abandoned after a couple of sips as it tasted like UHT (although the carton suggested otherwise, so maybe semi-skimmed?).

But I really wanted it to work - friendly people, lovely spot - so I ordered my usual, an oat flat white. I checked the carton and was a little nervous as it wasn’t a Barista version….but hey, surely it’ll be ok?  This was almost undrinkable for me. I took one sip and abandoned. Thin, oversweet, horrible. I’d have been better off at Starbucks. :(

I realise (1) I’m in the spiritual home country of the drink I love, so I should tread lightly (2) this was easily the cafe with most potential we visited and (3) coffee is a VERY personal thing - my undrinkable is another persons unbeatable.

But for me, this place has it all, except for the milk. Oh well.

name: Subs Freelance
review: This was a cute coffee place. The coffee was good, freshly brewed. The two flavors we took were average. The red velvet muffin was very soft and well done. The joint was cute and attracted a lot of crowd. The decor was quite unique and attractive.

name: RAYRAY
review: Had an amazing cafe latte with croissant de crema aux fruits before grocery shopping. It was delicious. The lady was very polite. I wasn’t too sure what kind of cafe I’d order since there were plenty of choices available. She recommended right away the cafe latte which I appreciated greatly since I’m an indecisive person. Lots of pastries choices too! I’ll be back.

name: Karl Oskar
review: Very nice place for a coffee. Family owned, and very friendly staff.

name: Humod Fadhl
review: If you’re a tourist they gonna add a lot on the bill we pay 70€ for 5 coffee and 5 croissant

name: Stephanie Vieira
review: Friendly staff. Excellent croissants & espresso varieties

name: Petra Bachrata
review: No complaints.

name: Jessica
review: Im Interspar ist dieses Caffè Veneto zu finden. Für ein Café in einem Supermarkt, macht es schon was her und die Preise sind grundsätzlich fair, wenn die Qualität der Produkte auch besser wäre. Das Essen war lediglich okay und hat teilweise nicht bzw. sehr künstlich geschmeckt. Das Personam war sehr gestresst, obwohl fast niemand vor Ort war. Der Kaffee war ebenfalls nicht gut.

name: Alesja Rusaka
review:
Хорошая кофейня с очень вкусным кофе и выпечкой. Кофе Латте и капучино очень вкусные.
Единственное, не ожидали цену 3,50€ за горячий шоколад. Просили какао, но нас не поняли.

name: Luciano
review: Una garanzia! Vasta scelta di piatti e insalate, componibili anche a piacimento. Ideale per pranzi al volo o aperitivi (c’è anche la formula a buffet nel tardo pomeriggio). Le ampie vetrate davanti ai tavoli danno un’ottima illuminazione. Davvero consigliato!

name: Lama Drebati
review: Got two espresso and paid 4€. It was very reasonable and the espresso was very tasty and strong

name: Avery Watkins
review: Great place to grab a quick snack and a hot beverage. The people are nice a pleasant. Kind of like a nicer airport Starbucks but with more options.

name: Shatha Ali
review: The coffee was great and the service as well. She is nice& friendly! Grazie🤍

name: tia azzuz
review: 👍Desert is delicious 😋😍,  staff is friendly

name: Галина Боровська
review: В кафе багато місцевих відвідувачів,їжа смачна і дуже швидке приготування.Брали пасту з морепродуктів там капучіно.Заклад хоча маленький,проте дуже атмосферний.Розташування чудове)

name: Lisely De León
review:
Cumple su función para
Tomar un café durante tu caminata turística. Tienen promoción de un Aperol Spritz y 3 cicchetti por 10 euros. El café muy rico. La atención excelente. Súper recomendable

name: Tania Leagun
review: Ottimo caffè e il personale gentilissimo!

name: Christopher Ciraulo
review: This was our second visit to Brunch Cafe while visiting Venice, and it was even better than the first! Even during the busy rush of the breakfast hour, the hostess prepared our table and took our order while we waited outside and we sat within 10 minutes. Our food and coffee was delivered within a few minutes of sitting and it was delicious. Avocado toast with Bacon did not disappoint and the Nutella Pancakes were fantastic! They took a little longer than the rest of the food, but the entire staff was on top of it and made sure we were comfortable and happy the entire time. The crew here is top-notch and the food is well worth the wait. Definitely recommend this cozy spot for anyone passing through the busy streets of Venice!

name: David
review:
The food was amazingly fun.
From what I gather everyone goes here for the “pancakes”. They are very light and fluffy, more like a meringue.
Very small establishment, there is a fresh pasta place right next door as well. There are warning of pickpockets everywhere. We never had any issues. Service and food was great all around. In most
Places in Europe we learned you must ask for the check. Or you will be waiting.

name: E
review: Wanted a egg omelette and came to the right place. We didn't make reservations and ended waiting about 3-5min in a que outside, the place was busy was seated quickly. I had the egg omelette and partner had the fluffy pancakes eggs Benedict type plate along with Apersol and cappuccino! Everything was delicious and would recommend if your looking for a non-traditional Italian breakfast.

name: Micheal Ostoforoff
review:
Nice place! Great service, excellent food, decent price.  We all enjoyed our meals!
The fluffy pancakes were delicious!  I had a savoury version with eggs and bacon - and ate everything! Including the salad on the side,  and I don't generally do that at breakfast...
Presentation on the plate was exceptionally well done.
Definitely recommend.

name: Matthew Drinkall
review: WOW!! Amazing breakfast had by all here! Bit of a wait to get a table, but the line moved quickly. Lovely cosy indoor seating. Kind, friendly service with quick food delivery. The highlight was the gluten free pancakes- they were out of this world! How they make them so fluffy and tasty, we would love to know! Highly recommend!

name: Shane J
review:
The Brunch Cafe is located in the San Polo neighborhood of Venice.  My hotel was nearby and when we would pass by we noticed the restaurant was crowded or had a line to enter the establishment.  The hotel we stayed at offered free breakfast, but we decided to changed it up and give the Brunch Cafe a try.

We arrived at 7:30a when they opened.  We ordered the French Toast & Scrambled Egg Platter.  The French Toast has melted cheese on it and the bacon wasn’t fully cooked.  We mentioned to the staff that melted cheese on French Toast isn’t a ‘standard practice’?  The waitstaff didn’t appear to comprehend the complaint or wasn’t interested in remediation of the matter?

Overall, the staff were polite.

name: Matt
review:
This place was so good! We got in with no reservation luckily around 9am at the end of May. When we left there was a wait however.

We ordered a cappuccino and a caramel macchiato to drink. The macchiato was a nice looking drink and very delicious. Not overly sweet either. Cappuccino was pretty as well and tasted like a normal cappuccino.

For food we got the breakfast combo with eggs, bacon, and toast. Came with a salad with a pesto dressing that was delicious. This was a solid, simply meal.

The puffy pancakes we tried the trio of Nutella, mango/coconut, and salmon. All of these were really good! I may swap the salmon out if we were to order again but it was good. The other two I’d definitely order again. They were great.

Service was good as well and nice we got in with no reservation. Weirdly the coffees were 1 euro more on our receipt than advertised on the menu, but no big deal. Was just something to note.

name: Jakob Bierbaumer
review:
The name doesnt do this justice, you can order everything at every time.. i think!
The Menus seem pretty good value aswell but i just went in for a quick snack.

I Got the Classic Avocado Toast and it was exactly what I needed.

Staff is very nice, the Atmosphere is calm & relaxed -oh and they are playing Christmas music in October.
Take from that what you will but to me thats a big plus😂

name: MARLENE LOPEZ
review:
Beautiful authentic cafe in San Polo neighborhood. Fairly priced, a great expresso and chocolate croissant (recently baked) for less than €5
Great place surrounded by locals and younger tourist
Real honey to use as a sweet your cafe

name: Louisa Sampson
review: We had breakfast at this lovely local café. Amazing croissants, especially the pistachio and the coffee is great too! A little off the beaten track but that just makes it feelore authentic. Our total was €7 for two coffees and two filled croissants. Highly recommend.

name: Donát Kováts
review: A small and cozy spot with excellent coffee and super friendly staff. I appreciated the option for soya milk, and the meatballs were delicious. The sandwiches looked amazing as well! It can get a little crowded at times, but overall, I highly recommend this place.

name: Sally-Ann Jackson-Chaplin
review: Definitely not in the most picturesque of areas  in Venice, but it's a charming little coffee shop tucked away in a quiet street. The pistachio croissants are the AMAZING and the coffee is tasty too! Feels like a locals spot but barista speaks fluent English and very friendly :)

name: Bengi Erciyes
review: We had breakfast at this lovely café, and the owner was such a kind and welcoming person. I have a severe allergy, which makes it difficult for me to find safe food options while traveling. When I mentioned this to him, he immediately prepared a fresh panini sandwich (also there are a lot of vegan options! ) for me with a big smile 💛🌱 He was incredibly understanding and polite. I highly recommend this place! It’s located in a charming little street, perfect for a cozy and authentic experience.

name: Damjan Pushkovski
review: Amazing place, amazing coffee, amazing sandwiches. AMAZING!! Highly recommend, even though it only has two tables to sit!!

name: Mary Kate Charron
review: We had our morning cappuccino here every day in Venice and it was perfect. The coffee was delicious, the baristas were quick and kind. Thank you Boresso!

name: Solange Bohling
review: Really nice little place that we went to for breakfast multiple times. It was in a quiet area that was a nice break from the crowds and it seemed like a place the locals frequented. Good coffee and good pastries! Highly recommended :)

name: M Torres
review: A true gem in VeniceDa Mario & I Fioi is the kind of café you hope to stumble upon but rarely do. The coffee is rich and smooth, the croissants are fresh and flavorful, but its the warmth of the service that truly stands out. Piero and Donar treat every guest like family, creating an atmosphere thats as comforting as the food. A favorite among locals, this spot is welcoming, genuine, and absolutely worth a visit.

name: PaulS
review: It’s a bar that happens to serve coffee. This is not a specialty coffee shop. Coffee was fine, nothing objectionable nor terribly exciting either. Did not enjoy the croissant. It was mushy and heavy. A good croissant is supposed to be light and crusty.

name: Cecilia Lu
review: Great coffee with simple local food from  two friendly good looking hosts - a great start to a perfect morning in Venice.

name: Aidas Jasilionis
review: Great service, tasty coffee and pastries. Nice and quiet street, no tourists.

name: Brian Schoene
review: We visited this cafe both mornings we were in Venice in June.  The servers were very nice, the pastries and cappuccino were perfect.  If you are a chocolate lover try a Marocchino!

name: Eric Pieper
review: Great little cafe. The food it was so good we forgot to take pictures ahead is fine. Fun atmosphere and full of locals in the morning. Just what we needed in Venice.

name: Hannah KHoury
review: This place was awesome! Great coffee and delicious sandwiches. The staff and service were friendly and made recommendations. This is a great local spot to start your day!

name: Irene Tan
review:
A superb place for breakfast with a great boss and assistant. Coffee and sandwiches were yummy.
Thumbs up 👍!!

name: Utkan Pazar
review:
--Atmosphere--
The atmosphere is great. The caffé is in a really lovely and gorgeous garden with an old but gold building. The whole building gives a vintage and cozy house feeling. You should definitely visit here if you have the time.

--Service--
Service was good and I liked it but I give 3 stars instead of 4 because they don't allow you to split the bill equally. Surely it is their choice if we are allowed to split the bill equally among customers. However it is my choice to deduct points from this lack of choice regarding the payment process. Other than that the staff was friendly and the service had no delays.

--Food--
I had just the right amount of coffee with my americano with milk on the side, however the amount of coffee was vague regarding their special recipes so my friends looking for some caffeine didn't feel like they had less coffee than what they wanted.

name: Dylan Vogt
review:
Caffé La Serra was such a beautiful spot for a late breakfast. The setting is quiet and peaceful, with the café nestled right next to a historic greenhouse filled with gorgeous plants. Sitting outside was refreshing and relaxing—a perfect way to start the day.

We enjoyed delicious paninis, great coffee, and brioche, all in a charming atmosphere. After your food or drinks, definitely take time to explore the greenhouse. Highly recommend this little gem if you’re in Giardini.

name: Allan Santiago
review: A cafe in one of the oldest buildings on the island that's part garden too? I'm intrigued. It's a very charming space with many opportunities for photos. The space is the highlight and main reason to check it out. As mentioned in other reviews, the food and coffee are decent/ok, and the service is not up to caliber. Though  still worth going at least once.

name: Vanessa Ma
review: Not very friendly staff, pigeons flying into the indoors which is terrible, but probably the best cafe option around the Giardinni Biennale area that’s of reasonable price and with a range of light food options, also pretty nice botanical environment (if there would not be pigeons!)

name: Baiba Grazdina
review: Tucked away in the heart of the Venice gardens, this charming café offered a delightful escape from the bustling crowds. We enjoyed their excellent coffee and a perfectly made panini, making for a simple yet satisfying meal. The serene setting allowed us to experience a quieter, more tranquil side of Venice—a refreshing change from the usual tourist spots. Highly recommend this spot for anyone looking to enjoy great food in a peaceful atmosphere!

name: Georgiana Anghel
review: We only had coffee. The place is amazing, such a gem, far away from the crowd and the touristy area. Coffee was ok. We found it by chance, and it was really nice to sit there and enjoy a coffee with so much greenery around. But the service is bad and the toilet is terrible in terms of cleanliness.

name: Sylvia Sylvie Burger
review: Great atmosphere and view, lovely music, very peaceful and cozy 💖🌻

name: Chun-Han Chen
review: I went there 1 year ago. The food is just okay you can find other good choice nearby. And then they refuse me for one cappuccino, a very tipical breakfast, this year. Just remind you they just want take your money. If your order is not enough, they will kick you out the restaurant even though 50% of seats are empty. This restaurant is just for nice Instagram photos not food.👆👎🤌🤌🤌

name: Kuan Hou “Angus” Lee
review: 7.5 euro for one drink and 2 ciccheti! This wasn't shown on the menu nor told to us, but luckily the staff were kind to do this deal by default if you order accordingly. Drinks were good. Food were good. Amazing place to come and relax after a long day walking around Venice!!

name: Chloe Neale
review: We always stopped by Dolce Vita for their cocktails, and they're the nicest ones we had in Venice. The staff were so friendly. The cocktails made, were impeccable. So much time and detail spent on making them/ making them look pretty etc. They do lots of little light bites as well, which looked inviting.
