I really recommend trying this place out if you have a chance, there are some lovely outdoor tables as well in a quaint alleyway where you can step away from the hussle & bustle of Venice..

name: Lydia Lincoln
review: Absolutely loved this place. Came and visited Venice for 3 nights and kept coming back to this place each night. Alveze makes the best spritz!! Personally loved the strawberry spritz the most. Only €5 too!! Sad to be leaving… 10/10 place in Venice if you’re looking for a relaxed bar with good vibes.

name: William Harrison
review: Excellent spot for a quick lunch in Venice.  The cicchetti we had all was delicious.  I asked one of the ladies for her recommendations and she suggested several of the small sandwiches; codfish, spicy salami, etc.  Not one disappointed, nor did the ice-cold draft beer.  Very reasonably priced and comfortable seating.

name: Jaime Osorno
review: Going to Dolce Vita was a great experience! We went in for the low key electronic music and stay for the food, the drinks!!! The service ❤️ and of course the price!!! It was a very high end experience for a GREAT PRICE!!!

name: Natalia Patterson
review: We came 30min before closing and they still had some cicchetti left. The one with salami was our favorite! The drinks were also delicious, especially the Negroni.

name: Amber
review:
Cute little cafe perfect for breakfast or a mid-day snack. They have the best croissants ever, they are so soft buttery and flaky (I had 5 🤭). The donuts with cream, chocolate or pistachio filling are awesome too.

Nice ambiance with Italian music playing in the background. Staff are smiling and pleasant to interact with.

name: Lou
review:
We walked all the way here from other side of town and it was totally worth it. Beautiful sandwiches and delish coffee (take extra shot if espresso with your oat cappuccino if that’s your usual coffee)
Beef and turkey sandwiches r to die for 🫶🏻 Perfecto

name: Bella Reviews
review: One of the best pistachio croissants I’ve had in Italy so far! I got a second one right after! It’s warm inside, the pistachio is a bit melted. Perfect for a cold morning! Went at 9-10am on a Sunday.

name: Jojee Khan
review:
This was my second visit to 1000 Coffee & Bakery in Venice—and somehow, it was even better than the first. You know a place is doing something right when you find yourself walking past a dozen cafés just to come back here.

The coffee is proper—smooth, bold, and made right every time. You don’t need to overthink your order here; whatever you get, it just hits. And the pastries? Ridiculously good. Fresh, flaky, and dangerously addictive. I had one… and then immediately went back for another. No regrets.

The vibe is relaxed, the staff are super friendly without being over the top, and everything just feels easy. No tourist-trap energy—just great coffee, quality food, and people who know what they’re doing.

Safe to say this place has officially made it onto my “every time I’m in Venice” list. If you’re in the area and looking for a solid coffee and breakfast spot, don’t even think twice—just go.

name: Patrícia
review: The croissants and cappuccinos were excellent; fresh, tasty, and definitely worth trying. The staff was very friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. The only downside is that it gets quite busy, so there was a bit of a line, and we were lucky to find a spot to sit. That said, the view is absolutely amazing, right in Venice overlooking the canal, which makes it a perfect place to enjoy a coffee and pastry.

name: Kevin Barker
review: Just stopped here to grab an afternoon Cappuccino, was pleased to see that they had non-dairy milk options to pick from. By far the most reasonably priced coffee I had in Venice. It was also one of the better coffees I had in my time here. Vibes were nice, sitting right in front of a canal / square.

name: Zena T
review: Great place to grab a slice of pizza and go. Perfect to eat something good and fast if you don't want to loose time in a restaurant. The location is pretty small, suitable for 1-2 let's say 3 persons. I had a slice of pizza, and ice coffee and a bottle of water and I paid 12.5 euros.

name: Cate Carroll
review: A little gem in Venice with coffee and panini for our brunch. Easy to find from the ferry and using Google maps! Outdoor seating facing the Maddalena church and square and close to canal with frequent gondolas passing. Staff were warm and friendly and in spite of efforts to speak Italian, they responded in English for clarification.

name: Suresh Kumili
review:
It's my favorite bakery in Venice. My daily routine was to wake up and head straight to 1000 bakery. The fresh croissants and refreshing espresso would wake me up and prepare for the 25K steps I would do each day :)

Try their flagship croissant, which is with pistachio filling. If you do not like sweet pastry in the morning, you can try their butter croissant as well. It's equally good.

name: Dana Montes
review:
I visited on weekday. The place has inside and outside places to eat. The pizza was nice and the ingredients were good but I won’t say is the best though a good one and the price was really good compared to other restaurants in the area.
The staff is friendly, there’s toilets, the place is clean and overall was a good experience.

name: Sergio (Serg19)
review: Fantastic experience, amazing paninis and ciccettis. Service was amongst the best experienced in Italy. Avoid the many tourist traps and high prices in Venice and seek this place out.

name: Kevin Barker
review:
We got to Venice very late and decided to checkout this Panini place. Unfortunately at 8:00 PM they were all out of cicchetti which we were looking forward to trying with the sandwich. I saw many other places still had them, but it is more of a early day food.

With that said, we ended up getting a panini and it tasted really good, I asked for it to be toasted and the server ended up having a conversation with locals while the food was cooking, so as you can see, it got burnt. Overall, it tasted good, but the service left a lot to be desired.

name: Tatiana Velandia
review: We got the special from the house, the cold meat, it was fantastic, tasty, a big size and good price. I got a cappuccino. Everything got delivered pretty quickly, and the service was very kind and nice. Totally recommend this place.

name: Eric Witiw
review: The cicchetti were awesome and the staff are so friendly and kind. Great place to stop for a coffee and some food!

name: Emily Jarrard
review: Best cappuccino and croissant I’ve had in Italy so far! The two women working were lovely and the vibes sitting outside were perfect :)

name: Gabriella Olivia
review: Great vibes, location, service, and the Cicchetti the server prepared was so beautiful! Great for an afternoon snack and a drink.

name: Jacqueline Bowie
review: Happened upon this place while detouring to the bookshop near by. Service was excellent! Had espresso and cicchetti sampler, both were delicious. Very reasonably priced, and excellent people watching too!

name: Nia
review:
Kind staff that was very good answering all of our questions about the menu. They had something to accommodate all of our needs - a meat lover, a kid that doesn’t like vegetables and a vegetarian :)
Paninis were fresh, prepared quickly and tasted great. The prices were very reasonable too. Thumbs up!

name: lg G
review:
Café Del Doge is a fantastic spot in Venice for coffee lovers! They offer a wonderful selection of rich, aromatic coffees and freshly made pastries that are absolutely delicious. I really enjoy coming here to relax, have a great cup of coffee, and indulge in some of their sweet treats.

The location is perfect, right in the heart of Venice, making it an ideal stop whether you’re exploring the city or just taking a break. The service is excellent, with friendly staff who make the experience even better. Definitely one of my favorite cafés in Venice highly recommended

name: Vlad Ivancu
review: I stopped at Caffè del Doge and had a double espresso and tiramisu. The coffee was excellent – strong, intense, and just a bit bitter, but in a positive way that really highlights the flavor. The tiramisu was classic and well made, a proper version of this iconic dessert. Cozy atmosphere and a great spot for real Italian coffee in Venice. Highly recommended!

name: Andrew Yit
review: Best coffee i had in italy in 10 days. They sell beans from various places in the world. Pastries are amazing. My barista is a cool chap

name: Tanya Carlson
review: The coffee is amazing. Smooth, strong but not bitter. The service is friendly, helpful (as we spoke little Italian) and the food is fresh. Be sure to try the pistachio croissant. We will be back.

name: Morgan Borthwick
review: Great spot to grab coffee and a pastry! Delicious pistachio croissant and cappuccino. So many locals popped in and out while I was here; validated it’s a spot worth visiting. They also sell bags of beans to take home and have a clean restroom for customers only.

name: Richard Owen
review:
Real fresh milk in the coffee! This was the first place we found that used fresh whole milk for the flat white. They are a proper coffee shop with beans sourced from all over the world for you to choose from.

We had a cannoli as well which was divine.

Unfortunately, I did not think to review this place until we had finished.

name: Will N
review: Went there with intent to buy some beans to bring home.  Staff was super helpful in helping us decide.  As a result we tried a number of delicious drinks to finalize.  Prices are a little inflated but worth giving a try still…

name: Preeyakorn Palm
review: First breakfast/coffee in Venice. Great selections of coffee. Staff are very friendly too. Perfect seating outside under shades.

name: Tibor Kaczur
review:
My best place in Venedig, very friendly and kind staff. I come to Tango Bar as a guest almost every day. You can have a good conversation with the Boss and the staff! Delicious, freshly made sandwiches, if you are at a loss or you get an offer from the staff, what you should try.
I think it's the most peaceful place in every way.
Thank you for being me!
Regards, Hans the world traveler from Austria.
If you are in Venice, you must visit here, you will not be disappointed.

name: tim 84
review: The taste of cappuccino is amazing. We tasted the best cappuccino in Venice.

name: Zhenqing YING
review: Very lovely restaurant . Maya is adorable and the food is delicious! We love this place.

name: G Sash
review: A little hole in the wall with. Fabulous atmosphere. A 5 min walk from the main bus and taxi station on Venice. Great for breakfast and some veggie options available.

name: Mayank Sharma
review: Lovely little place close to the train station. This is my second visit to this place and they have great chicchetti along with chichettos (mini sandwiches). Service is pleasant and cocktails were quick. Overall this place has great vibes

name: angiewong600
review: We were on the way to the Piazzale Roma bus station and spotted this little bar along the happening riverbank. We went in to enquire about the food and we were very pleased with the friendly boss who introduced the food to us. It is his friendliness that attracted  us. The food we ordered were Lagsana ,Spagetti and Italian popular drinks , Spritz Aperol. Very authentic italian food and drinks! Highly recommended to All.

name: Laura Zarina
review: Very sweet staff who was very kind and helpful! Their menu online isn't really the same as on the location, but it doesn't matter cause the service was superb! And the sandwiches were so good! Same with the prices!❤️

name: Gabi Sz
review: I really enjoyed my late breakfast here!! c:

name: Maggie Chang
review: This was one of the best paninis I have ever had. I got the vegan panini (and I’m not strictly vegan or vegetarian) of roasted seitan, beetroot salad, caramelized onions, and artichoke. I ordered at the bar and was lucky to snag a table outside right along the canal. The panini was made fresh to order. The bread was perfectly toasted and so hot and fluffy. Sandwich was really flavorful. I was really impressed. I’m thinking of going back just for that sandwich alone with my few days here in Venice. I grabbed a spritz bianco for my drink for 3.5 euros. Really light on the alcohol, but decent for the price.

name: peter prana
review: Best panini I had in Venice.  It was made to order and the meat, the cheese, the veg are fresh sandwiched with a hot tasty bread.  It came surprisingly big in portion. With a glass of red wine to complement the meal, my dinner sitting next to the canal was perfect.

name: Tim Brown
review: There is a notice outside that they don't do cicchetti. However, what they do serve are platters and big sandwiches with cheeses and cooked meats. My small cheese platter was actually a generous size and my glass of red wine was excellent.

name: Marija Lagutina
review: Great freshly made panini. You can choose from the menu or create your own panini. Also you can get selection of cheese and meat accompanied by your favorite drink. Canal view is great.

name: Max Urazaev
review: Love this place! Truly wine connoisseurs, they gave us the opportunity to taste several varieties of wine, perfectly meeting our needs. We'll definitely be back!

name: Alice M
review:
Really delicious panini. We created our own which was great and so tasty & fresh. The bread was still slightly warm!
The staff were very friendly and helped us decide. Good selection of wines and everything is excellently priced!

Nice little seating area around the side by the canal.
Would’ve gone back if I had more time in Venice!

name: Jessica Du Bois
review: We absolutely loved La Bottiglia! We stopped by for a couple of glasses of wine during the afternoon. They have a wonderful selection at an affordable price. They have inside seating and outdoor seating in the square. The bar cat Sylvester is cute but prefers to not interact with humans :)

name: lara addison
review: Incredible service and the wine was amazing. It was great value, had lots of choice and great grazing board options

name: Václav Johanna
review: Was looking for a coffee place that wasn't a tourist trap. Found it. Great location where you can sit outside and just enjoy the rush in the street, right next to a church. Prices are great, staff was all smiles and absolutely top class. Coffee and deserts were also fantastic. I don't understand why this place isn't full!

name: Zak Dominczak
review:
Good tiramisu👍
Great Caffe👍
Great staff👍
Immediately when we went in, the great waiter created such a warm atmosphere and he was just lovely, he set a great mood and was really the person for the job. The 5 stars is mainly for the staff but also the tiramisu was good and we got free chips once we ordered spritz so it was nice!
I ordered a smoothie and a few other things the waiter asked if we want it with ginger, we said no but he gave it with ginger he took it back and gave us a smoothie without ginger straight away!
He was very nice and sorry and you can just tell, what a nice guy.
Give that guy a well deserved raise👍
Great restaurant, thanks.

name: Felix Dominick
review: They serve a very traditional espresso which is quite bitter. The capuccino is well made. They charge a bit of a premium and I don't think the coffee is really better than somewhere else. The waiter was super friendly and I really liked his service.

name: Kevin Lottis
review: I chose this cafe because I heard they are serving some of the best coffee in Venice and it's true. There were welcoming and friendly to us. It seemed like mostly locals outside which is another reason to come here. The pastry and cakes were delicious.

name: Arwin
review: I stopped by Bottega del Caffè Dersut and was pleasantly surprised by the quality of their fresh juice. I ordered the L’Arancione and it was truly refreshing — vibrant, naturally sweet, and bursting with freshness. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients. The atmosphere is cozy, with a relaxed Venetian charm, and the staff are welcoming and attentive. It’s the perfect little spot to take a break, enjoy something light, and soak in the local vibe. Highly recommended if you’re looking for a genuine and refreshing drink experience in Venice. 🌿🍊

name: Jake (developerjake)
review:
Very inhospitable staff. Wouldn't let my partner use the restroom without purchasing something, so she bought a coffee, and while waiting for her we sat on the chairs outside. Once she came outside with her coffee they gave us a total of 5 seconds on their seats before warning us that we couldn't sit there because the coffee was takeaway unless we wanted to pay them more. We were literally reading the map to see which way we were going, and every table except ours was empty... They really set a low bar, especially compared to some of the other lovely local restaurants.

I'd recommend you stay away.

name: Ella Taubman
review: I honestly wish I could give this cafe a higher  rating they are truthfully one of the kindest staff cafés. I have been to in Italy. I have been studying abroad here in Venice and this coffee shop has been my go to every single morning before class as an American. I truly do enjoy my iced coffee and they have been nothing but kind to make me an iced coffee every single day. Their croissants are better than any I’ve ever had and they truly make my morning better.

name: Ketan Bhavan
review:
Not as good as expected.
The coffee was nice so no complaints there, but the service felt really pressured and not what you'd want from a relaxing coffee break.

As soon as we entered there was a rush to get us seated (noting that you pay more to sit in). We tried to get up to look at the pastries on display and were told to sit down and order from the menu, so we didn't get a chance to look, even though we told them we just wanted to check the food on sale.

Pastries were stale (already by around 2pm) so we didn't enjoy them much.

I'd say there are much better coffee shops and pastries sold around the area so I would recommend walking around and trying others.

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Nils Paellmann
review: Great coffee bar in central location on the Lido. Great atmosphere and always great music. Super friendly service. Nice decor.

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Алена С
review: Delicious coffee, pleasant service. I would like to see more choice of desserts to go with such delicious coffee. Will definitely come back again.

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Kais Jessa
review: Amazing pastries, great coffee and kind service

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Konrad J
review: Great value. A lot of local people.

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Wolfgang
review: Great Croissants and nice coffee!

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Free Hands Art Academy
review: Very good coffee and pastries and the prices are very reasonable. Plus the locations is very nice. Highly recommended!

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Piotr Filonowicz
review: It’s a very friendly place and the prices are really low.

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: Khaldoun El Ahmadieh
review: Good coffee, friendly staff and good prices. Highly recommended.

name: Eyal Tsin
review: We wanted good coffee, and what place would have it? A coffee with the name caffe, that is what I thought. I was right, me and wife visited, drink coffee and eat small tapas. The food was tasty, coffee was good and overall atmosphere was quiet and cozy, we had experienced kind service.

name: Sam Symons
review: Barman was a legend! Had a quiet beer and a feed for a great price, and had some great conversation as well. Thanks!

name: Edward M
review: The best coffee I've ever had. Lots of flavor and great crema on the espresso. They also sell their blend so you can take it home and enjoy it while reminding yourself to schedule your next trip Venice.

name: Mały Pierd
review:
Very nice café with lots of things to order
Cappucino costed 1.8e and was, to my pleasant surprise, bigger than any other in the town for this price. Taste mild, not burned- it was nice :D
Very good place to hide when it's hot outside. Not a lot of noise from the outside was big plus for us

name: Kristina James
review: We spent a lot of time there. A super nice bartender (the owner) I would come back anytime 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

name: James
review:
Cappuccino was nice,  has a slight bittered taste.
Seems like it's an authentic place, as there is no "to go" option and no place to sit, so be ready to stay.
Also you can buy coffee beans inside

They accept card payment.

name: Tom
review: Best cappuccino I've had in Venice hands down. Super friendly. The price seems like it's from 4 decades ago (€1.3). A must visit if you are in Venice.

name: Reaction Timing
review: Decent creamy coffee (3/5) friendly service, best prices coffee in Venice, nostalgic atmosphere could do with some work and some seating, heaps of potential serving coffee like that.

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Oldrey Leite
review:
Local café away from the tourist crowds

Place
Located a bit far from the main tourist areas, but worth the visit. At first there were locals around, though it emptied out quickly — likely because Italians don’t usually have coffee at that hour.

Food & Drinks
Plenty of sweet options available. The ones we tried were good, though not outstanding. The coffee was strong and enjoyable.

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Adrijan Veta
review: Great place for a quick sweet treat and a coffee. Everything is of great quality! Gets a bit crowded during the day (depending on time), come earlier in the morning when everything is still super warm and fresh out of the oven 🤤🥰

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: ModeParChocolat
review:
We found the bakery and stuck to it for breakfast for the entire time in Venice.
The pizza were quite something. We took them and saved it for later and STILL just has yummy. We came back for the pastries and it did not disappoint.

As I type this, I’m missing Venice even more. What a place

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Michael Moore
review: This place looks amazing as you walk by and makes you want to immediately come in. It was pretty busy as we entered around 1030 and hoped it was worth all the hype. Instead of something sweet we opted with two savory options with their quiche and ham and cheese sandwich. The quiche was delicious for a couple of warm bites but then there were a couple of cold bites since it was not heated all the way. The ham and cheese sandwich was ok. The bread and ham had no really taste but the cheese was really good. I would recommend other options in Venice if you have the chance.

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Astrid Xue
review: I came here last year when I was traveling with my family, and then again this year by myself. The staff were so friendly and patient. The desserts are of a great variety and of all the ones we tried they were really good. Not too sweet or heavy as other Italian ones I’d say, and the price is decent!

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Richard Pettigrew
review: Pizzas are wonderful! Square and a thicker base than the usual ones you get in Venice. Very tasty sauce. We got extremely kind customer service following an incident with a seagull!

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Julia Hörmayer
review: We had high hopes but the desserts weren’t quite as good as expected. We thought the berry tart had custard but it was really just whipped cream with sugar, nothing special. The coffee was good. The small tart was very good according to my mum.

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Katharina P.
review: Very good coffee and amazing pistachio croissants. We enjoyed our breakfast a lot! 🥐

name: Pandaz
review:
I stopped by after the museum to refuel.
There's a wide selection of food (pastries, cakes, little bites or even meals) and beverages (soft drinks, coffee, cocktails etc.)
Service was quick and super friendly. The experience was pleasant with an exceptional view of the square.
Price was reasonable compared to the neighbourhood!
I quite enjoyed the tiramisu😋

name: Nurettin Tuncer
review: Nice place to take a break, and if you’re lucky, you can get a table with a beautiful view of St. Mark’s Basilica. Perfect spot for a coffee or dessert while enjoying the scenery.

name: Ethan Choi
review:
This is a place where you can enjoy exceptionally cheap and delicious coffee compared to other cafes in the square. It used to be Napoleon's office, and you can access it by going up to the museum entrance. Since it’s a small space, seats might not always be available (a museum ticket is not required).

You’ll need to go through a security check upon entry, so don’t be surprised. Once inside, head to the second floor, and you’ll see it on the left.

name: Nick Gray
review: The view over San Marco is the obvious star but good coffee and cake, with willing attentive service makes this a great place to rejuvenate.  We visited after a couple of hours walking around the museum, very pleased to take a breath here before continuing to the next sight-seeing adventure.

name: Jackson Lam
review: Good coffee and croissants. Had a nice view of the square, the basilica and the tower. Good quiet location as well. Note: need to pass security checkpoint but need not have a ticket for museum.

name: Raul va
review:
We went to have a coffee at this place, we had to wait about 20 minutes before they even gave us the menu. The waitress was very rude from the start, telling us we’re not the only table there. She then went to take the order of another table that came after us and when we were complaining about it, she told us to go get the food or coffee ourselves from the kitchen and that she cannot split herself. We ended up leaving without having anything, wasting our time on our last day in Venice.
We respect waiters and their work and understand when it’s crowded, but that doesn’t justify a rude and disrespectful behavior.
We would recommend changing the staff.

name: Jonathan Lane
review: Location, location, location! In the middle of many overpriced and overrun cafes in the Piazza San Marco, this was a breath of fresh air. I imagine it remains quieter because you need to enter through the museum security to get to it. Provided perfect views of the Piazza and a quiet place to retreat to during the busy day.

name: Javier Sanguinetti
review:
Great spot to grab a coffee with views to Piazza San Marco. If you are able to get one of the tables by the window it would probably be the best coffee spot in the piazza.
The prices are really good considering the setting.
You must go through a queue and security because it shares entrance with museum

name: Beth Miller
review: We came here a few times back in March 2024 and returned today for really delicious and fresh juice, coffee, super fresh sandwiches and a really lovely employee. This place is immaculate, well priced for what it is, and in a quiet and clean neighborhood with easy vaporetto access. I think the negative reviews come from people not understanding the type of sandwiches they'll get; there aren't sauces, spreads, or condiments that come on these sandwiches, just fresh ingredients and freshly sliced deli meats, which can be super delicious. No bathrooms. When you tell the sandwich maker that you'd like take away, they, without fail, warn you of the seagulls, which is kind and not something many people would do. If your children like sandwiches without a tomato or whatever, it's easy to remove for them and still share a great sandwich.

name: Shoon Lae Lae Oo
review:
The fresh lettuce & tomato with the big size of sandwich humbles my stomach!!
I love the way the food is yummy while having the fresh taste of vegetables inside the sandwich!!!
The best to try in Venice!

name: Roman Goncharenko
review: My god, those sandwiches are absolutely delicious! A rare find in Venice—grab one and head straight to Punta della Salute to enjoy it with the view!

name: Jenna Park
review: A hidden gem!! We accidentally pop by this little cute sandwich nook, and we came twice in our staying in Venezia. All the sandwiches are so fresh and delicious!!! Great place when you don't want to go with a big hotel breakfast!

name: Amelia Johnson
review: Fantastic! Really amazing sandwiches, crispy, made fresh then and there. Lovely fresh ingredients. Go for the fresh juice, delicious! Great coffee. The fact lots of locals came in was a great sign. The young lady serving was so friendly, efficient and fun!

name: Jas Y
review: The sandwiches were good, and tasted fresh, the fresh juice was really good! A little tucked away which made it feel special in a way...

name: Lisette Marin
review: Cute little sandwich shop right by the basilica! San Marco panini was simply delicious! Fresh orange & lemon juice was refreshing

name: Michaela Tangredi
review: We were able to stop at 60/40 Take Away on our recent vacation. I highly recommend this location for the food, drinks, and service! I tend to dislike coffee since I live in the US, but I loved it here. This was certainly the best cappuccino of my entire holiday. I hope to return someday.

name: Farimah
review: We had a wonderful experience here. We came for breakfast, and it was amazing! We tried both the salted and the sweet breakfast — and both were absolutely delicious. The atmosphere was lovely, especially since we sat in the garden. It was such a nice and relaxing spot. Definitely a must-visit when you’re in Venice!

name: Kevin Barker
review: We had planned to stop by Moro Cafe, and unintentionally wandered in when walking around Venice. The grounds of the restaurant looked spectacular, but the cafe doesn't share the same room and is in the back half of the garden. The staff was very friendly, probably some of the better service we had in Venice. We however, only stopped here for drinks. We got a coffee and tea. I dropped the rating down to 4/5 because two drinks was 15 USD...that's wild regardless of presentations.

name: Tatiana Velandia
review: The food was fantastic, with a snack before dinner and some cookies at the end. The restaurant is absolutely stunning, the ambiance was lovely, the service excellent and setting quiet elegant. I ordered gnocchi, a aperol spritz and a sea bass. Everything was delivered pretty quickly and the taste was fabulous. I think it is a hidden gem in the city that should be more popular

name: Yuchen Wang
review: Great location and beautiful view. We sat down for lunch and we were amazed by the dedication for details. The menu is catered by a Michelin starred chef, everything was delicious and pretty, and the staffs were super friendly as well. It’s a good chill out stop for a trip in Venice. Will definitely come again next time we visit.

name: Heloise St-Pierre
review: We came back 2 days in a row. Everything is perfect here. Very good food a pastries are amazing. Staff is friendly and gives good recommendations. The place is beautiful in a peaceful garden. Absolutely recommend for coffee, cocktails or breakfast.

name: Diana Cecilia Barahona Rivera
review: A jewel in Venice. Moro Café feels like stepping into a living masterpiece — vibrant colors, palace-like décor, and desserts that look and taste like art. The garden is serene, but inside you’ll find a true fairytale, especially with the ‘Exotic’ dessert. Don’t hesitate, just cross the bridge across the Accademia!

name: Tyr M
review: A perfect cafe for breakfast or lunch while exploring Venice. The pastries are a delight! I fell in love over and over again with the Venetian sandwich. A delectable offering I could enjoy every day. We will definitely return on our next trip to Venice.

name: Diệu Ngọc Ngô
review:
We've had the Moro Breakfast for 2 people and we were so so full after this🤣. But everything was delicious, from sweet to savoury and drinks.

We did not reserve in advance, when we got there it was not busy, so we asked to get a table outside and the staffs were quick to get us one. They were also friendly and helpful throughout the meal.

Moro Cafe is also a nice place to be with pretty decorations and garden. We had a nice time enjoying brunch, talking and taking pictures as it was a girl's trip😂.

Enjoyable experience!

name: Dath 1983
review: Lovely little cafe down by the docks. The saffron drink made from a saffron syrup was amazing. The views are to die for and it was really nice and quiet (off the beaten track for most tourists).

name: Nik Sar
review: Lovey little cafe. Very conveniently located in proximity to the Arsenale. A lovey view, a small delicious offering of refreshments and friendly staff.

name: Le Kumara
review:
Very nice place, will be one of my favorites memories of Venise ! 😎
Prosecco con Safran : i will made it at home, going back to France !

name: Gigia TravelCat
review: Wonderful place to chill 🫸🫷

name: Thierry Pulver
review: Sweet! 🥰

name: Nikola Sinković
review: Kafić smješten u blizini Arsenala, obično radi u vrijeme bijenala. Mjesto je van turističkih ruta i nema gužvi, cijene su povoljne, a osoblje je ljubazno.

name: laura padoan
review: Un posto meraviglioso in un angolo sperduto di Venezia.

name: Nataliya Rakutina
review: Was searching for a glass of wine after Japanese restaurant. After two restaurants which had rejected me as I didn't want to eat I was happy to meet these friendly guys who immediately brought me what I searched for. Food looked nice. Cannot say about taste though

name: Roman Riabtsev
review: I ordered hummus and an Aperol. I was extremely satisfied with both the price and the food. I’d also like to mention the very friendly waitress who gladly switched to English and brought me a lighter and an ashtray. I highly recommend this place

name: Nikola F
review: Great place! Everything in the photo costed me 10€ but sitting there was so great that I ordered another wine and get tomato chips for free 😊

name: Ralitsa Grebenarova
review: There is something special about this place, this café, and this square. Yes, you can find much better coffee shops, but the atmosphere is so authentic and pleasant—it truly makes you feel Italy with all your senses. You sip your coffee in the sunny square, enjoy the beautiful weather, and watch everyday Italian life unfold—what more could you ask for? :)

name: Braulio A.
review:
Tucked away in the heart of Venice’s artsy Dorsoduro neighborhood, this charming little café has quickly become one of our absolute favorite hideaways. With its eye-catching red façade, it’s impossible to miss as you stroll through the piazza — and once you step inside (or better yet, grab a seat outside), you’ll feel like you’ve found a secret oasis. Far from the bustling crowds, this spot oozes laid-back charm and local flavor.

Their small bites? Absolute gems — fresh, flavorful, and made with love. Whether you’re popping in for a morning coffee (which, by the way, is excellent) or winding down with a picture-perfect Aperol Spritz, everything here feels just right. It’s the kind of place where time slows down — where you can sit back, sip, snack, and soak in the Venetian vibes.

Whether you’re people-watching in the piazza or chatting with friendly locals and travelers alike, this café is all about simple pleasures done right. It’s not just a stop — it’s an experience. Trust us, once you find it, you’ll want to return every day you’re in Venice.

name: Betül yangöz
review: Definitely not recommended. The staff were very rude and unprofessional, and the service was terrible. You can only pay the bill as a single person, which makes no sense, especially if you come with friends. This place is absolutely not student-friendly and definitely not worth the money. Very disappointing experience in Venice.

name: Su-Lyn Teo
review: when we came over and asked if we could have a seat, the waitress said okay and we sat down. she proceeded to serve 2 other customers who arrived after us and left without even acknowledging us. we raised our hand and tried to wave at her to get her attention each time she passed by, but she completely ignored us and we decided to leave without even ordering anything. the difference in service this cafe’s staff provided to us tourists and the locals was disappointing, and i would not recommend visiting this cafe.

name: Steve Hall
review: A must go for a simple breakfast coffee with a variaty of different pastries and the authentic Italian vibe on a nice little townsquare out of the touristic part of the venice. The bathroom is in the back ally looking up to an old house which also gives it a unique feeling. Friendly staff, pay in the end at the counter by simply giving them your table number. No fancy smuck place or anything like this, just real Italian atmosphere.

name: John M
review: Convenient stop while walking around Venice.  Seemed more corporate than small business, but the cheese pizza was excellent and portions were large. Price was very reasonable.. Service was great.   Facility was clean.   Bathroom  stops are a few and far between, so this was a good opportunity to take care of several needs.

name: M4l2K T4N
review: Can’t miss this place. Traditional Italian quick and easy breakfast. Nothing starts a good morning like a good coffee and a warm focaccia sandwich. Stop by for a delicious no frills brekkie.

name: SteakEm All
review: Pizzas, cold soda, pastries, and coffee. Small seating area. If there’s a line, there’s a reason why. These light and fluffy pizzas are packed with flavor and a crust that can be eaten by itself. The pizzas are worth the wait.

name: Danijela Todorovic
review:
This was my second time visiting Farini in Venice, and once again, it didn’t disappoint. The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, with a cozy charm that makes you want to stay longer. The pizzas are absolutely outstanding, the dough is light, airy, and incredibly flavorful, which makes every bite a real delight. The balance of toppings and quality ingredients truly sets it apart from other places I’ve tried.
What I appreciate the most is the consistency: both times I came here, the service was kind, and the taste was just as wonderful as I remembered. Farini has definitely become one of my favorite spots in Venice, and I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to enjoy delicious pizza in a relaxed and authentic setting. A true gem that deserves every praise.

name: Hans S
review: there were a few branches of Farini so it's quite a convenient place to stop for a quick meal. price is quite reasonable too. we had croissant for breakfast. croissant was what you would expect from good croissant! however found that the pizza was just normal, a bit hard since it's been on the display for a while I guess. still pretty enjoyable overall!

name: Ozge Ustundag
review: A quick breakfast spot with pizza slices and pastries, but honestly a bit overrated. We paid €16 for two cappuccinos, two pizza slices, and a croissant — not terrible, but not great value either. The quality is fine, nothing special, and it gets crowded easily.

name: 林庭安
review:
This spot boasts an impressive variety of croissants, with many different sweet and savory flavors.

I personally tried the pistachio croissant along with a cappuccino. Both items were delicious and perfectly balanced, not overly sweet.

Despite the cloudy weather, the staff were exceptionally friendly, making it a lovely place to enjoy breakfast.

Total €8.6 : 2 croissants ( pistachios/strawberry) and 2 coffee(cappuccino/ americano )

name: Pinar Oezalp
review: We ate two bites of margharita and mozzarella pizza. They were super fatty and nothing special. Ok to feed the hunger. Toilets were closed and not allowed. We didn’t understand the hype of the superb ratings.

name: lg G
review:
Café Del Doge is a fantastic spot in Venice for coffee lovers! They offer a wonderful selection of rich, aromatic coffees and freshly made pastries that are absolutely delicious. I really enjoy coming here to relax, have a great cup of coffee, and indulge in some of their sweet treats.

The location is perfect, right in the heart of Venice, making it an ideal stop whether you’re exploring the city or just taking a break. The service is excellent, with friendly staff who make the experience even better. Definitely one of my favorite cafés in Venice highly recommended

name: Vlad Ivancu
review: I stopped at Caffè del Doge and had a double espresso and tiramisu. The coffee was excellent – strong, intense, and just a bit bitter, but in a positive way that really highlights the flavor. The tiramisu was classic and well made, a proper version of this iconic dessert. Cozy atmosphere and a great spot for real Italian coffee in Venice. Highly recommended!

name: Andrew Yit
review: Best coffee i had in italy in 10 days. They sell beans from various places in the world. Pastries are amazing. My barista is a cool chap

name: Tanya Carlson
review: The coffee is amazing. Smooth, strong but not bitter. The service is friendly, helpful (as we spoke little Italian) and the food is fresh. Be sure to try the pistachio croissant. We will be back.

name: Morgan Borthwick
review: Great spot to grab coffee and a pastry! Delicious pistachio croissant and cappuccino. So many locals popped in and out while I was here; validated it’s a spot worth visiting. They also sell bags of beans to take home and have a clean restroom for customers only.

name: Richard Owen
review:
Real fresh milk in the coffee! This was the first place we found that used fresh whole milk for the flat white. They are a proper coffee shop with beans sourced from all over the world for you to choose from.

We had a cannoli as well which was divine.

Unfortunately, I did not think to review this place until we had finished.

name: Will N
review: Went there with intent to buy some beans to bring home.  Staff was super helpful in helping us decide.  As a result we tried a number of delicious drinks to finalize.  Prices are a little inflated but worth giving a try still…

name: Preeyakorn Palm
review: First breakfast/coffee in Venice. Great selections of coffee. Staff are very friendly too. Perfect seating outside under shades.

name: Pandaz
review:
I stopped by after the museum to refuel.
There's a wide selection of food (pastries, cakes, little bites or even meals) and beverages (soft drinks, coffee, cocktails etc.)
Service was quick and super friendly. The experience was pleasant with an exceptional view of the square.
Price was reasonable compared to the neighbourhood!
I quite enjoyed the tiramisu😋

name: Nurettin Tuncer
review: Nice place to take a break, and if you’re lucky, you can get a table with a beautiful view of St. Mark’s Basilica. Perfect spot for a coffee or dessert while enjoying the scenery.

name: Ethan Choi
review:
This is a place where you can enjoy exceptionally cheap and delicious coffee compared to other cafes in the square. It used to be Napoleon's office, and you can access it by going up to the museum entrance. Since it’s a small space, seats might not always be available (a museum ticket is not required).

You’ll need to go through a security check upon entry, so don’t be surprised. Once inside, head to the second floor, and you’ll see it on the left.

name: Nick Gray
review: The view over San Marco is the obvious star but good coffee and cake, with willing attentive service makes this a great place to rejuvenate.  We visited after a couple of hours walking around the museum, very pleased to take a breath here before continuing to the next sight-seeing adventure.

name: Jackson Lam
review: Good coffee and croissants. Had a nice view of the square, the basilica and the tower. Good quiet location as well. Note: need to pass security checkpoint but need not have a ticket for museum.

name: Raul va
review:
We went to have a coffee at this place, we had to wait about 20 minutes before they even gave us the menu. The waitress was very rude from the start, telling us we’re not the only table there. She then went to take the order of another table that came after us and when we were complaining about it, she told us to go get the food or coffee ourselves from the kitchen and that she cannot split herself. We ended up leaving without having anything, wasting our time on our last day in Venice.
We respect waiters and their work and understand when it’s crowded, but that doesn’t justify a rude and disrespectful behavior.
We would recommend changing the staff.

name: Jonathan Lane
review: Location, location, location! In the middle of many overpriced and overrun cafes in the Piazza San Marco, this was a breath of fresh air. I imagine it remains quieter because you need to enter through the museum security to get to it. Provided perfect views of the Piazza and a quiet place to retreat to during the busy day.

name: Javier Sanguinetti
review:
Great spot to grab a coffee with views to Piazza San Marco. If you are able to get one of the tables by the window it would probably be the best coffee spot in the piazza.
The prices are really good considering the setting.
You must go through a queue and security because it shares entrance with museum

name: Doug Sant
review: Food was simply terrible. The advertised menu was not available (not sure if that was a one off or the norm) but after ordering 5 different dishes of which 4 were inedible, I can safely rate the food as 1 star. Even the edible one was only just passable.

name: Rajiv Iyer
review: Sandwiches were too bland. Waitress agreed to add Tomato to the sandwich but didn’t arrive with one. A creme bun costed us €1.5 in Rome & Florence but here it was €3. For a single meat ball, I was charged €3. Criminally high price.

name: Omer Matityahu
review: Great coffee and fun little location.

name: P Niva
review:
Food is bad in this place - or service/interest to understand customer needs - or both. Communication was about bruschetta portions next to us in nearby table, waiter told how he made them and blaa bla bla. We maybe mistakenly read their fussy list but he didn’t care to straighten it out and in pic is what we got. Nor all happy and did’t eat it. It tasted as bad as it looks in the picture.

Edit. Place owner below claims that we got what we ordered. Not really. As I stated out, we tried to get small eating like in the table next to us was (with bruschetta bread etc). The waiter didn't care to make it right though he certainly understood what we meant.

And we actually like anchovies. It was not that what was bad. Everything was deep fried, cold and disgusting to eat in the portion we got.

name: Paul Schofield
review:
Another visit from the Manchester City fan!
Had Aperols a few times this week as we were staying at the Al Mori. Stunning location to wind down after a hectic day.

name: Milad Zarey
review: That’s the best place for Italian breakfast in Venice. The most delicious and freshly backed  croissant with pistachios,  tasty coffee and the best location. I love it. Gracious ❤️

name: Marty Dino
review: Kind staff, strong coffee, good snack, great location. If you wanna chill little bit out of all the crowds, it's a good place.

name: Parker Yen
review: Sit by the canal and enjoy the view! A relatively quiet corner of Venice to enjoy coffee, beer or sandwiches. Owner was very friendly and attentive (kinda rare in Venice). Not hard to know why people could just sit here for hours when the weather is fabulous.

name: Farimah
review: We had a wonderful experience here. We came for breakfast, and it was amazing! We tried both the salted and the sweet breakfast — and both were absolutely delicious. The atmosphere was lovely, especially since we sat in the garden. It was such a nice and relaxing spot. Definitely a must-visit when you’re in Venice!

name: Kevin Barker
review: We had planned to stop by Moro Cafe, and unintentionally wandered in when walking around Venice. The grounds of the restaurant looked spectacular, but the cafe doesn't share the same room and is in the back half of the garden. The staff was very friendly, probably some of the better service we had in Venice. We however, only stopped here for drinks. We got a coffee and tea. I dropped the rating down to 4/5 because two drinks was 15 USD...that's wild regardless of presentations.

name: Tatiana Velandia
review: The food was fantastic, with a snack before dinner and some cookies at the end. The restaurant is absolutely stunning, the ambiance was lovely, the service excellent and setting quiet elegant. I ordered gnocchi, a aperol spritz and a sea bass. Everything was delivered pretty quickly and the taste was fabulous. I think it is a hidden gem in the city that should be more popular

name: Yuchen Wang
review: Great location and beautiful view. We sat down for lunch and we were amazed by the dedication for details. The menu is catered by a Michelin starred chef, everything was delicious and pretty, and the staffs were super friendly as well. It’s a good chill out stop for a trip in Venice. Will definitely come again next time we visit.

name: Heloise St-Pierre
review: We came back 2 days in a row. Everything is perfect here. Very good food a pastries are amazing. Staff is friendly and gives good recommendations. The place is beautiful in a peaceful garden. Absolutely recommend for coffee, cocktails or breakfast.

name: Diana Cecilia Barahona Rivera
review: A jewel in Venice. Moro Café feels like stepping into a living masterpiece — vibrant colors, palace-like décor, and desserts that look and taste like art. The garden is serene, but inside you’ll find a true fairytale, especially with the ‘Exotic’ dessert. Don’t hesitate, just cross the bridge across the Accademia!

name: Tyr M
review: A perfect cafe for breakfast or lunch while exploring Venice. The pastries are a delight! I fell in love over and over again with the Venetian sandwich. A delectable offering I could enjoy every day. We will definitely return on our next trip to Venice.

name: Diệu Ngọc Ngô
review:
We've had the Moro Breakfast for 2 people and we were so so full after this🤣. But everything was delicious, from sweet to savoury and drinks.

We did not reserve in advance, when we got there it was not busy, so we asked to get a table outside and the staffs were quick to get us one. They were also friendly and helpful throughout the meal.

Moro Cafe is also a nice place to be with pretty decorations and garden. We had a nice time enjoying brunch, talking and taking pictures as it was a girl's trip😂.

Enjoyable experience!

name: Yana K
review:
The best sandwiches all over Venice.
Affordable prices.
Great cappuccino.

Personal favourite is ham with mushrooms and crab with an egg.

name: mrsm
review:
Creat coffee and great selection of cicheteria! Corner shop location, easy access whether on the go or quick pit stop.

Staff was great and friendly, food great and prices were reasonable. The place is quirky and lots of memorabilia collection. Place is clean, accepts cards or cash.

Also sells cold drinks. Tight to get in a pram or wheelchair.

name: Xiaoxing Lyu
review:
It just sooooo good, the panini, egg, coffee just perfect
And it seems has a long history

name: Kathy Palladini
review: A treasure!  This cozy little place is perfect for people watching, especially if you can get a window seat.  We loved it here and it appears the locals do as well.  We stopped in each day for a spritz (or 3) and some chips while staying at a hotel down the street.  Fair prices and great service!

name: Guide Mike
review:
Just WOW!

On the corner of a busy street you will find this small gem or a bar. Overlooked by many and mostly full with italians we thought, we have to visit here.

The best (for us breakfast) little dishes and snacks you will find here, for an even better price! (Same with drinks!)

I was so amazed by the price-quality that we went to grab food here every day during our stay in Venice!

I had one of the best prawns I have ever eaten here! sad I did not try the kalamaria.

The tuna tramezzini’s were amazing! And only for 1.80 per piece.

Also the little baguettes with meat on them are delicious.

I will always miss this food here in the Netherlands.

Thank you for letting me experience these flavors and hospitality!

name: Mahesh Kumar
review: Cosy place with lot of options, coffee, tea, prosecco, craft beer and many more. Right on the canal bank & close to the bridge. Only indoor dining. Good place to hangout for small group. Enjoyed the place.

name: Natalia Agapiou
review: You will find here among the fresher and tastefuller tramezzini in Venice, this is the place to meet the locals and feel welcome every day. One of the few authentic places left in Cannaregio.

name: Hassan Al-Ramadani
review: We enjoyed having Spritz with some snacks in this nice corner bar. Sitting outside in this busy area with nice vibes was really nice.

name: Pichet P
review:
My friends and I visited Venice for leisure.

We  have dinned in this restaurant. Everything is outstanding, food services and views.

T-bone steak, spaghetti, salad, salmon and others are fantastic. Everything is cooked here in their restaurant.

Ari was our waiter. He is the best!!!

Best restaurant in Venice in my opinion:)

name: Stacey Berhardt
review: Loved this place! The atmosphere and vibe sitting outside by the water was an exceptional experience. We had reserved this restaurant a few weeks before and the table was waiting for us as soon as we arrived. Massimo our main waiter was very friendly and attentive. He chatted and joked with us and accommodated our needs. The waiter who filleted and served our fish (can’t remember his name now) did such an awesome job and also served our party well. Both him and Massimo made our dining experience there an excellent one!

name: Monica Wilson
review:
Saracen’s restaurant and Café is an absolute delight! Nestled right along the beautiful Venice canal, it offers not only a stunning view but also an atmosphere that’s both relaxed and welcoming. The food was delicious — fresh, flavorful, and clearly made with care.

Ari, our waiter, truly made our experience unforgettable. He was warm, attentive, and had great recommendations — all delivered with a friendly smile and genuine hospitality. It’s rare to find service that feels both professional and personal, and Ari struck that perfect balance.

name: T. Wang
review:
Massimo is the BEST. Speaks multiple languages, recommended the best wines both local and import. Also he is great with food selection. Him and his team cleared everything fast and professionaly. My wine glass was never  close to empty. True Venetian hospitality.

Would recommend every time!

name: Johnny Mills
review: Our waiter was Deniii and he was fantastic. All the staff that looked after us were fantastic. We've had a fantastic experience. If you are lucky enough to visit venice definitely visit Ristorante Caffè saracento. The food was incredible too. I recommend the litre of peroni to wash it down too.

name: kean poh
review: Amazing steak, warm ambience, and top-notch service. The steak was cooked to perfection, and the atmosphere was both classy and cozy. Special thanks to Massimo for his excellent service — friendly, attentive, and made us feel truly welcome. Highly recommended!

name: Sdoubledub 1
review: If ever there was sixth star, this would earn it. I’ve been blessed to bounce around the globe a bit, and my expectations in a tourist area are usually a  bit lower. Simply BLOWN AWAY! Massimo was literally one of the best servers I have ever come across- I’ve owned a restaurant and been in Michelin rated restaurants. Top tier server. The service exceeded all expectations, the food is sublime and the location is amazing. Best money I’ve spent in Europe, let alone Venice.

name: Judith Kippax
review:
Massimo looked after us well. Steak and chips (changed from mash). Asked for a well done to be cooked a little more was no problem. Beautiful setting, prices as expected to be looking at the Rialto Bridge, Grand Canal side.
Not hurried. A lovely evening.

name: Ceren Erkut
review: The food was tasteful. The waitress was very nice and kind, except she hurried us to order in 2 mins 🥲 Prices were cheaper compared to touristy places (pizza 11eur, sandwich 7eur, lasagne 18eur). No cover nor service fee was charged!. I recommend.

name: Bridget Tobin
review:
Bar all'Angolo is a lovely little coffee shop with baked goods and sandwiches. Food and espresso drinks were great. They have a good selection of breakfast pastries and espresso drinks as well as an assortment of lunch sandwiches. We had breakfast there then bought sandwiches for later in the day.

The staff was incredible. They are fast and friendly and accurate, what more could you want? They were also very accommodating to us as tourists (very willing to make our teenage son a breakfast egg sandwich when he wanted more than a cornetto). My Italian is very limited but their English thankfully was not.

If I lived here I could see this being some place I would visit nearly every day. Would 100% recommend and will visit again on another trip to Venice.

Several of the sandwich options shown, though it was rainy outside and some of them are harder to see through the glass.

name: Bennie Botha
review: Thus was a really nice place to visit. They had some of the best pizza we tasted while in venice and they serve a lovely breakfast. I would definitely recommend them if you are ever in the area

name: Cindy Lai
review: Walked by this small bar with 8 outdoor tables and 5 indoor.  I was attracted to the happy hour special for 7 Euros for a spritz and 2 cichetti.  One cod spread and one mortadella.  I also had the gnocchi with ragu (meat) sauce.  Friendly waitress, right by the canal.  Had a nice time.  We asked for sparkling water but she brought still water but changed it when we informed her of the mistake.  View of the sunset.  What else can you ask for?

name: johann dalli
review: Great spot for breakfast, coffee or brunch. Fresh food, friendly staff and reasonable prices.

name: Morgan Delac
review: In my opinion, this is a must visit during your stay in Venice! As a tourist, this place suited us perfectly as it was open early, well situated and has some of the best croissants in Venice. The coffee is also very tasty, and the amount of locals who visit attests to this. The women who work here are insanely efficient and do not miss a beat. The blonde woman was particularly friendly and remembered us after our second visit 😊 grazie mille!

name: Mireia Clemente
review:
Spritz 🍹 e ciccheti
It was delicious 😋😋😋
Warm welcome 🤗
Very nice place

name: Sheng Sicheng
review: This bar is a pretty underrated place to eat. I went there with 6 other people. The staff was very kind and gave us outside seats right away for seven people. The staff also explained to us everything we wanted to know about the foods. For appetizers, I had the carpaccio, it had a really good flavor with lemon juice, fancy pepper and salt for only 12$. I ordered the prosciutto pizza and it was one of the best pizzas I’ve ever had with amazing seasoning. An overall great experience.

name: Motor4150
review: Mama Mia!! Locals only here. They should have posted that out front. This place would probably have more reviews (under 70 as of this writing) if they didn't have a rude woman working the bar. (heard she is the owner) Walked up on a nice Venice evening.   The place looked to be pretty busy outside. About 6-7 tables still waiting to be filled. We walked inside and waited a minute or so to be greeted. Finally the woman asked if she could help us. My wife asked if there was a table we could sit at outside. She looked around mockingly toward the front and the back. After putting on quite the show she said, "No". Mind you, no one was sitting inside (she could have said, no, but we have inside seating) and clearly there were many empty tables out front. She obviously doesn't want tourists at this establishment. Locals only I guess. Perhaps she's the one who writes all the hatred towards tourist around Venice? Fortunately, due to her rudeness...we walked out and found a very friendly and delicious restaurant not far away.

name: Bernd Müller-Pflug
review: We come to Venice since many years. We are always at Guidecca to visit Biennale. We found in 2025 this bar and it was like being with friends. Mother and her daughter doing service on high level. Very friendly, very charming and smart. You are here with Italian neighbors watching football or chilling in the evening. Great opportunity to dive into Italian live. We come back - next year - promised 😍

name: Berlie Strass
review:
Two Venetian women 💪🏻have taken the helm of this gem in early 2024...and turned this already good space into an even better one.

Excellent views of the city if you are at the terrace by the water or near the window. Food is good, home made and varied: foccacie ripiene, tramezzini, polpette, everything delicious. Drinks include classic aperitivi and Cicchetti (aka Venetian tapas). Staff is very friendly and amicable. Lots of local residents come by to enjoy the space and "due chiachere" (two words) to catch up. There is a big space at the back and an open air garden.

To be improved: the music can get loud and pounding. Three fridges take space at the prime space by the window, which limits the space available to sit with a great view.

name: Parmit Kler
review: Amazing coffee and food, Great views and amazing service

name: Robert Russo
review: The best bar with the best snacks in Giudecca. Take the #2 vaporetto across and enjoy drinks while looking at Venice. Prices are very reasonable compared to anything closer to San Marco. Come here for great service and scenery.

name: Nurdaulet Kaskyrbekov
review: Omg the best! Only locals sit here! The best aperol spritz, with olive!!! You know what it means!

name: Ivy Bischoff
review: Best Atmospher, amazing service! I had a great time , thank you very much 💜

name: T S
review:
ヴェネツィアのジュデッカを訪問した際に、雰囲気に吸い込まれ注文。

結論、ヴェネツィアのバーガロでは群を抜いて1番神★

このテラスを利用できるにも関わらず、しっかりグラスに入った美味なアペロールスピリッツはなんと4ユーロ。。！

ヴェネツィアですよ？？日本でもこの景色を見ながら飲む酒は8ユーロは最低でもいくかと。

とりあえずジュデッカというエリアは情報こそ少ないですが、サン・マルコ広場近くの水上バスで行けますので、絶対行くべしです！

店員さんや街の人の雰囲気も優しくて素晴らし過ぎました、、！また行きたい、、、

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Yana K
review:
I was thinking of writing the reviews to all the places visited at the airport, but here I have to do it right away.

That might be the best cappuccino I’ve tried so far (and I go to many different places throughout the day when travelling). Price is 2 euros or around it.
Various goods for a tasty breakfast. My personal favourite was the egg cupcake.
Cannoli is great. The dough in it tastes exactly how it should - savoury.

Almond cake is heavenly.

Service amazing, friendly stuff.

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Vadym Pinchuk
review:
A lovely local pastry shop where both locals and tourists stop by for a quick espresso and something sweet (or savory!). It has that authentic neighborhood feel — cozy, welcoming, and full of tempting aromas.

I tried the focaccia with Parma ham, and it was absolutely delicious — fresh, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. There were plenty of other pastries and sandwiches that looked amazing, but I couldn’t try them all this time.

Definitely recommended for a casual bite, good coffee, and great sandwiches. I’ll be coming back to try more! ☕🥐

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Mateusz Wojnach
review: Majer is a great spot for a morning coffee. The coffee was well brewed, with an authentic, aromatic flavor that was both rich and energizing. We also had two Italian sandwiches – one with prosciutto and the other with grilled vegetables served warm – both were a real delight for the palate in the morning. A glass of chilled prosecco made the experience even more enjoyable.

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Golnar Ashkboos
review: Just perfect, delicious pizzas and cakes with supper friendly stuff that make the atmosphere kind and comfortable. Specially the beautiful Iranian women that gives services with a nice smile.

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: M A
review:
The best bakery I visited during my stay in Venezia. Everything they had looked super delicious and everything I took was indeed super delicious.
Their maritozzo was perfect : perfect amount of cream, texture and fluffiness of the brioche , overall sweetness and apparence.

I also took a pizza and arancini which I loved to the airport. They were great even if it was sitting in my back pack for half a day and was cold so idk how amazing it would’ve been tasting it warm.

Nice peaceful place to take a break with friendly staff. Grazie mille!

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Kyle Strobel
review: Visiting Italy and this was the first bakery we wondered into after arriving in Venice; everyone was so nice and very helpful in navigating what to order. We plan to come back before leaving the city

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: nami shibata
review: This place is really delicious, the croissants and pizza are both very delicious. I came here twice.

place_title: Majer - Piazzale Roma bakery pastry coffee bar
name: Ollie Roddy
review: Love this place. Pistachio croissant and cappuccino every day during my trip. Perfect. Grazie Mille to the lovely ladies who run this place too.

name: Nataliya Rakutina
review: Was searching for a glass of wine after Japanese restaurant. After two restaurants which had rejected me as I didn't want to eat I was happy to meet these friendly guys who immediately brought me what I searched for. Food looked nice. Cannot say about taste though

name: Roman Riabtsev
review: I ordered hummus and an Aperol. I was extremely satisfied with both the price and the food. I’d also like to mention the very friendly waitress who gladly switched to English and brought me a lighter and an ashtray. I highly recommend this place

name: Nikola F
review: Great place! Everything in the photo costed me 10€ but sitting there was so great that I ordered another wine and get tomato chips for free 😊

name: Ralitsa Grebenarova
review: There is something special about this place, this café, and this square. Yes, you can find much better coffee shops, but the atmosphere is so authentic and pleasant—it truly makes you feel Italy with all your senses. You sip your coffee in the sunny square, enjoy the beautiful weather, and watch everyday Italian life unfold—what more could you ask for? :)

name: Braulio A.
review:
Tucked away in the heart of Venice’s artsy Dorsoduro neighborhood, this charming little café has quickly become one of our absolute favorite hideaways. With its eye-catching red façade, it’s impossible to miss as you stroll through the piazza — and once you step inside (or better yet, grab a seat outside), you’ll feel like you’ve found a secret oasis. Far from the bustling crowds, this spot oozes laid-back charm and local flavor.

Their small bites? Absolute gems — fresh, flavorful, and made with love. Whether you’re popping in for a morning coffee (which, by the way, is excellent) or winding down with a picture-perfect Aperol Spritz, everything here feels just right. It’s the kind of place where time slows down — where you can sit back, sip, snack, and soak in the Venetian vibes.

Whether you’re people-watching in the piazza or chatting with friendly locals and travelers alike, this café is all about simple pleasures done right. It’s not just a stop — it’s an experience. Trust us, once you find it, you’ll want to return every day you’re in Venice.

name: Betül yangöz
review: Definitely not recommended. The staff were very rude and unprofessional, and the service was terrible. You can only pay the bill as a single person, which makes no sense, especially if you come with friends. This place is absolutely not student-friendly and definitely not worth the money. Very disappointing experience in Venice.

name: Su-Lyn Teo
review: when we came over and asked if we could have a seat, the waitress said okay and we sat down. she proceeded to serve 2 other customers who arrived after us and left without even acknowledging us. we raised our hand and tried to wave at her to get her attention each time she passed by, but she completely ignored us and we decided to leave without even ordering anything. the difference in service this cafe’s staff provided to us tourists and the locals was disappointing, and i would not recommend visiting this cafe.

name: Steve Hall
review: A must go for a simple breakfast coffee with a variaty of different pastries and the authentic Italian vibe on a nice little townsquare out of the touristic part of the venice. The bathroom is in the back ally looking up to an old house which also gives it a unique feeling. Friendly staff, pay in the end at the counter by simply giving them your table number. No fancy smuck place or anything like this, just real Italian atmosphere.

name: sahin
review: Really cheap and delicious coffee and hot-served pastries in there. Smiley barista will start your day with the best coffees with the cheapest prices in Venice!

name: Annette Wright
review: Best pastries and coffee. Love the apple and the nut one. So soft and delicious.

name: Loreal Prystaj
review: Brasilla is amazing! The croissants the Coffee — absolutely delightful! And Susan has such a welcoming and magical presence! Highly recommend this spot to anyone ☕️🥐♥️

name: Autumn Hagemann
review: Fabulous little spot. The ginger croissant was incredible and of course, the coffee was fantastic.

name: Es J
review: TRAVELLERS DONT GO! They charge different price for visitors. I saw them charge 1.3 croissant for local. Then 2.6 for us! Learned in Venice - don’t buy anything without a price tag.

name: S Hosseini
review: Good spot for a quick stop and a nice coffee

name: Lance Reed
review: Possibly the best macchiato I've ever had. Croissants were great as well.  Very friendly staff.

name: Jamie Berglund
review:
The best cappuccino in town!! Open early also :)
Great service, friendly and quick!

name: Juliana van Kolck
review: This is a great spot on a central location to grab a quick breakfast, a small bite to eat, a coffee, juice, or spritz. Despite its location, its tucked away on a slightly less busy street so you aren't fighting through masses of people to get in and out of it. Highly recommend it!

name: Marco Lopez
review:
I’d love to rate with 0 stars this place
The waiter was so rude and start to shout to us, because it was full the terrace and we ask to take away our drinks, when one table was available we were trying to take a sit and he said that plastic cups were not allowed in the place when they sold to us!!!
Even the bartender told him he (the waiter) was wrong he keep shouting
I leave a picture of him I’m very disappointed

name: Maria Golovco
review: I am not used to leave bad reviews, but this is an exaction. The waitress was so rude and did not speak properly to our group. We do not speak Italian, but we had a strong feeling that they gossip about up in front of us. The spot is great, but the waitresses should be more careful how they treat people…

name: Ex FN
review: Idk why this is rated so high, went here because of review. Pastries are like old and not great. Coffee average. I had better somewhere. Good thing the staff speak english

name: Braulio A.
review:
Caffè Brasilia quickly became our go-to spot during our stay in Venice, whether for a morning espresso and pastry or a refreshing Aperol Spritz in the early afternoon before dinner. Nestled in a quiet corner just a few steps from our hotel, Ca’ Alvise, this charming café offers an authentic Venetian experience at exceptionally reasonable prices.

The friendly staff create a welcoming atmosphere, enhancing the café’s appeal. Inside, you’ll find a cozy setting with good music playing in the background, while outside seating provides a prime location for people-watching. From this hidden gem, you can observe the steady flow of visitors heading towards iconic landmarks like the Rialto Bridge and Piazza San Marco.

What makes Caffè Brasilia truly special is its ability to offer a peaceful retreat from Venice’s bustling tourist areas. Despite its central location, it retains a sense of exclusivity, making it feel like your own little slice of Venetian paradise. Whether you’re stopping by for a quick cappuccino or lingering over a spritz, this café embodies the charm and relaxed pace of Venetian life—an ideal way to start or pause your day while exploring the city.

name: theia wallis
review:
Was in Venice recently for art, and searched  for “egg sandwich” and this became our little go-to in our brief time here.
Lisa was amazing - warm, efficient, quick service.
Nutella waffle was So good, veggie omelets were great, we ordered freshly juiced drinks (the ‘Digestiva’ (pricy but so so good and necessary)).

(photos are from numerous visits)

Seems like soy (“soya”) milk is the main alternative milk option in Venice - our “latte macchiatos” were delicious!

name: Temp Janek
review:
Coffee was great!
Piadina was good, but some vegetables and wholemeal flour might be good addition
omelette was ok

name: Tom Moriarty
review: Lovely local bar in a side street in Venice.  Reasonable prices, great service and very friendly staff.  Minutes walk from all of the popular places but more authentic and local and significantly cheaper.  Totally recommend, also a great place to find the shade, was 38° when I was there and the shade and cool was very welcome, as we're the beers😁

name: John M
review: Convenient stop while walking around Venice.  Seemed more corporate than small business, but the cheese pizza was excellent and portions were large. Price was very reasonable.. Service was great.   Facility was clean.   Bathroom  stops are a few and far between, so this was a good opportunity to take care of several needs.

name: M4l2K T4N
review: Can’t miss this place. Traditional Italian quick and easy breakfast. Nothing starts a good morning like a good coffee and a warm focaccia sandwich. Stop by for a delicious no frills brekkie.

name: SteakEm All
review: Pizzas, cold soda, pastries, and coffee. Small seating area. If there’s a line, there’s a reason why. These light and fluffy pizzas are packed with flavor and a crust that can be eaten by itself. The pizzas are worth the wait.

name: Danijela Todorovic
review:
This was my second time visiting Farini in Venice, and once again, it didn’t disappoint. The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, with a cozy charm that makes you want to stay longer. The pizzas are absolutely outstanding, the dough is light, airy, and incredibly flavorful, which makes every bite a real delight. The balance of toppings and quality ingredients truly sets it apart from other places I’ve tried.
What I appreciate the most is the consistency: both times I came here, the service was kind, and the taste was just as wonderful as I remembered. Farini has definitely become one of my favorite spots in Venice, and I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to enjoy delicious pizza in a relaxed and authentic setting. A true gem that deserves every praise.

name: Hans S
review: there were a few branches of Farini so it's quite a convenient place to stop for a quick meal. price is quite reasonable too. we had croissant for breakfast. croissant was what you would expect from good croissant! however found that the pizza was just normal, a bit hard since it's been on the display for a while I guess. still pretty enjoyable overall!

name: Ozge Ustundag
review: A quick breakfast spot with pizza slices and pastries, but honestly a bit overrated. We paid €16 for two cappuccinos, two pizza slices, and a croissant — not terrible, but not great value either. The quality is fine, nothing special, and it gets crowded easily.

name: 林庭安
review:
This spot boasts an impressive variety of croissants, with many different sweet and savory flavors.

I personally tried the pistachio croissant along with a cappuccino. Both items were delicious and perfectly balanced, not overly sweet.

Despite the cloudy weather, the staff were exceptionally friendly, making it a lovely place to enjoy breakfast.

Total €8.6 : 2 croissants ( pistachios/strawberry) and 2 coffee(cappuccino/ americano )

name: Pinar Oezalp
review: We ate two bites of margharita and mozzarella pizza. They were super fatty and nothing special. Ok to feed the hunger. Toilets were closed and not allowed. We didn’t understand the hype of the superb ratings.

name: M *
review:
This place is quite hectic, especially during lunch time when it’s crowded. The food arrived quite late, and the fish was okay, cooked perfectly. However, the chicken was a bit bland and dry.

It’s understandable considering it’s Murano and a tourist destination.

name: Thanath Gopalan
review: Food was alright. Enjoyed the truffle cheese pizza the most. There was a crowd when we got there and therefore waited around 20 minutes to be seated. Friendly service too.

name: Marian alexandru Rovid
review: Excellent service, very relaxing location, tasty food, and the fries are the best I’ve ever eaten in my life, even though I come from Belgium, where fries are a tradition.

name: Janelle
review: Beautiful patio dining with excellent food and even more amazing service! The frittura misto is extremely fresh and light! The Hugo spritz and select spritz were refreshing for a nice summer afternoon! The staff and service was top notch and the restaurant is super close to the glass shops. Would come again!

name: Meth Herath
review: Came here expecting to have good food. We ordered pizza and drinks. We also said to put extra salami, however the pizza we got hardly has any salami as you can see in my pics, Despite getting charged extra for it. The pizza was okay. The dough could have a bit more salt and should have added a bit more mozzarella. The prosciutto pizza also lacked enough mozzarella. Also ordered fried chicken with potatoes. The chicken was dry and was not that good. Potato was a bit hard.. The staff is nice and friendly.The place is good as it’s got inside seating. Also the food took a while to get to the table. It’s understandable given that this was busy when we went. If the food was a bit better we would have enjoyed more.

name: Daryl Chow
review: The food and service is amazingly affordable and delicious

name: Tracie Curtis
review: Beautiful staff, delicious pizza. Lemon hello spritz was devine. Everything and more that we wanted for our lunch stop. Gluten free pizza available.

name: Caroline Holloway
review: Friendly staff - Bellini’s were so good I had to have 2! Pizza and arancini freshly made and limoncello shots on the house - Great place for a stop in Murano.

name: Milan Nikolic
review: One of the best cappuccino in my opinion. Food is also delicious. Recommended.

name: Michelle Lum
review: Cicchetti was $2.50 euro and was pleasantly nice. However 4.50 euro for cappuccino and 2.50 for espresso is a bit steep. Atmosphere was pleasant.

name: Bob Donovan
