review: Lovely little cafe at good prices. Avoid the tourist traps and stop here for a drink.

name: David Forster
review: Highly recommend!!! Really cheap prices compared to the rest of Venice, great little cafe / bar, owner was great fun and had us trying different drinks by the time we'd finished. €16 for a round of 4 drinks.

name: Toney
review: Great spot to relax and grab a good coffee. Creme croissants were amazing and service was good. Cannolis were so so.

name: Timothy Vanliefde
review: Here you can find the cheapest Spritz (€3 at table and €2,5 standing). They told us in Italian (by showing us the bottle) that we should try the Spritz Al Select. It's a Venetian drink worth the try. Definitely stop here on your way, it's worth the stop!

name: Simon K.
review: Best beer tasted syrup, mozzarella tasted chewing gum and decent italian background music in town

name: sabxu
review: Super good croissants!

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
Like any Italian place you’re going to have great coffee that’s a given.  Here, the raison d’être to patron is their amazing selection of freshly made sandwiches. They are to die for.
If you have a hankering for a bit, stop and get a beverage and a sandwich and you won’t think twice.
Atmosphere is lovely and clean.
Service is super friendly.
Food is delicious.

name: Eleftherios
review:
What an absolute hidden gem right next to the Rialto Bridge! This is exactly the kind of authentic, local spot you hope to find in Venice.

The coffee is great and the tramezzino is incredible. If you're looking for a quick, delicious, and budget-friendly lunch or snack while exploring, this is the place. It serves up truly great food that makes it well worth seeking out.

The place itself is small and not fancy it proves you don't need elaborate decoration when the food speaks for itself.

name: Total Medical & Dental Supplies
review: Bar Rialto is the perfect spot for a quick  bite. Their panini are amazing, but the coffee is incredible the best. The team is always ready to help with whatever you want. Ask for a latte, so good coffee, and the panini di mortadella is amazing. I really enjoyed my breakfast hwre every morning as they open very early at 7:10 am. Dont miss the panini di porchetta pera e gorgonzola, it's just incredible. The bread is so crocant. This is a very small hidden gem in Venice.

name: Tunay Mehmedov
review: A very good place to grab a bite at lunch. Their sandwiches are AMAZING. They are filled to the edge and very fresh. Highly recommend this place! The workers were great and played good music 🎶

name: Maya
review: Really really liked the focaccia! The food overall was very tasty. My only complaint is that the rice in the arancini was kinda undercooked. Staff was kind :) Be careful where you sit outside, there was a faint toilet smell near our seats

name: Trent Ng
review: Wonderful spot to pick up a panini and a coffee for breakfast or a mid-day break. The paninis were absolutely delicious!

name: Matheus Santoro
review: Great food! Cheap, delicious, and friendly staff.

name: David Seligson
review: Really great place for sandwiches. We asked for a special order with sopressa, prosciutto, sheep (goat?) cheese, and artichoke. Yum! Very friendly and accommodating.

name: Vilma Vanessa Diaz
review: We had a great time at Hard Rock Café Venice! The atmosphere was lively and fun, and the staff made us feel very welcome. We ordered wings and nachos — both were delicious and perfectly served to share with our drinks. The beers were cold, and the cocktails were amazing! Our waitress was extremely friendly and attentive, always checking on us with a smile. Overall, a great experience — highly recommend it for good food, great service, and a fun vibe right in the heart of Venice!

name: Salmana
review: I had a fantastic experience here. The servers were all very kind and the food was really tasty. My server for the evening was Ibrahim, he was quick, efficient and very friendly. The gentleman at the reception area was also very nice and took some lovely photos for us. The only thing I would say ( if I had to say something ) was the seating arrangement was a little tight as it was close to other tables so it was quite noisy and covered up the sound of the music but that’s maybe because of the space.

name: Mark Walsh
review: Probably one of the most unique Hard Rock Cafe’s in the world. Certainly a good one to tick off our list. It’s quite a small venue for obvious reasons but still offers a variety of seating settings. Loved the view from the top overlooking the big glass window at the front watching the Gondolas come and go. Service and food was great and it was good to get the consistent Hard Rock Cafe experience we have come to love and enjoy

name: Keira Osborne
review: We booked a voucher through Get Your Guide. The booking was easy, when we got there we had the set menu. The options were good. I got the burger which was really nice along with the chips that tasted so good. The burger was cooked well and the onion ring was chunky and cooked perfectly. My partner had the ribs and again tasted really good. Would definitely recommend. The staff were lovely and attentive. Great view of the Gondalas. Good music and great displays on the walls.

name: Bangtini Fans
review:
I have been here two times - each one great! When I called to make a reservation the guy on the phone was really kind.
The food is extremely good with big portions. Music is not always rock but still catchy. The 2nd time the waiters even prepared a surpise for my mom's birthday and made a discount!
Definitely recommending it.

name: Maria João Sá Marques
review: We love to go to a Hard Rock Cafe in each new country or region we visit and the Venice one was a very nice surprise with its beautiful red Venetian chandelier! The staff was very nice and the food was great. Looking at the gondolas passing by while having our meal was absolutely lovely!

name: Nancy Cazarez
review:
Ibra our server was great. Friendly and efficient. Right away he asked if anyone had any allergies, we didn’t but I thought it was thoughtful of him.
The food was amazing too. I would definitely visit again

name: Diana Restrepo Copher
review: This is the 21st HR cafe I visit. I usually do not go twice to any of the cafes, but this one is the one that I definitely will not return to. We were directed to a table, and no one came to take any order. I had enough time to walk around he cafe, take pictures (I always do that at every cafe, as I am a rock and roll devoted fan), and when I returned to my table, my son told me that no one had approached him to ask if he wanted to drink anything. Wondering if we were running out of good luck while in Venice? Hahaha!! I hope you have a better experience there. :)

name: Nerazzurri
review:
Nice Coffee, But Expensive
The cappuccino was really good—smooth, rich, and served with a small chocolate truffle with a whole coffee bean, which was a nice touch. The place has a lot of history, and you can feel it in the atmosphere.
That said, 12 euros for a cup of coffee feels a bit much. You're definitely paying for the experience and the location. It's a nice treat if you're in the area, but not something I'd make a regular stop for because of the price.

name: Monserrath Flores
review: A must when you go to Venice! It is a 7 euro cover charge per person for the music. But worth it for the vibes at least just one time ahha the Apple tart was the best pastry I’ve ever tried! And I just got some tea to go along with it. The music was nice and the cafe was beautiful. It was like 65 euros for 2 teas and a pastry so make sure you come knowing that this will be the most expensive cup of coffee or tea you’ll ever have. Haha

name: N Williams
review:
Nice cafe with a lot of history. It’s had a lot of famous guests through its doors and has been in continuous operation since 1720’s.

Our experience was a pleasant one, we decided to sit outside due to the good weather conditions and the live music that was playing and managed to get a view of St.Marks Square from our table.

Thing to note is that you do have to pay a charge for siting outside while the music is playing of €7 per person. I feel this is a little steep as a small family that is seeking to attend could end up paying nearly €30 before they have even ordered anything.

We ordered two cocktails at €24 and €25 each along with a cappuccino which cost €12 and the waiter brought complimentary crisps and water for the table. This added with the €14 music charge resulted in a total of €75. A little expensive for what we had but with the atmosphere and location the cost of the drinks can be expected.

We were served quickly and after our order was taken it arrived about 10 minutes later. When we arrived there were only about 2-3 tables available so it was somewhat crowded.

We ventured inside to where the small rooms were located to have a look around and view the little shop which sold snacks and merchandise. The decor of the interior is very impressive and has managed to keep its charm from its beginnings it seems.

If you’re after a couple of drinks while in the Square I would recommend however if you’re not directly in the area there are other places that will offer a similar experience at half the price.

name: Christos Ioannou
review:
Caffè Florian in San Marco Square is one of those rare places where history, elegance, and atmosphere blend into an unforgettable experience. The moment you step inside, you’re transported to another era—the interior is refined, romantic, and steeped in Venetian grandeur, with gilded details and antique charm that make simply sitting there feel special.

The live music adds a magical touch to the setting. Listening to the ensemble play as the piazza glows outside is nothing short of enchanting—truly the soundtrack of Venice.

As for the treats, the "Sunrise Cake" was absolutely delightful—fresh, bright, and beautifully balanced. And the signature "Florian Spritz" is a must-try: refined, refreshing, and unique, a perfect Venetian indulgence. While most offerings shine, the chocolate cake was a rare miss for me—surprisingly flat in flavor compared to the rest and slightly overbaked.

Despite that small hiccup, Caffè Florian remains a timeless Venetian icon. Come for the beauty, stay for the music, and savor the flavors that make this historic café so special. A quintessential Venice experience.

name: AP
review: Beautiful historic café situated in the stunning St. Mark’s Square. We sat outside and enjoyed the views of the Doge’s Palace and the lively atmosphere. The café was also beautiful inside. However, the refreshments were extremely expensive—about three times the price of those at the neighboring cafés. This would have been acceptable, taking into consideration the café’s history, but I have to say that the mulled wine and hot chocolate we ordered tasted very bad, which was very disappointing.

name: Taisiia Didukh
review: Yes the atmosphere is nice, yes the location is good and music amazing. But I still think that the prices are too high for that. Also it would be good to say in advance that during summer they have other kind of tiramisu and not the classic one. Generally it was good, but not worth 42€ for one drink and dessert. You can just sit nearby and still listen the same music for free.

name: Albert K
review:
Cute cafe with a lot of history.
Prime seating outside in the square.

Drinks are 15 euro each+
Dessert is 20 euro each+
Cover fee 7 euro each

While it seems crazy, it makes sense given the location, included music by extremely talented violinist and celloists, and because people average one drink and sit there for an hour.

Great spot to listen to great music, absorb the art of the square, and escape the heat!

name: wolfshot_k
review: Such a delightful place that looks really grand! Delicious food and sweet treats! The cappuccino was incredible! Highly recommend despite the hefty bill. ("When in Venice"... right?)

name: Sebastian N
review: There’s probably no better place for a sweet breakfast at St. Mark’s Square in Venice. Delicious coffee, tasty croissants, friendly and elegantly dressed staff, a beautiful view of the square, and the perfect weather — simply dolce far niente! We highly recommend taking a moment to relax and enjoy this experience.

name: Gabriella Olivia
review:
I'd say this cafe is a tourist trap, and it profits from its location. While the croissant was nice, as we left, we realised the prices we paid were quadruple what they would be to take away the same items!

We paid €8 for a 500ml bottle of water and only €2 to take one away. Our sandwich was alright, but again, it was cheaper to take away. My friend paid €7 for an espresso!

name: 마창재
review: The hot chocolate I had at Auroa Café in St. Mark’s Square was so sweet and delicious! The staff were super kind and even helped take great photos. The manager was so funny when he asked me to leave a review on Google Maps. Definitely coming back again—see you next time!

name: Maria-Anna Κyrgiafini
review:
The place is amazing, with a stunning view right on San Marco Square — a truly iconic setting. The cappuccino costs €10, which might seem high, but honestly, prices in the entire area are similar. You're paying for the location, and it’s worth it for the experience.

They even treated us to some cookies, which was a lovely touch. The staff were friendly and polite, which is why I honestly can’t understand some of the negative reviews. Sure, it's not the kind of place you'd go for coffee every day, but if you're visiting Venice and want to soak in the iconic view you can have a coffee here.

name: Srinidhii Ganeish Mallyaa
review: Overpriced coffee and croissants. Food was good and located near the Basilica if you like a good view in front of you with good food this place is for you. Service was good too. Seating is fine but I can’t give more than 2 stars because the prices are exorbitant and I think there are other places where you can get coffee for a reasonable price.

name: Manthan Shah
review: Low quality, tasteless, bad texture gelato. Had to buy a little something because of the heat, and unfortunately got something from this place. If you want to taste low tier gelato, try this place. Tourist trap, avoid.

name: Tam Nguyen
review:
This reminds me of America.

Food is terrible and price is expensive.

Don’t go here. Period. Save yourself some money and time lmao.

I got a cappuccino and this little pizza here. How much? 25 euro.

Don’t believe me? Look at their prices for a regular cappuccino.

I have to get it because I was in a tourist group setting.

Don’t go here.

name: Sławek Nowak
review:
AVOID! Don't even sit there! Pathetic place. TOURIST TRAP ! AVOID !
Crappy coffee -€15
Small Heineken 330ml -€15,
exorbitant prices. I didn't even look at the food... who would want to eat there anyway? All the tables were empty, which isn't surprising.

name: Laura Herbert
review: Visited here multiply times during our 4 day stay. Best coffee shop in Venice and so much choice, from iced coffees, alcoholic lemonades, ice cream, milkshakes and frappes it was all delightful!! Definately worth a visit!!

name: charlotte porthouse
review: Such lovely and attentive staff. Before going to this store, I purchased an ice cream from a shop around the corner and it unfortunately fell off the cone, so I then asked for a new scoop from this store. The man understood the pains of icecream loss and kindly provided me a new scoop for free after I had removed the old scoop off the streets. Overall great and flavourful icecream and sweet staff, would highly reccomend!! 😄❤️

name: Szabo Akos
review:
Best ice cream I have had in Venice, no doubt. Cheap and Huge portion. Highly recommended.
The seller was super nice!

name: Katelyn Fagan
review: Best place in Venice for drinks! We came back multiple times for its great selection of coffee, fresh lemonade, cocktails, fresh juices and much more. So much choice and everything we tried was delicious and good value as well. The people working there were lovely too- great service all around 😊

name: dana tynalieva
review: i think its the only place apart from starbucks that has iced coffee in Venice. not only that, but they also work till 10pm, whoch is veeeery rare in Venice. loved the caramel iced coffee!

name: Ke Dong Yu
review: In the blazing summer heat, this unique coffee shop does God's work by offering iced coffee drinks (a rarity in the city for some reason) at decent prices. A strong recommendation from me is their espresso tonic, which has tided me through hours of stomping around the city. Certainly not the most traditional coffee drink in Italy, but the two owners -- who also happen to be brothers -- welcome all those who crave their iced lattes and iced americanos, the standard fare of most cities in the world. I've had one from their store almost every day of the 7 weeks that that I've spent in Venice. If you're feeling a bit cheeky, they also do a fantastic alcohol-spiked latte -- take your pick of vodka, rum etc.

name: Dani Girl
review: Stumbled upon Zacca while exploring today. So glad we did. The juices were freshly made and delicious. Huge menu. Something to satisfy everyone. 💚 We’ll be back tomorrow for iced coffees!

name: Karina Lau
review: spritz tastes really good, the owner is very kind👍🏻 price is also reasonable

name: Yuki Takahashi
review: Good cafeteria on the San Giobbe campus. The price is reasonable. You can also do some work here with a laptop as Eduroam is available, and the views from the upper floor are nice.

name: Chris Bassi
review: Avoid the hustle and bustle, grab a drink and a cake here instead. €3.50 Aperol spritz.

name: Sahar Baghayeri
review: One of the best Cafeteria a university could have. The view is magical!

name: Caterina Calocchio
review: Nice university cafe with low prices and lots of sandwiches and sweets

name: Elettra Filippi
review: Nice food but maybe too expensive for a university student

name: Michał R.
review: Nice and calm place.

name: Luca Uliana
review:
Ho scoperto questo luogo, oggi per la prima volta dal treno.....posto tranquillo e pulito, dove fra l'altro i Ragazzi dietro al banco sono simpatici e professionali; forse solo uno di Loro è leggermente smemorato, ma sarà dovuto dal fatto che è innamorato.
Naturalmente la mia voleva essere una battuta per chi non l'avesse capita

name: Mattia Baglieri
review:
Che cosa c'è di più bello di un pranzo con una spettacolare veduta sulla laguna? Tutto questo dopo una mattinata stancante in università e con un personale gentile e volenteroso di farti lo sconto per la tua permanenza in Ca' Foscari.

Quest'oggi all'ora di pranzo la maggior parte del personale era dedicato alle feste di laurea e si spera che la suddivisione del personale per chi mangia in mensa sia meglio distinto nelle mansioni da chi debba servire alle feste di laurea, aumentando la velocità nel considerare i bisogni di tutti, ma con un po' di pazienza anche io ho potuto giovare dell'attenzione al cibo e ai tanti ingredienti veg e biologici sia nelle insalate che nei panini e nei primi, così come nella frutta di stagione. Il caffè è molto buono e viene servito in un locale Open space che ha una sala ristorante e una caffetteria. Il personale è attentissimo nell'elencare gli allergeni eventualmente presenti nelle preparazioni. Il mio consiglio è quello di gustarsi il pranzo davanti al sublime panorama lagunare, capace di donare calma anche tra il brulicare del centro di Venezia. Ricordo che il porticato esterno ospita anche dei tavolini all'aperto con il tetto coperto, molto utili in tutte le stagioni per una pausa rigenerante.

name: natalie rhone
review: perfect spot for tourists missing a taste of home. got a biscoff donut and toffee iced coffee, mom got orange juice and muffin. all wonderful! coffee wasn’t the best but Italy isn’t known for it’s iced coffee anyway lol. rlly great spot!

name: Antony Nobilo
review: Good coffee, nice staff, toilet and kids area. Good little cheap and easy cafe with wifi

name: Nabil R
review:
The cold drinks are mostly sugar and there’s not much coffee taste in them. I would recommend just buying the hot coffees if you’re not into sweet drinks that are too sugary.

The plus is the coffee comes out fast, there is plenty of seating inside, and the service is quite quick. Good for fast hot coffees but not good for cold drinks on a hot day. Will probably not return here.

name: Modar Ali
review: I was surprised to find such a place in Venice. It's a good place to grab a cup of coffee and relax while working on a laptop or just resting from touring the city. The coffee is really tasty, the prices are reasonable, and the staff is nice.

name: Jaga Jeevan
review: Mariana quite friendly and helped out in choosing vegetarian options ! Either a quick grab or just relaxed coffee after a long walk worked for me ! Quite Indian Friendly 🤩

name: Americaninparis2012
review: I ordered ice coffe qnd oreo milkshake, which i and my daughter enjoyed very much.   the person manning the shop was by herself but very friendly and efficient.   Highly recommend the treats.

name: Uros Sestovic
review: Highly recommend! Prices are better than neighboring caffe shops and the atmosphere is also enjoyable! You want to warm yourself with hot cup of coffee? This is the place! 😁

name: Katie P
review:
Got 2 coffees here as a means to get off the tourist street.
Ordered 2 iced cappuccinos, one of the best coffees I’ve had here in Italy- not too strong like some coffees. Would have been 5 but there’s a pigeon roaming around 🤣

name: Pritesh Patel
review: We needed a quick meal before one of our tours and walked into Cafe Venezia without any prior research.  In a pinch, the place fills the void for a quick eat and enough seating to accommodate a group of 6 or 8.  Of all the food we ordered, the Salame Piccante Pizza was pretty good.  The staff was efficient, but not overtly friendly as other reviews have indicated.  Overall, it gets the job done for a quick lunch.

name: bartika dutta
review: Three stars because the host could have been friendlier. No, we weren’t charged extra for the food we received. I liked the cannoli with cream, and the breakfast I had was hearty, although the omelette was runny in places. The pizza with salami was spicy and tasty, although tough in bits.

name: Eric
review: Just… don’t do that to yourself. They charged me 2€/ person for using utensils and 5.50€ for a can of coke. Considering the atmosphere, the service, and the attitude of the restaurant’s workers, this is literally … a scam (I don’t even try to take a picture of the food, it looks unappealing and unappetizing anyway).

name: Yiğitcan Olcay
review: It was bad experience, we ordered one tiramisu and it was frozen,  it was the worst tiramusu in my life and everything is overpriced don’t go there.

name: Partha Bakre
review:
SCAMMER CAFE ALERT!!!!!!!

I recently had an extremely disappointing experience at Cafe Venezia in Venice. The staff were exceptionally rude and dismissive, showing a complete disregard for customer service.

The coffee served was not only subpar but also noticeably underfilled, indicating a lack of quality and care. Additionally, the prices were exorbitant, clearly targeting unsuspecting tourists.

They did not allow us to use the washroom and upon asking they started joking about it.
They charged us 12€ for two cappuccinos.

The Chinese owner is a racist too. They don’t accept cards and scam you with cash payments.

The owner’s behavior was particularly inhumane and impolite, further tarnishing the establishment’s reputation. I strongly advise fellow travelers to avoid this café to prevent being scammed and mistreated.

name: GY
review: I don’t recommend to eat here. Unfortunately we didn’t look at the ratings before entering this Café. It sells only convenience foods, which is warmed up in a microwave. Pasta with Seafood had no seafood at all, pizza was rock hard and had no taste. To pay nearly 40€ for this is too much.

name: Sven
review:
This is why you sometimes have to experience things first hand.

There are a decent amount of negative reviews. This is a positive review. 4 of us sat for cappuccinos, one of us got a breakfast: ham, cheese, eggs, bread.

Cappuccinos were made delicately, ratio was spot on, fresh pour, milk not scalded and perfectly frothed and creamed.

Dish was nothing intricate or complex, but everything was fresh and flavorful.

Overall very pleased with our quick stop for a pick me up.

name: Danny Yang
review: Astronomical drink prices where a can of coke costs 6€ .  Personal sized pizzas ranged from 12€ to 17€ which wasn’t too bad and tasted ok, but once you add in the drink the meal gets a lot more expensive.  Girl at cash register didn’t want to take credit card and asked for cash even though our bill was over 50€.  My mistake for not checking Google reviews before going inside.

name: Christopher Chung
review:
Arguably one of the best Grand Canal views from a Venetian cafe — water traffic seen just steps from the coffee table. A modern revamp that respects the historic architecture within this regal palazzo. Perfect for some refreshment before and after visiting the Ca’ Pesaro exhibits.

A must-visit when in Venice.

name: J Law
review: Incredible view, reasonable price and good service. The girl reminded us that the orange juice isn't fresh made which was really sweet of her 😊we didn't expect it anyway given the price. It was my first time having caffe orzo, which was an interesting flavour 😂 really enjoyed the time chilling there after walking around the gallery.

name: Robert T.
review: Come here to experience a service that is everything but Italian: not friendly, not smiling, not saying hello or goodbye. Throwing the menu on your table while walking by. Please don’t go here and spoil your museum visit!

name: Oksana Od
review:
The location of the bar terrace is absolutely incredible, right on the Grand Canal.
There are two tables in the first row and we had to wait a little to change seats and enjoy the view, but we didn't have to wait for our coffee to be served.

name: damla yavru
review: for tiramisu and a cappuccino i paid 9 euros i think tiramusu wasn’t that good but you pay for the view. It would be better if those glasses weren’t there because it really affects the view badly

name: Expor ter
review: While being lost in Venice, here could be a pleasant stop  for a break and coffee with great Grand canal view

name: Adriana Zbanț
review: Recently, very rude staff. Do not recommend it all. We came here before with two very small suitcases to enjoy a quick cafe before leaving for the airport, as there are nice views of the canal and ample room next to the table for the small trolley. Sadly, this time the security staff were very unpleasant and rude when telling us that we cannot enter with the small luggage for a coffee. Again, there is plenty of room for a small trolley and the cafe is separate from the museum, there were NO other clients and there was no reason to deny us our entry. The male security staff was particularly nasty and rude, completely unlike any other place in Venice. As we return every year to Venice, this is a museum and a cafe, we won't be revisiting. There are other better places here, with polite and decent staff.

name: Sol Perez Martinez
review:
You can’t leave small carry bags in the cloakroom.

The man at the door was very unpleasant

name: A L
review:
Charge touriste more than locals
Orange Juice for 6$
I was tired i didnt wanted to discuse just paid.

name: mark stanford
review: I got a good flat white the first day. Very friendly and efficient so went back the next day and the guy remembered me, didn't need to order! Great service!

name: Oscar Kerkhoven
review: Two little cups with warm orange juice, opportunisticly prices 12€. 👎

name: Jamie Winder
review: 7euros for 2 coffees and a choc croissant, pretty happy with that. Service was friendly, very quick even with other customers there.

name: Filippo Lisiero
review: Be careful and always check your receipt. One of the staff members tried to overcharge me, so make sure everything is correct before you pay.

name: Ted Narine
review: The food was great, very authentic, the hot chocolate was very rich.  Croissants  were good size and delicious.  Service was friendly

name: Helene Vinh
review: Nothing made me happier than paying the tourist price on my first morning in Venise! 20e for a coffee, 2 orange juices, 1 croissant and 1 toast. Maybe speak italian to pay less

name: chris bernard
review:
If you are a tourist do not come. You will bé overcharged, it is way better inside the old city even paying the tax + breakfast is cheaper.
However guys weere smiling thus 1.

name: Mohamed Abdelmalek Balthazar Fradi
review: Al Ponte del Lovo is a charming little spot in Venice with great service and a cozy atmosphere. I tried the cannoli, and it was absolutely delicious — crisp shell, creamy filling, and beautifully presented. However, the price was a bit high compared to other places in the city where the cannoli tasted just as good. Still, it’s a lovely stop for a sweet treat if you don’t mind paying a little extra for the experience.

name: Rosy Strong
review: We stopped here so we could have a coffee and use the restrooms. Kids wanted something sweet of course! The cannolo was too sweet for my liking and the traditional Venetian Doge biscuit was bright green like shrek! Anyway, can’t complain when in Venice! Service was friendly, coffee good and an extra star for free water without asking :-)

name: Andrea Begović
review: Stuff very friendly, fast serving and cappuccino and cannoncini pistachio are fabulous! The best I tried in Venezia! 🤤🤤🤤🥰🥰🥰

name: Javier Campos
review: Absolutely do not buy from this establishment. I was charged double the standard price for a small piece of turrone. When I realized the blatant overcharge and politely asked for a refund, they flatly refused to correct their error or return the difference. This is less about the price and more about the principle: they are clearly gouging tourists and then displaying appalling customer service by refusing to make things right. Spend your money elsewhere, where you'll get fair pricing and decent treatment. Avoid this place.

name: illion
review: It was a long day of walking so I didn’t mind paying for a seat. The drink was great. The Canolli was ok. I wished I had more stomach space to try all the flavors but I’m sure how traditional these flavors are 😂

name: 몬리치
review:
Coffee was good.
But the cannoli tasted like it wasn't freshly made.
I don't think this is a good place to relax. I expected a more comfortable atmosphere since the dine-in prices are quite high. However, it feels more suited for a quick visit.

name: Cristian Teodorescu
review: They try to rip you off, added two extra items on the bill with inflated prices, and when I pointed it out, they didn’t even apologize. The coffee was weak, the tiramisu mediocre at best, and the marzipan overly sweet.

name: Terry Mclell
review: Beautiful display of cannoli got us in the door.  Nice place to rest but our cannoli's   didn't taste fresh; outer shell was stale.(4.5Eu each.) I had a capaccino and my husband ordered a coke.  Total bill 21Eu. With that said we stayed a while to catch our breathe and map out the rest of our journey in Venice.

name: Jim Elsenbeck
review: We booked a cicchetti tour and this was the first stop.  We ended up sitting down outside because we were right on the Grand Canal with an incredible view.  This was also our introduction to spritz, so we had to try the aperol, the hugo and the cynar.  They weren't our last spritz's for this trip.  ;)

name: Oliver Wehner
review:
Never go there with children.
Very unfriendly.
Children where sitting calmly on the table playing some Table games.
After the second warning we were requested to leave.
They were never loud nor disturbing someone.
Just ridiculous.
Never again

name: Eric3 Skywalker
review:
Sat down at a table at 2pm to have a dessert and a drink. Most of the other tables were empty. We got sent away because "they only accepted people for lunch".
Clowns...

name: Gábor Majer
review: We bought 2 ice coffee. One of them was delicious, other one is not. Everybody was kind and you can enjoy the coffee next to the Grand Canal. Price is 10-20€ if you buy coffee for 2 person. If you wanna it somethinh then 20-30€.

name: Esra Zengin
review: Very close to the Rialto Bridge, with a beautiful ambiance and a location near the waterfront. Our coffees were delicious, but the tiramisu was average. The waiter who assisted us was very polite. Recommended for those who want to enjoy a pleasant time with a view.

name: Taylor Zang
review: We stumbled upon this place while walking on the grand canal at Venice. The place is perfectly located in front of the canal very near to Rialto bridge. We ordered one of their vegetarian pizzas called Pizza alle verdure. It was perfectly served with right amount of vegetables. It was delicious and filling. In Venice they already charge sitting fee so people normally don’t tip here, which we found out towards the end of our trip :-)

name: Alan Hewitt
review: Lovely atmosphere by yhe Rialto bridge with the water right next to you. The service was very friendly, but the food was average. Not enough flavor or brightness in the pasta dishes. Better value for similar prices elsewhere.

name: Katie Hall
review: So good we came here twice on the same day! Great coffee and cakes, good service, and a really nice casual and cool atmosphere inside. Or you can sit outside by the canal. If you stand at the bar you can get much cheaper coffee, but if you're walking around Venice you need to sit - and this place is great.

name: Joe Vo
review: We came here for a night cap on a Sunday night. The staff were welcoming and we received fast service. We ordered a Tokyo Island and a Scotch Fashioned. Both were delicious!

name: Abby Altman
review: We ordered an aperol spritz, they filled our cup up half way which is fine, but then we saw other people ordering them and they filled their cups to the top. We are Americans- not sure if this has anything to do with it. The woman that was helping us was extremely rude. Would not recommend.

name: josie hendrick
review: We wanted one coffee and the sour faced waitress said no we had to order more. Appalling. No atmosphere and quiet anyway. Avoid at all costs!

name: Anna Ehlert
review: Great coffee, tasty cupcake (red velvet), stunning view!!!

name: Norm P.
review: Excellent food and terrific service. Great for both the food; atmosphere and for 'watching the world go past'

name: Joanna Audino
review: The worst waiter I have ever met, the worst experience! Waiter mocked guests behind their backs, was charged $11 for two coffees! Worst experience. Don’t go! -0

name: Paul Archdeacon
review: Pleasant and welcoming staff. Food was good enough for a lunch mid week. Wine excellent.  All in all a reasonably priced and enjoyable experience.  Don't be put off by the negative reviews. Well worth a visit.

name: Robert Rozek
review: Best Cafe in Venice

name: Patrick Moran
review: Oh my goodness—this was the best pizza we had during our trip to Italy this summer! The crust was perfectly done, the ingredients were incredibly fresh, and the flavors were unforgettable. I wish I could go back just for another bite! The service was also kind and welcoming.

name: I Shah
review: Handsdown, the best pizza I’ve ever had… like literally. Everything is baked inside the bakery fresh.

name: Mzazi
review: Very nice piece of pizza, highly recommend to stop off here for a quick bite to eat and a drink after crossing over the Rialto Bridge whilst you’re exploring Venice

name: Carl Clark
review: Great place to get a quick breakfast or brunch. Yesterday we stopped by about 10 am and had focaccia and cappuccino. This morning yogurt, croissant, and cappuccino. Friendly staff who spoke excellent English. We ended up eating here three times!

name: J Law
review: Absolutely fell in love with Farini! We were in Venice for 5 days and ate here 3 times at 3 different locations. The pizza was beyond amazing, to the point that I already started missing it BEFORE I finished eating. The dough is so deliciously soft and full of flavours. This has been one of the best pizzas I've ever had (my other favourite is Aromi in Cambridge). Wish Farini grow fast and come to the UK asap!!!

name: Misha K.
review: For those on the go in Venice, this spot offers a convenient slice of pizza to take away. While it might not claim the title of the best pizza in town, it's a decent option when hunger strikes. The pizza is satisfactory, providing a quick and easy solution to satisfy your cravings without the need to sit down in a pizzeria. If you're in a hurry or simply looking to grab a bite while exploring, this place can fill that need with a no-fuss, tasty enough slice.

name: George Dy
review:
I absolutely love Farini. We stumbled upon one of their many storefronts within the labyrinth of streets in Venice. On our first visit, we were greeted with a smile, pleasantly conversed with using choppy tourist Italian, and then easily ordered pizza - salsiccia e pomodoro. We found another location the following morning and ordered some focaccia and coffee. Time and money well spent.

It was so good, I was completely unfazed when a pigeon decided to leave me with a souvenir from above.

name: Tunay Mehmedov
review: A quick lunch avoiding the rain with fantastic pizza with thick dough and lots of crunch! The prices are good considering the closeness to Rialto bridge.

name: Gary Williamson
review:
Great little spot for a quick bite to eat.

We had a couple slices of pizza and a slice if focaccia. The focaccia was a little burnt on the bottom but didn’t taste bad by any means. Just not the best we’ve had.

Would still recommend.

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
You know almost every place in Venice seems to have excellent coffee and baked goods???? It’s crazy the high quality and high standards they have as it’s on par with Paris.  I enjoyed this place as the atmosphere is cool and fun as well as fresh. Merchandising is tantalizing.
Service is warm and friendly.
Food is fresh and delicious.
No reason to not go - you have to go it’s a great place to enjoy great food.

name: Nurettin Tuncer
review: Fresh pizza and focaccia slices, good coffee, and friendly staff. Perfect for a quick lunch or snack near the center. Clean, modern, and reasonably priced.

name: Chasing Flavor
review:
Farini is one of my favorite places in Venice. The pizzas are always fresh, light, and super tasty. The quality is great, and the prices are really fair. I’ve tried many kinds, and I liked them all! It’s a clean, quick, and easy place to enjoy good food.
I just wish there was a Farini in Vienna—I’d go all the time! 😊

name: Max Mismetti
review: We were walking aimlessly and this place caught us by the delicious smell. Great focaccia sandwiches and pizza slices.

name: F S
review:
Worst pizza i have had in italy
+ racist staff - deliberately let other customer cut although he knew i was first (he gave me that look)
should had just left.
food was really bad - unnecessarily salty, each bite gets worse
also staff did not use and standardised timing to heat up. he went by his own feeling. others pizza were severely charred.

Please stay away from here, head to Antico Forno instead it’s so much better there, great staff + amazing pizza (NOT HERE) it’s literally 1 min walk away. Antico’s pizza is not as salty and crunchy to the bite unlike here.

thanks for reading, i hope this review saves you

(Imagine the atmosphere scores 3 stars more then their food, service and overall rating;
Overall 1/5
Food 1/5
Service 1/5
Atmosphere 4/5 (good music with great lighting and toilet for use)

name: Clay
review: It was a good bakery/cafe. Not super flaky/fluffy pain au chocolate but still good.  Quick service though you may need to be quick with your order.  We stumbled a bit and the guy wanted us to finish up our order quick. It helps to look at the pictures of the menu before going.

name: Nic Banister
review:
Stopped in for a quick snack mid morning. Decent selection of pizza, foccacia and pasta at what we thought were reasonable prices. Great cappuccino as well.

Modern and light decor inside with a number of stools and benches. If you want a break from the more rustic places, would recommend.

name: Corri Zerebeski
review:
traditional venetian food - had beautiful tasty pizza & pastas for our group of 10 for lunch.
great value for money and the staff were lovely. also provided gluten free pasta which we’ve discovered is hard to find in venice! highly recommend this restaurant - even if you just stop for a drink. it was very affordable for central venice ps. wine was lovely too

name: Sylvain Querné
review: They may not have the best tramezzini in Venezia but they are legit. The bar has a small room inside, good for a break. Prices were actually ok given it’s a highly touristic place.

name: Dan Pinkston
review: Came here every single day of our holiday in Venice. Coffee was great, sandwiches were delicious. Arancini was tasty. Great and friendly service. Great restaurant!

name: G B
review:
Great, friendly services with deliciously fair priced food.

Grazie millie!

name: Hayley Nicolette
review:
Open early (one of the few places I could find). Quick service and perfect if you want to grab a takeaway sandwich to save € later in the day.
Good espresso ☕

name: Sarah Badat
review: Very unfriendly owner. My brother and I bought a bottle of water and asked if we could use the bathroom. I’ve travelled all over Italy and it’s very common practice because public bathrooms are few and far between. The owner told us we could only go if each person buys something. Ridiculous. Rude. Avoid this place!

name: Theresse Pereira
review: Authentic and really reasonable pricing for breakfast or a midday snack

name: Lori Fern
review: We had a great experience here and staff behind the counter were pleasant and efficient.  The tramezzinis were very tasty as were the paninis. Beer and bellinis were refreshing. Locals definitely frequent here, and we saw numerous gondoliers grabbing a bite to eat.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Ozge Ustundag
review: A calm little café tucked inside the museum garden, perfect for a short rest between galleries. We shared a slice of chocolate cake and had two cappuccinos for about €20. The atmosphere is peaceful, surrounded by sculptures and greenery, but the food itself was fairly ordinary. Nice to take a break here, though not remarkable enough to visit on its own.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Heinrich A.
review: I had an espresso, and it was good. My mobile phone battery was almost flat, so I asked the waiter (Christian) if it was possible to have it charged. Without hesitation, he said he could manage and he kindly returned it to me later. The garden in which the Café is situated is stunning.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Kristo Vaher
review: Coffee is good. Service is extremely slow. And food is average, even dry to extent. I'd suggest them to just offer cafe and cakes, food menu is not worth it.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Jenny N. Wilde
review: It's just grotesquely expensive.  €8 for a soft drink. $6 for coffee. At least double the price of other cafes in Venice, why? The service is terrible.  It feels very exploitative.  I recommend taking your money elsewhere.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Matt Blake
review: Beautifully situated cafe with wonderful food. Was a little pricey but perhaps reassuringly expensive. Service was a bit edgy in moments but overall lovely, would recommend without hesitation.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: V Sveidqvist
review:
Oh Dear. A museum café, I should have known better. We went on a guidebook recommendation that said it was "expensive but very good". We don't mind paying a bit more for good food, and our expectations of portion sizes are average European, not North American. In order to make an informed choice, I asked the waiter about the dish of the day (eggplant parmigiana) - is it served with any side dish or not? Is it a main meal or more of a starter potion size? He just looked at me blankly and said he didn't understand my question and when I tried to clarify he said "it's the normal size you get in a restaurant". Wow.

What we ended up with was: 2 small round slices of eggplant with some sauce and parmigian for me (basically a small starter, though officially a main meal), chicken strips and potatoes for my husband (about 1/3 of an average main meal) a small basket of bread sticks and a 65cl bottle of water. All for the bargain price of €48.

On the plus side: the café is beautifully situated in a quiet, lush garden with jasmine flowers everywhere. It's a calm spot to have a quick coffee break from touristing, but only if you also plan to fully enjoy the superb art collection (you can't access the café without a museum ticket).

Save your money, eat elsewhere.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Itamar
review: Amazing place, the service was great, we got our food in no time. I recommend the club sandwich 👌, delicious. Very recommend. Also there is a kid section and a dessert section.

place_title: Museum Cafe at Peggy Guggenheim Collection
name: Predrag P
review: Excellent food! Great ravioli stuffed woth buratta cheese, delcious salad with feta and nectarines ... Friendly and fast service!

name: Bahattin Çiniç
review:
We had a salty croissant, a focaccia sandwich, and two sweet croissants.
Everything was great! The focaccia sandwich was the best one, but the others were also really good.

name: Jože Habinc
review: All three pizzas we tried tasted great, the service was solid and the price, considering the location, was also good. However, there were a lot of leftovers on the tables (but everyone could clean up after themselves, as they have trash cans installed), the pigeons between the tables were not pleasant to watch, and what bothered me the most was the strong operation of the air conditioners and ceiling fans. They really overdo it with the cooling.

name: Sarah Petry
review: We are staying in Venice and wanted a nice easy dinner. We found Farini, they were friendly and welcoming to some tourists with little Italian. We ordered and range of pizzas and the boys ordered some tarts for dessert.

name: Bhawana Agarwal
review:
🌟 5 stars

One of the best pizza places you can try—an absolute hidden gem! The pizza is a must-have, even if you’re not hungry. It’s that good! A total steal for the quality you get. Perfect for dine-in or takeaway, and the coffee is just as amazing. Friendly and welcoming staff make the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit—Bon appétit! 🍕

name: Moji gh
review: We ordered two caffè lattes and two croissants. The coffee was average but acceptable compared to other places in Italy. The croissants were fresh and tasty. The main downside was the service — the staff member who served us was unfriendly and unwelcoming.

name: Ari Sastre
review: My uncle says that the best pizza starts with the crust, and Farini's crust is superb. Simplistic homegrown ingredients combine together to make a pizza that you think would be found in an expensive celebrity chain. Prices are affordable and reasonable too.

name: Kaitlyn Affuso
review:
The BEST pizza I had while in Italy.
Went to Venice, Florence, and Rome and this was by FAR my favorite. We visited several times while we were in Venice for 3.5 days.

Highly reccommend. Get several different slices and share because they're all delicious!

name: CCG G
review: Good place for croissants and fresh yogurt with fresh fruit and granola. The coffee was honestly one of the grossest coffee ups I’ve ever tasted - it was very burnt I just threw it away. Place is small with limited seating so it’s chaotic and not a good place to sit and eat. Highly recommend if you’re craving yogurt though.

name: Facundo Cesa
review:
Our experience wasn't as bad as others I've read. They didn't try to con us at least.

But it's true, this was the absolute worst cannolo in the world. If by accident you try this disgusting thing filled with a sugary hydrogenated vegetal crap, please, don't believe you've tried an ACTUAL cannolo.

The waitress didn't understand our Italian. Which, come on... Isn't THAT bad...

Maybe Italian wasn't her first language either. Maybe she comes from a country without formaggio grattugiato or smiles.

name: Joana Pack Melo Sousa
review: The worst coffe shop ever!! The taste of the cannolli was greasy. The price of the cappuccino outside says 3,50. They charge 6,00 inside + 2 euros of service. They were rude when we asked about the price.

name: Mitch Tornquist
review: DO NOT be fooled by the great location and beautiful window display. Absolute worst cannolo I’ve ever had in my life. In fact I’m not sure how they could have made it any worse. The filling tasted like icing and sugar. Sickeningly sweet. I did not finish it, and that’s crazy, cause I eat everything.

name: F.M. Zowczak
review: Terrible service, you have to pay table cover fee - 2€ per person (even though you’ve ordered 2 small cookies). Food is very poor quality. Cookies are extremely sweet without any flavor (it’s so sweet that you want to vomit). Toilets are not clean, tables are placed to close to each other so you can’t barely move. Do not recommend…

name: Giovanni Balzi
review: Don’t fund this kind of business, the guy overcharged a spremuta just because we sat down and this extra charge was not specified anywhere for the client to see. The person at the counter was very rude when he confronted us. I would say to stay away from this type of establishment.

name: Leo Babayan
review: Place was one of the only spots open late so we went without checking the reviews, we spend $49 euros for 3 ppl and we all got sandwiches, let me tell you the sandwich’s at the gas station and train stations taste way better. It was plan and 1 start out of 5. The workers were nice but food sucks l.

name: john fing
review: We stopped in for coffee and tea very early one morning. Now asking for a black tea with milk on the side is not a usual Italian request. Accomplished, water in a pot steaming hot, good quality tea brand and milk on the side in a little jug. Came with a little biscuit on the saucer and a sachet of honey. The coffee was, well, coffee, not great but also not bad, a well made average coffee.

name: Megan Prosise
review: We sat down and enjoyed two coffees, three cannolis, and another little pastry. Everything tasted amazing. This was about 22 euros. The restaurant is very close to the Rialto Bridge, so I imagine that's why the price is higher because of the tourism aspect. We thoroughly appreciated the very kind service provided by the ladies working there. The only part that was a bummer was that they asked us to pay with cash instead of card. I'm not sure if card would've been accepted or euros just highly preferred.

name: Ficho Stainov
review: I sat down in the Café on a weekday in October and I had to work for a few hours on my laptop. The coffee, food and Aperol were great and the staff was friendly and did not object me working there. The prices are also reasonable for Venice. All in all a great café and good for home office.

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
We stopped at this café for a quick coffee, even though we had seen some negative reviews beforehand. The prices for coffee at the table are on the higher side (I know it’s common to have a difference compared to the counter, but it still felt a bit much). The coffee itself was fine, though not among the best we tried in Italy.

The atmosphere was lively, and we noticed that local regulars were greeted very warmly by the staff, which gave the place a certain charm. Our own experience was more neutral — the service was efficient but not particularly attentive. Overall, it’s a stylish café, though perhaps better suited for locals who already know it well.

name: Sebastian Römer (sibbedaiah)
review:
We had breakfast at this café in Venice, and while the food and atmosphere were decent (3/5), the service was unfortunately one of the worst experiences we’ve had during our trip.

The waiter was incredibly unfriendly towards tourists, even though we were polite and courteous the entire time. What made it even more uncomfortable was seeing how warmly he treated the locals at the nearby tables — a stark contrast that left us feeling very unwelcome.

Despite our friendly attitude, it felt as if he had taken a personal dislike to us for no reason. Even when we left a tip (which, frankly, wasn’t deserved), he accepted it only very reluctantly, almost as if it bothered him.

Such a shame, because the café itself has a nice setting and the breakfast was fine. But the poor attitude and lack of basic respect from the waiter completely spoiled the experience

name: David Cho
review: I liked the food and Morning staffs were great but one of the Afternoon staff came and threw the menu on the middle of table and slammed the tissue box on top of it. Was light throw and slam but was not necessary because I already finished my order. Still giving 3 stars for Morning staffs were very friendly with big smiles

name: Saffron Koinch
review:
Extremely rude staff – completely ruined our morning!

This café is close to our hotel and looked like a nice, local Italian spot, so we decided to have breakfast here. Big mistake. The staff was unbelievably rude — honestly on another level.

I simply asked if they make a flat white, and the man’s response was shockingly rude and dismissive. As a tourist, I was just trying to understand their menu, but he acted like my question was ridiculous. He’s a local and obviously knows what coffee they serve, so he could’ve easily explained it politely instead of responding with such an attitude.

I was so shocked and upset that I just stood there in silence, not wanting to argue or let it ruin our morning.

name: Elizabeth Heiner
review: Delicious food. Ignore the comments regarding rude service. We did our best to speak Italian and it’s amazing what happens when you try to speak the native language. We saw some very rude Americans which was embarrassing for us as fellow Americans. We ordered cappuccinos, caffe, and hot chocolate along with a myriad of sandwiches. Highly recommend!

name: Hiten Umarnani
review: Firstly served cold food despite clearly stating on the menu that the food was hot. Secondly, it was incredibly overpriced with the menus online being cheaper clearly lying about the prices. Thirdly, we specifically asked for non seeded bread as a member of our group had a severe food allergy, it wasn't even a modification as on the menu the bread was non seeded. Yet still they served our freezing cold sandwiches with seeded bread because they ran out apparently. After this attempted homicide, they refused to give us a new sandwich and that member had to go outside. There's specifically a bearded waiter/manager who was incredibly rude. He shouted at us  to not drink outside drinks despite the fact that we only had the bottles in our bags and gave us a darn ultimatum to leave despite people on our table already having food. Genuinely, one of the worst experiences I've ever had in a restaurant. This is worse than some  inner city Denny's I've been in.

name: Martin Kluk
review: Not sure how anyone could complain about this shop. The service and food/drinks were great and the prices were very reasonable compared to the insane prices of the square down the road. Thanks for having us!

name: Noor Yassine
review: We dropped by this cafe in Venice for a mid-afternoon coffee break. Their Cappuccino was delicious. Their veggie pizza was average, kinda disappointing how it was not wood fired & we’re literally in Italy. Their outdoor seating was open with plenty of fresh air & pretty views. Only con was that the owner was kind of rude & wasn’t as welcoming when we placed our order.

name: Nadya Fetisova
review: We stayed next door, and came for breakfast the day we were leaving. Esther, the owner, was very nice and welcoming, and explained the menu for us. Service was fast, coffee was great, all good impression. I also liked how they bring coffee in big bright cups all colour mixed, very cheerful. They also sell packaged coffee, we got it as a nice souvenir from Italy.

name: Jennifer Claros
review:
DO NOT go to this place. They are very rude. Specially the owner. We were ordering capuchinos inside and the guy told us to go outside and get a table, the owner grabbed my 9 year old daughter and pulled her outside and told her they don’t serve capuchinos for 5 people. My husband tried to ask why and told us to leave. This is the guy who grabbed my daughters shirt. YOU DON’T TOUCH, you need to be respectful.
To the old owner guy- if you have a business with a lot of tourists you need to be respectful. If you are not respectful then close your business because we as tourists are giving you money we are not begging you for anything.

name: Craig Bentley
review:
Valerio  and Ricardo hosted us today on2 separate sittings.

You could not ask for a nicer or more welcoming restaurant to eat and drink.

The drinks are fresh and fast as soon s you order. As well as the foot and ice cream.

I will always come back here.

Highly recommend!

name: Noemi Garcia
review:
wish I could give this place zero stars. From the second we sat down, the staff (guy in the picture) was unwelcoming. Our group of five simply wanted to order cappuccinos, but we were told that they “don’t serve cappuccino to five people.” They refused to explain and instead told us to leave. The guy put his hand on my niece. YOU DON’T DO THAT, especially to a CHILD.

This was hands down the worst service I’ve ever encountered. If you expect even a basic level of respect as a customer, do yourself a favor and take your service where you’ll be respected!

name: tokozon
review: The group of young girls may be treated ok here. Outside seatings are nice. There are several kinds of coffees with photos in the menu. That was thoughtful, but young waiter was super rude. After seeing the other reviews, it did not surprise me. Just to be aware of not getting a friendly service. They may be tired of dealing with tourists.

name: Yuriy Yakovenko
review: I should have checked google reviews prior to going there. They brought me a cold cappuccino. When I pointed that out, they just added some hot water, so it didn’t taste like anything at all. No apology. Paid 24 euros for just two croissants, watered down cappuccino and horrendous service. Avoid this place. There are a lot of good places around.

name: Qatar4ever
review: Very good coffee & dessert, but the chairs for seating outside a bit uncomfortable

name: Rak P
review: A very lively and popular bacaro. Prime position. Location is picture perfect, opposite church, across ponte/bridge overlooking canal. Popular with locals and workers but also passing tourists coming into the area. Stopped over in the morning for coffee but the real unexpected star were the croissants,  had these wirh chocolate,  orange and pistachio fillings, they are delicious.  Best croissants we had whilst staying in Venice. Prompt and efficient service. Chichetti looked good, nice selection and popular as well. Good selection of local wines. Nice vibe and ambience, both mornings and evenings, people in and outside the bar. Very reasonable prices. If you are visiting, come over and have a slice of typical Venice.

name: Sebastian N
review: We stopped by this bar while strolling through Venice and noticed a great selection of cicchetti displayed in the window. We decided to stay and try a few, along with a couple of drinks. We sat by the window with a lovely view of the city — such a pleasant spot! Delicious cicchetti, friendly service, and a wonderful atmosphere. Highly recommended!

name: A L
review: This was our favourite bacaro in Venice. We returned here a few times as it was so central and lively in the evening. The bar is right on the steps of a bridge, next to large open area and church which makes it the perfect spot for a select spritz and cicchetti. Well recomended.

name: Patrick Kluver
review: Fantastic experience. Great value and quality for money! Best priced cicchetti in town! Meat 2€, fish 2.50€. We’ll be back!

name: Ricky Lamar
review:
This little bar and cicchetteria has it going on! Georgia with her mischievous twinkle will mix you up a traditional Italian spritzer offering up tasty tapas to tempt those taste buds.
And Lorenzo, in charge of the fun made this a night to remember.

name: Nima Lama
review: We made a quick stop for some drinks and ended up trying their cicchetti. It was delicious! The staff were super friendly, and we were lucky to get a seat right by the airco. Grazie mille!

name: Rhakria and or Embisch
review:
Impeccable bacaro experience.
I'll list some positives :
- Wine selection, as well as enough other drinks for those who don't want wine
- freshly prepared Cicchetti with enough variety to suit all tastes
- very stylish music playlist of classic 80-90s disco
- lovely / lively people behind the counter

The negatives :
- can't think of one...

Thank you for the well spent evening 🤗

name: David Wilson
review: We stopped in for a coffee and water.  Everyone was so friendly and the cappuccinos were VERY tasty. Grazie Mille!

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
A lovely place to have coffee and a variety of pastries and baked good such as a surprisingly delicious croissant.
Food is delicious and fresh.
Service is polite and friendly.
Atmosphere is modern and clean and merchandise is clean as well.
Reasonably good value.

Great place to nosh

name: Sara
review:
I was looking for a good pistacchio croissant and couldn't find it...One older lady told me there is pasticceria so I checked and it was amazing!
I tried pistacchio croissant and also frittella, they were absolutely great!

name: Valeriya Smirnova
review: Awesome lemon roll cake - very tender cream, not too sweet - just perfect

name: Alexis Kokonuzz
review: Cant speak about the croissants, maybe those are good, but the pistacchio cannoli is disappointment made pastry. Terrible artificial cream flavor, horrible value for money, sad sad sad.

name: RnV Marty
review: We tried a few flavors of cannoli and they were all delicious. Glad we stopped in.

name: Alex O.
review: No pistachio in my cannoli, only sugar

name: David Zemdegs
review: Lovely place for coffee and pastries.

name: Mike Tretola
review: Wanted to try the cannolis that were in the window and were highly disappointed. The filling was like a whipped cream, not traditional ricotta or mascarpone. Cappuccinos were excellent, though.

name: Oren Bar
review:
Great food, fantastic service and good price!
The toast / focaccia was super fresh and very tasty! Would definitely recommend! Not a tourist trap! I came here 4 years ago and it is still amazing!

name: zeke monroe
review: Fantastic spot, the first night we arrived in Venice we had arrived late and even though they were in the process of closing the man running the shop, Sandro, sold us some water. The next day we came up he remembered us and set us up with a fantastic meal and even set some roses and a lamp on the table. Ordered the Focaccia Ricotta E Spinaci and the Tiramisu. 10/10 recommend. You will not be disappointed.

name: Allie Arnaiz
review:
Great food for the price, meals came with so much. A coffee, entree, and a bread. Really good food. Good place to stop at if you're hungry in the morning before going further into the city.
Great view
Don't specifically remember the service but I feel like that is a good thing. Not exceptional but not bad.

name: Gabriela Gisca
review:
J’ai bien aimé les pasta 😍😍 it was so delicious
I recommend it for price and for its taste

name: Nonso Come
review:
A nice little spot in Venice
Stopped by for a quick coffee and snack. The service was friendly and the atmosphere cozy. Prices were reasonable for the area. Nothing fancy, but a pleasant place to take a short break while exploring the city.

name: adam schonert
review: We were here everyday during our trip to Venice. They have reasonable prices, great quantity, and kind and fast service. They have a comprehensive menu with quality items. We would get eggs, bacon, toasts, croissants, and coffee in the morning. We had a Malibu coke in the afternoon, and it was wonderful. Highly recommend!

name: Bosanski Ljiljan
review: Great Pizza, low Prices and a great Customer Service

name: Susanna Kantelinen
review: The Bellini was beautiful and the vegetarian chicetti delicious and fresh. My waitress, Alice, was just wonderful.

name: Ken Tao
review:
Walked passed many many local cafes, looked at many gorgeous food items on display, but didn't like sitting outside in the steamer and watched by countless other tourist like animals in a zoo, dodged into this Starbucks and went upstairs to hide out in the most precious resource in Venice - AC.
There were 2 staff called Ana, one is a cashier and the other is supervisory.  Both were helpful and able to converse in English.  The food there was local, forget about everything you see in the US.  I had a club sandwich!  It was good despite it might have sat there from morning until noonish when I ordered it.  From the display to the sandwich toaster, grilled until there was a crosshatch.  Cut in half and shared.  The bread was regular white bread size.  Costed only 7 Euro, it didn't have the raw ham but can't complain.   Separately ordered a raw ham croissant, that was small but it was good.  There was this melon pearl iced drink that I was told it was pretty good.  Not here in the US yet.
There were power outlets everywhere upstairs.  The setting has a couple large tables for 5-6 and then lots of smaller table for 4 and then some small rectangular bar tables.  It has the best of the local Los Angeles store setups (regular plug + USB power, chairs, napkins, restroom, inside seating).  No refill, Starbucks App does not work- asked in detail with the Ana's.  DO NOT remotely think about half and half in your coffee.  Polizia might show up.  You won't find it either anywhere.  The tables were all bolted down to the floor.  The two lay back chairs upstairs were not recommended for it's low to the ground/hard to get up and lack the length to support your head weird low angle but you'd need to hold you head/neck strain.  Ground floor is extremely crowded because of the constant long line for the restroom. Americano was superior than the ones here.  Can't comment on the other drinks as I didn't try.
One thing that is somewhere alarming but inescapable was the sewage smell that was Venice in hot weather.

name: Melanie Sarah “LiA” Eggink
review:
Overcrowded, super loud.
What’s somehow ok for the only Starbucks in Venice. But how the situation is handled by the staff is not appropriate.

The line for the toilet is through the whole restaurant between the tables. Tables are not cleaned and dishes and plastic cups from 2 earlier guests piling on tables.

Staff doesn’t care, but tells me to not place my bag on the stairs to pack the mug I just bought, for safety reasons.
It took literally 30 seconds. 😜

name: Dione
review: I speak English & staff were very understanding while I ordered. I young lady remembered me from the day before & knew my order, so excellent service

name: Paulina 89
review:
We had a very disappointing experience at this Starbucks in Venice.
We only had a couple of hours in the city, didn’t want a big lunch, and decided to stop here just for a coffee and  food and to use the facilities. The place was understandably busy, as Venice is full of tourists, but the service was extremely poor.

The coffee arrived quickly and looked nice, but the toast we ordered took over 20 minutes to arrive, by which point the coffee was already cold. When the toast finally came, it was half cold, with artificial-tasting cheese — honestly inedible.

To make matters worse, there was only one toilet in the entire  large) Starbucks, and it was out of order. We only found out after ordering, as the tiny sign was hidden behind products at the counter. Considering they serve drinks and food, having no accessible toilet is absolutely unacceptable — especially in a city like Venice, where public toilets are very hard to find.

Overall, this was a poor experience. We came to Starbucks expecting reliability and basic comfort, but instead left disappointed. I will definitely avoid this location in the future.

name: Monika Esseová
review: The coffee was good, but the toilet could have been cleaner, and there was only 1  available.

name: Cellist ESTHER
review:
The steps are really friendly and fast. I was happy with the service they provided.
The inside of the store was pretty clean and well organized. If you hate waiting in line, I recommend you go in the morning.

name: Fi Lip
review: Franchise owner, can you please review the toilet maintenance in this starbucks. Very inconvenient and totally visitor unfriendly to keep the toilet closed for a long time

name: SaliiH
review: Starbucks Venezia Rialto is often bustling with tourists, making it challenging to find a seat. Despite the crowds, the staff are impressively quick and efficient, so the lines move fast. Great spot for a coffee break if you don’t mind the busy atmosphere.

name: Bryan
review: Tiny place with fresh gelato with delicious flavors. This was probably the best gelato I had in Venice. The owner was very friendly. Definitely recommend, if in the area and it was fun to eat next to a not-so-busy canal.

name: Bernardo salcido
review:
A lovely spot in a quiet neighborhood.  Right by the gondola repair place.

The marscapone with fig was fabulous.

Salted caramel was great with a stronger flavor.

The little bits of crunchy fruit in the Chantilly were delicious.

name: Kris
review: Easy 5 stars! Friendly, reasonably priced, no queues and… amazing ice cream!! The best we had all summer!

name: G M
review: What to say? A very good ice cream spot in Venezia: great location near the gondolas workshop, many students in the area as well, great ambiance and of course very tasty ice cream: few choices for an efficient selection with creamy and tasty ice creams. Very nice welcoming and service level, I recommend the place.

name: Moon
review: This was such a nice gelato shop. It was so tasty and creamy. The guy was also very friendly. Wish I could come back here again.

name: na na
review: Maybe I would have rated this place more highly were it located anywhere else other than the country of amazing gelato. The texture was too icy and hard, more like regular ice cream and not really like gelato — the picture and shape of the scoop already shows this is not a regular gelato. The flavors we got, pistacchio (recommended by other reviewers) and nocciola (always a safe choice) tasted way too artificial. I’d gladly go to a chain before coming back here.

name: Daniel D.
review: Best ice creams in Venice and we have tried all other „best” places. This place is the one you don’t want to miss🔥💥🧁

name: alan chang
review: Amazing flavours! The owner is super friendly. I got the salted Carmel and pistachio.

name: Jim Elsenbeck
review: We booked a cicchetti tour and this was the first stop.  We ended up sitting down outside because we were right on the Grand Canal with an incredible view.  This was also our introduction to spritz, so we had to try the aperol, the hugo and the cynar.  They weren't our last spritz's for this trip.  ;)

name: Oliver Wehner
review:
Never go there with children.
Very unfriendly.
Children where sitting calmly on the table playing some Table games.
After the second warning we were requested to leave.
They were never loud nor disturbing someone.
Just ridiculous.
Never again

name: Eric3 Skywalker
review:
Sat down at a table at 2pm to have a dessert and a drink. Most of the other tables were empty. We got sent away because "they only accepted people for lunch".
Clowns...

name: Gábor Majer
review: We bought 2 ice coffee. One of them was delicious, other one is not. Everybody was kind and you can enjoy the coffee next to the Grand Canal. Price is 10-20€ if you buy coffee for 2 person. If you wanna it somethinh then 20-30€.

name: Esra Zengin
review: Very close to the Rialto Bridge, with a beautiful ambiance and a location near the waterfront. Our coffees were delicious, but the tiramisu was average. The waiter who assisted us was very polite. Recommended for those who want to enjoy a pleasant time with a view.

name: Taylor Zang
review: We stumbled upon this place while walking on the grand canal at Venice. The place is perfectly located in front of the canal very near to Rialto bridge. We ordered one of their vegetarian pizzas called Pizza alle verdure. It was perfectly served with right amount of vegetables. It was delicious and filling. In Venice they already charge sitting fee so people normally don’t tip here, which we found out towards the end of our trip :-)

name: Alan Hewitt
review: Lovely atmosphere by yhe Rialto bridge with the water right next to you. The service was very friendly, but the food was average. Not enough flavor or brightness in the pasta dishes. Better value for similar prices elsewhere.

name: Katie Hall
review: So good we came here twice on the same day! Great coffee and cakes, good service, and a really nice casual and cool atmosphere inside. Or you can sit outside by the canal. If you stand at the bar you can get much cheaper coffee, but if you're walking around Venice you need to sit - and this place is great.

name: Rak P
review: A very lively and popular bacaro. Prime position. Location is picture perfect, opposite church, across ponte/bridge overlooking canal. Popular with locals and workers but also passing tourists coming into the area. Stopped over in the morning for coffee but the real unexpected star were the croissants,  had these wirh chocolate,  orange and pistachio fillings, they are delicious.  Best croissants we had whilst staying in Venice. Prompt and efficient service. Chichetti looked good, nice selection and popular as well. Good selection of local wines. Nice vibe and ambience, both mornings and evenings, people in and outside the bar. Very reasonable prices. If you are visiting, come over and have a slice of typical Venice.

name: Sebastian N
review: We stopped by this bar while strolling through Venice and noticed a great selection of cicchetti displayed in the window. We decided to stay and try a few, along with a couple of drinks. We sat by the window with a lovely view of the city — such a pleasant spot! Delicious cicchetti, friendly service, and a wonderful atmosphere. Highly recommended!

name: A L
review: This was our favourite bacaro in Venice. We returned here a few times as it was so central and lively in the evening. The bar is right on the steps of a bridge, next to large open area and church which makes it the perfect spot for a select spritz and cicchetti. Well recomended.

name: Patrick Kluver
review: Fantastic experience. Great value and quality for money! Best priced cicchetti in town! Meat 2€, fish 2.50€. We’ll be back!

name: Ricky Lamar
review:
This little bar and cicchetteria has it going on! Georgia with her mischievous twinkle will mix you up a traditional Italian spritzer offering up tasty tapas to tempt those taste buds.
And Lorenzo, in charge of the fun made this a night to remember.

name: Nima Lama
review: We made a quick stop for some drinks and ended up trying their cicchetti. It was delicious! The staff were super friendly, and we were lucky to get a seat right by the airco. Grazie mille!

name: Rhakria and or Embisch
review:
Impeccable bacaro experience.
I'll list some positives :
- Wine selection, as well as enough other drinks for those who don't want wine
- freshly prepared Cicchetti with enough variety to suit all tastes
- very stylish music playlist of classic 80-90s disco
- lovely / lively people behind the counter

The negatives :
- can't think of one...

Thank you for the well spent evening 🤗

name: David Wilson
review: We stopped in for a coffee and water.  Everyone was so friendly and the cappuccinos were VERY tasty. Grazie Mille!

name: Bahattin Çiniç
review:
We had a salty croissant, a focaccia sandwich, and two sweet croissants.
Everything was great! The focaccia sandwich was the best one, but the others were also really good.

name: Jože Habinc
review: All three pizzas we tried tasted great, the service was solid and the price, considering the location, was also good. However, there were a lot of leftovers on the tables (but everyone could clean up after themselves, as they have trash cans installed), the pigeons between the tables were not pleasant to watch, and what bothered me the most was the strong operation of the air conditioners and ceiling fans. They really overdo it with the cooling.

name: Sarah Petry
review: We are staying in Venice and wanted a nice easy dinner. We found Farini, they were friendly and welcoming to some tourists with little Italian. We ordered and range of pizzas and the boys ordered some tarts for dessert.

name: Bhawana Agarwal
review:
🌟 5 stars

One of the best pizza places you can try—an absolute hidden gem! The pizza is a must-have, even if you’re not hungry. It’s that good! A total steal for the quality you get. Perfect for dine-in or takeaway, and the coffee is just as amazing. Friendly and welcoming staff make the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit—Bon appétit! 🍕

name: Moji gh
review: We ordered two caffè lattes and two croissants. The coffee was average but acceptable compared to other places in Italy. The croissants were fresh and tasty. The main downside was the service — the staff member who served us was unfriendly and unwelcoming.

name: Ari Sastre
review: My uncle says that the best pizza starts with the crust, and Farini's crust is superb. Simplistic homegrown ingredients combine together to make a pizza that you think would be found in an expensive celebrity chain. Prices are affordable and reasonable too.

name: Kaitlyn Affuso
review:
The BEST pizza I had while in Italy.
Went to Venice, Florence, and Rome and this was by FAR my favorite. We visited several times while we were in Venice for 3.5 days.

Highly reccommend. Get several different slices and share because they're all delicious!

name: CCG G
review: Good place for croissants and fresh yogurt with fresh fruit and granola. The coffee was honestly one of the grossest coffee ups I’ve ever tasted - it was very burnt I just threw it away. Place is small with limited seating so it’s chaotic and not a good place to sit and eat. Highly recommend if you’re craving yogurt though.

name: Ficho Stainov
review: I sat down in the Café on a weekday in October and I had to work for a few hours on my laptop. The coffee, food and Aperol were great and the staff was friendly and did not object me working there. The prices are also reasonable for Venice. All in all a great café and good for home office.

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
We stopped at this café for a quick coffee, even though we had seen some negative reviews beforehand.
