review: Awful experience! The staff was shockingly rude. While I was paying for a bottle of water, my friend simply sat down, and one of the staff members hit her in the face. We didn’t even get to eat, and to make matters worse, they almost locked us inside when we tried to leave. I would never recommend this place to anyo

name: Michael Pistone
review:
Horrible meal. Ordered pizza and pasta dishes. The "sausage" pizza was hot dog on a pizza. The pasta had no flavor at all.

Extremely disappointing, avoid.

name: Johann Murillo Prieto
review:
No miam miam, no 5 stars

Nah, for real, the orange juice don’t have flavour, I really thought it was half water 🫩.

No patience, the corporal expression says a lot.

And really expensive, I know is in a tourist place, but come on, I paid a lot for a orange juice that doesn’t taste like orange at all 🥀

name: Asiful Haque
review: Many of verity...2 scoops €4.5 it was big.....

name: Jessica Coe
review: Really disappointed with the quality of pizza and pasta we got for takeaway.

name: Anisa
review: A very nice place! I enjoyed my Venetian spritz, the staff was super kind, and the Wi-Fi was great. The location was also perfect. Definitely a spot worth visiting!

name: Asif N
review: club sandwich was very nice and big....

name: Mithila Soyal
review: Was very nice cappuccino....

name: Beny Škoda
review: we had breakfast there. simple but tasty.

name: Tiffany Atherton
review: Delicious sweets and cappuccino! We stopped here for breakfast a couple days of our trip and had a great experience. We were able to get seats both mornings and the staff were very friendly! We got a croissant to go one day (not pictured) and that was also very good.

name: Allan Santiago
review: All the treats were delicious and I want to come back for more. My main objection is that my cappuccino and macchiato were cold as if left at room temperature. Not sure how that happened as they served it right away. Not sure if that's normal, but it's a first for me. The service is great as are the treats.

name: Maja Jarecka
review: cute little local café. we walked in early in the morning — lots of locals were having their espressos and hanging around. Asia said they have the best pistachio cornetto ever.

name: Fly
review:
Delicious and fresh tiramisu & ricotta.

One of the rare places in Venice where you can enjoy a quiet cup of coffee.

name: George Forgan-Smith
review:
Tasty pastries hidden away in the middle of Venice.
Definitely worth the fun exploration to find this gem.
Be aware it’s right on the edge of a bridge and the door faces the canal. It’s easy to walk past so I hope this helps save you missing it. (See pics with this review).
Wonderful selection of sweet treats.
The pear tarts are amazing.
There is the option to eat in with a couple of tables with perfect view of the canal.
It’s a little tight so you may not be able to get a seat at busy times.
We visited around 3pm and there were seats available.
Definitely recommended.

name: Hanne Arts
review: We arrived at Pasticceria Ponte delle paste at 10:00 and it was still fairly quiet. Lovely atmosphere. There was no vegan banana bread, but the plain vegan croissant and incredibly buttery and flaky. There is also a vegan jam cookie and jam-filled croissant option, plus soy milk for coffees. I had a decaf cappuccino and it was very strong: exactly as I like it! There was sufficient space to sit at this time too.

name: Minnie Chu
review:
Visited for breakfast since the place opened at 7am. Very nice small shop with an accommodating owner. We had coffee (goood) and macarons.
Took a seat beside the window and enjoyed watching the canal and people crossing the bridge in the morning.

name: Megan Hounam
review:
Lovely, authentic treats. It was a location recommended by a local as having great pastries and it definitely met my expectations!

Had a bit of trouble finding it from google maps at first as it's very easy to walk by and miss, but the inside was nice and quiet with a few small tables to sit down and enjoy.

name: Jason
review: They charge tourists more money than what is posted on their menu. Food and drinks were very bland also and the employees were unsanitary, I saw them grab food with bare hands after touching money.

name: Federico Ascolani
review:
Small bar near the station. Service was good and fast.

Food was aceptable. Good variety of food and drinks for a small bar.
Prices were high.

I don't recomend buying Orange juice, is just is a small glass of juice for 4,5 euros.

The place was normal. Noisy. Very concurred.

name: Darren Cox
review: Didn't have food. 11:15 am. 3 coffees and a can of pop, €10.30. Staff were friendly. Handy rest stop waiting for coach to the airport.

name: Elias C
review: The menu prices are half of what they charge you if you are a tourist. They make prices up when you check out.

name: Byron Khan
review: Great service fresh Juice was serve with the smile ! Fair price the location is right near to the parking

name: S_Zeel Pdf
review: Cocktails are bad, the service is worse and they charge you more just because you take a seat, they argue that the prices are different in the menu, however there are no extra fees advertised anywhere. THEY JUST CHARGE YOU MORE FOR BEING A TOURIST.

name: Casper Singh
review:
To the naked eye this looks like any other corner store. When we walked in we were greeted with weird aggressive looks from two of the customers eating and from the worker.

We wanted to buy 4 waters and the guy working there looked at us like we had just insulted his whole family tree. When we paid and said ‘thank you’ he turned around, rolling his eyes. Guy eating near the exit stared at us like we were breaking the law.

After seeing the reviews, I’m glad we only got water. Don’t go. Buy your things somewhere else.

name: Chan Le
review: We had a horrible experience here, first they lied about the prices on the menu. When I confronted the lady she just pretended not to understand. I was going to let it go but I got very sick after from the food I ate. The place was very dirty with no soap in the bathroom, do not go!

name: Arevik Ford
review: Far from your typical tourist menu, this corner spot overlooking a piazza serves up delicious food and great service. Good variety on the menu and even entrees are well portioned. Would come back here again!

name: Williamson Transport, LLC
review: we're sitting inside and saw some bread drop on the floor,  next to the blue glasses.  They picked them up and put them back into a bread basket.  YUK!  Guess it's better not to know what happens to your food sometimes.

name: Quentin Brandwood
review:
Fantastic food in the centre of Burano. We had the fried sardines (with a side of "French fries" which were fried thin cut potato, I was a bit skeptical when they were suggested but yes order these) and lasagne both were exceptionally good.
The menu is in multiple languages and the waiter that we had spoke good English. There is a small cover charge (1,8 euro each) which is on the menu. This was in a nice out door setting in the middle of Burano. Easy to find and well worth the visit.
Highly recommend eating here.

name: Karel Petrášek
review: Nice looking place at the square, so we decided to celebrate our engagement there. At first, we read through the menu and decided to get drinks only, maybe dessert. Although there was nobody inside, and at the time maybe 5 tables occupied, nobody noticed. So we picked our seats and sat down. In about a minute or so waiter came and asked if we’d like to eat. As soon as we told him we are there probably for drinks only, he immediately asked us to move to a different area of the restaurant, as “our” seats were for lunch customers only. (No sign or obvious hint to assume such thing). As politely as he asked us to move, we stood up and left and took our business to much nicer establishment. Not surprised the place was almost empty.

name: Carmen Macota
review:
The food is good here, we had fried fish plateau, spaghetti with squid ink, spaghetti bolognaise and spaghetti with sea food and their famous biscuits.
The food came pretty fast and the waiter was nice.

name: Steven Cheng
review:
Great food, great drinks and Great service!

Very inviting of the outside seating area and wonderful seafood selections !

We stopped by to having lunch, very friendly staffs speaking enough English for you to order your meals!

We hope we can visit this little island and this restaurant again!

name: Fiamma Seda
review:
Let me start with the warmth and happiness we received as soon as we approached the restaurant, Fabio (the owner), his family and employees super respectful and 100% attention to details. Their food is extremely fresh, from their cocktails, to their fish tapas all the way to they heart melting FIG CAKE (i wanted to steal the recipe!!!) no joke the BEST experience by far.

They let us sit were we wanted, we love a specific cocktail and they made it for us, introduced us to an unfiltered local beer, brought us some tapas for my mom and myself to share & finally a fig cake (you’ll want to take home)

Please have in mind these islands depend 100% on tourism and the pandemic hit them hard so please go support and tip these lovely people.

Thank you Fabio, Emely and the team.

name: Adelina D
review: The restaurant is exactly in the main square. The service is impeccable, the waiter was very polite, and the order came quickly. The food was very good, big portions. The sepia ink pasta are delicious, I recommend.

name: Michael Pistone
review:
Horrible meal. Ordered pizza and pasta dishes. The "sausage" pizza was hot dog on a pizza. The pasta had no flavor at all.

Extremely disappointing, avoid.

name: Johann Murillo Prieto
review:
No miam miam, no 5 stars

Nah, for real, the orange juice don’t have flavour, I really thought it was half water 🫩.

No patience, the corporal expression says a lot.

And really expensive, I know is in a tourist place, but come on, I paid a lot for a orange juice that doesn’t taste like orange at all 🥀

name: Asiful Haque
review: Many of verity...2 scoops €4.5 it was big.....

name: Jessica Coe
review: Really disappointed with the quality of pizza and pasta we got for takeaway.

name: Anisa
review: A very nice place! I enjoyed my Venetian spritz, the staff was super kind, and the Wi-Fi was great. The location was also perfect. Definitely a spot worth visiting!

name: Asif N
review: club sandwich was very nice and big....

name: Mithila Soyal
review: Was very nice cappuccino....

name: Beny Škoda
review: we had breakfast there. simple but tasty.

name: Chris H
review: Avoid this. The only real bad spot we went to in Venice. Panini barely had any meat, had to remove the salami from one side to the other just to have flavour. Washrooms had their garbage can IN the toilet and had to move it myself. Dirty and wet paper towel shoved under the faucet and needed to remove that too so I could wash my hands. Cost 25 euros for two glasses of house white wine with the panini. Literally any other choice is better. Waste of money.

name: Shahin Ahmed
review: Very cozy place in the heart of Venice! Friendly and smiling staff, great sandwiches and delicious homemade gnocchi. The organic drinks are a nice touch. Highly recommended!

name: Tim Chingos
review: Got a couple of sandwiches for takeaway. Added a cookie ring last minute. They charged €5 euros for it (see photo). Definitely taking advantage of tourists. Skip this one (I should have checked the reviews).

name: Juhel Ahmed
review: Super positive experience! Cozy atmosphere, perfectly crispy cutlet, and fluffy omelet. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Venice.

name: Jireh Joan
review:
This place is honestly terrible. We came here thinking it would be decent because it looked busy and we assumed that meant good food or service. We were very wrong.

We sat down and waited about 10 minutes—nobody came. So we went up to the counter to either place an order or ask for help. The staff rudely told us to sit down and someone will come to take your order. Fine. We did.

But guess what? 30 more minutes passed, and still no one came. We kept trying to get the attention of the waiters—they saw us and deliberately ignored us, pretending to be busy.

The customer service is absolutely awful, borderline disrespectful. The food is ok. Definitely not worth the time, frustration, or money.

name: Feng Wu
review: All the Foods are overpriced. Waiters with a poker face and act super rude. Toilet doesn’t have a seat and disgusting. AC barely works. They added $.50 on $4 for a cappuccino which you can get for under $2 elsewhere like we found out later, with much better quality. I wouldn’t recommend anyone to enter.

name: Giacomo
review:
I don't understand all the hate towards this bar. We ended up here for breakfast and had a very pleasant experience. The staff and service were absolutely polite, kind and efficient. The croissants were very tasty and the coffee/tea selection was great. Prices are a little higher than a typical Italian bar but you're in the heart of Venice and location matters a lot. I'd definitely recommend this place again.
Disclaimer: I haven't used their restrooms but lots of people seem to complain about the cleanliness of them.

name: Paul-Julien Lafont
review:
To start, don’t really understand the low star rating of this place and the negative comment.
OK it’s not the best coffee place in town but this place is really cool and great,
To tell a bit more about my personal experience, I was with my mum and we really wanted to go to pee and have a break so we stopped in this place.
I ordered a hot chocolate with chantilly cream and a classic tiramisu. They were both correct and very good quality.
I previously had some teeth pain during my stay in Venezia and I didn’t feel pain at all while eating the tiramisu and drinking the chocolate, they were not full of sugar or ultra sweet (compared to few comments I have read about the pastries here).
The waiters were really nice to us, we didn’t stay long inside as we had to visit other parts of Venezia but they were super polite and respectuous.
It was full of Italian inside so I presume it’s quite popular for locals/regulars.
Last thing, it was not super cheap as I paid around 15 euros for both but it’s kind of worth it regarding the city (touristic Venezia) and the quality of the products.
I recommend this place if you are close and want a small break during your day !!

name: Sebastian N
review: I don’t quite understand why there are so many negative reviews about this place. We ended up there completely by chance for breakfast on our last day in Venice. The café was fairly busy, and the selection of treats was quite good — both sweet and savory options. We went for sweet and savory croissants, a small cannoli, and cappuccinos. The coffee was very tasty. The food might not have been the best you can find in Venice, but it certainly wasn’t bad either.

name: Bella Reviews
review: The worst cannoli I’ve had. It was soft - probably had been sitting outside the whole entire day. Also tried the pistachio brownie after the worker recommended it and it was so bad. It had the fake pistachio taste which I personally hate.

name: Adrianna M
review: Absolute nightmare. That was one and only time we went to a café without checking the reviews first... And that was a mistake. Cappuccino 6.50€??? With soy milk that turned out to be regular milk, even tho I asked for soy but after it was served they said they ran out so I got one with normal milk without any info. Pastries tasted like they were laying there for 3 days, not creamy, not crunchy at all (small one 3€, big 5. 50...). Please avoid this place at any cost!

name: Aimee
review: Stopped here for cannoli and a drink to relax. They had a nice selection of cannoli lots of different flavours. I got a strawberry cannoli and it was delicious. However, one couple did make a run for it after they “didn’t like” something so I do feel bad for the staff who lost money to them. The staff were very friendly and overall it was a nice spot.

name: tip tiptip
review:
Chocolate cannoli for us is so good, rich ,creamy and crispy, not too sweet.

In term of service as a relative term  compare to other landmark sweet shops or coffee shops in Paris (Some) or Switzerland ( Many).
We could say that the service of this place is normal & not bad at all. I mean , far better than the others I had mentioned :)

name: Chris
review: When my wife asked the server about a specific drink, she was met with rudeness. Despite this, we decided to stay and had a coffee and cannoli, as we were tired. However, things took a turn for the worse when we witnessed the same server ask two other customers to leave because they were only drinking bottled water they had purchased from the cafe. To make matters worse, I was overcharged when paying, and when I brought it to the attention of the pot-bellied staff member, he became aggressive and told me to leave. Fortunately, there were many people around, so we chose to exit instead of escalating the situation and the staff member having to take a few weeks off work. I'd strongly advise against visiting this cafe - it's possibly the worst in Venice.

name: Simonna & Mitchell
review: We walked by this place and the tiramisu macarons looked good so we bought two to go without looking at the reviews. Big mistake because these were SO dry and stale and not good at all. Just a complete waste of money because we took one bite and threw them out. I looked at the reviews later to see if we just got a bad batch but looks like a lot of people have the same experience.

name: melissa osorio hurtado
review: Awful experience! The staff was shockingly rude. While I was paying for a bottle of water, my friend simply sat down, and one of the staff members hit her in the face. We didn’t even get to eat, and to make matters worse, they almost locked us inside when we tried to leave. I would never recommend this place to anyo

name: Sheyla Arango
review: The best: available toilets and plugs to charge phones. A place to stay for a while if you need to charge your phone or laptop. Coffee is good and attention is fast.

name: Winda Natasya
review: Get the croissant! It is so good! The sandwich is not really. 1 croissant and a cup of cappuccino cost around 5 euro. There is toilet inside.

name: Marta D
review: Good food, good drinks, toilet is available for customers.

name: Dheeraj Negi
review: Inside Venezia S L station. Thank god it was open for our early morning train on a rainy winter morning. We were able to grab hot latte(milk) and croissants before our train journey. There’s toilet available as well.

name: Galina Chzhen
review: Wow! We had a train at 6:10 am and this place opens up at 6 am. They were able to make us coffee latte and the croissants were so fresh and flaky (must’ve been baked at night). They have soy milk for vegan coffee, the croissants are not vegan — I didn’t clarify if they have plant-based desserts since I was in such a rush!

name: Saleena
review: It was fine for a bite while waiting for the train, with some healthy food options compared to most other food vendors in the station. Salmon salad grain bowl was decent portion wise, while the pistachio croissant was super dry/stale and barely had any filling. Seating is nice and a good break away from the hustle of streaming passengers.

name: Roy Anthony
review: Food is just okay. The service is so so as they forget to provide me with Nuttela Cookies despite we ordered Nuttela Coffee. The atmosphere is cool.

name: Gunel Abbaszadeh
review: I asked to heat a bit even it was not take away they refused and it is cold to eat in the morning ..

name: James Thompson
review:
A quick stop where we got some focaccia pizza. It was nice see that the pizza was heated which made it a much more enjoyable experience.

There was still some good selection even at 8:30pm at night.

Owner was friendly and prompt with dealing with us.

Would recommend.

name: Flikas
review: Great service, great food and this place is the best if you get hungry at late times, you can always get food and drinks until 04:00.

name: Qinyuan Zhou
review: Bad coffee... Taste very watered down.

name: Katherin Morales
review: I loved my Nutella and banana crepe. it was so yummy.. the staff were friendly..

name: Şebnem Sena GÜLYEŞİL
review: Amazing person runs this shop kind and trusty.He let me stay in shop till I catch my night train. Make sure to try the pizza it is cheap and really tasty.

name: Ryan Bright
review: Went out looking for crepes late at night with my nieces and everywhere was closed. We noticed they had a crepe machine and asked if they could make some. They said they were closed but if we wanted to wait a few minutes they would turn the machine back on and help us out. After they made them, we were going to take them to go but they insisted we eat them inside so they didn't get soggy. Great experience! Great staff! My nieces loved the crepes!

name: Maciej Baranowski
review: A great place open up until 4 a.m. A great owner and very hellpful. Decent prices and testy food! It is a must-see during every night in Venice.

name: Ryan Gustafson
review: Absolutely fantastic. His mega spritz was the highlight of our trip.

name: nafiseh Amini
review:
Poor service, rude staff, i went there to sit and have a coffee because on the map they are shown as a cafe and they refused to give me a table because it was after 6pm, they gave me a take away bad tasting coffee that the led isnt even the size of the mug and on my way hot coffee split on my hand and you can see that my hand is red cause of the burns. Im really mad at their service and the prices, and the staff who were really rude to me.
Dont ruin your precious time by choosing them.

name: Echo Lai
review:
Extremely poor service.
My friend was having a drink there, and not only did the staff roll her eyes at my friend, but she also took away my friend’s unfinished drink without asking.
She even put the change on an empty table silently and left, instead of placing it on my friend’s table or handing it to her directly.
Miss, are you a racist or what? That you cannot even make direct contact with an Asian customer? Shockingly rude and completely unacceptable.

name: Christian Weiser
review:
I went in cause it's
air conditioned, the waitress was friendly , WiFi available , 4,50€ for a cappuccino is not that cheap since the place is not in the main tourist area

name: Jenny Qin
review: Meraviglioso host, e i cicchetti buonissimi. Great spritz, and awesome music. All add to an intimate and relaxing vibe. Grazie Alessio per consigliarmi di posti interessanti da visitare. Nothing beats recommendations from a local.

name: Gio Eburne
review: Stopped here (now called All’Angelo) for a very reasonable Happy Hour offer, €8 for a Spritz and 2 chichetti. Very friendly service, lovely little place to sit outside, even in the rain! Tasty chichetti and well made Spritz - will definitely visit again on another trip to Venice!

name: Jono
review: This place is amazing. We were hard to find a good cocktail bar with good atmosphere... this completey filled the void. Yum!!!!

name: Надежда Топалпенчева
review: It’s actually a new restaurant called Bar all’ Angolo- from 2 months. The food, service and atmosphere are excellent! Food was very, very delicious. The server was really nice to us and checked everything all the dinner! The atmosphere was so calm, romantic and far away from the crowd!

name: Anthony
review: Really bad pizza, at very high prices. Pizza seemed to be from frozen.

name: Desi
review:
We had a small breakfast in here. Croissants were delicious.  We had cappuccino, which was perfect. Atmosphere Inside is not very welcoming, but outside on the terrace is brilliant during the hot July days.
The lady was very polite and helpful.  Thank you!

name: LF
review: The most Beautiful lady serving :)

name: Ratko Joksimović
review: Good place for caffe ☺️

name: Dori Barrells
review: Great place for a cocktail

name: Rohan Hossain
review: Ok

name: Łukasz Jarosinski
review: Doskonałe miejsce aby posiedzieć i wychylić szklaneczkę albo dwie a może i więcej ze znajomymi. Bardzo duży wybór piw i innych trunków, przystępne ceny

name: Giacomo Bonazzola
review: Abbiamo fatto due volte colazione qui gustando dei cappuccini fantastici: belli e buoni. Personale gentile e locale pulito.

name: Alejandra Caicedo
review:
Nuestra visita al bar fue más bien corta. Hicimos un pequeño recorrido con el monorail y paseando por la zona, entramos alli para tomar algo.
El encanto del bar radica en salirse de la Venecia turística, ver a los lugareños y compartir con ellos, un ratito.

name: Aybüke Novosád
review:
We are having a coffee and a beer. Enjoying it so far.
The staff was helpful.
Silent, no people around. Beautiful nice ambiance.

name: Taiwan Cherry
review:
Must visit
Nice coffee
Good price

name: Egor Korshunov
review: As of August 21, 2023, nobody blocked the access to Building Bridges. The place has a restroom/toilet/WC, the only one in the area. The coffee is good and affordable.

name: Zvezdelin Dimitrov
review: Very good coffee and croissant for very good price. Lovely place 😊

name: Daniel Gardiner
review: The Building bridges is a beautiful location to visit. DO NOT GO TO Tesa 105, the service there is the worst I have ever experienced. We brought drinks and proceeded outside where there were other customers already sitting. We proceeded to get and own chairs but told that we couldn't sit down outside??? Not sure why not as there were already customers there and plenty of space and chair's.

name: Szymon Bartnik
review:
Control the people blocking access to the Building Bridges.
…
I haven’t been in the bar but on bar’s area there are people who are rude and don’t communicate properly (they don’t speak English of course) why the access to the Building Bridges is closed and (what’s the most important) when it will be open back again.
…
You should feel responsible for anything which happens in your property. Really bad experience.

name: Enrico Ostorero
review: Very good Originals Signs 2023

name: Ramon M Castro
review: Cafetería menús don't stipulate which prices apply for evening or day snacks. Poor selection and expensive. 'hot chocolate' small cup was not hot and expensive. Insufficient bathrooms, no paper and broken hand driers. Staff quite friendly.

name: Dylan Vogt
review: Some of the best spritz! Delicious cicchetti. And great prices + vibes. Nice spots to sit along the canal (though watch out for the seagulls). The staff is chill and the location is in a quieter part of Venice. Definitely recommend for a great afternoon hang out! My wife and I were excited to discover this spot.

name: Alberto M.
review:
Only had some drinks there, mostly different types of Spritz (select & aperol), although the different cicchetti they had look amazing and definitely appealing.

More a place for locals than tourists, due to the university grounds nearby, I totally recommend to escape a bit from the main crowded areas while enjoying a drink next to a beautiful channel.

name: Vadim Lipatov
review: Great Spritz, nice Ciccheti, low prices. A little amount of tourists - which is awesome 🫡

name: valeryanka
review: Good prices! Aperoll and Nigrini cost araund 3,50 and are good. Music was too loud for me but you can sit outside by the canall. i recomend coming here. Non alcoholic drinks were also amazing.

name: Greg G
review: Nice one for a quick cicchetti, but nothing special tbh

name: Kateryna Dmytrenko
review: Nice snacks and good aperol spritz and Campari spritz. Also you can take a drink to go. Aperol was just 3.5

name: Florian Ehrlacher
review: Real and authentic place in Venezia. Its nice to still have venue like this, especially in this city.

name: Dawid Gocek
review:
Great place to feel local life, lots of people getting a drink or small traditional Italian snack and fall into endless conversations
Worth it!

name: Eileen Loh
review: We had an incredibly good experience here. The service was top notch. Too bad we didn’t get her name but she was very patient yet efficient answering our questions and applying for our tax refund. Excellent!

name: Per Haga
review: I come to Venezia 4-5 times every year and always come to the Bialetti store to buy coffee, accessories, etc., to bring back. We have both Moka Express and machine for capsules! Our Moka Express is the old style, but now they have many new modern designs. Always good and friendly service and the best coffee!

name: Lina H
review: the shop assistant is nice, she kindly teach us how to brew coffee.

name: Velin Nikolov
review: Bought a set of espresso cups. Lovely products

name: Ciara Walton
review: The coffee and pastries were affordable and delicious! The owner is the kindest man. He was thoughtful and made an already incredibly special trip that much more memorable simply because we got to meet him.

name: Renatus W. Fischer (Renatus Cartesius)
review: Delicious food and nice service. You should definitely get the menu, best value for money or a pizza. I cannot understand the bad reviews, it's not mega delicious but it's also not bad. Prices are okay and a bit higher.

name: P.D.
review: Excellent. Again we have another brilliant example of why it is worth walking just a few minutes away from the super-busy tourist areas. Very friendly staff. Fresh tasty authentic dishes. We had the daily special menu and a bottle of nice wine. Affordable relative to other places but no less quality. No coperto no servizio either. Recommended for a nice easy meal in a relaxing place.

name: Susana Alves
review:
Tasty food, but we could notice it was not fresh.
Environment was nice but staff was too friendly, not in a comfortable way.
They have charged us a fee to stay inside - 2€ per person

name: Richard Rosenbaum
review: Stopped by for a late lunch one day. Tried the gnocchi and lasagna. Both were quite good but not outstanding. The fresh bread served with the meal was very good. Service was quick and friendly. Prices were ok for Venice.

name: Md Shariful Alam
review: The food was too expensive compared to its tastes!! They charged 20 euros for one normal pizza!! 5 euros for water bottles! 10 euros for sprite cans!! The cook was too racist! Was smiling at us and talking with one of the waiters about how they tricked us with the food price!!

name: Becky Raymond
review: Yummy pasta!  My husband had the carbonara and loved it. I had the pasta with mushrooms and shrimp and it was very good. We had the house white wine Pinot Grigio. The place was busy. Very delicious and great service!

name: Navin Jawanda
review: Amazing food and service. The waiter was a sweetheart and we loved everything about the restaurant. We went for a late dinner around 9:30pm and the service was really quick. :)

name: Shivshankar Podder
review:
This morning we had breakfast at Bar Olimpia Caffè, right across from Ponte Degli Scalzi on the Grand Canal. The place might be connected to Caffè Molinari since the cups and tissue holders carry that name, though we’re not fully sure. It’s a relaxed cafe and bar with indoor seating and an outdoor area that faces the church of San Simone Piccolo. The view is really nice. The menu has plenty of options, from different coffees and croissants to brioches, burgers and juices.

Here’s what we ordered with our ratings
1.	Morocchino 5/5
2.	Cappuccino 4/5
3.	Brioche with tomato and mozzarella 4/5
4.	Brioche with ham, mozzarella and tomato 4/5

The food was good and the staff were friendly. Overall it was a very good experience.

name: Ramesh D Foodie
review: Right next to the train station and opposite the bridge. They have outdoor seating available. We were greeted and handed menus. We ordered lunch ,  spaghetti, and pesto pasta. Both came out in less than 15 minutes, Al dente. The food was good and the service is good too. It is more of a quick grab and go place and also has table service.

name: Donna knights
review: Our experience was a pleasant one. The staff were friendly and helpful and portions good size and food was nice. Being on the canal was  beautiful and yes pay a little more but the prices were listed.

name: Barry Ashley
review: Ordered a capacino at the stand up bar inside. Drank it and then was handed a bill of €6 ($9.70 Canadian ). I felt that was WAY too much for a small drink but what could I do except pay it. I would not recommend this caffe as there are many in the area and their cost is MUCH cheaper.

name: Ammar Amjad
review: Easily one of the worst coffee shop experiences I’ve ever had. The staff were rude from the start. It was humiliating and completely unprofessional. The coffee was just as bad — bitter, watery, and overpriced. If you value good coffee and basic respect, skip this place and go anywhere else.

name: Greek Aliti
review:
Excellent food with quick service 😋
Bonus was they had toilets inside saving us 3€ total (1.50€ each) in the paid toilets

name: Arvind Yerram
review: A nice little restaurant in the main island. This is close to the main water bus stop and at 2 min walk from the railway station. We found this to be the perfect place in Venice for a good and healthy breakfast. Chocolate and nuts filled croissant is worth trying. Ravoli with arabbiatta sauce is my favorite here

name: Catherine Cornell
review: We spent a lovely time here with the server named Cecilia who guided us to the best things to order . We were not sorry to have stopped by and recommend Cecilia and this bar highly

name: Shugufa Kakar
review:
Really good espresso and cosy familiar vibe!

The people were really friendly, which is a breath of fresh air opposed to all the vendors who are tired of tourists and tending to them with annoyance. Food was tasty too!!

name: Lucy
review: Definitely a spot for locals, which is fair enough. We only stopped for drinks. €9 for 2 lemon sodas. The locals sat at the tables outside mainly seemed to be on early lunch/spritz breaks. Good people watching though.

name: Michael Mischke
review: Loved it!!! Old school italian. Food and wine is delicious and excellent prices. Sandwiches are the main fare. Locals inside chatting it up, italian music playing in the background. Every other restaurant playing north American music....I am in italy, I want italian music lol. Come by, have a bite and enjoy the ambience!

name: David Coulthard
review:
Authentic Italian bar.
On both occasions we were the only tourists and very popular with locals and workers who stopped in for coffee and a snack.
Excellent stop for coffee and food.
Freshly made rolls and sandwiches, or bakery items for those that need a sugar hit.
Only 4 seats on wall, but the locals stand at counter.

name: emily benfield
review: I come here literally every day Monday- Friday. They have some of the best cornetti (croissants) I have had in Venice and maybe in Italy (I live here). My macchiato is always amazing- good coffee in general.  I’ve had a spritz before and it was not my favorite. To the negative reviews about prices changing, I can see this happening. We have had this happen once to us here but my husband said something and it was a mistake/ confusion on what we ordered and quickly corrected. My husband who is Italian will speak up however if there is a problem and it could be true they treat foreigners differently.  It is a local bar that many Venetians who live here go to. I have to say that staff/ women who serve us every morning however have been so kind and remember us. I love my perfect macchiato every day! *EDIT: For those who do not know, the price is different if you drink your coffee standing up at the bar as opposed to sitting outside. Sitting outside at the tables will always cost more than going inside.

name: Ana
review: Excellent service, beautiful place and extremely kind people surrounded by enchanting scenery. Good price. Extremely recommendable.

name: Carl H
review: A cheapish local bar and sandwich stop. Pleasant and prompt service, delicious sarnies, and a cold beer. Thumbs up from me :)

name: Michał Nawrot
review:
Worst service ever. We have been waiting 30 min for the waiter, who has been serving 8 out of 10 tables, completely ignoring us and other clients at the table next to us (also tourists).

New clients were coming and ordering, while the waiter wasn't able to take our order (despite a clear signs from our side, that we are ready and would like to order). Finally he came to us, but in the meantime started to serve other table and forgot about us!

Btw. the clients next to us wanted to eat something with the cheese, but heard from the waiter "we don't have food without cheese, even pasta goes always together with parmiggiano".

name: Mark Day
review: Good atmosphere, good beer, good food 😀

name: Extraseiten
review: Wurden auf dem Weg zur Bar von der Security weggeschickt. Gehen ohne Tagesticket verboten. Schade.

name: Antonella Bodin
review: Provate si sta super bene🥰

name: Marco Rizzo
review: Ottimo servizio! Bar e ristorante da favola! Da provare 🙂

name: Alberto Masut
review: Metto 1 stella perché non posso metterne 0

name: Maria-Anna Κyrgiafini
review: One of my favourite places in Venice! The view is amazing and the stuff is kind and helpfull. We had the famous ice cream (amaretto was the best) and aperol. Totally recommend!!

name: J W
review:
The first gelato we took in our Italy vacation, arguably one of the best!
Nicciola was splendid. A bit windy and chilly at night.

name: Kathleen Budny
review: This was one of our favorite gelato stops (and we had lots) because of the lovely outdoor seating, best prices in Venice, and the best Tiramisu flavor we tried on our trip. Bathroom on premises too, great for kids who needed a stop.

name: Anusha Halsnad
review: The flavours were interesting, but the taste and the texture wasn't to our liking. The texture was too frozen and the flavor didn't feel natural. A bit disappointed after having good gelatos in Italy.

name: Emer Foley
review: I love this place, the view is unbeatable as it overlooks the Giudecca canal. They serve the ice cream in flamboyant hurricane glasses. It opened in the 1920s so they’ve served everyone from Peggy Guggenheim to Angelina Jolie. Great spot to escape getting sun burned under the parasols too 🫶

name: Al Cal
review: This is one Not to Miss Experiences when in Venice! Get here and sit down or take away the amazing Gianduiotto da Passeggio da Nico! A rather sizable slab of supreme hazelnut chocolate ice-cream topped with whipped cream! Trust me,you won't be disappointed 😘

name: Christoffer Blomvik
review: You know what. Best ice cream I had in Venice. Flavour, among the best. Price, very fair. Size of scoops, among the biggest. The buy behind the counter, a pro. I wanted ice cream, he had ice cream, I said what I wanted, he asked "cone?" I said "yeah" he said "to side" and oriented himself sideways to the other counter. I walked there. He asked "card?" I said "yeah". He pointed to the terminal, I put my card on the terminal. We both said Gracie, I got my ice cream. Easy going, minimal amount of obstacles from wanting for ice cream to getting ice cream. I will definitely return here the next time I am in Venice.

name: Chip Schlemmer
review: cute spot on the water with polite staff.  Most people had gelato but we just got coffees. They also brought a check with our coffee so we weren’t trapped waiting on a server.

name: Jack Cooper
review: Charged twice for two drinks, said that the payment hadn't taken on my card and had to pay cash. Showed the lady that the transaction had gone through on my phone and she wouldn't have it. Watch out.

name: yiqin zou
review: The salesman wasn't very friendly.

name: Kateryna
review: Хотели сесть у них за столиком, выпить что-то. Я хотела пойти вперед занять место, пока мой муж заказывал напитки. Женщина которая стояла на кассе сказала мне подождать и не садиться. И потом добавила, что посадит нас за столик (он один и с красивым видом) только если мы закажем по её мнению достаточно напитков, а не просто кофе или вино.

name: TV Ivan The miner channel
review: Servizio e qualità eccellente...

name: Mr. Davis
review: The desserts looked so good through the glass casing. We were excited honestly. I got the pistachio brownie and she got a blueberry with strawberry macaron. Our server was unfriendly and kind of a jerk too be honest. I mean I guess I might be as well. Heck, If I had to peddle treats to tourists that tasted like something you would find on the back of a shoe, I might be a little salty. There are so solid food options in Venice but this place was out of that loop. My brownie tasted like a pile of dough mixed with the pistachio flavored pudding. You know the kind Cosby put out back in the day. The macron was as hard as a rock and loaded with food coloring to hide what was really in there. Just one bite of that and I’ve been pooping green since. Even the pigeons gave up after a few pecks and flew off. Keep walking past this place.

name: Vanshaj Bindlish
review:
📍 Location: Café at Piazza San Marco, Venice
💶 Spent: €18 for 1 cappuccino, 1 tiramisu, 1 lemon pie

I was drawn in by the charming display of cannoli outside—classic tourist trap move. 😅 The café is located in one of the busiest (and most iconic) parts of Venice, Piazza San Marco, and I was genuinely excited to tick off “authentic Italian coffee” from my list.

Unfortunately…
☕️ The cappuccino was disappointing—bland, overpriced, and lacked the boldness you'd expect from an Italian coffee.
🍰 The tiramisu was a letdown too—more cream than flavor.
🍋 The lemon pie was the only thing somewhat decent, but still not worth the premium.

We ended up paying €18 for three below-average items.
Honestly, any random bar in a quiet alley would’ve been a better (and cheaper) choice. Lesson learned: don’t judge a café by its cannoli display. 🙈

📝 Still on the hunt for that true Italian coffee experience—Venice, you owe me a good espresso!

name: Ray Miranda
review: The colorful display of sweet treats lured me in. I got a large blueberry/strawberry macaron (€6) and a latte (€7.50). The macaron was hard, barely edible. And the coffee was alright, nothing special. Wouldn’t recommend.

name: Murat K
review: It’s a quick “eat and leave” place for cannoli and espresso, but the desserts were absolutely terrible. I wouldn’t have wanted to experience cannoli like this — it was filled only with whipped cream, and it tasted awful. It stays busy just because it’s in a lively location, but the flavours were really bad. I would never recommend it.

name: Boris Ciglenečki
review: Everything looks nice but the cake tastes bad. The tiramisu was spoiled and rancid. After I pointed this out to the waiter, he still charged me for the tiramisu. Very unprofessional and bad experience. I do not recommend...

name: Daze
review: Came in to warm up. Ordered a few items two of them were hot chocolate drinks that weren't hot at all but ok. Two hot chocolate and three pastries (ok donut, stale macaron and small artificial & overly sweet cream puff) costed $28. Quite expensive. But the vigilant male staff returned my backpack which was left on chair. Super nice! Thank you!

name: E S
review: I had a great experience here. The croissants, cappuccino, and Aperol were all excellent. Considering the location, the prices are very fair and offer great value for money. The waiter was extremely attentive and friendly, which made the visit even more enjoyable. Highly recommended.

name: Rana Siroosian
review:
The place is cute and has a good vibe.
My husband got the macaroon and he did not like it at all. I got the pistachio one that is in the picture and it was good, I liked it. The cappuccino was ok.
All these cost almost 19 euros which I think it’s a little bit pricey compared to other places with even better quality.

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Jua Lee
review: Very cool comic book shop with vegan snacks. The owner is supadupa friendly.

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Sally Good
review: As vegans, it can be difficult to find places to eat, so we went here for food and drink, not for the games.  The first time, we just had drinks, then for something to eat later in the day.  Both times, the owner/staff were very friendly, the food was excellent.  The vegan cheese made on the premises was very tasty.   Although, we weren't there for the games, we could see why it would be popular, as it is a very chilled space, ideal for gaming enthusiasts.  Should we visit Venice again, we would definitely return.  We will be recommending to friends.

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Deside Döner
review: Irs a really good store for Comic/Manga lovers and vegans too they had all the books i wanted and the stuff was nice and Helped me finding jojo part 5 Golden wind :)

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Elisavet Kinali
review: Great little place! My kids enjoyed shopping and looking around and I enjoyed a drink and the friendly atmosphere!

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Thorsten Knarre
review: Super cozy unpretentious little comic shop offering vegan sandwiches and coffee. Perfect for a quick bite in the morning or afternoon. The owner is very friendly and warm-hearted.

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: M R (MC)
review: Super cute place with tasty vegan sandwiches and good coffee. Very nice staff!

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Ted Tru
review: Very cool collection and chill vibe

place_title: Super Zazà - Comic Shop and Vegan Bar
name: Marc (Marc)
review: A hidden gem in Venice. Definitely check out the store. They got cold drinks and a great staff.

name: Jason
review: They charge tourists more money than what is posted on their menu. Food and drinks were very bland also and the employees were unsanitary, I saw them grab food with bare hands after touching money.

name: Federico Ascolani
review:
Small bar near the station. Service was good and fast.

Food was aceptable. Good variety of food and drinks for a small bar.
Prices were high.

I don't recomend buying Orange juice, is just is a small glass of juice for 4,5 euros.

The place was normal. Noisy. Very concurred.

name: Darren Cox
review: Didn't have food. 11:15 am. 3 coffees and a can of pop, €10.30. Staff were friendly. Handy rest stop waiting for coach to the airport.

name: Elias C
review: The menu prices are half of what they charge you if you are a tourist. They make prices up when you check out.

name: Byron Khan
review: Great service fresh Juice was serve with the smile ! Fair price the location is right near to the parking

name: S_Zeel Pdf
review: Cocktails are bad, the service is worse and they charge you more just because you take a seat, they argue that the prices are different in the menu, however there are no extra fees advertised anywhere. THEY JUST CHARGE YOU MORE FOR BEING A TOURIST.

name: Casper Singh
review:
To the naked eye this looks like any other corner store. When we walked in we were greeted with weird aggressive looks from two of the customers eating and from the worker.

We wanted to buy 4 waters and the guy working there looked at us like we had just insulted his whole family tree. When we paid and said ‘thank you’ he turned around, rolling his eyes. Guy eating near the exit stared at us like we were breaking the law.

After seeing the reviews, I’m glad we only got water. Don’t go. Buy your things somewhere else.

name: Chan Le
review: We had a horrible experience here, first they lied about the prices on the menu. When I confronted the lady she just pretended not to understand. I was going to let it go but I got very sick after from the food I ate. The place was very dirty with no soap in the bathroom, do not go!

name: Marlon Blake
review: This place was solid. We randomly found this spot and glad we did. While the food was just ok the wine selection was amazing. I can see why folks like this place especially during warmer times. We traveled during the winter so seating was only inside. The staff was awesome.

name: sunisa wanmuen
review: Cozy café with a mix of modern and vintage style. Friendly staff and affordable drinks. Great vibes!

name: Ben Carey
review:
Nice a quite place for a drink. You get chips with the drinks
Food also looked very nice.( people.sat next to us looked like the enjoyed what they ate.)

name: Dennis H
review: Tasteful, good ambience and quick service. Communication was clear, we were warned that 1,5 hours later the table was reserved so we were happy that they let us sit and did not rush us. Good food, drinks and view. Everything necessary for a solid Venice experience

name: Natalie Perme
review: We ate here for lunch today and enjoyed it so much! After coming from touring Doge’s Palace, we were looking for a quiet, scenic spot to eat and get away from crowds - and this was perfect! Just saw it on Google maps and it had good reviews, so we sat down to give it a try. The cicchetti platter was delicious, as were the meatballs. We got glasses of wine after asking the waiter for recommendations, he was very helpful. He also brought extra non-fish cicchetti to our table after hearing that my friend wasn’t a huge fan of seafood (unlike me)- very sweet of him and it was also on the house which was extra generous. We watched a painter by the canal making lovely art and enjoyed our peaceful lunch tucked away in the canals. We were the first people at the restaurant for the day (arrived just after 12pm), but so many people sat down in the time we were there or took cicchetti to go, seemed like many locals. Can’t recommend enough!

name: Rebecca Hanley
review: Nice setting but the service was so terrible. We were in the middle of asking the waiter for a wine recommendation when he suddenly began helping another table. We had to call him back after waiting 10 minutes to order, we ordered and we were left 30+ minutes waiting for a beer and a wine only to be told there was no beer left (fine we ordered two wines) we were then left another 10 minutes waiting for 2 glasses of wine? The waiter didn’t even acknowledge the delay. Honestly would not recommend given the extremely poor service.

name: Jordan Byrne
review: Staff really friendly and attentive. Lovely spot for a wine away from the hustle and bustle.

name: Hilda Obeng-Kingsley
review: The service and the drinks were amazing. It's a great place to relax and have some reasonably priced cocktails. The servers have great attitudes and are very friendly.

name: Yana K
review: I cannot judge anything else’s mentioned in reviews, but cannoli at truly the best I’ve tried. With actual mascarpone and not some cheap oiled cream.

name: A L
review: A decent bar and well worth a visit if in the area. Our cocktails were not the best we had enjoyed on this trip though. Close to a canal where you can sit and chat.

name: Lily Mai
review: Servers were incredibly nice! The restaurant is small but clean and cozy, and the pizza my friend and I shared was a perfect snack/lunch during our day in the city. Thin and crispy pizza if you like that :)

name: Akınalp Fidan
review: We had aperol from here two times. Can easily say that it was the best aperol we had in italy and we had aperol in lots of cities and places. Its cheap, just enough ice( not filling entire cup with ice) and delicious. The servers were also very nice too

name: Montserrat Islas
review:
I want a picture, but we ate it so fast. The service was so good and attentive that the girl at the front explained everything patiently. We tried Cicchetti, and they were delicious, €2.50 each. We also had coffee, even though the cups were small.
The place is clean, and we didn't have to wait for seats. I recommend this place!

name: Michelle Reindl
review: Stayed close to here during my recent visit to Venice. Pizza, pasta and drinks were great. The spritz window was so fun! Staff was wonderful and made us feel at home.

name: Oksana A
review: Food was fine, but there're complete unsanitary conditions. The cutlery smelled of a dirty rag. I saw that the waiter went to the restroom wearing gloves, which he then used to serve drinks

name: Britt Furlanis
review: Great air conditioning, clean toilet, yummy Aperol Spritz and pizza. Thank you, Grazie!

name: Vuscor V.
review: they dont do separate checks, filthy cutlery straight out of the pack, overpriced. service charge is normal, but they also include a mandatory tip. i believe it's listed as a "beauty salon" to avoid legal troubles so i requested an edit. hopefully they will be closed for their hygiene problems and fraudulent fees. the food is not worth the money, the service is terrible, the place is dirty.

name: Daniel Jonsson
review: Bought a small pastry that tasted OK. However, the staff felt rude and stressed, so it wasn't a pleasant experience to visit this cafeteria. They made me feel like I was in the way for other customers.

name: jernej zivic
review:
The worst service in my life!
The waiter was agressive towards guests and potential guests. He blew them away with: WE DON'T SERVE COFFEE OUTSIDE....

name: WAR Al
review: The food is good ,and the staff are nice

name: Antonio Grana
review: 16€ for two warm cappuccinos and two day old pastries. Not only theft but the toilet is not cleaned. Don’t think because you are stopping there before going into the city that it will be cheaper. Up the road are a dozen cafes with the same drinks for 1/3 the price. Disgusted!

name: Alexander Bayona
review: The waiter took out the garbage and didn’t wash his hands before serving us. So disgusting. We asked to change or food and drinks and they refused, so, also, too expensive. No recommended at all

name: Daniel
review: Very expensive, not worth it.

name: SIMIUC LIVIU DANIEL
review:
Te clava el cafe a 6€ como les da la gana , no llevan los precios puestos.hay sitios con vistas mejores en el centro y 1.80€ capucino.
NU VA DUCEȚI, 6€ PE UN CAPUCINO ,

name: Matteo Mikulić
review: Sas Bar is an absolute gem! The coffee is exceptional – rich, smooth, and perfectly brewed every time. Their selection of pastries and sweets is equally impressive: fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is cozy and stylish, making it the perfect spot to relax or catch up with friends. The staff are friendly, welcoming, and clearly passionate about what they do. If you’re a coffee lover or have a sweet tooth, this place is a must-visit!

name: Jairo Lopez-Sierra
review: Honesty prob the worst gelato I’ve eaten, theirs so many other real authentic Italian gelato that’s so much better than this one

name: Tom Joyce
review: Great value and a friendly atmosphere.

name: Santos Alvarez
review: La trabajadora emplea el 100% de su tiempo en mirar y escuchar el teléfono móvil. No recoge, no limpia, no coloca… no hace nada salvo usar el móvil.  Y tampoco es amable… ¡ un auténtico desastre !  Después apareció otra trabajadora con, exactamente, la misma actitud.   Las posibilidades de que el negocio sobreviva con semejantes empleados son nulas

name: Korneliusz Dziki
review: Nowo otwarty klimatyzowany lokal, espresso 1.20€, Cappucino 2€, Limoncello 3.50€, smaczne przystawki, bardzo miła obsługa❤️ Warto wstąpić na poranną przekąskę przy kawie.

name: Krzy
review: Pyszna kawa, aperol. Miła obsługa, polecam :)

name: francois brigo
review:
Le café est bon mais la serveuse n'a pas l'air heureuse d'être là.
Dommage la place est jolie et elle pourrait en faire un lieu chaleureux.

name: Louis BURCK
review: Établissement géniale. C’est très simple mais fait l’affaire or les toilettes ne ferme pas à clef dommage mon ami aurait voulu aller lâcher les gosses à la piscine

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Rocío Serrano
review: Avoid at all costs! We stepped in to escape the cold — rookie mistake. I wish I had checked the reviews first. The owner was shockingly rude and unkempt, with a sink full of dirty dishes right in sight. When I politely asked, “How are you?” he replied, “Not well, what do you want?” The food was overpriced and downright awful. I genuinely hope you don’t make the same mistake I did.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Pola Zaki
review:
Food was not fresh and we waited 15 min although no one was there in the place except us.
He charged extra €1 when we ate the food inside

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Kamil Zdziennicki
review: Extremely rude owner. You cannot even touch the chair if you order takeaway. Canollo di pistachio was expensive, artificial, not very tasty. Staff talking behind the back about customers (one of us speaks Italian). Not recommending this place.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: V YNWA
review: I was served by a nice lady with tattoo. Not sure if she work there or she is the boss. Another man has a poker face and I think they are Father and daughter. Anyway, the lady was nice, service oriented. Recommended.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Josh Funnell
review: Absolutely disgusting place, dirty and rude owner. Wouldn’t even let me go to the toilet despite ordering. Would never return.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Kristin Shanahan
review: Lovely man behind the counter, delicious coffee and sandwich

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Malen Pugliese
review: We were treated rudely by the staff. We felt unwelcome and uncomfortable the entire time. Definitely not a place I would recommend or ever return to.

place_title: Bar Caffè Ciangol di Squarcina G & C
name: Aarne Raudik
review: Price what you see is not true. They will charge you an how they feel like it.  Espresso was 1.50 charge 2.50. Did say if you sit down every item is an extra charge, espresso + extra, Coca-Cola + extra, slice of pizza + extra, slice of pizza + extra. Don't buy anything from there

name: Sarah Petry
review: Great little spot for some gelato and bruschetta, it was great to sit outside and watch the world go by.

name: Shaen Catherwood
review: Lesson from reviews seems to be: focus on the drinks. We paid €36 for two beers, two generous glasses of white wine, and a very (very) generous cocktail. Comfy seats, lovely setting plus the added interest of it being beside a gondola stop. We were very happy. Can’t comment on the controversial cannoli…other than to say that they’re from Sicily and we’re in Venice.

name: Kevin McCabe
review: Grabbed a coffee and some pastries. I don’t remember anything particularly bad about this spot. They had some cold drinks and fans going to chill everyone off. Looked like ample seating outside. No complaints really. Just your average Venetian cafe.

name: xristina_eski
review: We ordered a beer and an Aperol, which were both very nice and refreshing! I loved this place because the view was stunning, and it’s perfect for a quick drink after a walk!

name: Roland Csontos
review: Confusing business with multiple names (Anastasis S.a.S., Bar Ai Miracoli, Antichità). No menu or prices shown, total for 4 gelatos + 1 cannoli was €19, far above local norms. Felt misleading, be aware before ordering.

name: Sierra Shellabarger
review: TOURIST TRAP!! Didn’t read the reviews beforehand, just walked in and ordered because I was exhausted and desperately needed a coffee and didn’t want to have to walk another block lol. My mistake. Paid €9 for an overpriced Latte macchiato and the worst cannoli I’ve ever had the displeasure of eating. That was not even a cannoli, it was a cannoli shell filled with frosting that tasted worse than canned Betty Crocker. Couldn’t eat more than a bite. Even the cherries on it tasted horrendous. I wish I could give it 0 stars. View of the church was nice though

name: Juan Antonio Garcia-Mendez
review: The best mojitos we’ve had in Venice.  Would definitely come back for another.  Oh we’re back already after our Gondola ride that we picked up just next door.

name: michel azzam
review:
Overpriced, not worth it at all, not recommended!

We ordered 2 hot chocolate and 2 Fornarina with a total of 19€!

On top of it, the Fornarina was extremely bad, that I had to ask the server how often do you change them since it’s made with creme, he said: “every 1 week!!”
It was awful!! I Informed him about that and he couldn’t care any less.

Not recommended at all!

name: Shayla Raine
review: First time trying an aperol spritz and it did not disappoint. The bruschetta was fresh and just the right snack on the beach. Decent prices for good quality.

name: Emma Nedelcu
review: A lot better than expected vs reviews. Seems like last year’s reviews complained about poor/no table service. Well now they just have big signs saying “no table service”, problem solved (the staff certainly don’t seem happy to be there and won’t spare a smile or eye contact for you). We ordered and got one of those buzz back things for when the food is ready. The food is better than it has any right to be for a place where we were surprised they even cook anything there, but that’s Italy for you!

name: catrine Waltersdorph Hansen
review: It seems like this bar doesn't like to serve anything for anyone who is a tourist. The attitude is so bad and poorly. When ordering it feels more of a pain than anything else. The service is shameless. I gave them 3 different opportunities, over 4 days, just to let the benefits come to the dough. Unfortunately, same bad poorly attitude

name: Zoltán András Dániel
review: Everything was delicious, the staff is cool and kind

name: Jules Philpott
review: Scruffy place, no atmosphere, but cocktails were good!

name: Buck
review:
We didn't eat here, just had drinks.  But I can understand staffs frustration with tourists as British couple in front of us had come back to cancel a lasagne order (who orders take away lasagne on the beach ?).  Still the Italian histrionics of bar staff were fantastic, particularly as they confirmed they had not started cooking the lasagne.
Points awarded for "basil faulty" style !!

name: Andrea Plesarová
review:
I highly do not recommend this bistro, if you are a foreigner. We left without any service provided. We came here with my husband just to enjoy a drink and some finger food. We were sitting at the table quite a while,  no one came to take our order. Later, we just went to the stand for order. Bartender spotted us, asked, whether we came for just drinks or for food too, we said food too, so he give us sign to sit at the table.
We were sitting at the table for like 20 more minutes, still no service. The bartender, meanwhile, took order from a couple sitting right next to us, served them food and drinks, and totally ignored me and my husband. After more than 30 minutes of waiting,  we just left without any service.

name: Mark Dunne
review: Good vibes on the beach  in Lido... managed to find a San Miguel beer and the cocktails are good as well, prices reasonable

name: Ruslan Salikhov
review:
🛑 Very disappointed with Bialetti moka pot (€100 model)

I trusted the Bialetti name, but the experience was frustrating.

The interior coating is difficult to clean, and residue builds up quickly. Even after careful use and gentle cleaning, the inside never looks clean.

The outer finish started fading and changing color after just a few weeks.

Coffee started tasting metallic, which is worrying for a high-priced item.

I contacted support — their advice? Clean it with baking soda (which is abrasive) and “watch the temperature”, but they gave no specific guidelines.

Honestly, this product isn’t worth the money. It looks great out of the box, but quickly loses quality. I expected better from Bialetti.

Wouldn’t recommend based on my experience.

name: J. Lepianda
review: Great selection of moka pots and kitchenware. A must-visit for coffee lovers in Venice.

name: Yixuan Lu
review: I had the worst shopping experience in the 2 stores of them during my 2 months travel in Italy. To be fair, I like the products very much, but the staff really need better training. Firstly, they didn’t remind me that I can do tax refund when I spent 90€ there for the first time. I came there again for buying gifts for my friends, but the staff in the bigger shop was VERY passive, she didn’t even answer or smile at me when I said hi, and was giving quick answers like “no we don’t have” in a cold way, instead of introducing the products. So I went to the smaller store, where the staff was nicer and did tax refund for me this time. But when I arrived at the airport they told me the passport number was wrong - the staff in Bialetti wrote I for 1… I was literally speechless… My experience told me this is not an occasional case. If you ever visit their store, be sure to skillfully dodge all the staff’s traps and obstacles before finally locating what you came for.

name: Yuşa Nuri Güngör
review: Super super friendly staff. I didn’t buy moka pot because I just bought my bialetti a few months ago. Next time I will buy moka pot with italy 🇮🇹 colors.

name: Sofía Piña Félix
review:
All the things they sell are divine, but the service is beyond terrible, the girl that was there didn't answer any of my questions, didn't help us at all and when I pay she took the money rudely and barely said a word.
TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE

name: George StandingBear
review:
OPENING HOURS

MONDAY

10.00-19.30

TUESDAY

10.00-19.30

WEDNESDAY

10.00-19.30

THURSDAY

10.00-19.30

FRIDAY

10.00-19.30

SATURDAY

10.00-19.30

SUNDAY

10.00-19.30

name: Nariné K. Q.
review: Very unkind and impolite saleswoman, being a loyal customer of Bialetti I don’t recommend at all this shop. The shame for the brand !

name: Abelardo Villarosa, Jr.
review: Beautifully designed store with a complete array of coffee brewers and kitchen equipment. The famous Bialetti Moka Pot in different variants are available. Got e 6-cup Dolce & Gabbana Limited Edition Moka Pot and Espresso Wafer Cups. Worth a visit.

name: Arevik Ford
review: Far from your typical tourist menu, this corner spot overlooking a piazza serves up delicious food and great service. Good variety on the menu and even entrees are well portioned. Would come back here again!

name: Williamson Transport, LLC
review: we're sitting inside and saw some bread drop on the floor,  next to the blue glasses.  They picked them up and put them back into a bread basket.  YUK!  Guess it's better not to know what happens to your food sometimes.

name: Quentin Brandwood
review:
Fantastic food in the centre of Burano. We had the fried sardines (with a side of "French fries" which were fried thin cut potato, I was a bit skeptical when they were suggested but yes order these) and lasagne both were exceptionally good.
The menu is in multiple languages and the waiter that we had spoke good English. There is a small cover charge (1,8 euro each) which is on the menu. This was in a nice out door setting in the middle of Burano. Easy to find and well worth the visit.
Highly recommend eating here.

name: Karel Petrášek
review: Nice looking place at the square, so we decided to celebrate our engagement there. At first, we read through the menu and decided to get drinks only, maybe dessert. Although there was nobody inside, and at the time maybe 5 tables occupied, nobody noticed. So we picked our seats and sat down. In about a minute or so waiter came and asked if we’d like to eat. As soon as we told him we are there probably for drinks only, he immediately asked us to move to a different area of the restaurant, as “our” seats were for lunch customers only. (No sign or obvious hint to assume such thing). As politely as he asked us to move, we stood up and left and took our business to much nicer establishment. Not surprised the place was almost empty.

name: Carmen Macota
review:
The food is good here, we had fried fish plateau, spaghetti with squid ink, spaghetti bolognaise and spaghetti with sea food and their famous biscuits.
The food came pretty fast and the waiter was nice.

name: Steven Cheng
review:
Great food, great drinks and Great service!

Very inviting of the outside seating area and wonderful seafood selections !

We stopped by to having lunch, very friendly staffs speaking enough English for you to order your meals!

We hope we can visit this little island and this restaurant again!

name: Fiamma Seda
review:
Let me start with the warmth and happiness we received as soon as we approached the restaurant, Fabio (the owner), his family and employees super respectful and 100% attention to details. Their food is extremely fresh, from their cocktails, to their fish tapas all the way to they heart melting FIG CAKE (i wanted to steal the recipe!!!) no joke the BEST experience by far.

They let us sit were we wanted, we love a specific cocktail and they made it for us, introduced us to an unfiltered local beer, brought us some tapas for my mom and myself to share & finally a fig cake (you’ll want to take home)

Please have in mind these islands depend 100% on tourism and the pandemic hit them hard so please go support and tip these lovely people.

Thank you Fabio, Emely and the team.

name: Adelina D
review: The restaurant is exactly in the main square. The service is impeccable, the waiter was very polite, and the order came quickly. The food was very good, big portions. The sepia ink pasta are delicious, I recommend.

name: Jenique K
review: Food is reasonably priced and nice for the price we paid. Coffee was good too. We enjoyed our breakfast here and we came here for 2 of the mornings we were in Venice. The cafe is modern. And the owners were nice. Do consider coming if you are looking for somewhere to grab a snack or breakfast!

name: Gianfranco Alvarez
review: Great place to have breakfast in Mestre. Very nice people and good selection. The bar itself is very modern and cozy, and the bathroom is extremely clean. Good prices too: Each Brioche was €1.20, each cappuccino €1.50 and fresh orange juice for €3.50. Great place, very satisfied.

name: mahsa shirmohammad
review: I tried this place a few times. I like the vibes and the staff are nice. I had breakfast several times and everything is always fresh. Prices are fair and in the usual range.

name: Sandy Lam
review: Food was good but pizza likely frozen or pre-made. Decent portion size and Price around 7-8 euro for pasta/pizza. Great place to sit and have your coffee.

name: Naveen Agrawal
review:
Didn’t expect the best spaghetti pasta I will eat during my trip in Italy will be at a Cafetaria and not at a restaurant or trattoria!!!
The tomato sauce was really well done! flavorful and tasty! Hidden gem!

name: Karol Adamiak
review:
Had really bad experience here. All of the food is prepared from frozen food. You can actually see it haw they are preparing it.
Pasta I have ordered it wasn’t cooked properly and some of the bits were hard...
Good prices for coffee.

name: Maciej Blacha
review: Nice tasty sandwiches,  very good coffee

name: Laura Valenzuela
review:
Unpretentious cafe with a limited selection of dishes, but that doesn't mean is anywhere ordinary.
We had a great dinner with 3 bottles of Pinot Nero, from Pasta Carbonara to Lasagna and Risotto plus Charcuterie and Cheese platter.
And the Brownie was to die for!
Had a blast and staff was so attentive! Thank you for your hospitality!

name: Bia&Pedro
review:
The Pizzeria Birreria Barbanera is simply incredible! If you are looking for a complete gastronomic experience in Venice, it is a fantastic choice. The cost-effectiveness is excellent and the price is unbelievable for the quality of the food. The dishes are delicious and served with perfect service!
​A major highlight is their attention to tourists: the staff speaks English, Spanish, and other languages, and the menu is available in Portuguese, English, Spanish, and other languages. This makes things much easier! It's the ideal spot to eat well, be well attended, and save money. I highly recommend it!

name: Kelsey
review: Found this place on Google and was not disappointed, very glad we came.  Service is great (they even bring hand wipes after you finish your food!!), food was very tasty and a great price. name: The LightHouse Porto Santo
review:
A magnificent restaurant in the heart of Venice. A pleasant, welcoming atmosphere. The service is excellent, and the staff are very friendly. The food was a delightful surprise! The best food I've had in all of Italy! The flavors were spectacular, with excellent presentation—a marvel! It was so good that we returned to this restaurant for another experience, and once again, it was worth it. Everyone was very satisfied.

I recommend this restaurant to everyone, and when I return to Venice, it will definitely be a must-visit! 🫶

name: Izy
review: Service good and food arrived quite quickly. Spag Bol was nice. The ravioli was good but the sauce for the dish and for the shrimp pasta wasn’t the best, very strong tomato flavour like a can of canned tomatoes had been emptied onto the plate. Chips nice.

name: Cesar Canales Hortelano
review:
Amazing place with nice people and nice food. It is very special that everything is included on the prize. Not extra charge! It's very important in Venezia where in every place try to get more money for nothing. All the staff are very friendly and kind. For sure all my family will come back!!
Tiramisu and Panacotta are the best!

name: Kristi Simone
review: Nasir was Our later. He was so fantastic and helped us understand the menu. The food here is absolutely amazing. Amazing. Definitely come here. It looks like it is all locals. I feel like we were the only Americans in here. Truly fantastic food. Such a delightful experience.

name: Victor Araujo
review: I'm not one for leaving bad reviews but the salami pizza looks like one I would see at my high school cafeteria. The fungi pizza was better, but the crust also seemed cheap. Taste-wise it was alright but I expected much better, especially in Italy. Service was fine and quick, just the food wasn't good.

review: A true hidden gem in Venice! Stumbled upon it by chance and had the best carbonara and pizza I’ve tried in Venice so far. Authentic flavors, great service, and fair prices..no tourist trap, just real Italian food done right.

review:
The most delicious food that stood out amongst all the touristic restaurants in the area. Their tiramisu was so satisfying and perfect, had it every day while in Venice guilt free. Not to mention the impeccable arancini, reasonable price points when you take away- and Hugo spritz converted me to enjoying an alcoholic drink- and an individual who’s typically alcohol free.

I have to recommend their pistachio muffin- when warm it just tastes like heaven

name: Ree
review:
We stopped for some food in Venice - which can be very overwhelming for places to try as there are so many options. This place had an extensive menu with some great options.

We got mushroom pasta, pizza, antipasti boards, burrata salad & caprese salad. Everything was fine, the cheese and salad a highlight! The pizza was also lovely.

Definitely by no means a groundbreaking meal but a good place to stop for food if you need somewhere!

name: Stefan Tivodariu
review:
The quality of the food was satisfactory. However, the experience was severely marred by issues regarding price transparency and dishonest service.
The waiter refused to provide a menu with current pricing and instead presented us with a different, more expensive menu than the one available online, resulting in inflated costs. A specific instance involved an order for rum, which was doubled in price and the incorrect brand was served. Furthermore, we encountered problems with the "plate of the day," which was not listed on any menu, and the staff were unhelpful in clarifying its cost. An unrequested and unannounced extra charge for burrata was also added to our bill after the waiter suggested adding it to a dish without disclosing the additional fee. Ultimately, when we questioned the bill, the waiter admitted to using a completely separate menu (not the one intended for customers) to determine our charges.

name: Paul Anderson
review: One of my favourite meals I have had while in Italy! Everything was delicious and the staff were friendly and helpful. I ordered the catch of the day and our server skinned and deboned the cooked fish at our table right in front of me which was really cool to watch! Also had my favourite tiramisu I've tried so far. This place is wonderful. 💖

name: samira
review: We went there like nearly everyday, one time for dinner, breakfast and lastly for lunch. The service is really good, they understood my  mom, even though she spoke French, which is kind of impressive. The prices are OK, the place itself is nice decorated and that comes from a picky person so yeah. It is also not really located near the tourist places so it's not that busy. Overall I recommend this place to ya'll.

name: Andrei Mihai
review: A ok Dinner. The waiter tryied to make us pay more for our order. He tried to play stupid after answering our questions and deliberately hiding the costs... we understood that at the end, and then he tried to make us pay extra for our drinks, bringing a menu from inside... while he told us to look on the internet menu, where the prices were different (half).

name: Sebastian Weißinger
review: nice little restaurant in a quieter area in Venice.  Aranginis taste good and so does the pizza.  For 8€ such a delicious margarita and only 1€ cover price, you can't complain. Value for money is fair, but please clean the water glasses better or polish them dry.

name: Kimberly Briscoe
review: By far the best food we had during our trip to Venice. The carbonara was beyond delicious and may have ruined pasta for me as now nothing can ever compare. The tiramisu was delicious, as well - the best I've had. I love how it was served in a little mason jar; it gave it more of a home-prepared and less of a mass-produced vibe. It was a wonderful atmosphere and the service was great without being overbearing. When I come back, this will be my first stop for food!

name: Liv Anderson
review:

I visited this restaurant twice in our 3 day visit to Venice. One night 1 we had a mojito (best I’ve had all trip), and an Aperol spritz. We ate a capri salad and shared a calamari plate. The capri salad was amazing, the calamari was a large portion and had a thick crumbing which isn’t our favourite, it was still a great meal. At the end our serve was relatively aggressive regarding a tip. He kept pushing and pushing for one. In the end we gave a very small tip… he was not fabulous- BUT Fabio on night 2, he was a great server.

Night 2 we ate the lasagna and a sea food boil bag in which were both good meals, followed by tiramisu and a cake type moose with cream, which I forgot the name. That cake was INCREDIBLE- the server Fabio suggested it to me. I would go back just for that dessert!! He did not disappoint! The service by Fabio was awesome and much better than the first night. He was happy, bubbly and just an overall good vibe!

To top the night off, Fabio gave us limoncello shots on the house. It was a great atmosphere, beautiful views and yummy food and drink. I would recommend this place to anyone visiting Venice- ask for Fabio!!

name: Lars Van der baan
review: Staff was really friendly, our waitress (Paula) was super sweet and gave us really good recommendations. She wasn’t pushy about it and gave us time to think. The view was gorgeous!
