name: Zosia Pietrzak
review:
Risotto was amazing, pizza was delicious.
We love the atmosphere here
Waiter Fabio is the best!!!
We recommend it very much! 🤍

name: MerijnGarry Home
review: Looking out over the water what an amazing location.  So friendly so professional food and service first rate…just relax and enjoy

name: Thao N
review: Great service even in a super busy evening with full of people waiting for firework. Everyone there including the lady asked what would we like til the staff who made drink for us were kind and smiling all the time. They were very helpful answering my questions related to my mocktail drink. The drink was yum too! Thanks team!

name: Cheryl Affleck
review: Had amazing meal tonight, food and service excellent. Our waitress was Paulo, she was really friendly and helpful. Put heated lamps on outside and brought us blankets when it turned cold outside. Beautiful food would highly recommend.

name: Cintia Szíjártó
review: We had a pleasant dinner here today. Our waitress, Paula, was very kind and helpful. The food was delicious, we were satisfied with everything. Thank you again, Paula!☺️

name: Omar Patel
review: I've been all around the world and I've never had someone be such a completely horrible person. I'm sure he gets asked about information all the time, but he could have just said "sorry, I think you should ask that person there". Instead, he gets all mad and starts getting upset that I have the audacity to ask him anything and I should only talk to him if I want to buy anything. He says he's super busy when there were no customers either. I also wasn't talking to him, but another person working there. Just a terrible dude.

name: Олег Подолян
review: They start work on time, delicious croissants

name: Elian Ozen
review: El boss of cafe is agresive, becose i didn't see that u need to pay with a card 5€ minimum. And he threw away my café

name: shahar tamir
review:
Eddy is doing the best fresh food!
Highly recommended!!

name: Jakob Kremling
review: Uno dei migliori panini che ho mai mangiato, veramente gustoso e saporito, pure veloce, e per i prezzi di Venezia economico, poi anche il venditore era gentile

name: il boss dei lupi (gianluigi)
review: Ho preso un cappuccino e mi è stato fatto direttamente da asporto, ho pagato 2.20€, prezzo un po’ più alto della media, il latte leggermente bruciato, ma tutto sommato bevibile, servizio super veloce e gentile

name: Jacopo Celesia
review: Orario di apertura alle 6.00 - bar aperto - proprietario scontroso che mi dice: "chiuso, apro tra venti minuti". Risultato: 4 ore di autobus a stomaco vuoto. Complimenti!

name: Giovanni Compagno
review:
Let me just tell you that after eating a hot dog from them, probably either the mustard or the ingredients of the sandwich themselves spoiled; I stayed 2 days with stomach cramps and regular diarrhea every hour; excellent experience and staff always on the ball !

Vi dico solo che dopo avere mangiato da loro un hot dog, probabilmente o la senape o li ingredienti stessi del panino avariati; sono rimasto 2 giorni con crampi allo stomaco e diarrea regolare ogni ora; esperienza ottima e personale sempre sul pezzo !

#reffevefora

name: Bia&Pedro
review:
The Pizzeria Birreria Barbanera is simply incredible! If you are looking for a complete gastronomic experience in Venice, it is a fantastic choice. The cost-effectiveness is excellent and the price is unbelievable for the quality of the food. The dishes are delicious and served with perfect service!
​A major highlight is their attention to tourists: the staff speaks English, Spanish, and other languages, and the menu is available in Portuguese, English, Spanish, and other languages. This makes things much easier! It's the ideal spot to eat well, be well attended, and save money. I highly recommend it!

name: Robin G
review:
AVOID LIKE THE PLAGUE!!!!

Lady with fake red hair was so MISERABLE and RUDE. I ordered a cappuccino. She decided for me that I would have a latte instead because cappuccino is apparently only served before 11 am.
While you are deciding for me what I will drink, I’m living my best life traveling the world and you are stuck  behind a sandwich counter heating sandwiches for tourists like me! BOOYAH!

name: Taisiya Taisiya
review:
Discrimination of tourists. The same order, 2 coffees, ordered at three same time with the locals, costed us €2,5 each. While for locals it costed €3,5. After my question if the prices are different for locals and residents the bartender just lied in my face and said “no”.
Also there is no difference between the price for coffee on the bar and coffee at the table (which is usually so in Italy). So do not recommend if you do not want to be cheated.

name: Christian Gagnon
review:
Avoid this horrible place
They just charge you what they want for coffee… we paid 5 euro for ordering a cappuccino and were given a latte

The staff is very rude and thieves… she asked for cash pretending the card machine was not working which was false

Hope they get arrested and shut down

There are other options

name: Max
review: If you’d like to experience the depths of hell on earth, look no further than Bar Cafe Palmisano. The wrap was inedible. Rubber meat and brown lettuce wrapped in raw pizza dough. The workers didn’t care to be there, and why would they? I couldn’t imagine working at such a train wreck of a place. Pigeons, live pigeons, INSIDE THE RESTAURANT AND ON THE TABLES INSIDE!!! This place is a disease. A filthy and wretched excuse for a bakery that genuinely detracts from the beauty of burano.

name: Tesha Blair
review: Great coffee, patio, and atmosphere! Plus a clean restroom! Also, Jesus is coming back soon! Pray and ask Him to come into your heart and repent of your sins to be saved. 💜

name: Stephen Bressi
review: Had a nice lunch. The service was prompt and the food good. My Ham & Cheese Panini was nice and hot. We also had a Burano biscuit. They also have a nice selection of baked goods. I do not understand the negative reviews.

name: Sarah
review:
Went their for lunch and only sandwichs have prices. Staff are very unpleasant as when you ask prices for the drinks look annoyed and complained about it in Italian thinking we won't understand ! Super unprofessional !
Sandwich wasn't bad but small left us hungry still so 8€ for it. Then cappuccino 3€ for half a cup being mousse... Prices as expensive as in London for poor quality food and service. Again could of stay in London for that rather than coming all the way to Italy. Definitely won't go there ever again.
Positive points toilets were clean, and they don't tax you to seat at the table

name: MS P
review: Best for tiramisu. But we went twice it was out of stock. Better to reach before 3 pm

name: Brandy
review: Best kept secret in Venice. Had dinner in the fairytale setting of the restaurant. Gorgeous views of the grand canal, amazing service and the most delicious meal we had in Italy. Highly recommend the octopus salad, sage butter ravioli, sea bass and tiramisu. All from scratch. It was the perfect evening.

name: Hovig Manjikian
review: We had the most magical post-wedding dinner at this charming restaurant in Venice with our family and friends—truly a night to remember! The staff were amazing with their warm, attentive service, even squeezing in an extra guest at the last minute without missing a beat. The food was exquisite, the ambiance cozy and romantic, and the value was incredibly reasonable for such a special occasion. We couldn't be more thrilled and highly recommend this gem to anyone celebrating in Venice! 5 stars all the way!

name: Mert Gündüz
review:
This was a true hidden gem of Venice. Our waiter was kind, fast, responsive, clear and had incredible knowledge of what he was serving.

Balcony was scenic and comfortable. It’s silent and had very beautiful looking interior. For a relaxed, romantic dinner this restaurant has everything to offer.

Food was delicious and light, fairly priced, drinks have vast and authentic choices.

At the end of our dinner, it started rain, our waiter asked if we have coats or umbrellas, when he learned we don’t have neither they supplied us with an umbrella. Incredibly kind again, thank you for this amazing experience.

name: Lindsey Martin
review: Gorgeous property that was once a private home. Didn’t stay at the hotel but popped in for a dinner. Took some great photos on the balcony and watched the sunset! We had to move inside because of the mosquitos, which the staff was happy to accommodate. The food was not the best, so I would say to come here for a lighter bite, a drink, and the view/checking out the gorgeous interiors!

name: Tony McBass
review:
We dined at Palazzetto Pisani on the 7th of July 2024, and it was an experience we can't stop raving about! The food was absolutely divine, and the view over the Grand Canal? Simply breathtaking. But the true highlight of our evening was the host, Carlo, this guy is just unbelievable. He was a star, very polite, proactive, and radiated warmth and friendliness. Don’t miss out on his special olive oil dip for your bread—it's a game-changer. We’re thrilled we stumbled upon this place; it's a true hidden gem in Venice! Worth a try...

@namhla_mbawuli

name: Juigné Nicolas
review:
Just amazing!
We've been served by Carlo and this guy is just unbelievable.
Thanks again for the moment and the view.
Take the balcony view, you won't be disappointed!

name: Rozy Floratou
review:
We truly enjoyed our dinner, the atmosphere was elegant and relaxed, the staff very kind, and the food was delicious. We chose seafood dishes such as shrimp tartare, cod, and umbrine, and everything seemed perfect at the time.

Unfortunately, just a few hours later, we started experiencing severe symptoms of food poisoning, which required medication and rest. The vomiting and diarrhea episodes were intense and continuous, leading to serious dehydration and extreme fatigue (we were vomiting all night and couldn’t sleep at all) .The situation was even more difficult because we had to travel the next day in this condition, and the whole experience was exhausting and upsetting.

It’s a real shame, as we were initially very pleased with the place. I hope the restaurant takes this seriously and improves food safety standards to protect future guests.

name: Daniel Leigh
review: We ate here a couple weeks ago and haven't forgotten about it. The food was incredible, the view over the grand Canal spectacular, but the real star was the host Carlo. Polite, proactive and very friendly and helpful, ask for his special olive oil dip creation for your bread, amazing. We are so glad we ate here, a real hidden gem in Venice

name: Cintia Szíjártó
review: We had a pleasant dinner here today. Our waitress, Paula, was very kind and helpful. The food was delicious, we were satisfied with everything. Thank you again, Paula!☺️

name: Amindha Fernando
review:
We accidentally stumbled upon this place trying to escape the crowds in central Venice. Glad we did as it was a highlight meal of our stay here.

Our intention was only to stop for a drink but when the apertivos arrived, we knew the food had to be good too. Highlights were the bruschetta and the meatballs with polenta. Service was great too and being on the quieter side of the Venuce, it was a beautiful spot to unwind.

name: Stella Gonzalez
review: Stopped here for lunch and it was pretty good! Would definitely recommend the lasagna. Service was great and we got our food and bill very fast. It’s also on a scenic yet not overly crowded street. Definitely stop by here for drinks or lunch!

name: erdal bana
review: We ordered spagetti with mixed seafood. İt was literally magnificient. Very delicious. And the waiter named NILOY was very kind and helpful. Thanks to the waiter and the chef. İ advise everyone this place. İf i ever come Venice again for sure i will visit this place and eat the same food.

name: Алеся Слюз
review:
It was amazing
Thank you very much for the delicious lunch
Very tasty food and extremely nice staff.
I tried the pasta, it's the best pasta I've ever had.

name: Joan Skelton Smith
review: We enjoyed the menu of the day for lunch, 2 courses with several choices of first course for 15.90€ plus coperto of course. The starter bruschetta and rigatoni were both excellent. The salad with the grilled chicken was great, the chicken breast nice but could use more spice.

name: Sadie Coulianos
review: Food was so delicious and well-priced! Amazing mozzarella. Service was very nice. Free limoncello shot. And the best tiramisu I’ve had in my life!

name: Ryan Hough
review: Thank you for a tasty meal in a lovely location with a beautiful sunset.

name: Stacie Givens
review: Tapas was our waiter and made our dining experience even more enjoyable. The food was incredible and I am proud to say I ate too much and enjoyed every bite. Would eat here again if it wasn’t our last night in Venice!

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Bohdan Shchensnevich
review: The only bar that truly feels Venetian. The tramezzini were delicious, and they offer a great variety of spritz. A perfect spot for an authentic local experience. 5/5

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Éric Tournie
review: Lovely, 16€ for 4 drinks, totally fair prices. Lots of locals too :)

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Joe Ross
review:
Total rip off. They will over charge you if you have a drink there.
Don’t mind paying a bit of a premium for Venice but when they blatantly over charge the listed price it spoils the whole experience. Local clientele were lovely. Such a shame the lady owner has no moral compass what so ever. No shame

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Marco Repola
review: Pretty small, very local spot for drinks and snacks. This bar has a lot of heart, the drinks are solid and reasonably priced, the tramezzini hit the spot, and the staff are lovely.

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Justin Laughlin
review: I ordered an Aperol spritz. I watched him make it and he put a literal splash of prosecco. All Aperol and water. I tried to make small talk and was told that only venetian and Italian are spoken in a very rude manner. There are a bunch of small bars around here, I'd go to one of them.

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Chelsey
review: Very beautiful bar in Venice:) Stopped for an espresso and a snack, both were delicious. I couldn’t speak Italian, but the keeper was very kind

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Leeann Cross
review: AVOID lady totally overcharged on the beers 2 small & 2 regular beers, 22 euros rip off steer clear if you are a tourist such a shame a nice little quaint place where we met a lovely local man & his dog

place_title: Cantina Antica Vigna Barwine di Benettelli Ferdinando
name: Markus Christensen
review: Tremendously bad experience for something that should be rather simple – a cup of coffee and some tour planning at my pc. Apperently using modern technology at your own discretion is against the store policy, and they do not tell that to you in a nice way.

name: jasmine xu
review:
Fresh air, beautiful and musical!
This famous and historical icon is located inside of the San Mark's  Square.  It has a lot of outdoor seatings and tables,  all year long and for many years.  It also has a great passion for classic and contemporary music in a formal and professional level!
I have seen those well dressed musicians playing high class music in the warm afternoon sun,  and I really admire their dedication and wonderful work!  This famous,  " World Living Room " ---San Mark's Square, has come to live when the live band and music started to play,  it makes you feel that this Square is full of the life,  music,  and fun!
An open air bar with concert,  playing all year long,  who doesn't want to come to San Mark's Square?

name: berke ayrancioglu
review: A fancy restaurant/cafe located right in the middle of St. Mark's square. Desserts and coffee are nice and waitors are kind but very expensive. Can be experienced once if you are visiting Venice

name: Patrick BICHET
review:
Yes we are in San Marco square
Yes, there are many tourists (including us)
Yes, there are establishments waiting for tourists...
We spent an hour quietly, in the sun, having a drink, eating a tiramisu and it was a very good time.
The service was neutral and without special attention it is true, but very professional...
The rest doesn't matter

name: Farzaneh Niazi
review: Everything was professional, everyone super friendly just overpriced. It was very hot outside in the morning so I asked if i could sit inside even though it was closed, he let me . I liked my order.

name: Ciara Walton
review: The food is obviously super pricy, but the location and the live music make it worth it!!

name: Jumana Amar
review:
The process in the invoice were double the ones in the menu , i was surprised with the prices in the invoice but was in hurry to argue with them but raised to them my concern.
Staff and management are rude, We were 5 people , so once we entered the cafe we asked to merge two tables (cause the tables outside are all for 1 or 3 people mqx) but waiter refused Rudy. Once we ordered he brought us another table and said now i can give you another table! What a rudeness! We understood that this is the policy of restaurants in the area… but still rude and effort to move the table will much less to loss reputation. I hope they can consider this

name: Pablo Balbuena
review: Slow and rude staff, they see you there and they don’t give a s*** about it. We finally got a table, the guy who served us was nice (Adriano). When I paid, I found a difference between the price on the website and what I actually paid. We asked the waiter, he excused the restaurant cause they have prices pre-pandemic and they have a “very complex system” that didn’t update the prices. It’s not his fault but…BASTARDS! NEVER AGAIN.  The live music was good (I means that other people will record the musicians on my face and it’s a bit uncomfortable). Location is great. #neveragain

name: Tobias Herzog
review: It's a trap and really untrustworthy! Higher prices for such a location are ok, but a Sprite for 14€, a Cappuccino for 14€ is ridiculous. This location is ruin the reputation of Venice.

name: Pichet P
review:
Ordinary cafe in my opinion. The cafe location is prime as it is about 100-200 meters from the metro station.

We did dinned in around 10am, it was crowded. Their staff is quite good, but everything is too salty for us.

Fresh fruit seems not fresh, a lot of water is in the plate and it tastes weird.

There is a lot of tables available, toilet is good (nice and clean).

name: Antonio Calabrese
review: Good place for a quick bite! Place was clean and had a good vibe. Location was great right by our hotel and across the street from the train station.

name: Tristian H
review: Really good food and service from Tanvir. I was staying at the hostel (Anda) around the corner and they offer a 10% discount for guests from the hostel! Excellent value for money. And the food was delicious!

name: Eva Batres
review: We were greeted and seated right away. Tanvir was wonderful to have as a waiter. He was very attentive and kept making some jokes :) he also helped me a little bit with my Italian since I’m trying to learn. The food was delicious and we received complimentary cookies at the end of our meal that were really good.

name: Lisa Suen
review:
We had lunch here just because of the location.
The manager is not nice at all. Cabonara spaghetti tastes awful. It’s the worst I have never had. Salmon salad has only a few lettuces with a few salmon, no salad dressing nor any other garnish.
There’s a €2 per person head charge if you dine in.
Overall, Not recommended.

name: Mel Fleming
review: Julia was very friendly, very attentive and the biscuits were delicious!  Food was fantastic, thoroughly enjoyed our meal, excellent price and value for money. Would recommend anyone coming to Venice to check out Sould Kitchen Cafe!!

name: Peter Muraglia
review: Amir is a wonderful waiter! He made perfect recommendations for us from the extensive menu. We thoroughly enjoyed our dinners and look forward to a return visit soon.

name: Lily V
review: A group of us dinned in here tonight and it was a 10/10 experience for us! It’s our first day in the area and we stumbled upon this absolute gem of a spot. As someone who is gluten free going out for dinner is often a hard task especially in other countries but the staff here were so helpful and accomodating. They have multiple gluten free options as well aa all the pastas can be made gluten free, in the back of the menu they had an allergy chart which made our experience so much easier. I can’t recommend this place enough. The overall look and vibe of the place is amazing the staff are so lovely especially Donald, and the food/drinks comes out quick as well. Definitely a place we will be coming back to :)

name: Betül yangöz
review: I used to go there almost everyday. I really like this place. The stuffs are so nice.

name: Isabelle
review: Good and tasty breakfast, in a nice part of Mestre. However staff not very nice.

name: Mona Abbas
review: Delicious Pastries, good coffee, Plesant environment.

name: Iraklis Zaimidis
review:
The place was excellent!
Helpful staff, good prices and excellent products!
We had four days in a row our breakfast there!

name: A S
review: Gorgeous fresh pastries and coffee. Very friendly and accommodating staff as well. I would recommend this shop over all the others in the neighborhood.

name: Alberto Antinucci
review: Great place, great atmosphere, great food, great pastries and, best of all, great looking and extremely efficient and gentle girls working there. ☺️

name: Jan Samek
review: Great place, very good coffee, kindly staff.

name: Viktorija Klepsiene
review: Very good coffee, friendly stuff and delicious pastry

name: Ina Amyza
review: forgot to leave a review for the place that made me fall in love with any pistachio filling! the croissants are flaky and filled, coffee tastes bold which is to our liking. love the ambiance and interior though it is quite crowded and u might have to wait longer for seats and to be attended, but staffs were great regardless and still answer to ur queries!

name: Jen Ticzon
review: Simply amazing. If you are in Venice, this is the only place to get quality coffee. Our family made it a point to make it here every day while we visited. The quality of the espresso and pastries is simply unmatched. For drip, you can even pick your beans, bring it to the counter and they’ll make it for you. Pastries are fresh and when they run out, they’re gone for the day so best to get there earlier if you want to try them all. Brother got a mango smoothie and loved that as well. Friendly staff that help guide everyone through the process of ordering and then paying after you are finished. Helps make you slow down and enjoy— I loved it. The line is nonstop so I think everyone else does as well :) I love everything about Venice but would actually come back just for this coffee shop alone. 20/10 recommendation.

name: Dmytro Sobokar
review: One of the best coffee shops I ever visited. They have great variety of different kinds of coffee beans. I tried everyone and everyone is awesome! Taste is so full of flavour. The beans are amazing quality and they are roasted perfectly. If you are coffee lover you must go here. You can also buy  some beans to have this perfect coffee at home. Extremely recommended.

name: Jenevieve Hornsby
review: We have been here twice and I have a hard time reading it because the food and coffee is good. The style of the place is decorated to be very cute and cozy, but unfortunately, both times we have been it seems like the customer race is a little pushy and rude before they ordered and just overall made the space a little uncomfortable. The reception was a little more welcoming. I think it would be a five star, but the feelings of this place is just off. Pricing of items varies based on if it’s takeaway, eating standing at a counter or sitting down to eat. This can be hard to tell so be aware if you care. So far the cappuccinos I have had here were good and they have the best cereali and wheat croissants I’ve ever had. I definitely recommend this place. I just wish that the vibes there were more friendly.

name: Shruti Dhoot
review: Make sure to join the right queue ‘coz the service can be slow. Need to first order and then pay before leaving. Still dreaming about the pistachio croissant I had here. It was just the right amount of flaky and the filling was delicious. Don’t miss out!

name: Rylan Verge
review:
Excellent spot to go for your coffee and baked good when in Venice. It's best to go early in the morning as it starts to fill up throughout the afternoon. The service staff here are incredibly efficient and are always cleaning the few tables available so that everyone can have the chance to sit down and relax -  very hard workers.

Enjoy your light breakfast by the canal and soak up the sunshine before your big day of exploring!

name: dixie509
review:
I’ve visited 6 coffee shops in Venice. I’m not sure to rank this top or bottom. It’s complicated and personal.

I like flat whites - a rare species in this town. According to smiley barista, only here and ….Starbucks (! Ergh) make them. Great start.

Lots of attention to the coffee, these guys really seem to care. But - and I’m so sad there’s a but! - the milk was, IMO, awful!  The first cup I abandoned after a couple of sips as it tasted like UHT (although the carton suggested otherwise, so maybe semi-skimmed?).

But I really wanted it to work - friendly people, lovely spot - so I ordered my usual, an oat flat white. I checked the carton and was a little nervous as it wasn’t a Barista version….but hey, surely it’ll be ok?  This was almost undrinkable for me. I took one sip and abandoned. Thin, oversweet, horrible. I’d have been better off at Starbucks. :(

I realise (1) I’m in the spiritual home country of the drink I love, so I should tread lightly (2) this was easily the cafe with most potential we visited and (3) coffee is a VERY personal thing - my undrinkable is another persons unbeatable.

But for me, this place has it all, except for the milk. Oh well.

name: Subs Freelance
review: This was a cute coffee place. The coffee was good, freshly brewed. The two flavors we took were average. The red velvet muffin was very soft and well done. The joint was cute and attracted a lot of crowd. The decor was quite unique and attractive.

name: RAYRAY
review: Had an amazing cafe latte with croissant de crema aux fruits before grocery shopping. It was delicious. The lady was very polite. I wasn’t too sure what kind of cafe I’d order since there were plenty of choices available. She recommended right away the cafe latte which I appreciated greatly since I’m an indecisive person. Lots of pastries choices too! I’ll be back.

name: Karl Oskar
review: Very nice place for a coffee. Family owned, and very friendly staff.

name: Humod Fadhl
review: If you’re a tourist they gonna add a lot on the bill we pay 70€ for 5 coffee and 5 croissant

name: Stephanie Vieira
review: Friendly staff. Excellent croissants & espresso varieties

name: Petra Bachrata
review: No complaints.

name: Jessica
review: Im Interspar ist dieses Caffè Veneto zu finden. Für ein Café in einem Supermarkt, macht es schon was her und die Preise sind grundsätzlich fair, wenn die Qualität der Produkte auch besser wäre. Das Essen war lediglich okay und hat teilweise nicht bzw. sehr künstlich geschmeckt. Das Personam war sehr gestresst, obwohl fast niemand vor Ort war. Der Kaffee war ebenfalls nicht gut.

name: Alesja Rusaka
review:
Хорошая кофейня с очень вкусным кофе и выпечкой. Кофе Латте и капучино очень вкусные.
Единственное, не ожидали цену 3,50€ за горячий шоколад. Просили какао, но нас не поняли.

name: Luciano
review: Una garanzia! Vasta scelta di piatti e insalate, componibili anche a piacimento. Ideale per pranzi al volo o aperitivi (c’è anche la formula a buffet nel tardo pomeriggio). Le ampie vetrate davanti ai tavoli danno un’ottima illuminazione. Davvero consigliato!

name: Lama Drebati
review: Got two espresso and paid 4€. It was very reasonable and the espresso was very tasty and strong

name: Avery Watkins
review: Great place to grab a quick snack and a hot beverage. The people are nice a pleasant. Kind of like a nicer airport Starbucks but with more options.

name: Shatha Ali
review: The coffee was great and the service as well. She is nice& friendly! Grazie🤍

name: tia azzuz
review: 👍Desert is delicious 😋😍,  staff is friendly

name: Галина Боровська
review: В кафе багато місцевих відвідувачів,їжа смачна і дуже швидке приготування.Брали пасту з морепродуктів там капучіно.Заклад хоча маленький,проте дуже атмосферний.Розташування чудове)

name: Lisely De León
review:
Cumple su función para
Tomar un café durante tu caminata turística. Tienen promoción de un Aperol Spritz y 3 cicchetti por 10 euros. El café muy rico. La atención excelente. Súper recomendable

name: Tania Leagun
review: Ottimo caffè e il personale gentilissimo!

name: Becky W.
review:
When we arrived in Venice this summer and needed cold soft drinks no supermarkets are open past 7pm.
We asked everywhere, the locals were not very helpful and told us everywhere closes at 7pm.
This Supermarket was closest to our accommodation, when open the next morning, it's a nice shop and seemed reasonably priced and friendly. Just closing time was a problem.

name: Didier Huyberechts
review: This well-maintained supermarket is a good size for Venice and therefore provides a quite reasonable across-the-board selection of essentials: meats, fruits&veg, wines&drinks, household items, etc.

name: chef chua
review: Beautiful chocolate place, nice gift but over priced at tourist place, tast are the same as those bought else where in Europe like laderach or even Lindt.

name: D M
review:
Let’s face it, Venice is quite expensive to eat at a restaurant and not always good either.
And sometimes you just want to have fresh salad using local ingredients, also want to try some local supermarket food. This location is perfect place for this. You have many option

name: Maria Arcuri
review: Small store but good selection of fresh food, wine, and produce.

name: Drew Senior
review:
Staying in an Airbnb so used this supermarket and for a small size there was an amazing choice of everything we needed and more that we then wanted.
Reasonable prices.

name: Christine Madison
review: A wonderful grocery store 5 minutes from Hotel Gardena, Venice, Italy

name: Dean Ku
review: Wonderful little grocery store.  Nice staff.  Too bad no ice.

name: Jojee Khan
review:
Stumbled across the Coop in San Marco (Riva del Carbon) and honestly, it was a total lifesaver. The location is perfect—right in the heart of Venice, just steps away from everything. Super convenient when you're exploring the city and need to grab something quickly.

They’ve got a great variety for a compact store—fresh produce, snacks, drinks, even some ready-made options. Prices were surprisingly reasonable, especially considering it's in such a tourist-heavy area. Definitely not one of those overpriced tourist traps.

Whether you need a quick bite, some groceries, or just a cold bottle of water on a hot day, this Coop delivers on all fronts. Clean, efficient, and well-stocked—exactly what you want in a city like Venice.

name: Lorenzo Quadrelli
review: The Coop near Ponte Rialto offers a pleasant experience overall. The tramezzini  are especially good—fresh, nicely filled, and priced fairly for what you get. Considering the high prices typical of this area, I found the pricing here quite reasonable. A solid choice for a quick bite or snack while exploring, with good quality food and a convenient location.

name: Ece Özmen
review: Fair prices, good location, compact grocery store. Especially wine/prosecco/aperol prices are relatively lower than the other stores in Venezia. There are bigger Coop points in city but this one’s location is advantageous.

name: Dan W
review: DO NOT GO IF YOU ARE A FOREIGNER -ESPECIALLY IF FROM THE USA!  THEY ARE EXTREMELY RUDE.  We Visited this Coop two years ago and had a great experience.  This time when we tried to use Google translator to ask where certain things were located, we were treated very rudely.  We speak French, English, and Spanish - but the girl at the deli counter had a totally negative attitude because we didn't speak Italian.  I recommend going to any other grocery store rather than be treated so poorly.

name: Shane
review: Great food super cheap. We skipped dessert in a restaurant and happened upon this supermarket. Low-and-behold there was a very large piece of tiramisu there for 5 euros! And the croissants are only 1.15 euros!! Fire!

name: Karina Grzywocz
review:
Nice shopping place. A lot of produce to choose from. Near a vaporetto stop so it's really convenient.
You can also buy there baked chicken and potatoes, but I didn't try it.

name: M Shakeeb Ahmed
review:
Coop is convenient but with limited options
The store is clean and easy to access, but compared to nearby Despar the variety of fresh products and cheeses is limited. Good for quick shopping

name: Allan Lim
review:
Busy place with tight aisles but the service was good.
Located very close to the Rialto Vaporetto.

name: Caroline Wood
review: I LOVE this Coop. It is one of the larger Coops in Venice and has a great selection of fruit/veg, meat, CHEESE, chilled and frozen. Also a good range of well priced foodie gifts that make good presents. But the main attraction is the DELI COUNTERS. Wonderful selection of olives, seafood, cheese, cooked meats, Venetian dishes - and you can have exactly as much as you like. Brilliant for building a bespoke lunch or if you are self catering and so much cheaper than eating out - but just as delicious! (in my opinion...) If you like seafood, the Octopus Folpetti alla busara is a MUST. And the staff are so nice!!!!

name: barry haffey
review: Good local supermarket has everything inc beer and wine at decent prices.. Would recommend for groceries if u r doing Venice self catering

name: Alida Stevens
review: Fun to see the local foods, especially the fresh fruits. Great pricing compared to the US.

name: North
review: It was pretty nice to see the local food. I like it when I find products that aren’t available in my country. Some of the staff didn’t know English, but they really tried their best to help us

name: Brian Muchmore
review: A great selection of products and reasonable priced.  The lines cam be long and the service a bit slow, but worth the trip

name: Sebastian Chiripus
review: Well supplied store with friendly staff who are able to communicate in English,  but the only thing is that the prices are higher than other side of Italy 🇮🇹,  that is happening because is a touristic city 🤔

name: Doreen V
review: Good market for basics. Reasonably priced. Close to our flat.

name: Alexander Vargas
review: Has lots of goods, pastries,  meets, cheeses, alcoholic drinks, house holds supplies etc.

name: Paul Vivas
review:
Great supermarket in Cannaregio
We stayed here a few days in February and basically came to this shop every day! It is actually much larger inside than it looks from the outside, this shop is really well stocked up and had everything you can expect for a supermarket.
We really liked it and it comes highly recommended from us.

name: Loong Su
review: Biggest grocery store I been in Venice and very cheap, Staff was very friendly, nice and helpful even though they can't speak English.

name: Clayton Baker
review: Freshly-baked croissants every morning. Excellent variety of produce and pantry items. All-in-one stop for daily or weekly groceries.

name: Jonny Barber
review: Very rude customer service directed at us only ( we belive this to be because we are British tourist). We were basically blocked off going through an aisle by rude staff members who purposely decided to bend down and put a trolley in front of us after waiting to get through. My basket ended up getting stuck. They moved straight after for an Italian couple and were very kind to them afterwards. At the checkout the cashier chucked the food very quickly at us, didn’t say Caio or anything, completely blanked us and was very grumpy and again was very nice to the next Italian customers. Not welcoming at all and not how we treat others where we work.

name: Emir Kadusic
review: Very well supplied, fresh pastries available, as well as fruits and vegetables.

name: Mark Johnson
review: Full service supermarket on Cannaregio neighborhood of Venice. Produce was good quality, always a sign for me.  Tip: you must bag, weigh and label produce sold by weight while in the produce section before checking out. A friendly customer helped us with this.

name: C.J. R
review:
Pretty awesome supermarket, they had everything we needed and toiletries such as shower sponges and Pull-Ups were reasonable prices.

It’s at the end of a residential neighborhood and no signs. I been searching for two other stores with no luck and we started questioning this location as well until i seen someone walking towards me with a bag of groceries. Speaking of bags, bring your own bag or you will have to pay for them at the counter.

name: Kari Q.
review: Awesome store, spacious aisles and extremely clean. Everything you need and cool stuff I've never seen before (horse meat, delicious and lean). Super friendly staff👍👍

name: Otilia Tibljas
review:
Gorgeous building.  Huge plant based section for all your vegan options, located across the isle from the fresh bread.

Prices were very reasonable and the selection was impressive.

name: Bartosz Przewor
review:
DESPAR Teatro Italia is a truly unique grocery store, combining everyday shopping with the charm of a historical landmark. Located in a beautifully restored former theater, this supermarket offers an unforgettable experience with its stunning interiors featuring elegant frescoes, intricate ceilings, and grand chandeliers. The ambiance alone makes it worth a visit!

Beyond its breathtaking decor, DESPAR Teatro Italia impresses with a wide selection of high-quality products, from fresh local produce to gourmet Italian specialties. Whether you’re looking for daily essentials or authentic delicacies, this place has it all. A perfect spot to admire Venetian architecture while enjoying a premium shopping experience!

name: Ralitsa Grebenarova
review:
Wow! Wow! Wow! We had never heard of this store or the fact that it’s housed in an old theater building. We were just walking around and decided to step in, and the surprise was absolutely unique. Definitely super cool! A must-see.

The great thing was that we also found the pasta our Italian guide had recommended!

name: Amanda Williams
review:
Great supermarket, remodel from a theatre. Brilliant range of products to bring home and if you need it fresh counter produce to. You have to scan your receipt to leave.
We bought lovely biscuits and nuts. We later saw the biscuits in the airport and they were much more expensive... 5e more at the airport duty free!

name: Elis Ali
review: We got to shop in the supermarket  and its beautiful. Also you can find anything you need. You can by all your take aways from Italy.

name: Ralf R. Gottschalk
review: Really a very unique place. Shopping food and beverages at a former theater.

name: Clizabeth
review: I really liked this market. It is located in an old theater so it is an interesting space. They had lots of fresh foods and a decent selection of plant based foods.

name: Filip Med
review:
Without an exaggeration hangs down the best looking supermarket I’ve ever been to.

Located in beautiful historic theater building with paintings on the wall and marble pillars.

Everything is super well organized and clean with good selection of items for affordable prices.

Staff was also very helpful and nice.

Absolutely 100% recommended :)

name: SIPRA VIAGGI CARPI Sipra viaggi carpi
review: SIPRA VIAGGI CARPI INDIRIZZO VIA DORANDO PIETRI 25CARPI I have to go there every day. My shop is near it and I am very fond of drinking milk. So I go there every day to buy fresh milk and this is a very good store.

name: Darlene jackson
review: 10/16/23. Arrived from Paris, circulated by all the water, I needed to get refreshments! I found cooked foods and beer and vodka! I ready!! Enjoy!

name: Manuel Roth
review:
Rain storm in Venice.
Most unfriendly supermarket in Italy!!! Conad San Basilio.
The rain was pouring down in torrents, we had paid our food, when the "security" threw us out in the street. We risked being soaked when the guy repeatedly INSISTED of throwing us out of the empty shop. UNBELIEVABLE

name: Rick Dimont
review: For the most part, very helpful staff,  has a large selection of foods, and fabulous deli. We usually shop there since we rent apartments for several weeks while in Venice and like to cook in. But one afternoon, I ran into one young nasty woman cashier. Don't know what her issue was,  but i won't let that ruin my review of Conad.

name: M. MAIZER
review: Fresh and good priced food(affordable), also polite and helpfull employees.

name: Charles the First
review:
Lots of items for packing for your picnic or vacation last minute items. Compact yet full of necessary food and drink items as well as fresh fruit and deli section.
Saw them unloading from tractor trailer which was very innovative solution.

name: Yolande Nardi
review: We shop here every week as Australians Living Abroad. Good choice and staff usually helpful. Great view from the check-out queue at Zattere.

name: Joshua Mensch
review: Good prices, wide selection of goods, fresh veggies

name: Cam
review: Great option for getting ready meals, salads or fruits & veggies in general. Their own brand big-sized salad had lettuce, corn, cherry tomates and Olives and was 1,99. Hot meals were around 5 or 6 euros, and they have a microwave for heating if needed. Self check out worked very fast, and staff were friendly and helpful.

name: Chris Who
review:
Small useful place for some bits and pieces, but very limited choice as it is rather small.
Has everything you expect from a corner shop type place, so small groceries, small selection of toiletries, crisps, snacks, drinks (alcoholic and soft drinks - soda to those from the US) sweets (candy for Americans) and even some fresh produce.

name: Skywalker Feng
review: A small "supermarket" conveniently located by the famous Rialto Bridge. It has a nice selection of daily groceries, and opens 8am in the morning. The staff are friendly and professional, quickly helped me to put labels on the fruit which I forgot to weight.

name: Michael McRoberts
review:
Probably one of the better smaller supermarkets we found in Venice.  despair actually had fruit and vegetables and a reasonable selection of produce.

Worth checking out if your in the area!

name: Samantha Marais
review: Great store but maybe the cashier should learn not to grab things out of customers hands and rolling her eyes at people after dropping their products.

name: AJ Chacon
review: Out of country tourist here. I'm not familiar with Italian shopping, the first time was here. Older gray haired woman was extremely rude. I am sure they are sick of tourists who have no idea how to shop Italian stores. Let me share with you so you don't get scolded by her. First, you grab your basket BEFORE going through the flaps(I didn't see them cause I was mother bird focused on my 2 little boys). I went to look for one, and she quickly scolded me, i wasn't going to go back through, but I was looking for a basket. Now, produce. Was only going to grab what i was buying, and before I laid a finger on one, she was on me like a fly...put on a glove, then grabbed a bag for produce to go in. All of these interactions I was huffed at. Mind you, I keep apologizing. Guess(once at counter, the girl was frustrated with me cause I didn't weigh my produce), so you weigh each one and put the sticker on the bag. I am very nice, considerate, and smile at everyone. I feel so belittled after leaving here. I didn't feel the least bit welcomed whatsoever. Just a smile would do, not even that. This surely is not a tourist store. If you don't care about getting scolded like a little kid, feel free. I personally won't come back here again.

name: Grace McP
review:
Great place to pick up essentials like water/drinks if you’re staying in the area as a tourist like us.

Staff were friendly and a good range of fresh food, drinks, bathroom products and snacks.

name: debjani singha
review: This is my favorite grocery store when we were visiting Venice. The food quality and price is perfect and rightly done. For tourists if you don't want to pay a high price for food this is your store. The attendants were very courteous.

name: Roland Marča
review: This store is a complete tourist scam. They charge 5 euros for a single can of Monster Energy, which is outrageous. When you question the price and tell them it is a scam, they change the price. The staff clearly takes advantage of tourists who might not notice. Definitely avoid this place — there are plenty of honest shops nearby where you won’t get ripped off.

name: mark mc
review:
A good little supermarket.
If you want a break from the inflated venice lunch menus, this little supermarket has everything ypu need to make your own lunch where you can sit in the square and enjoy while listening to the music and enjoying the views.

Fruit, bread, cold meats, cheese, olives, crips, choclolate.
All the potential ingredients to keep you going while you explore the island.

name: Flo Yeow
review: Hidden at a random corner amongst one of the many lanes of Venice, this supermarket is well stocked, and does the job it is supposed to. It's near to San Marko square, so you can swing by if you need anything. We got in, grabbed a couple of bottles of water and some sweets, and left shortly after.

name: Patryk O.
review: Tourist trap, look at receipt before paying, tried to scam me twice. First time for 4 euros when I caught it and said the price is wrong he changed it but 2nd time it was 1 euro too much. I left after that

name: S F
review:
Opens until 2300

Prices a bit high, but probably understandable due to the location

name: Hilton OConnor
review: Handy convenience store - open till  23:00 or even later - just bought fresh milk after sightseeing and relaxing at St Mark's Square. Cold Water as usual is €0.4 extra - you don't find this policy in my country South Africa.

name: Den Newbold-Powell
review: Great selection of snacks, beverages, fruit. prices are reasonable for being in a tourist area. staff very helpful.

name: Greg T
review: Tourists look.at.your receipt we.bought a bunch of water and cans of soda for kids bought 1 fanta was charged for 4. Didn't want to give a receipt but didn't look at it until the next day. Beware of this place! Expensive also!

name: Tata Gryff
review: Surprisingly quite a large store almost in the center of Venice, very conveniently in it you can buy almost everything you need. The prices are good, but the bread is very expensive, which amazes me. Great sandwiches, pastries, all kinds of sausages and a large selection of takeaway food

name: mark mc
review:
Great store

Much larger than expected and has everything you need for either dinner of lunch.

name: Andrew Hancock
review: Shop generally good but packaged fruit in cool store immediately on right as enter always past its date or very short date.

name: Michael Offord
review: Do not Despar, for the finest mini market in Venice is here.

name: Raider Giant
review: Easy to get to from Palazzo Paruti. Great place to go for anything you need!

name: Jas & Deb Nicolson
review: Great supermarket, excellent prices considering this is Venice.

name: Dustin Jones
review: Good grocery store in Venice. New, two entrances and everything was clean and reasonable prices, would recommend.

name: Sebastiano T.
review:
Good to have a small supermarket in the heart of the city.
Plenty of options and also a nice selection of ready made food.
Prices of course are more expensive than in big malls outside of Venice historic town, but it's more than justified by the convenience of having a supermarket at your door steps.

name: Olga Duka
review:
Largest shop on the island, great cheese and meets choice, wonderful seafood section and so on and so forth. The only place working on a Sunday (till 21-00), if you forgot your shopping.

google maps pin is incorrect, you need to go down the street a little bit further to OVS on the corner.

name: A. E. F.
review: What the Google shows for the shop, is only a sitting park with benches. You should be more precisely with writing the address

name: Linda Floris
review: I found this place super crowded at all times, over priced and tight ailes, shopping here was extremely uncomfortable for me due to so many people shopping here all at the same time all the time, the staff however were very nice and helpful.

name: Marko Unt (Mirx)
review: !!!NOT A REAL LOCATION!!! Google maps shows its wrong but i uploded an image showing wheres actually the shop and i hope this helps you who are you reading to find the shop. Or you can just put to search “OVS, Granviale Santa Maria Elisabetta, 39, 30126 Venezia VE, Italy” But atleast normal shop with low prices and located in the city centre! 🇪🇪

name: Árni Meistari
review:
Conad City is a fantastic grocery store that exceeded my expectations during my recent shopping experience. I was particularly impressed by their wide selection of fresh produce and pantry staples. The convenience and variety of products available made my visit enjoyable and efficient.

The chocolate ice cream I picked up from Conad City was a delightful treat. It had a rich and satisfying flavor that instantly won me over. It’s clear that Conad City prioritizes quality, as evidenced by the freshness of their products.

Moreover, the staff at Conad City were exceptionally friendly and helpful. They were knowledgeable about their inventory and eager to assist with any inquiries. Their positive attitude added to the overall pleasant ambiance of the store.

Conad City is now my go-to grocery store. Whether you’re looking for everyday essentials or special treats like chocolate ice cream, Conad City is sure to meet your needs with top-notch quality and service. I highly recommend paying them a visit!

name: Jamie Terrell
review: Excellent little supermarket, great selection of products 👌

name: Behrooz Ebrahimi
review: The prices are OK, and you'll be able to find about everything there and they close at 9 which is a good thing because all other stores will be close by 8 pm. They have fresh fruit and and fairly good collection of wines and some good discounts on them.

name: Spencer Hawken
review:
Nice big store located underneath the OVS store.

Went in to try the other half a hair brush, ended up buying much more. Loads of selection and potions if things to buy, including a lot of food you don’t find on the main island.

At peak times the store can be VERY busy, we went about 6.30pm and the queues for the tills were 20 people deep.

name: Chris Who
review:
Good place.  Has most things you need if visiting Venice.  We bought food , paper plates, ice cube bags, drinks etc.  Has a good fresh produce section too.
Bought wine, beer, some meats and even a bag as well as everything else and came to about 50 euros.
So not cheap but that is Venice for you.  Nothing is cheap here so this is a good option for the budget conscious.
The staff were very friendly and even other customers were... I guess they are used to having to deal with tourists... And they do it well.
I didn't get any photos but the general vibe of the place is really light airy and fresh.
Will probably go back before we leave for some more bits.

name: Aarani Sahaj
review: Was visiting Venice for a week and we came here frequently to get groceries for our cooked meals. Good prices, especially on alcohol. Everything was always in stock and there was tons of variety. Vegetables, fruit, and bread were all great quality too

name: Stephanie
review:
Large supermarket for all your grocery needs. There’s a deli section and some prepackaged sandwiches if you just want a quick bite. They also have some toiletries in case you need shampoo or personal hygiene items. Prices seem fair.

It’s located on a busy “tourist” street that leads to San Marco so it’s a nice place to grab a water or drink as you’re walking around. It’s very well air conditioned for an added bonus.

name: Vita Shaikhulina
review: Large supermarket on a tourist street.  Here you can buy products at normal prices.  No overpricing for tourists.  There is even shampoo and shower gel.  There are also different foods, fish, meat, sugar, spices, cereals, fruits and vegetables.  Worth coming here to buy food to take home.

name: Ozan Sozuoz
review: Extremely rude cashiers. The 2 ladies forced me to remove my items from the cart without explanation. Good prices but use self checkout.

name: Nassim Jammal
review: Very unpleasant experience with the cashier. The blonde employee at the register was extremely rude, tossing our items onto the conveyor belt simply because we didn’t know they had to be weighed beforehand.

name: Jack Lindblom
review: After 5 days of the Italian diet, my American digestion was languishing from lack of meat.  The rotisserie chicken here was a godsend that literally saved my life.  6/5 stars, will absolutely return.

name: Anh Dang
review: Great location for tourists. Many wine and snack options at reasonable prices. Self checkout / exit security may not be a good idea if you don’t speak Italian.

name: Heather Morrison
review: My nephew bought a 23 euro bottle of wine which was a Coop product. The cork was so hard that it broke 2 cook screws, leaving part of one stuck in the bottle, so you could not possibly get the cork out. He took it back to them with the receipt & they refused to replace it. I have always shopped there  as it is near to where I stay. I was very disssapointed in the lack of customer service, & will not be recommendingit to any future visitors.

name: Brian Muchmore
review:
It is very convenient if you are staying nearby.  Better selection early in the morning.

The prices seem to be quite reasonable for Venice.

name: Matej
review: Nice Coop grocery shop. It looks small, however, it's big inside. If you don't want to spend money on bars and restaurants this is the right place to go. Outside are some benches to sit on.

name: Howard Norton
review: Helpful, friendly staff, well stocked, reasonable prices for Venice.

name: Aleksandra Budnik (AMBudnik)
review: People have issues with opening the door to leave the shop and there is no juice. Besides that, everything is great.

name: Stathis Amoi
review: loved it! i loved the layout of the store inside, and the selection of products, especially 'little snacks' and specialized snacks. it was actually a pleasant surprise, that fact! quality of course, at ok prices, i mean you can expect it won't be particularly cheap. very good service, very friendly staff, which is also important to me. go shop!

name: Angela Barlow
review: Sma coop but can get an ice cream on hot weather.

name: Kaz F
review: Although the wait time at the till can be very long, this is the only place where you can buy chilled mineral water, 500ml bottle, at 0.60Euro! Don’t get ripped off in other places!!

name: Kurt Schulte
review: nice little grocery store but was hard to see you can walk right by it

name: Daniel Whitehurst
review: Grabbed some snacks and breakfast items here. Cashier was very nice and helped us figure out we were supposed to grab labels for our fruit before going to self checkout.

name: Chelsea Poole
review: The cashier automatically bagged everyone else's groceries, but didn't give us a bag. When I asked for one, he charged for it (which is fine), but then wouldn't give me change for a twenty which was the smallest bill I had. So we walked off with no bag, carrying our groceries in hand.

name: Christopher Carmichael
review: Very nice store for staples. Remember, on two levels. Prices are similar to US. Store brand is good. Be nice, staff is great. Don’t be the rude American

name: Charles Cuny
review: It's a small grocery by many people's standards, but it's actually pretty good size for Venice. Here's the trick: as you enter, immediately look to your left, and notice that there are stairs up to the second level. When you include the (somewhat more upscale) second level, this is a five-star grocery store by Venice standards. Good prices and good selection

name: Joaquin Rodas
review: Small supermarket with two floors of merchandise. Upstairs fresh products milk, cheeses, ham, etc, downstairs bread, coffee, jams, fruit & veggies. Staff very busy but somewhat helpful. Prices are Ok. Since we are doing Airbnb getting breakfast for the kids beats any restaurant around here.

name: Karl Burgess
review: I am so endebted to the staff at this shop.   I lost my wallet yesterday and was in a panic as I was returning home to UK today..! on retracing my steps this morning I found that I had left my wallet in the store.  It was returned intact and I can not say how happy I am.   During my shopping trip the staff were polite, efficient and welcoming... But if you add to that the qualities of honesty and integrity then you have got superb staff.... Thanks again and I shall return to Venice again and shop in your store

name: Rob Musci
review: Pretty big supermarket with a wide selection of deli meats, meat, and poultry on the second floor. They also have good selection of cheeses. Produce is always lacking with the Punto Simply stores.

name: Ururka Ururu
review: You can enter the market from two sides. There is a lot of nice and affordable food. It's very clean inside. One of the entrance is next to lot of place where you can sit down and eat your snacks without bothering people walking around. There is also no crowds there and it's in shadow.

name: Robin
review: Bought our real Italian pasta here to take home. It's way cheaper and tastes amazing compared to UK pasta.

name: Debra Haylett
review: Great supermarket,nice size with a really good deli meat section where we had fresh salami and ham cut to order.Prices a lre good as well. Views from inside the supermarket pretty good as well

name: Merry El
review: This review is for the owner of the supermarket, last night 04/29/25  I had a horrible experience with the cashier women a hour before the closing , she was very mean, rude , racist and abusing, it was my first day and first time visiting Italy from USA and things are different here that we don’t know , she was very rude throwing the bag of bananas just because I should weight them myself , and she insulted me just because I didn’t asked for plastic bag , well we don’t weigh groceries but cashier does and  plastic bags are free to put the shopping , how we will know all this if it’s first time trying a supermarket in Italy , instead of she says it’s okey and show me how things are here , she started insulting me and she was aggressive and racist , horrible experience and employee like this will destroy the business and she doesn’t want know that customer is king and how to treat tourists , please look at the camera , I wanted to call the police and call USA embassy but I decided to ignore all this and finish my vacation . I never saw a rude racist cashier like her in any country in the world.  God bless America they have the best customer service .

name: Sarah Ryan
review: This place is amazing if you are coeliac and need to eat gluten free or even if you have to eat lactose free, they have so much! They even have pre-made/pre-packed gluten free sandwiches which can be usually impossible to find. Just look for the packages that has on it senza glutine. Great shop!

name: ABIR ISLAM
review: Despar is a great place to shop for fresh produce, quality groceries, and unique local finds. The staff is always friendly and helpful, and the store is well-organized and clean. I especially appreciate the wide selection of organic and natural products. I highly recommend Despar for your next grocery shopping trip!

name: Nikolai
review: Nice, very little place where you get everything that you need. You can also get some italian food which is of high quality and you need not go to the restaurants even for breakfast. nice ona and staff.

name: Shin H.
review: The place is small, not much choice, and the staff are't friendly. Not like other Despar stores at all -- but maybe that's just the area...

name: sandy colombo
review:
OMG worth buying something just to visit this Italian beautiful supermarket - just amazing

Always good to visit a supermarket in Italy but this one is extra special

And of course you get better deals than buying from a cafe or our in the street

I also wanted just some fruit for lunch …something fresh and good for me - rather than bread pasta or cheese

Don’t get me wrong it’s great ….but sometimes you just need some fruit !

Anyway grabbed some food for the train and a few drinks - low cost and really interesting place just to visit

Love a supermarket particularly an italian supermarket

Just around the corner from our hotel - Ai Mori d Driente …also good location !

Go mad as it’s hard not too in Italy !

name: Kevin McCabe
review: Nice little spot right near the train station. Note: it is tight and narrow. If you have luggage you will be crowding the aisles. Pretty much anything you would want for a ride on the train or to take back to a hotel/apartment. They had plenty of premaid sandwiches etc. and a lot of beer/wine to select from at typical Italian cheap prices. Good spot to know about before taking a long train trip.

name: Rina G
review: Very unfriendly, rude, scary manager who shouts at you if you accidentally enter store too early not knowing it's not yet open. Unfriendly apathetic staff. Never enough people serving. Always long lines. Produce is satisfactory but customer service zero. Never a smile from any of them except one young man on one of our  visits and one nice lady at the deli counter. Otherwise miserable staff who do not like tourists. The irony 🤔 Recommend the more professional, better theatre supermarket further down 👍

name: Paco Oceano
review: Good market, lot of things. But longs queues sometimes

name: Patrick Schnell
review: So refreshing to be smiled at and greeted by  staff. The lady at the deli counter was very friendly and helpful as was the cashier, not fed up by the constant stream of tourists. Great place to go to get food at very reasonable prices. Best way to visit Venice would be to stay in a self catering Airbnb place and nip to this store to stock up olives, salads, meats, cheeses, treats and sweets, soft and harder drinks as well as all the other bits n bobs one generally needs on a daily/weekly basis.

name: Theo P
review: Very excellent customer service! Easy pay with Google pay register. Very good selection of food products, fresh, frozen, and deli counter of all kinds. Very reasonable prices. Super clean store.

name: George StandingBear
review: Grocery store if you need it, got some water and snacks. Open from 0800 to 2030 M-SA...

name: DNL GRMN
review: Good supermarket for when your on the island and is in a rush. Various ready to eat food options to grab and go. Besides the regular supermarket products.  Very convenient location

name: Christopher Guerra
review: Avoid if you’re a foreigner. The workers appear to dislike foreigners very much. I was shopping and used the restroom that was available, and was yelled at by multiple employees for using the restroom. Me and my wife felt very hostile after that encounter and rushed out of the store.

name: Tryfon Vigos
review: Take care to check your change.  The cashier short-changed us by 15 Euro, and we lost nearly an hour from our day waiting for them to accept their error.  In the end, I am sad to think it must have been deliberate.

name: Cezary Kosiński
review: The prices are quite high, but there are many things to choose from. Also, the opening hours are great.

name: DGR
review: Decent store, but a bit pricey.  Hard to find anything less expensive around, so that's just the going rate for convenience.  They had a lot of grocery items.  We cooked in our air bnb one night and got everything we needed for a dinner for 6. We also got snacks for the apartment for the week.

name: Dave Kennedy
review: Decent little store with lots of options in a small space. One of the only stores open to 10:30pm. Just be prepared to overpay heavily for the “convenience”.

name: Gigia TravelCat
review: I met my competition!

name: nora
review: high prices, small store but has all the essentials + is open for quite a long time. it’s okay, the staff is okay.👍

name: Bodhi Goldstein
review: Terrible the workers were so rude

name: Karielle Stephanie
review: In an otherwise very quiet and rather empty neighborhood, Tuday Conad was a lifesaver for stocking up on essentials like snacks, bottled water, fruit, and toiletries! Reasonable prices and friendly and efficient staff. The outside is unassuming but the inside is pretty spacious with everything you need, standard European chain convenience store. It was surprisingly difficult to find a store like this in the area so I’m very glad this was near my hotel.

name: Terence Narainan
review: Convenient little store on the water in the quieter part of Venice stocking a variety of goods

name: Gregory Tolston
review: Nice little store, good selection of food, friendly service, conveniently located.

name: Berlin Gal
review:
Fantastic selection of groceries including fresh fish but the cashier I encountered today was incredibly rude. Did not stop chatting to the local customer even when I asked for a bag (in Italian), and treated her very kindly. She ignored me, even when I paid and thanked her - she continued her chat. Felt like she is talking over my head. If staff does not like interacting with customers she might be better off refilling shelves only.
I shop here regularly, but today was v unpleasant.

name: Cloudswiper
review: I nearly wept when I stumbled across this entry. Such fond memories of Tuday Conad from when I stayed just around the corner. Introduced me to primitivo wine that seemed cheaper than water but tasted like Italian summer. Always had everything we needed for lunch and dinner. Never made me feel like an outsider. Grazie mille 😘

name: S Apte
review: Decent selection of fresh and dry goods. The cashier lady was very lovely and kind when I accidentally dropped my phone here and kept it safe for me until I got back an hour later. Many thanks to her!

name: Jina Bajkor
review: Has everything you need at descent price.
Affordable prices and relatively good choices

name: Md Ariful Islam
review: I had a fantastic experience at the grocery store! The owner was incredibly friendly and helpful, and the store was well-organized, making my shopping trip a breeze. They had a wide selection of fresh produce and products, and I left with everything I needed. Overall, a great place to shop!

name: Ken Barron
review: Very kind gentleman working here, very fair prices and open until late. 5 stars.

name: Lys N.
review: Six tall bottles of water and a small esta thé cost us 9.50€ 🥹

name: MAHADI HASAN
review: Large variety of food, drinks and items, great prices, helpful staff👌❤️❤️

name: Maciej Syguda
review: Best place in Venice. Number One store here. Nicest owner in the city. Hope to see next year ❤️

name: Dumitru Cutie
review: Bro just gave such positive vibe, recommend this shop A+++

name: Shane Aebischer
review: Absolutely fantastic! I was in Venice for a week and went to this shop every day.  Since I'm still awake very late in the evening, it's perfect to buy a few more beers and snacks!  The staff is also extremely friendly and nice.  The store is very clear and clean, no bad smells. I also think the prices are very fair.  Conclusion: Definitely recommended!!  Kind regards to Star Di ;)

name: Lucy
review: Friendly service, reasonable prices for Venice. Offers exactly what you'd expect from the name.

name: Inna Syzonenko
review:
It doesn't exist here. Look for Super Venecia a bit further north on the Google maps!
Took us several circles around this place and 3  locals to finally figure out where ConAd City  really is.

name: Aidan Connolly
review:
The map location is currently wrong, it’s actually way to the north of where it appears. Look at the screenshot I attached.
Took us ages to figure out after asking three people for directions and seeing a dozen other people walk down the random dark alley, I submitted an edit so hopefully it get la fixed soon.

name: Kelly T
review: 1 of the cashiers treats customers differently. Pls be aware and ask for the public to help translate if you are not speaking their language.

name: Shin H.
review:
The CONAD CITY that used to be further south near the Grand Canal has closed, and it seems to have reopened here instead. Since it wasn’t on Google Maps, I went ahead and added it.
The store is small, but the quality of the deli food is good, and most importantly the staff are very friendly and helpful. so I’ve been choosing to shop here.

name: Aleksandra Miletic
review: Good offer of products, taking into consideration that it is located in the oldest part of Venice it is not expensive at all.

name: hj Han
review: A cashier was so kind and checked a bag of apples closely whether they were good or bad. He was pretty impressive and I think he is the one who runs the CONAD that must thanks him. I really appreciate him. Being kind makes someone's day.

name: Anja Bohatschek
review:
This shop is not here anymore!
(However for water you can also go to the beauty shop next to it ;) )

name: Chegothy W
review: Incredibly rude staff on 26/03/25 but I will say the selection was good for such a small space.

name: M Chu
review: Conveniently located near the Hyatt Centric, we stopped in here a few times to pick up snacks. Fair prices and selection of choices. We did not find any cold drinks like we were looking for though nor too many candies to take back for family and friends.

name: Kevin McCabe
review: This CoOp was two stories! Perfect little spot for all your household (or hotel room) needs. Was happy to find it open till 19.00 on Christmas Eve. What a relief. And we were all pleasantly surprised by the size of the place and the offerings on hand. Prices were normal (of course wine was 1/3 the price as in Ireland 🙄) and there is plenty of fresh fruit and vegetables on the first floor. Along with a butcher and fresh baked goods. We all loved the variety of sweets available. Especially during Christmas time! If/when in Murano again, this is where I’ll come for groceries of any sort. Staff was friendly too!

name: UnDiplomatic Wife
review: This place saved us from buying expensive sparkling water bottles from our hotel. We also bough a few snacks like this amazingly delicious popcorn (see pic). A 10 min walk from our hotel in Murano (NH collection)

name: Karen Wand
review: large bottles of water for 0.30€ as opposed to  4€ if you get at restaurant. ice cold peronis for 2€ each

name: David Barder
review: Conviently located near the Hyatt. Reasonable prices for water and juices. Nice little supermarket.

name: Pj deButler
review: Great supermarket in Murano. Excellent produce, small wine selection, cheese, meat and fish.

name: Lucas Humphrey
review: Everything we needed, some parts were a bit untidy and the floor was dirty but most was clean

name: e tradecom (e-tradecom)
review: Pretty good supermarket in the area. On holiday here and bought some local wine and beer here at decent price. Got everything we needed for meals and breakfast + other stuff. We didn’t to weight the vegetables and some fruits we picked up, the lady at the checkout went and helped us to weight and put the price on the items which is really helpful. Opened until 7:30pm. There is lift to go to 2nd floor.

name: Alex Andru
review: Great variety of products, the fresh cooked meats and the cheese variety are vast and delicious. Had a lovely evening night at our accommodation on the terrace sipping prosecco and enjoying some home made bàcari with ingredients bought from there. Their coop line of products is high quality. Spent about 70 euros for 4 full bags of groceries.

name: Renata Škovroňová
review: Good place to make grocery shopping in Venezia but dogs cannot enter. I couldn’t stay between two doors with my dog (and two months old baby in 30+degrees)! there is no shade in front of the shop. The stuff was very rude.

name: Linda Marzolla
review: Lots of grocery with good prices

name: Kadir Durdu
review: That’s really good fresh pizza and cheaper in this town. I highly recommend it.

name: Sarah de Villiers
review: Wish there were more Coops in the Castello Sestiere! This coop is well-stocked, and has space to move around, much better than the one on Via Garibaldi. The entrance is in a beautiful place, just of Fondamenta Nove.

name: John Andrews
review: Well stocked shop with reasonable and fair prices. Helpful staff. Added bonus: a customer toilet!

name: C C
review: A great selection of goods. Friendly staff.

name: Mark Graham
review: Had everything we needed and the team there were super helpful.

name: Proper Sale
review:
I shopped there three years ago, rude staff. I shopped there three years later, very rude and unhelpful staff. It’s just not worth going there if you’re a tourist, they hate tourists and they let you know that they hate tourists.
Take a vaporetto to Coop Querini CASTELLO instead. It’s friendly to tourists, wide isles, well stocked and clean. The staff smile at you, they help you out, they welcome you and they bid you goodbye when you go. Just what a shop should be.

name: Thom Uber
review: BEST GROCERY STORE IN VENICE. Arrive at 8.30am when they open for the choice items.

name: Sebastian Weißinger
review: The supermarket on the more secluded corner of Venice is close to the Biennale (Giardini) and convinces with its size.  At the same time, it has to be said that it is better if you know what you want, as the aisles are very narrow and turning around is hardly an option.  It has everything you can imagine and is relatively cheap.  Unfortunately, there were few chilled drinks, but that wasn't a problem.

name: Maciej Gurgul
review: Great supplies, nice choice. Everything you need and good prices. Arrive just before closing for 50% discounted ready made food.

name: David Ross
review: This has to be one of the worst designed convenience stores in Western Europe; possibly the world. It also tends to have fruit and vegetables that cannot be worthy of the name "fresh fruit and vegetables ". However, since it is the only one in this area of Venice, it is also the best in this part of Venice!

name: Olivia Fösleitner
review: Nice and clean, but quite small selection

name: John Andrews
review: Good store with variety of goods, everything you might need at reasonable prices. I cannot understand why some other reviewers thought the staff and local people are rude. My wife and I have been using the shop for about six years (we stay in Venice three times a year using an apartment nearby) and have always found the people polite, friendly and helpful. I guess people are as you treat them - show them respect and consideration and they reciprocate.

name: Christina Barton
review: The variety is alright, the opening times are good, great location. However it gets extremely busy in the small space, the supply of groceries runs out extremely quickly, and on most occasions I've visited the staff have been very snappy and rude. I get it's a hard place to work, but it makes visitors uncomfortable and creates unnecessary stress.

name: Marta D
review:
Good mini market.
It has everything we need.

name: Vito
review: Giuseppe is the best. He helped us to refund an item in no time. Get him a promotion asap! Very friendly.

name: Doc Explorations
review: It was a nice minimarket. The cashier was kind. We went over in the evening so I guess most people were getting off and they were about to close as the lines were long and it was full of people,  but overall they kept the lines moving.  We bought soap and bug spray.

name: Reilly JeckerByrne
review: Very nice little grocery, good selection for it's size. Lines can get long, all 4 times I went they only had 1 person working the registers.

name: David Oltmanns
review: Charming neighborhood grocery store, we purchased fruits, cheese cold cuts and wine. Neighbors and staff helped us navigate our purchases

name: Charity Loput
review: Quality food, groceries. Probably better than some of the tourist restaurants in town. Save yourself the expense, I highly recommend getting some prosciutto é formaggio here.

name: Nathan Biette
review: The cashier casually tried to steal from us by pretending we gave her 10€ bill when we actually handed her a 20€ bill .

name: Empty Gaze
review: Good selection of groceries and nice staff :)

name: M
review: Good Market, large selection of bread, large selection of meat, interesting delicatessen. Much larger selection than in other stores in the city.

name: F'burg Foodie
review: We stopped in to take a look at what the local grocery store was like.  They had plenty of fresh veggies, meats, and cheeses.  Lots of various sizes and style of pastas as well.  Ended up buying some dried pasta to bring home.  Easy check-out and they offer bags to carry your groceries too.

name: Viviana Beltrametti Walker
review: Never had an issue with staff rudeness - been coming here for years when staying nearby in Venice. Good selection given the small size of the store and the quality is good.

name: Eng H Ooi
review: Very rude staff and they were very unhelpful. We were visiting Venice and popped in to get some stuff on our way back to our rental stay. The cashier was rude to use and seemed annoyed that we were buying groceries and drinks. When we were asking the staff inside for help with buying meat and cheese they were not helpful. I get the feeling they were like that because we are tourists. I would not recommend visiting this place unless you were desperate.

name: Cooper
review: Very rude staff and will try to skimp you out of your correct change, if you pay cash. I would avoid, even if paying with card, the staff are very rude and act like they don't want customers.

name: Alessandra Camargo
review: Nice little market has groceries and all necessities, aisles are really small though and it gets crowded

name: Frank Henningsen
review: Nice little shop. They had everything we needed. Staff is patient and friendly. Good choice.

name: Corey Gagnon
review: Arrived at the shop 15 minutes prior to closing. Some staff very helpful where others made us feel very uncomfortable grabbing a few items. We were still out of the store before their official closing time of 9pm but cashier was telling my husband to shut up and they were treating my young children who were acting accordingly in the store as if they were an inconvenience.
